title: Advanced Training: Fresh Snow
abstract: Explore the latest Snowflake features released over the past 12 months in this fast-paced workshop, tailored for experienced users. Immerse yourself in Snowflake’s newest features and broaden your working knowledge of Snowflake and how it fits in your use cases and solutions. You'll need to purchase the training package in your attendee portal by clicking on "Manage Your Add-Ons" or "Get Certified At Summit".

title: Beginner Training: Snowflake Foundations
abstract: This course covers the foundational concepts, design considerations and Snowflake best practices intended for stakeholders who will be working on the Snowflake AI Data Cloud. This course is a prerequisite for the role-based courses: Administering Snowflake, Snowflake Data Engineering or Snowflake Data Science. You'll need to purchase the training package in your attendee portal by clicking on "Manage Your Add-Ons" or "Get Certified At Summit".

title: Data for Good Hackathon
abstract: Kick off your Summit experience by joining data experts, Snowflake partners and Data Cloud evangelists for a hands-on hackathon on June 2. Work with an international team of Summit attendees to build tools and data visualizations to deliver data-driven insights for humanitarian emergencies. Learn from researchers and experts from NGOs like Direct Relief about the importance of geospatial data in humanitarian response. Snowflake's End Data Disparity initiative accelerates progress toward the UN Sustainable Development Goals by ensuring data sets, decision-making tools and AI models represent everyone. Participants will explore geospatial analytics for humanitarian efforts, perform data transformation in Snowflake and create interactive visuals with Streamlit. The Data for Good team will share insights and tools in future workshops, fostering collaborations that benefit communities and the planet. Space is limited and preregistration is required. If you are interested in Hackathon, please log in to your Attendee Portal and answer the Hackathon interest survey.

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)

speakers.bio: Karen Beaune currently serves as the VP of EMEA Marketing at Snowflake, bringing over two decades of marketing experience and a wealth of expertise to the role. She also leads the End Data Disparity movement as Chair of the Expert Panel. From her early days fuelling regional marketing at Genesys Conferencing, Karen has been dedicated to uncovering people's strengths and fostering a team culture grounded in mutual support and acceptance of differences. Karen is passionate about building teams and developing talents in fast-growing organizations like Polycom and Palo Alto Networks.

With experience in analytics, healthcare administration, international public health, and cloud data architecture, Mike has a unique perspective and capability to build new strategic partnerships. He has worked with life science companies, healthcare providers, NGOs, and health agencies within state government to expand their analytics capabilities and build a firm data foundation to accelerate their work.

Dr. Andrew Schroeder is the Vice President of Research and Analysis at Direct Relief and the Co-Director of the CrisisReady project with Harvard Data Science Initiative. His work focuses on data-driven approaches to crisis response and health and has been featured in publications such as Science, The Lancet, The New York Times, and The Washington Post. He has served as an advisor to UNDP and the World Food Programme. Andrew co-founded the Covid-19 Mobility Data Network, a global initiative for real-time digital epidemiology , and is the Co-Founder of WeRobotics.org, a nonprofit supporting robotics applications for humanitarian aid and global health in 40+ countries.

Fawad is a results-driven technology leader with 20+ years of experience across 50+ countries. He excels in enterprise data warehouses, big data analytics, and shared economy business models. As Global Field CTO, Fawad advises C-level executives across diverse sectors on leveraging data and technology to drive success. His expertise in data monetization, data as a service, and data products enables him to develop innovative strategies that fuel revenue growth. Fawad is passionate about sustainability and utilizes data and analytics to achieve impactful business outcomes and foster positive environmental change.

Michael is a data scientist on the Research and Analysis team at Direct Relief. He holds a BS in Environmental Studies and a PhD in Geography, both from the University of California, Santa Barbara. Prior to working at Direct Relief Michael was a professor of Geography at Santa Barbara City College.

Farhan Choudhary is a Senior Manager, Product Marketing and Evangelist at Snowflake India. He brings over a decade of experience in data science, ML, and AI after having worked extensively on engagements with Fortune 500s, Pub Sec, and startups. Prior to Snowflake, Farhan has worked with Gartner, IL&FS, EY in a practitioner, industry analyst and advisory capacity. He has guided several global organisations on their priorities around AI Strategy, Governance, Responsible AI, Automation, to name a few. At Snowflake, he helps organisations leverage the power of the data cloud, drives developer programs, influencer relations, product positioning and thought leadership for the India market.

title: GROUP BY US - Central
abstract: Join fellow attendees at the U.S.–Central meetup. Meet your peers, get to know local community leaders and share your Snowflake expertise. Don't miss out on this chance to build new connections!

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: As one of Snowflake's original Data Superheroes, he has a deep knowledge of cloud data platforms, data modeling, and analytics technologies such as dbt Cloud, Snowflake, AWS Redshift, ThoughtSpot, and Looker. He has led multiple migrations to cloud data & advanced analytics platforms from on-premise platforms such as Cloudera and Oracle. He also know as a international thought leader on embedded analytics, data monetization, and data product management. He has a passion for data and analytics and is always looking for ways to help organizations leverage their data to drive business value.

JD Taylor is Product Delivery Manager for the Analytics Hub Data Platform team at Edward Jones focusing on automation and internal structures in Snowflake enhancing productivity. JD's career has provided exposure to the views and interests of a wide range of data stakeholders including time as a Political Science doctoral candidate, data analyst, system analyst, support technical consultant, ETL/application developer, and now data platform. To better understand the needs of financial stakeholders, JD has attained Series 7, 66, and 24 licenses. To take a break from technology, JD volunteers as a Cub Scout and Scouts BSA leader for his three children in St. Louis.

Data Enablement Manager with 12 years of experience in Tableau and analytics. Expert in creating impactful visualizations, optimizing data sources, and implementing best practices. Skilled in managing Tableau Cloud and leading teams to deliver high-quality results. Passionate about transforming data into actionable insights and driving informed decision-making across organizations.

I’m a Snowflake Data Engineer/Architect at NBH Bank, handling everything Snowflake-related. I build metadata tables to control what data gets loaded in from external sources, along with creating tables, views, procedures — you name it. I help manage how data flows through our medallion layers and keep things running smoothly. I’ve also worked on ingesting data from Kafka and setting up data sharing, so I'm pretty comfortable with real-time and collaborative data solutions too.

title: “Show Me The Money” - How To Measure The Business Impact of AI Investments
abstract: As organizations continue to expand their AI initiatives, one critical challenge remains: proving the business value of AI investments. While theoretical discussions on "what data is worth" have persisted for years, today’s executives must answer a more pressing question: How do we track, quantify, and communicate the impact of AI on revenue, efficiency, and business outcomes? In this session, we bring together leading executives from data-driven enterprises to share their real-world experiences, discussing the frameworks, KPIs, and hard-won lessons that enable organizations to justify AI investments and secure long-term executive buy-in.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: As Principal Data Strategist at Snowflake, Jennifer advises customers on data strategy and best practices in building world-class data organizations. Prior to Snowflake, Jennifer spent over a decade as a Forrester Analyst, held management positions in tech sales and marketing, designed urban policy programs in E. Europe and Russia, and taught math as a Peace Corps volunteer in Central Africa. She earned a Ph.D. and an M.A. from Stanford University and a B.A. in econometrics from the University of Virginia.

speakers.job_title: Head of Data and AI Accelerators

speakers.bio: Mercedes Pantoja is a seasoned global IT executive with over two decades of experience with state-of-the-art technologies. Specialized in driving innovation and growth through Data and AI, She has successfully led organizations of all sizes in transforming strategy into actionable impact. With a proven record of designing sustainable enterprise solutions and pioneering products, She combines deep technical expertise with clear communication, making complex ideas accessible at all levels. She is known for fostering collaborative, diverse teams that unlock potential and deliver tangible business value. A positive and energetic individual, passionate about technology, health and well-being.

title: "Ask me Anything" with Product Leadership - Data Engineering
abstract: Join us for an exclusive Ask Me Anything (AMA) session with Snowflake’s top product leaders, where no topic is off-limits. This interactive conversation offers a rare opportunity to engage directly with the minds shaping Snowflake’s strategy and innovation in Data Engineering. Whether you're looking for insights into product vision, best practices, or upcoming capabilities, this is your chance to go straight to the source.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Chris Child is Senior Director, Product Management at Snowflake. Since joining Snowflake in mid-2017, Chris has focused on Snowflake’s product experiences, including the UI, account management, and billing and cost governance. Prior to joining Snowflake, Chris led product teams at a number of companies specializing in data, including Segment, Salesforce, RelateIQ, and Foursquare. Chris also spent time as both an early stage and late stage technology investor. He is an alum of MIT and Stanford and loves to be doing anything outdoors with his family.

title: Build an AI App with the Snowflake Native App Framework and Snowflake Cortex
abstract: Join this session to build a Snowflake Native App that delivers personalized, real-time movie recommendations from semi-structured data using Snowflake Cortex functions. You will set up a retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system within Snowflake, then build and deploy the app, making it available for publication and monetization on the Snowflake Marketplace.

speakers.bio: Charles Yorek has spent the last 20 years working in various capacities ranging from consulting to his current role as a Principal Architect in the Field CTO group at Snowflake. During this time he has always been focused on enterprise information management from implementing full Enterprise Data Warehouses to building a custom software development practice focused on delivering data centric products. He has been with Snowflake for 5 years and is currently focused on helping Snowflake customers and partners achieve success bringing their application workloads to the Snowflake platform.

Tim Buchhorn has worked in a range of technical fields for the past 20 years, spanning research, engineering, consulting, to his current role as a Senior Architect specialising in Data Applications and Collaboration. During this time, he has worked with iconic organisations in the Asia-Pacific region across a number of different verticals including Financial Services, Government, Manufacturing, Energy and Technology. For the past 3 years Tim has been at Snowflake, he has been focussed enabling customers and partners to derive maximum value from their data products by leveraging Snowflake for their application workloads.

title: Creating Data Pipelines with Zero Touch
abstract: In response to the growing demand for integrating new data into our data platform, the Data Engineering Team at Okta has developed a solution utilizing Snowpark for Python to automate construction of data pipelines. Discover how Okta's Zero Touch Platform creates end-to-end pipelines that ingest events arriving on S3 and transforms data in Snowflake using Streams and Tasks. The platform features integrated capabilities to detect schema changes in data streams, facilitating automatic evolution of Snowflake table schemas. Crafted with privacy in mind, it also allows for data classification through tags and systemically masking data using tag-based masking policies.

speakers.bio: Poornima has over a decade of experience in the data industry and currently holds the position of Staff Data Engineer at Okta. With extensive expertise in SQL and data analysis, she is passionate about creating scalable data solutions and optimizing data pipelines to ensure data integrity and performance. Committed to addressing complex data challenges, Poornima excels at designing efficient, automated, and high-performing data workflows that drive success.

Brett has years of experience as a programmer and manager in the data industry and currently holds the position of Data Engineering Manager at Okta. His experience ranges from programming in multiple languages (C, Java, C#, Python, SQL etc) to help run a team of programmers. He also hates inefficiency and slowness which has led to him creating different solutions to speed up data onboarding.

title: Delivering On-Demand Metrics with Cortex Analyst at Azul Brazilian Airlines
abstract: This session introduces Cortex AI Chat, a real-time solution for querying Azul Brazilian Airlines' operational metrics. Azul, one of Brazil’s leading airlines, employs over 16,000 staff, operates 1,000+ daily flights, and connects 150+ destinations with 300+ direct routes, ensuring unmatched national and international reach. Cortex, powered by a semantic model, allows users to interact via a Streamlit UI, delivering instant insights as tables or visualizations. Deployed with CI/CD pipelines and improved through feedback, it reduces static dashboards, refines KPIs dynamically, and accelerates time to market for key metrics, streamlining insight-driven decisions.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Gabriel Santos is a Data Engineer at Azul Brazilian Airlines, specializing in data analysis, artificial intelligence (AI), and FinOps. With extensive expertise in cloud computing, big data processing, and machine learning, Gabriel designs and optimizes robust, scalable data solutions that unlock strategic business insights and significantly enhance cost efficiency. His passion for leveraging advanced analytics and innovative technologies positions him at the forefront of driving data-driven transformation within the aviation industry.

I am a seasoned data professional with over a decade of experience in the field. I have been working with data since 2013 and have specialized in Snowflake since 2021. Currently serving as a Data Coordinator at Azul Linhas Aéreas, I am passionate about leveraging data to solve complex problems and drive business insights. My expertise spans data engineering, data governance, and cloud data solutions, ensuring scalable, secure, and well-structured data environments. With a strong analytical mindset and a results-oriented approach, I focus on optimizing data processes, enhancing decision-making, and maintaining data integrity across organizations.

title: DevDive: Shaping the Builder’s Journey
abstract: Step away from the booth and into the conversation at DevDive — a hands-on and open-dialogue design thinking session created for attendees who want to shape the future of building on Snowflake. Whether you're exploring your first quickstart or deploying production pipelines, we want to understand your goals, challenges and learning journey. This interactive experience invites you to share feedback, from onboarding and learning resources to the tools and assets that support your work. No slides, no panels — just real talk, real challenges and real collaboration. On Dev Day (6/5), you will also have the opportunity to join small-group discussions tailored to your specific AI use cases! Let’s build better, together.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

title: Driving AI-Readiness with Trusted Data: Insights from Financial Services Leaders
abstract: Hear expert data leaders from top financial services firms share insights on navigating the opportunities and challenges of becoming AI-ready with data + AI observability. They'll discuss how they modernize data strategies, tackle governance and compliance, and leverage Snowflake’s capabilities to unlock AI-driven insights. Gain practical approaches to data democratization, risk management and accelerating AI adoption in the highly regulated world of financial services.

speakers.bio: Barr Moses is the CEO & Co-Founder of Monte Carlo, the data + AI observability leader, backed by Accel, GGV, Redpoint, ICONIQ Growth, Salesforce Ventures, and other top Silicon Valley investors.

A transformation-driven c-level executive with more than 20 years of experience directing teams overseeing development and launch of challenging and innovative data projects and programs for industry-leading organizations across the U.S. I am an industry expert in data governance and have served as chief data officer for 3 large-cap financial services firms building teams and instilling a fundamental understanding, level of comfort, and empowerment in cross-functional data and analytics initiatives resulting in several significant achievements over the years, including implementation of cloud-based enterprise capabilities that drove both revenue and efficiency outcomes.

speakers.company: Intercontinental Exchange / New York Stock Exchange
speakers.bio: Durgesh Das is a seasoned data and analytics leader with over 20 years of experience in data strategy, governance, and policy development. As the Vice President of Data, Analytics & Governance at ICE, the parent company of the New York Stock Exchange, Das plays a pivotal role in driving enterprise-wide data initiatives, ensuring robust governance, and implementing innovative solutions. Das collaborates with engineers and IT leaders to optimize data management and support strategic growth across business units. He has been with ICE for more than 16 years and holds a Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering and a degree in Management from Indira Gandhi National Open University.

speakers.job_title: Former Chief Data and Analytics Officer
speakers.bio: Lee Davidson is an accomplished Chief Data and AI Officer recognized for pioneering data-driven growth strategies in financial services. As the former Chief Data and Analytics Officer at Morningstar, Lee led a global organization driving the company's data and AI initiatives, including commercializing generative AI products and achieving substantial operational efficiencies. In 2024, Lee was named among DataIQ’s Top 100 Data Leaders in the USA, a testament to his leadership in the analytics and AI space. Lee’s thought leadership on enterprise AI adoption and data analytics has been featured in the media, including Forbes and Financial Times.

title: Elementum + Snowflake: Unlocking Business Value Through Agentic AI Automation
abstract: This session explores how agentic AI automation is transforming the way organizations extract value from their data assets. Join us as we demonstrate real-world examples of how AI agents can be used in process automation to autonomously discover patterns, automate decision workflows, and drive business outcomes. We’ll showcase how leading organizations are deploying these solutions to reduce time-to-value by up to 80% while enabling nontechnical business users to leverage complex data.

speakers.bio: Karen Jenkins is a dynamic, customer-focused executive with 15+ years in SaaS workflow software. She drives revenue and growth by making strategic improvements to the overall customer experience from product roadmap through delivery. Fueled by a keen technology acumen, Karen thrives on simplifying complex problems into indispensable solutions for business users.
At Elementum, Kyle Westwood has helped thousands of enterprises define and deploy their AI Driven Workflow strategy.

title: End-to-End Migration to Snowflake in Action: Data and Pipelines
abstract: Want to migrate to Snowflake in less than 90 minutes? Come to this hands-on lab. We will work through Snowflake's migration strategy and automation tools — such as SnowConvert and Snowpark Migration Accelerator — to assess, move and validate an entire pipeline with data into Snowflake. Our data will be in another data warehouse, in a data lake and even on premises. Can we move it into Snowflake? Our pipelines are written in Jupyter notebooks utilizing Spark. Can those fully exist in Snowflake? Can all of it happen in Snowflake? Let's find out.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.job_title: Sr. Product Manager, Tools and Applications
speakers.bio: Fernando is a Senior Product Manager, specialized in Tools and Applications, with over 10 years of experience in product development and 5 years specifically in code migration. He is leading the SnowConvert product strategy and roadmap to help Snowflake customers accelerate their adoption of the platform. With a degree in Software Engineering, Fernando spent five years working as a software developer before transitioning into product management.

Brandon Carver solves problems. He is currently building scalable migration solutions for Snowflake, but has experience creating meaningful change that scales across industries and data platforms in both the public and private sector.

Kuo Lun Lo, an Engineering Manager at Snowflake and migration expert, leads the development of Snowflake SnowConvert and other migration tools. He drives the creation and delivery of robust features, accelerating customer migrations from various SQL dialects to Snowflake.

title: From Clicks to Cortex: Build AI-Ready Data Pipelines on Snowflake in a Snap
abstract: Want to try the latest Snowflake features, but don’t have the time to do it? Find out how to accelerate dev cycles by 10x and create anything in Snowflake in a few clicks using Coalesce’s data transformation platform and your favorite features like Snowflake Cortex and more. Please bring your laptop and create a Snowflake trial account before the event if you don't have a Snowflake account already.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Josh Hall is a data engineer and product marketer with a passion for helping others unlock the power of data and AI. He creates video content that breaks down complex technologies, writes blogs that distill technical concepts, and teaches data practitioners how to build first class data products. With over half a decade of data consulting experience and years working in data products, Josh is now focused on helping others accelerate their AI and data journeys. Whether it’s leveraging machine learning for smarter decision-making or building scalable data pipelines, he’s dedicated to making AI and data accessible, practical, and impactful.

title: Future-Proofing Your Data Team: Embracing DataOps and Modern Best Practices
abstract: Today's data teams are evolving beyond pipeline builders to become strategic business enablers. This fundamental shift requires embracing principles that transformed software engineering — agile methodologies, CI/CD practices and infrastructure-as-code — and are now making their way into data workflows as DataOps. This session explores essential strategies for building resilient, high-impact data operations. We'll dive into team structures that foster collaboration, testing frameworks that ensure reliability, automation approaches that eliminate toil, and modern orchestration tools that scale with growing demands.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Steven Hillion has been leading engineering and analytics teams for twenty years. He is currently Head of Data at Astronomer, the New York-based company behind Apache Airflow, the popular open-source data orchestration platform. Previously, he founded Alpine Data, a collaborative data science platform later acquired by TIBCO. Before that he built a global data science team at Pivotal and developed a suite of open-source and enterprise software in machine learning. Earlier, he led engineering at several successful start-ups. Originally from Guernsey, Steven now lives in San Francisco. He holds a Ph.D. in Mathematics from UC Berkeley, and read mathematics at Oxford University.

title: Governance and Analytics: A Banking Journey with Snowflake Horizon and Polaris
abstract: To meet stricter compliance and reporting requirements, banks must prioritize customer risk data and customer experience, maintaining trusted data management across diverse sources. When a large European bank was faced with a complex data ecosystem with legacy systems, temporary solutions and governance issues, it successfully established a modern data foundation for AI. This transformation enhanced compliance, security, privacy, data discovery and collaboration, empowering stakeholders to safeguard and leverage the value of sensitive data, applications and models.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Anoop leads Data & Analytics for LTIMindtree across Nordics, and Africa, driving innovation and delivering AI-driven solutions with a proven track record in scalable deployments.

title: Governing Data in an Era of AI and Consumer Consent
abstract: Customer data is an organization's most valuable asset. It’s also the hardest to govern and use in a dynamic business environment. Consumers can revoke their consent in an instant, regulations continue to grow, and internal data policies change. How does an organization manage this data and policy fragmentation while enabling teams to use data appropriately for the business? Join leaders from BT Group and OneTrust as they explore modern data governance practices and new technologies for data policy enforcement. Learn how Snowflake's native data controls orchestrated by OneTrust’s new data and AI governance products accelerate value-creation from customer data.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.job_title: General Counsel Data and BT Group Data Protection & Ethics Officer
speakers.bio: Janine leads a multidisciplinary team of data lawyers, compliance, and assurance professionals #gettingdatadone for BT Group in the UK and internationally across 180 jurisdictions. Her team are responsible for advisory and assurance obligations relating to existing and emerging data laws, including AI, data ethics, data governance, privacy and security. Prior to joining BT, Janine was an SVP of Legal and Business Affairs at Warner Bros. for many years and in private practice with Pinsent Masons (UK) and Bowmans (RSA). Janine is a dual-qualified English and South African lawyer and an advocate for responsible technology.

Stephanie McReynolds is the Vice President of Product Marketing at OneTrust, where she leads the product and portfolio marketing efforts for the company's Consent & Preferences, Privacy, AI Governance and Data Governance products. With 14,000+ customer organizations, OneTrust is the most widely used platform for operationalizing privacy, consent, AI governance, and data governance. Stephanie is dedicated to driving OneTrust's vision and ensuring its solutions meet the evolving needs of organizations globally. Prior to OneTrust, Stephanie led product and marketing teams at Oracle, Teradata, Trifacta, Alation and Ambient.AI. She holds masters and bachelors degrees from Stanford University.

title: Humans Out, AI In: Reinventing the Workforce and Driving Unmatched Efficiency
abstract: Join us as we explore how evolv Consulting is driving a shift in automation with AI systems that replace routine human tasks, leading to notable productivity improvements. We’ll review real-world evolv success stories — such as our machine learning model factory, decision engine automation and domain-specific chatbots — to demonstrate how these solutions are enhancing operations and reshaping work processes. Discover the strategies that empower organizations to leverage AI effectively, achieving more with less human intervention and setting a new standard for efficiency in today’s competitive landscape.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Rose specializes in using Snowflake’s cloud data platform to improve AI-driven projects, combining data science with Snowflake expertise to tackle complex data issues. She focuses on practical AI applications for business optimization and is recognized for her innovative strategies that help clients stay ahead in technology and market trends.

Tyler Stern is a machine learning, data science, and AI consultant with evolv Consulting in Dallas, TX. He primarily works with clients to establish and realize their AI strategy and to demonstrate what is possible with these exciting new tools. During his time off, he loves rock climbing, ultrarunning, and music!

title: Hurricane Response with Cortex AI: How TECO Restored Power to 600,000 Homes
abstract: Imagine a major hurricane leaving 600,000 homes in darkness. Every second counts. We'll take you behind the scenes of a real-world disaster recovery, where Tampa Electric used Snowflake Cortex AI to orchestrate a rapid and effective storm response. We'll share the challenge, the solution architecture, and the exact steps taken — from LLMs for text extraction at scale to building a chat-based interface for decision-makers.

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)

speakers.bio: Doneyli is a Principal AI Architect at Snowflake, where he leads strategic GenAI and ML initiatives as the primary technical advisor. He provides deep expertise to craft tailored AI & Data solutions that align with customer goals. With over 20 years in Data and AI, from development to solution architecture, he leverages his robust skillset to guide organizations toward innovative solutions that transform their operations. Inside Snowflake, he is deeply involved in cross-functional product development and deployment collaboration. He is often spearheading demonstrations and presentations to showcase AI capabilities.

Ken Beard has worked with all areas of Electric & Gas Utility Data over the last 20 years. He is a full stack data engineer across multiple platforms. Snowflake is his favorite new toy and he enjoys using its newer features to expand and simplfy the data landscape. He leads the technical architecture for Tampa Electric's Enterprise Data Analytics Platform including Smart Meter and other IoT data.

title: Migrating and Modernizing: Business User Workflows in Snowflake
abstract: Business users need better data access, but outdated, siloed systems at EOE rely on file shares, database extracts, Access and analysis tools. Remote work during the pandemic further exposed the limitations of client tools run over VPN. The EOE cloud team is modernizing workflows with Snowflake, leveraging worksheets and Notebooks. Snowflake enables real-time data interaction, reducing reliance on outdated methods and bringing users closer to data for improved efficiency and decision-making.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Miranda Dynan is a Project Manager and Business Analyst at the Executive Office of Education (EOE) and is part of a team leading a modernization effort. Her area of focus is with business user groups and their interactions with data. Within her time at EOE, she has assisted with two cloud migrations from on-premise data centers to the AWS cloud. She is passionate about helping to solve complex user problems using cloud and Snowflake technologies.

Danielle Ondrick is a leader in the design and implementation of enterprise-scale architectures and IT, with a focus on secure cloud architecture, cost effective strategies, data governance and information modeling. She has led numerous transformative technology and business modernizations in state government, financial services and commercial industries. Most recently led the migration and modernization of an on-premise data center to the cloud for MA Executive Office of Education including the migration from Oracle Exadata to Snowflake.

title: The First Line of Defense: Secure Network Connectivity Strategies for Snowflake
abstract: Network security is the first line of defense in Snowflake's defense-in-depth security paradigm. This session will cover integrating Snowflake with your environments (such as Iceberg tables and other resources), using private connectivity, and designing network policies that balance security, scalability and manageability. It will unveil the latest network security innovations, real-world architectures, best practices and hands-on guidance to help participants build a robust security posture.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Prasoon is a senior product manager at Snowflake owning various aspects of database security. Before that, he led products at Uber, Balbix, Splunk, and Palo Alto Networks spanning domains from risk posture management, enterprise IAM, behavioral threat detection, security analytics, cryptography, encryption, etc. In the last 13 years, Prasoon has explored cyber security through the roles of a security practitioner, product guy, marketer, and above all a student of the field.

speakers.job_title: Security and Governance Field CTO Office
speakers.bio: Seth has over 20 years experience in the field of Cybersecurity data governance and data privacy. He covered both the public sector, and highly regulated industry globally. In August 2020, Seth is currently working as global Field CTO, focussing on the security, security data lake, data governance, and data privacy

title: The Prescription for Data Scaling with Snowflake and Slingshot at Capital Rx
abstract: As a leading enterprise health technology company, Capital Rx leverages Snowflake to deliver high-quality healthcare data insights at scale. Data demands soared as our company scaled and Judi®’s feature set expanded, so we partnered with Capital One Slingshot to optimize resources, reduce compute costs and unify warehouse management. Join us to see how we consolidated our warehouses, significantly cut expenses and maintained performance — enabling better patient outcomes through advanced analytics.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: For almost three decades, Paul has helped tech companies make the most of their data assets. He is a hands-on leader who knows how to build strong Data Engineering, Data, Analytics, and BI teams, implement effective data strategies, and drive real business results.

title: Transforming Free Text Prompts into Automated SQL Workflows
abstract: Discover how Snowflake Cortex AI automates complex list generation and other tasks by converting plain text prompts into automated SQL workflows using SNOWFLAKE.CORTEX.COMPLETE and Snowflake Procedures. Learn how this innovation streamlines processes for end users, enabling rapid, accurate data queries without SQL knowledge. This session highlights practical applications, technical strategies and real-world impact, emphasizing time savings, user empowerment and scalable automation for diverse use cases.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.job_title: Analytics Manager, Product Operations: Insights & Analytics
speakers.bio: Charles Mollica is an experienced analytics and operations leader, currently serving as Manager of Product Operations: Insights & Analytics at athenahealth. With a strong background in AI-driven automation and data analytics, he focuses on enhancing research and design operations through advanced data solutions. Charles has successfully implemented AI tools to streamline analytics workflows, uncover previously inaccessible insights, and improve research efficiency.

speakers.job_title: Product Operations: Insights and Analytics Sr. Manager
speakers.bio: I lead the Analytics Team for our Product Operations Org under Product, where we centralize, standardize, and provide visibility and expertise on operational cross-divisional data assets and tools to enable company-wide insights, transparency, and alignment on outcomes

title: Unlocking Superpowers: Ramp Up Your ML Team on Notebooks
abstract: Do you lead or administer a team that does data science or machine learning? Come learn why Snowflake Notebooks is the premier surface for exploration, visualization and machine learning in Snowsight interface, and how you as an admin play a simple but powerful role in getting your team off to the races.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Annissa Alusi is a Principal Product Manager for developer tools at Snowflake. Her teams are responsible for various applications within Snowsight, including Snowflake Notebooks, which brings code, data, and visual exploration together in one seamless, collaborative experience for data teams. Prior to Snowflake, she worked at Google and Waymo. She has an MBA from Stanford GSB and holds multiple industry patents.

title: 42 Multi-Agent Opportunities for Data Modernization
abstract: In today's AI era, companies must build new data capabilities, and AI can accelerate this progress. This talk presents 42 opportunities for using multi-agent AI systems to enhance data modernization. These include speeding up migrations from legacy systems, augmenting BI reports with conversational AI, and preparing data for AI applications. Whether moving to platforms like Snowflake or using Cortex AI to handle enterprise data, we'll show how AI is used to capture, process and publish structured and unstructured data securely, setting the stage for AI readiness.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Teresa is Global lead of Data Capability at Accenture developing vision, assets, and talent. She is Accenture's most prolific inventor with 250+ patents and patent applications. Teresa specializes in bridging the "enterprise gap" taking innovation from startups, academia, digital natives and making it work at enterprise scale and with enterprise requirements. Teresa has a Ph.D. in Electrical Engineering from UC Berkeley.

title: AI That Delivers: The Power of Data Trust
abstract: AI can only deliver real impact when built on accurate, trusted data. Without strong data quality and governance, AI models underperform, insights fall short and opportunities are lost. Join Jessie Smith, VP of Data Quality at Ataccama, as they share how enterprises are using Ataccama Data Quality and its pushdown processing to fuel trustworthy AI initiatives — from automating data cleansing at scale to continuously monitoring data pipelines for quality drift. With Ataccama and Snowflake, organizations can scale AI confidently, ensuring reliable insights, better decisions and real business value.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Jessie Smith is the Vice President of Product Management at Ataccama and leads the Data Quality portfolio. With over a decade of experience and eight patents to her name, Jessie is an expert in Enterprise Data Quality, Data Governance, AI, AI Readiness, Data Integration, Data Fabric, and Cloud Modernization and Deployment. She frequently speaks at major conferences, contributing to blogs, podcasts, and webinars, where she shares her insights with CDOs, CIOs, CTOs, and other data leaders. Jessie focuses on sharing her extensive experience in helping enterprises accelerate business initiatives with data to provide tangible guidance and advice.

title: Best Practices for Building Streamlit Apps
abstract: Join this session to learn best practices and tips from an avid Streamlit app developer since 2019. Let's take a journey through all the mistakes I've made building dozens of productions dashboards used by thousands of people hundreds of thousands of times over the years, using everything from dynamic tables to Snowflake Cortex, with a ton of live coding in between.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Tyler Richards is a Senior Data Scientist at Snowflake. He's also the author of "Streamlit for Data Science" and a resident Streamlit expert

title: Cisco Breaks Data Silos to Power Real-Time Insights Across the Business
abstract: Cisco’s data team supports multiple lines of business, each needing fast, secure access to enterprise data. With Fivetran, they move data from systems like Salesforce, Qualtrics and S3 into Snowflake. Join us to hear how Cisco powers real-time data access across the organization. Learn how Cisco is breaking down data silos, accelerating time-to-insight, and enabling a data-driven culture across the business.

speakers.bio: Virendra is the Director of Data & Analytics at Cisco IT, where he leads the management of Cisco's Data and Analytics Platforms and Ecosystem. He brings extensive experience in designing, building, and operating platforms that support the end-to-end data lifecycle
Leading a team responsible for aligning customers key objective and business outcomes with Fivetran's proven data ingestion platform.

title: Connect Teams with Iceberg Tables Using Snowflake
abstract: Iceberg tables are becoming an integral component of an Organization's lakehouse strategy. In this session, we will look at all the different ways to onboard Iceberg tables and share Iceberg data with both internal and external stakeholders.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Gopal Raghavan is a Field CTO specializing in Collaboration and Business Continuity at Snowflake. He has over 25 years of experience working with customers worldwide to help them accomplish their business objectives using data and analytics. His areas of interest at Snowflake include Iceberg tables, dynamic tables, universal listings (private, marketplace and organizational) and related monetization strategies.

title: Data Now, Personalization Later: How Afterpay Gave Self-Serve Data to Marketing
abstract: Afterpay’s success relies on Snowflake’s strong data foundation. However, Afterpay’s marketers weren’t empowered to use this wealth of data without data team support. This meant delayed, less tailored customer experiences. Afterpay flipped the script by adopting a composable customer data platform on Snowflake. In this session, learn how Afterpay built marketing data proficiency and trust, powered every channel with fresh data and even automated testing and one-to-one personalization with AI decision-making.

speakers.bio: Strategic marketing technology leader specializing in AI-driven automation, data intelligence, and scalable growth solutions. Builds high-impact ecosystems that drive efficiency, streamline operations, and empower teams with self-serve capabilities. Passionate about leveraging technology and data to accelerate performance, ensure compliance, and deliver impactful marketing through innovation and strategic execution.

Specializing in data-driven analytics, marketing optimization, and strategic decision-making, Matt has a proven track record of leveraging data intelligence to enhance efficiency, maximize ROI, and drive business growth across enterprises and start-ups. Passionate about building scalable data solutions, fostering high-performing teams, and translating complex data into actionable insights that accelerate performance.

Kashish Gupta is the co-founder and co-CEO of Hightouch, the best in class Composable CDP now helping marketers build AI agents for personalized marketing. Kashish has a background in Machine Learning, and has been working on startups since 2018. He did his masters in ML at Penn, and he went through Y-Combinator in 2019. Kashish works across product and go-to-market at Hightouch, and enjoys working hands on with marketing teams at large consumer brands. When he’s not helping marketers with data and AI, you can find him playing squash, tennis, or golf on the weekend.

title: Empowering the Enterprise for the Next Era of Analytics
abstract: The next era of data transformation is here. AI is supercharging developer workflows, downstream teams can collaborate thanks to governed self-service, and SQL comprehension is generating detailed metadata that powers unprecedented developer efficiency while promoting data quality and cost optimization. See it come together firsthand with dbt’s data control plane, a centralized platform that gives organizations repeatable, scalable and governed ways to win with data in the modern age.

Most recently, he was the Co-Founder and Head of Product at SDF Labs, a cutting-edge transformation layer and SQL compiler recently acquired by dbt Labs. Elias is focused on launching the next evolution of dbt's engine powered by SDF's technology.

title: Executive Industry Booth Tours - Healthcare & Life Sciences
abstract: Join an exclusive, guided tour of the Snowflake AI Data Cloud and its premier partner ecosystem, tailored for leaders in Healthcare & Life Sciences. This immersive experience will take you through five curated stops that showcase the art of the possible—real-world solutions, cutting-edge AI use cases, and data-driven innovations transforming your industry today. Gain direct access to experts, see industry-leading technology in action, and leave with actionable inspiration to accelerate your data strategy. Space is extremely limited—registration is first come, first served. Please note that this experience includes walking between stops; comfortable footwear is recommended. Please note that the Executive Booth Tours are reserved for customers only—we kindly ask our partners not to register.

times.room: Vertical Village: Healthcare & Life Sciences Booth

title: Fostering Operational Resilience by Using Snowflake Cortex AI
abstract: Using Snowflake Cortex AI, we have developed use cases to identify potential risks early and ensure appropriate mitigation strategies before implementation and during post-incident RCA. This helps determine whether preventive measures failed or external factors caused the issue, creating a closed-loop process for managing change risks. This dual approach improves operational resilience by proactively managing risks while driving continuous improvement through incident-driven insights.

speakers.bio: Sachin Sangtani is the Director for Enterprise Intelligence at DTCC, managing the Technology Data Office and the Centralized Layered Analytics Warehouse (CLAW) built on Snowflake. Sachin has over 26 years of Information Management experience in Consulting, Retail, Fashion, Financial Services and Technology.

title: How Snowflake Enables Petabyte-Scale Data Migration, with Real-World Examples
abstract: This session will explore strategies for migrating large volumes of legacy data to Snowflake as part of a comprehensive migration project using the prebuilt Data Migration and Validation Accelerator (DMVA) tool by Snowflake Professional Services. We will dive into the tool’s capabilities, the validation techniques employed to ensure accurate data migration and real-world customer examples showcasing its use in migrating massive data quantities.

speakers.job_title: Head of Engineering and Data Operations
speakers.bio: An award-winning data leader with over 20 years of experience, Niall has been instrumental in helping organisations unlock the potential of their data. As the Head of Data Engineering and Data Operations at Aviva, the UK's largest insurer, Niall specialises in empowering businesses to become data-led, leveraging data assets effectively. With a proven track record in delivering leading data capabilities in both greenfield environments and complex multinational corporations, Niall is passionate about data democratisation. Niall is dedicated to creating ecosystems where data is accessible and valuable, driving informed and innovative decision-making.

I have been doing database system development and design for over 40 years, and have worked in a number of industries including financial, healthcare, telco, insurance on systems from very small up to 1.4 Petabytes and have been a part of many migrations. This varied experience has given me an ability to anticipate things that could and many times do come up in migrations from one platform to another. Currently I am part of a team at Snowflake that leveraged our collective experience to create the Data Migration and Validation Accelerator (DMVA) to provide an efficient method of getting data moved to Snowflake.

title: Improve Your Snowflake Data Quality and Readiness for Analytics and AI
abstract: Discover how your wider Enterprise Data Stack impacts your Snowflake data quality and readiness. Proactively address issues using data observability powered by agentic data management to enforce operational governance, while enabling collaboration between technical and business teams — all while managing costs and improving operating efficiencies to deliver trusted, AI-ready data with confidence.

speakers.bio: Cam Davie is an experienced data and analytics professional with over 25 years experience in Analytics and Data Management. He specializes in helping customers and partners with their needs for analyzing information, developing insightful reports, and creating compelling visualizations to uncover key trends and drive strategic decisions. Cam is passionate about working with customers and partners to leverage data to solve business challenges and foster a data-informed culture within organizations. His expertise helps teams understand and identify opportunities to improve and trust data through enhanced data quality and data reliability.​​​​​​​

title: Scaling Smart: How Okta and Komodo Health Use AI to Optimize Snowflake
abstract: Okta and Komodo Health operate in vastly different industries — identity and access management vs. healthcare intelligence — but both rely on Snowflake to deliver secure, high-performance and cost-efficient data at scale. In this session, leaders from both organizations share how they use AI-powered automation to optimize workloads, reduce spend and improve governance. Whether you’re managing real-time access or sensitive health data, you’ll gain practical strategies to boost performance, cut costs and improve data accessibility — without compromising security.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Prior to founding Keebo, Barzan was an Associate Professor of Computer Science at the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor. He has spent the last 15 years of his career conducting award-winning research at the intersection of machine learning and database systems, across Univ. of Michigan, MIT, UCLA and several companies that have commercialized his research.

Jenny Kazarina, Senior Manager of Data Engineering at Okta, leads the Okta platform and Federal infrastructure. She has been with Okta for nearly 10 years, having been hired as the second data engineer on the team. Jenny has played a key role in the ongoing modernization of Okta’s infrastructure, pipelines, and processes, and possesses extensive knowledge about all the impressive things Okta does.

Komodo Health is a leading healthcare technology company that harnesses the power of data analytics and AI to drive insights and solutions in the healthcare industry, empowering healthcare stakeholders with actionable insights. Dineshkumar Munusamy, Staff Data Architect at Komodo Health, is an innovative and analytical Senior database administration and Site Reliability Engineer with career-long history in IT specializing in engineering, implementing, and supporting database operations.

With over a decade in the software industry, Mary, the VP of Engineering at Keebo, thrives in high-growth startups, reshaping the engineering landscape with innovative solutions. She’s dedicated to building high-performing teams and fostering an authentic and psychologically safe workplace. Beyond work, Mary indulges in culinary adventures, gardening, and globetrotting in pursuit of wine and olive oil.

title: The Silo-Busting Snowflake Journey of Cboe Global Markets
abstract: Cboe Global Markets is a world-leading derivatives and securities exchange network, and it depends on Snowflake to centralize, harness and monetize over 3PB of data that grows by 10TB every trading day. Join this session to learn all about Cboe’s four-year, silo-busting Snowflake journey across business intelligence, big-data analytics, data engineering and streaming, generative AI, data monetization and more.

speakers.bio: Ellen directs the Business Intelligence team and AI Center of Excellence at Cboe Global Markets, a leading provider of market infrastructure and tradable products around the world.

title: Transform Your Data Estate for Agentic AI and Apps with Snowflake and Microsoft
abstract: Explore how Snowflake and Microsoft collaborate to transform data and AI workflows. Learn to operate on a single data copy between Microsoft Fabric OneLake and Snowflake via Apache Iceberg, eliminating duplication. Discover real-time RAG AI agents that integrate Snowflake's trusted data and enterprise systems for instant Microsoft Copilot responses, without copying data. Unlock real-time actions using PowerApps with live query and writeback to Snowflake, all with no code. Simplify and innovate with these powerful tools.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Shankar Narayanan SGS is a Principal Architect at Microsoft leading up the forefront of innovation between Microsoft and Snowflake. He is a Snowflake Data Superhero and the co-author of The Ultimate Guide to Snowpark", a definitive resource on building scalable AI and data applications using Snowpark. He is also the co-creator of the Snowflake AI Toolkit.

Josh Caplan is the Partner Director of Product for Microsoft OneLake in Fabric. His team focuses of defining and delivering a single SaaS data lake as a service for the entire organization. Prior to his role at OneLake, Josh played a pivotal role in shaping Power BI, leading the development of developer experiences, Power BI Premium, SQL Server Analysis Services, and Azure Analysis Services. Josh also spent several years on the Bing team, where he leveraged his expertise in business intelligence to drive insights from Bing's massive data.

title: Unlocking the Power of Dynamic Composability in Snowflake Native Apps
abstract: Learn how dynamically composable Snowflake Native Apps accelerate innovation and streamline operations. In this session, we’ll explore how a modular, microservices-style architecture — powered by gen AI — enables effortless deployment of key marketing capabilities, such as identity resolution and audience activation. Discover how an adaptable, low-overhead approach helps businesses scale faster to meet evolving needs. Plus, see a real-world example demonstrating how this framework drives rapid innovation and improves marketing outcomes.

speakers.bio: Morten is the Global Head of Merkury Engineering, a position he has held since 2011. With a focus on Snowflake since 2018, Morten leads a dynamic team of 100 engineers behind Merkury, the premier global data and identity platform for modern marketers. His leadership has been instrumental in driving innovation and excellence within the company. Before joining Merkury, Morten was a Principal Enterprise Architect at Capgemini, where he collaborated with industry giants such as Disney, AT&T, and HP. His extensive experience and deep expertise in data technology have earned him recognition as a respected speaker and thought leader in the field.

John currently serves as the Senior Vice President of Product R&D at Dentsu where he leads the architecture function and prototypes cutting edge innovations in data management, analytics and applications. He is also developing best practices for a modern data engineering pipeline leveraging Snowflake, and a broad spectrum of 3rd party tools. As an early adopter of Snowflake, he has led the redesign of core architecture for customer databases to include Snowpark for embedded analytics and Streamlit for visualization and has also spearheaded the development of Snowflake native applications and worked with Snowflake on many pre-release features.

title: Visual AI: Boosting Product Quality and Customer Experience in Consumer Goods
abstract: Explore visual AI's impact on product inspection in consumer goods. Using a sports equipment case study, we'll examine: Production line quality control: Improving efficiency, quality and safety while reducing costs. Consumer applications: Enhancing user experience and product maintenance. We'll discuss challenges, opportunities and best practices in visual AI deployment across consumer goods sectors. Discover how these innovations set new standards in product quality and customer engagement.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Quinn Killough is driven by the goal of empowering enterprises to achieve value from visual AI for automation. Leveraging his engineering background and experience in manufacturing, and computer vision, Quinn has a proven track record in deploying game changing machine vision solutions across various industries. At Landing AI, he collaborates with customers to deliver and scale enterprise-wide adoption of Landing AI’s LandingLens platform.

title: Building an Iceberg Lakehouse for Snowflake
abstract: Are you looking for a way to build Iceberg tables for Snowflake? In this session, we will present a simple, battle-tested way to create your lakehouse architecture. We will dig into the toughest challenges of using Iceberg for low latency at scale and we will demo solutions to achieve minute-level freshness for ingestion, and how to run SQL, Spark and dbt pipelines to prepare your data for Snowflake.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Kyle Weller has been building data platforms and data products for over 10 years. He currently runs product development at Onehouse.ai building a Universal Data Lakehouse that automates data platforms to run on Apache Hudi, Delta Lake, and Apache Iceberg. Before Onehouse, Kyle was a Product Lead for Azure Databricks, he built AI products with AzureML, and has architected several planet scale data engineering platforms for Cortana, Bing, Office. Kyle has seen how hard it is for data engineers to build DIY data lakes and he looks forward to the rise of the managed Lakehouse. Outside of work Kyle volunteers as an entrepreneurship coach for incarcerated individuals.

title: Contract Intelligence: SoFi's Contract Search Solution
abstract: AI is transforming contract analysis, making it faster, more accurate and efficient. SoFi leveraged Snowflake’s AI Data Cloud to automate contract ingestion, search and review. Snowflake’s data ingestion uploads supplier agreements, and Document AI preprocesses them for text extraction. Contract language is embedded using Snowflake Cortex AI for efficient search and retrieval. An LLM analyzes results to pinpoint relevant contracts and extract precise term locations. Expert review ensures accuracy, with a human-in-the-loop validation process. The session highlights efficiency, accuracy and best practices for AI-driven contract analysis, enabling smarter, data-driven decision-making.

speakers.bio: Tristan Fry is Director of Supplier Engagement and Performance at SoFi, where he leads initiatives to enhance supplier governance, performance, and risk management. With a background in strategy and operations, he brings a data-driven approach to optimizing supplier relationships and enabling cross-functional collaboration. Tristan has a track record of driving innovation within the Supplier Management Office to support scalable, high-impact solutions.

title: Find My Data: Chatbot Powered by Streamlit
abstract: BP, a global energy company advancing sustainability and digital transformation, transitioned from Azure SQL Server PaaS to Snowflake on AWS for enterprise reporting, adopting a curated data products model. To improve data discovery, BP developed DataXplore, a Streamlit solution that leverages Snowflake’s object sourcing to create a clear lineage graph. With an AI chatbot, it references metadata to show object dependencies, helping users track raw data sources. Join this session to learn how BP leveraged Streamlit and Snowflake to improve data discovery, lineage tracking and user accessibility.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Brett Rinne is a seasoned Senior Data Engineer at BPX, where he specializes in leveraging advanced data analytics and machine learning techniques to drive business insights and optimize operations. With over a decade of experience in the energy sector, Brett has played a pivotal role in developing and implementing data-driven solutions that enhance decision-making processes and improve operational efficiency. His expertise in data engineering, coupled with his passion for innovation, has led to the successful execution of numerous high-impact projects, including the development of a master data management platform.
Father - Data Platform Lead - Outdoor Enthusiast Over 10 years of data experience, passionate about adaptable architecture and empowering developers. Virginia native, currently located in Denver, CO.

title: Fueling AI Innovation with Better Data Infrastructure
abstract: Michel Tricot, Airbyte CEO, will lead an interactive discussion with Raman Singh, Tech Lead at Symend, on their application of Airbyte and Snowflake to solve key data infrastructure challenges. Symend is the leader in intelligent collections, designed to help enterprises maximize delinquency management. Raman will discuss Symend’s business and data architecture, sharing insights on how better infrastructure powers business initiatives, including AI applications. Michel and Raman will then engage in a deep discussion on data architecture and movement. This will be an informative conversation featuring a Q&A session that allows the audience to learn more about building data movement solutions.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Michel is the co-founder and CEO of Airbyte, the open data movement platform. Airbyte’s library of 600+ connectors allows organizations to transfer data freely between multiple destination types. Airbyte’s ability to ingest structured and unstructured data in a unified data plane empowers businesses to build generative AI applications while maintaining data sovereignty and control of their pipelines. The Airbyte community has over 25,000 members and Airbyte has been deployed over 170,000 times. Airbyte aims to break down data siloes and make data accessible and available to everyone, everywhere.

Raman Singh, an esteemed Engineering Manager and Tech Lead at Symend, is a visionary in data engineering. With over a decade of expertise in Data Engineering, Architecture, and Business Intelligence, he crafts scalable data architectures for large organizations, ensuring streamlined analysis and decision-making. Armed with a Master's degree from Concordia University, his commitment to learning and staying updated on data and technology trends is unwavering. Raman is a sought-after speaker at global data conferences like Snowflake, DBT in North America, amplifying his impactful presence as a driving force in data innovation and technology advocacy.
