speakers.bio: David is a Principal Data Architect at National Grid, and a Snowflake Data Superhero since 2021. With experience across industries like gaming, finance, and energy, they thrive on solving data challenges. They enjoy dogs, games, puzzles, and math.

Innovative and results-driven Data & Technology Leader currently serving as Field CTO at DataOps.live, where I lead customer engagement and product strategy for our growing portfolio of Snowflake Dynamic Apps. My focus is on helping organizations operationalize modern data engineering practices, from CI/CD to transformation, through scalable, governed, and easy-to-adopt solutions. Recognized as a ❄️ Snowflake Data Superhero, I contribute to the data community through conference talks, blogs, webinars, user groups, and my Past or Still Present thought series.

Madhivanan is a 2x Snowflake Data Superhero, who are a select group of individuals who are highly proficient in Snowflake and actively contribute to the Snowflake community through various forms of engagement, including content creation, event participation, mentorship. He is also 4x SnowPro: Advanced Certified with over a decade of extensive experience in various database technologies and currently building solutions on Snowflake Data Cloud. Additionally, He is an active participant of the SnowPro Certification SME program & the Chapter Leader for Snowflake Calgary User Group.

speakers.job_title: Head of Data, Cloud & Security Architecture
speakers.bio: As a seasoned technical leader with over 20 years of expertise in digital product delivery, I bring a hands-on approach to driving innovation in data, security, data, and cloud. As a recognized Snowflake Data Superhero, Subject Matter Expert, and Co-organizer of the Snowflake Toronto User Group. I am passionate about guiding organizations through complex data challenges and scaling high-performing engineering teams.

title: Hyatt Self-Service Analytics: Empowering Data-Driven Decision Culture
abstract: In today’s competitive hospitality landscape, real-time data and AI-driven insights are essential for delivering exceptional guest experiences and optimizing operations. This session explores how Hyatt Hotels can harness ThoughtSpot’s intuitive analytics and AI-powered tools to uncover actionable insights, enhance personalization and drive revenue growth. Attendees will learn how to empower teams with self-service analytics, streamline decision-making, and see how to use AI to predict trends, maximize occupancy and refine marketing strategies. Join us to discover how ThoughtSpot can revolutionize Hyatt’s approach to data-driven hospitality.

speakers.job_title: Director of Data Architecture and Platform Engineering
speakers.bio: Rooshana Purnyn is a visionary technology leader specializing in data architecture, analytics, and AI innovation. As Director of Data Architecture and Platform Engineering at Hyatt, she builds enterprise-scale data and AI platforms that power real-time insights and innovation. With over a decade of experience she bridges strategy and technology, enabling AI/ML integration, customer-centric analytics, global business transformation. Her leadership is defined by building intelligent ecosystems, ethical innovation, inspiring the next generation of technologists. Rooshana’s work shapes a future where data and AI unlock human potential, business agility, and sustainable growth on a global scale.

Brian Jung is a Lead Analytics Consultant at Hyatt with 18 years of experience spanning consulting and data-driven business problem solving. At Hyatt, he focuses on uncovering opportunities through data and currently leads the Self-Service Analytics Initiative, empowering colleagues across the organization to generate insights independently. Brian holds an MBA from Emory University

title: Ohio Bureau of Workers' Compensation: Scalable RAG-based Policy Assistant
abstract: The RAG policy assistant enables the Employee Service Group to provide objective, transparent, timely, consistent and reproduceable guidance based on Ohio policies and regulations. The solution leverages Snowflake Vector data types, Snowflake Cortex AI, Cortex Guard, and Streamlit as part of a scalable generative AI framework. It incorporates automated user feedback, LLM-as-a-Judge, Human-in-the-Loop validation, LLM configuration testing, and infrastructure optimization dashboards.

speakers.bio: Jamie Roberts has extensive experience in data management and data systems development, with a combined 24 years of experience at multiple State agencies within Ohio -- the last two years at Ohio BWC acting as a member of the AI/ML solution delivery team. He holds an M.S. in Geology from Kent State University and an MBA from Columbia Southern University.

speakers.job_title: Director of Data Management and Analytics
speakers.bio: Aaron has been this role for over 3 years and have been at BWC for a total of 11 years since starting as an intern in 2013. I live in Columbus, OH and am a proud graduate of The Ohio State University.

title: Quenching the Thirst: Swire Coca-Cola’s Data Discovery and Governance Journey
abstract: This session explores how Swire Coca-Cola USA is building a federated data platform across the Coca-Cola bottling network to make it easy for business users to find the information they need. Learn how salespeople, analysts and data scientists can access information about any data artifact — such as its definition, how it is calculated and where the information comes from — to improve decision making, support AI initiatives and accelerate innovation. Hear best practices on how to support data discovery by building data governance and literacy programs at scale.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Bharathi leads Enterprise Data and Application at Swire Coca-Coca USA, where she is responsible for Data Strategy, Data Governance, Data Engineering and Data Systems and Architecture. A results-driven executive with extensive expertise in enterprise data management, applications optimization, and strategic technology implementation, Bharthi brings a cross-industry perspective on transforming data into business value. Prior to Swire, Bharathi held leadership positions at Goldman Sachs and Optum Health.

David is Chief Marketing and Strategy Officer at Alation, leading Alation's Marketing, Business Operations and Strategy teams. David has more than 15 years of experience building and scaling go-to-market functions for hyper-growth B2B enterprise SaaS companies.

Gordon has been successfully leading the Data Governance Team at Swire Coca-Cola, USA for the past three years. This team is dedicated to managing the Data Governance Program, setting standards for Operational Master Data, and ensuring enterprise Data Quality.

title: Scaling IT Architecture: The Virtual Data Architect POC
abstract: Join us for an insightful session to learn more about how DTCC is building and applying virtual data architects across their enterprise. We will share how a collaborative engagement among DTCC, Slalom and Snowflake led to a self-service approach that allows DTCC's ITA team to improve the accuracy, consistency and timeliness of its guidance at scale using gen AI. We will also demonstrate how a virtual data architect built on Snowflake using Streamlit and Cortex AI addresses common challenges facing architecture and delivery teams today.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

title: Snowflake Cortex AI meets BI: Transforming Self-Service Analytics
abstract: Self-service analytics is now as intuitive as a conversation with your data. With Snowflake Cortex AI, users across industries can extract insights effortlessly using generative AI — no advanced expertise required. Learn how Northeast Georgia Health System boosts efficiency with AI-driven insights, and see how Cortex AI powers real-world applications, automates workflows and enhances decision-making. Join us to explore the future of automated analytics.

speakers.bio: As the Chief Technology Officer and Co-Founder of Cardamom Health, I oversee the development and delivery of simple, low-cost solutions that unlock the potential of healthcare data assets and provide meaningful insights. I am driven by the mission of simplifying data ecosystems, lowering costs, and accelerating insights for healthcare providers. I bring diverse perspectives and experiences to the team, having worked with leading companies such as Microsoft, Epic Systems, and Nordic Consulting Partners.

speakers.job_title: Chief of Analytics and Data Management
speakers.bio: Jeff Morrison is a healthcare technology leader passionate about innovating with data to enhance decision-making and customer interactions. At Northeast Georgia Health System, he drives operational efficiency by transforming complex datasets into actionable insights that improve patient and provider experiences. With a keen eye for detail, Jeff proactively solves business and technical challenges, streamlining workflows and developing scalable solutions. His expertise in data-driven innovation empowers organizations to navigate the complexities of modern healthcare and optimize performance through smarter, more efficient use of information.

title: Unifying Transactional and Analytical Data in Snowflake with Unistore
abstract: Data teams face challenges when managing both transactional and analytical workloads. In this session, we'll explore how companies like yours can better unify both transactional and analytical data on Snowflake with Unistore, allowing you to better manage workflow and application state, do data serving, and build lightweight transactional apps. Hear how Project Lead The Way are leveraging Unistore to better unify transactional and analytical data.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Phillip is the product manager for Snowflake Hybrid Tables.

Britton Gray leads the Business Intelligence team at Project Lead The Way, a national nonprofit organization providing transformative learning experiences for PreK-12 students and teachers in computer science, engineering and biomedical science pathways. He is a Snowflake certified professional and also holds a European data privacy certification. Within the data field, he is passionate about using data for good, data privacy, and data engineering.

title: Unleashing ML Scalability at VideoAmp to Transform Media Measurement
abstract: It's no secret that Snowflake really excels at performing distributed workloads, but what do you do when you're hit with resource constraints of a single node (we're looking at you XGBoost)? Scale up of course! Learn how Snowflake helped VideoAmp’s media measurement platform overcome previous scalability constraints by introducing more powerful compute options that redefine the limits of media measurement and optimization.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: John Adams has been a Snowflake advocate since first becoming a user back in 2016. Lately he has found himself leading efforts to simplify the management of a multi petabyte deployment, and is finding Snowpark to be the special sauce in that recipe.

title: Vendor Data Migration to Snowflake: A Metadata-Driven Approach
abstract: In data analytics, seamless integration is vital. This presentation explores how Progressive leveraged Snowflake to elevate its data capabilities. We integrated streaming data from a vendor app into Snowflake using Snowpipe for real-time processing. Using an event-driven, metadata-based approach in Snowpark Python, we ensured efficiency and flexibility, avoiding script overload. Discover our strategy, challenges and solutions, and learn how this project boosts data-driven decisions. Ideal for data engineers, architects and analysts.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Results-driven Data Engineer with extensive experience in designing and implementing cloud-based solutions, specializing in data integration, ETL pipelines, and AI-driven analytics. Proven leader in driving innovation and optimizing data architectures across diverse industries, with a strong focus on leveraging AWS, Snowflake, and ERP technologies. Experienced in management consulting, adept at collaborating with stakeholders to identify business needs and deliver scalable, high-performance data platforms. Committed to mentoring teams to achieve technical excellence and strategic goals.

With nearly 20 years in data architecture, Nipun is an expert in cloud solutions, analytics, and migrations. Specializing in Azure, Snowflake, and AWS, he transforms complex data challenges into strategic opportunities and leads teams with a focus on innovation and efficiency. Passionate about generative AI, he explores advancements to enhance data-driven solutions. As a thought leader, Nipun shares insights at various forums, empowering organizations in a digital landscape. Beyond his professional endeavors, Nipun enjoys writing blogs about life, creating music, learning astrophysics, travelling with his family and exploring new places.

title: Accelerating Market Intelligence at the NYSE with Sigma, Snowflake and Cortex
abstract: At the Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) and New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), analysts must quickly interpret billions of daily transactions and dense regulatory documents to detect market manipulation and meet compliance standards. But traditional BI tools can’t keep up with the scale or the complexity. Learn how ICE brings Sigma, Snowflake and Cortex together to dramatically reduce the time it takes legal and compliance teams to find answers. Join us to see how ICE is transforming legacy workflows with Sigma’s scalable Cortex- and AI-enhanced platform.

speakers.company: Intercontinental Exchange / New York Stock Exchange
speakers.bio: Durgesh Das is a seasoned data and analytics leader with over 20 years of experience in data strategy, governance, and policy development. As the Vice President of Data, Analytics & Governance at ICE, the parent company of the New York Stock Exchange, Das plays a pivotal role in driving enterprise-wide data initiatives, ensuring robust governance, and implementing innovative solutions. Das collaborates with engineers and IT leaders to optimize data management and support strategic growth across business units. He has been with ICE for more than 16 years and holds a Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering and a degree in Management from Indira Gandhi National Open University.

As Sigma's Product Director, Stipo Josipovic is pivotal in setting the strategic direction for analytics, business intelligence, and data apps – pushing Sigma ahead of conventional tools through innovation. Stipo joined Sigma in 2018 as one of the company’s first employees and served as the first product manager. In 2021, he was promoted to lead the organization in redefining the space. Stipo has over a decade of experience in analytics, engineering, and research. He came to Sigma from PagerDuty where he developed and implemented a business intelligence framework. He holds a Bachelor of Arts in International Relations from The College of Wooster.

title: Amazon Bedrock and Snowflake: Powering the Next Wave of Generative AI Innovation
abstract: Experience the next evolution of AI with Amazon Nova, a groundbreaking suite of foundation models that's redefining what's possible. With exceptional multi-modal capabilities across 200+ languages and seamless integration with Amazon Bedrock, Alexa+ and Amazon Q. Nova helps organizations transform customer experiences and streamline operations. Join us to explore how leading companies are leveraging Nova's unmatched price performance to turn cutting-edge AI into real-world success stories. Learn how Snowflake data users and engineering teams can build generative AI applications using Snowflake and Amazon Nova powered by Amazon Bedrock.

speakers.bio: Ashwin Swaminathan is the Director in the AGI Foundation's organization and currently leads the efforts on pre training multimodal foundational models. Prior to Amazon, Ashwin led a team at Magic Leap working on World Sensing and Metaverse. Ashwin holds a PhD in Electrical Engineering from the University of Maryland College Park where he conducted research in several topics in image processing, multimedia forensics, security and water marking. He's a IEEE senior member with seven journal papers, 30 plus conference papers, 40 plus US patents and 50 patent applications with a combined 4300 plus citations.

title: A Practitioner’s Guide to Data Observability Success at Scale
abstract: Miles Braddock, VP and Head of Data at Fringe Benefit Group, shares lessons learned, best practices and pitfalls to avoid when deploying data observability at scale. This fireside chat — aimed at data leaders — will be hosted by Kyle Kirwan, Co-Founder and Chief Product Officer at Bigeye, the leading enterprise data observability company.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Kyle is the CPO and co-founder of Bigeye, a leading data observability company. Prior to founding Bigeye, he led the development of data management tools at Uber including data catalog, lineage, quality, and fin-ops tools. He began his career as a data scientist.

Miles Braddock is the Vice President and Head of Data at Fringe Benefit Group, a leading provider of benefits solutions for government contractors and variable hour employers, including many Fortune 500 and ENR 400 firms. He has over 20 years of experience in data, including data architecture, data product management, business intelligence, and database administration.

title: Best Practices for Dynamic Tables
abstract: Come learn the best practices around monitoring (alerts); backup/restore (cloning, replications, practicing failover scenarios, using finalize constraints); scheduler configurations (intuition around target lag, scheduling tick overview, target lag vs scheduling refresh); debugging best practices (walk through some debugging workflows); running thousands of DTs and much more!

speakers.bio: Igor Belianski is a seasoned software engineer with 20+ years of experience in large-scale distributed systems, cloud infrastructure, and data analytics. He has led engineering teams at Microsoft, Google, Oracle, and Snowflake. Passionate about performance optimization and scalability.
Engineer on Snowflake's Incremental Processing team, working on Dynamic Tables and Streams, with a long background in Stream Processing.

title: Campaign Intelligence: AI-Powered Insights at the Speed of Conversation
abstract: The media landscape drowns marketers in data complexity, but AI delivers unprecedented clarity. Join Horizon Media and Akkio to explore how an LLM-native platform utilizing Snowflake can transform raw data into instant campaign intelligence through natural language interaction. See how teams can create precise, data-driven insights in minutes instead of days while maintaining enterprise-grade security.

speakers.bio: Jon Reilly is the Co-Founder and CEO of Akkio. He has over 20 years of experience in product management, business development, marketing, and operations. Prior to Akkio, Reilly was VP of Product and Marketing at Markforged, where he drove key growth initiatives. He has also held leadership roles at Sonos and started his career at Sony Electronics as a product manager and electrical engineer.

title: Getting Started with AI Agents
abstract: We’ve seen an explosion in AI tools come to the market over the past few years, but none has had quite as much potential to transform business operations as agentic AI. While the potential is high, many companies are struggling to understand where agents provide value and how to get started. In this session, we’ll provide a practical guide for understanding the value of AI agents, where to start, and how to prepare your data ecosystem and internal teams for success.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Holly Anderson is a process automation expert with over 20 years in the high-tech and process automation industry. As a customer-focused marketing executive, she is passionate about connecting with clients and helping them find the right technology solutions. At Elementum, Holly leads the product marketing team, overseeing go-to-market strategies, messaging, and positioning.

title: How Snowflake Uses Model Registry and Model Serving for Securing AI Assistants
abstract: RAG-based applications face AI-specific risks like prompt injections, PII leaks and toxic content. This session introduces the LLM Gateway, a framework that enforces guardrails, enhances trust and ensures compliance for safer AI interactions. Learn how the Snowflake data science team uses Snowflake Model Registry for seamless online and batch inference, and how Snowpark Container Services and GPU compute pools enable secure, scalable model deployment. Attendees will walk away with practical insights on registering, deploying and optimizing inference workflows for both real-time and large-scale generative AI applications — without external MLOps complexity.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Aditya is a Senior ML Scientist at Snowflake specializing in securing and scaling Gen-AI applications for enterprise use. His current work includes detecting AI-specific threats and enforcing policy alignment in RAG-based applications to ensure compliance and relevance. His primary interests revolve around addressing the complexities of scaling machine learning (ML) systems, real-time monitoring of ML systems, assessing model/system performance, as well as the development, deployment, and ranking of ML models and results. He also has experience in anomaly detection, NLP, time series forecasting, and real-time bidding (RTB) for ads.

title: Maximizing Connected Data at T-Mobile with Apache Iceberg™
abstract: Iceberg is transforming data architecture in the telco industry by enabling seamless data management, high-performance querying, and real-time analytics across distributed environments. Its open table format allows telcos to unify batch and streaming data, ensuring scalability, governance, and cost-efficient processing. With schema evolution, time travel & optimized data compaction, it eliminates data silos and enhances AI-driven network optimization, customer experience and more. With Snowflake Iceberg, telcos can leverage connected data ecosystems to drive innovation, improve operational efficiency, and deliver next generation 5G services with unparalleled speed and agility.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Anand is a Member of the Technical Staff at T-Mobile Network Data Engineering Team. He is responsible for the oversight of all of T-Mobile's Network data to support advanced analytics and data collaboration across the enterprise. He has over 10 years of experience in the telecom industry and has a passion for data-driven innovation and customer satisfaction.

title: My Polaris: Apache Polaris (Incubating) Extensibility and Customization
abstract: Apache Polaris (incubating) is designed with extensibility in mind. We'll deep dive into the architecture of Polaris with a specific focus on customizing a Polaris deployment for your own specific needs. We’ll start with a high-level overview of the Polaris service runtime, including the server runtime and dependency injection framework. Then we’ll dive into the extension points and default implementations that deal with multitenancy; authentication, including auth federation, authorization grants and application; and metadata persistence. By the end of this talk, you should have a good understanding of how Polaris works and how to customize your deployment for your needs.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Michael is a senior engineer with Snowflake and one of the founding engineers of the Apache Polaris (incubating) project. He's been working on data systems for 15 years and is involved in a number of open source projects, including OpenLineage and Apache Polaris. At Snowflake, he works on Apache Iceberg support, including Snowflake's hosted Open Catalog product based on Apache Polaris.

title: Reimagining the Future of Marketing Impact with the Compound Marketing Engine
abstract: Agentic AI is poised to revolutionize how marketing and data teams unite to fuel revenue growth in 2025 and beyond. Join this session to discover how marketers can leverage AI agents applied directly to their first-party data on Snowflake to suggest, build and execute marketing campaigns that compound growth over time. Learn how GrowthLoop’s Compound Marketing Engine, built natively on the Snowflake AI Data Cloud, leverages agentic AI to automate campaign launches, accelerate experimentation and deliver hyperpersonalized experiences at scale.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Anthony Rotio is the Chief Data Strategy Officer at GrowthLoop, where he leads partnerships and generative AI product features, following his tenure as Chief Customer Officer. Prior to GrowthLoop, he was head of the Owned Retail Business Unit at Anheuser-Busch; following stints as a marketing director, digital product manager, and sales strategy manager. Anthony invented and founded Pizza Shelf (a consumer product) and led Amazon Services and Data Analysis at Crimson Advantage (a marketing strategy consultancy). He is a certified Google Professional Cloud Architect and has his B.A. in computer science from Harvard.

title: Target Opportunities with S&P Global’s Visible Alpha Estimates over CRM
abstract: Banking, strategy and portfolio teams must identify the right opportunities at the right time. Join S&P Global to learn how firms assess granular data on market trends, financial forecasts and industry shifts with Visible Alpha Estimates over Snowflake. Screen on financial and operational metrics, then dive deeper to find the perfect target. Embedding Visible Alpha in your CRM allows users to conduct peer analysis, examine market expectations for future growth and evaluate targets.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Emily is a product management professional at S&P Global Market Intelligence, specializing in solutions for Sales and Marketing, including CRM data. She oversees the enhancement of Reverse ETL offerings and collaborates on Xpressfeed™, Snowflake, and API products. With a decade of experience spanning manufacturing, advertising, and enterprise software, she's recognized as one of Virginia's Top 50 Women Leaders and an INvolve OUTstanding Future Leader.

David Murdock, CFA is Vice President, Product Management - Data Services at S&P Global's Visible Alpha where he is responsible for delivering Visible Alpha’s unique forecast and historical company data through various distribution channels such as APIs, Feeds, and Cloud. These solutions are designed to give quantitative analysts, data scientists, application developers and other programmatic users the content they need in the platform of their choice. He has deep subject matter expertise in delivering financial content via an array of technologies to the investment community and is passionate about developing industry leading solutions.

title: Transforming Out-of-Home Advertising: VIOOH’s Geospatial Innovation
abstract: Join this session to see how VIOOH leverages geospatial analytics to optimize ad placements across 70,000+ digital panels worldwide. Using Snowflake and CARTO, VIOOH enhances media planning with data-driven insights and intuitive visualizations. Discover how their geospatial dashboards refine targeting, automate campaign creation and improve data accessibility, plus how Overture Maps enhances targeting precision and maximizes ROI in digital out-of-home advertising.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Rémy is the Data Innovation Lead at VIOOH, specializing in Snowflake-powered analytics. With 10+ years in data science and geospatial analytics, he develops data-driven products that enhance inventory discovery, campaign performance, and real-time insights. At VIOOH, Rémy integrates Carto and Snowflake to optimize geospatial analysis, empowering advertisers with location intelligence. His expertise spans data science, machine learning, and cloud technologies.

Ryan Miller is a Solutions Engineer at CARTO where he works with customers identifying how they can best integrate geospatial technologies in their business processes. Prior to CARTO, he was a Data Engineer at Cisco. He also founded, built, and sold a clean-tech geospatial company that focused on increasing rooftop solar adoption through data-driven prequalification approaches. Ryan has a Ph.D. in GIS and an MBA and is passionate about leveraging AI and machine learning to solve complex geospatial challenges.

title: Transforming the Real Estate Industry with Data and Predictive AI
abstract: Left Main REI is transforming the real estate industry by providing a purpose-built CRM solution that streamlines operations, enhances efficiency and supports business growth. Learn how they are bringing in multiple first- and third-party data sources while leveraging predictive AI to create a data platform that their real estate professionals can use to grow their business.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Pranabesh Sarkar is a seasoned data practitioner with a strong track record of driving accelerated business insights and outcomes through modern, optimized data platforms. Passionate about turning insights into impact, he empowers organizations to build business-focused data and AI ecosystems that enable smarter, data-driven decisions.

With a foundation in engineering, I transitioned to client-facing roles, accumulating over 6 years of consultancy experience with Siebel and Salesforce technologies. For over 13 years, I played a pivotal role in nurturing the AppExchange ecosystem into the premier enterprise SaaS marketplace. In my most recent roles as the CTO of VIIZR and Left Main REI, both Salesforce ISVs, I continued to drive technical excellence and innovation.

title: Unlocking Kroger Precision Marketing's Measurement Power in Data Clean Rooms
abstract: Data Clean Rooms are the premier solution to providing secure, privacy-compliant measurement. Kroger Precision Marketing (KPM) delves into the foundational principles that enabled identity transcoding and our best-in-class measurement, in collaboration with our partners. We will discuss how KPM used DCRs for attribution measurement and incrementality testing, and how we resolved challenges in data integration. Come discover how DCRs unlock measurement capabilities while protecting consumer privacy.

speakers.bio: Ryan Lau is an accomplished data professional with over seven years of experience, specializing in building scalable measurement pipelines with an emphasis on composable architecture. At Kroger Precision Marketing, he leads the engineering team building Data Clean Rooms, which leverage Kroger's robust data to unlock insights while ensuring data privacy. A true data enthusiast, Ryan can often be found on Chicago's Lakefront Trail, tracking his swimming, biking, and running activities on his Garmin.

speakers.job_title: Product Leader, Data Clean Room & Collaboration
speakers.bio: Nathan has been a member of Kroger Precision Marketing for four years; starting as a data scientist in offsite partnership innovation initiatives before leading the data clean room product efforts beginning in 2024. Nathan has a background in finance, fintech, and martech, and a passion for all-things Data Strategy

title: Accelerating Agentic AI with Data + AI Observability
abstract: Agentic success starts with trustworthy data. In this session, Monte Carlo CEO and Co-Founder Barr Moses will explore how companies can accelerate AI readiness through data + AI observability. Learn why reliable, high-quality data is the foundation for effective AI initiatives — and how first-party data is your greatest competitive boat in the AI race. Barr will share strategies for ensuring data reliability, minimizing risk and scaling AI projects with confidence, drawing on real-world examples from leading data-driven organizations.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Barr Moses is the CEO & Co-Founder of Monte Carlo, the data + AI observability leader, backed by Accel, GGV, Redpoint, ICONIQ Growth, Salesforce Ventures, and other top Silicon Valley investors.

title: Affirm’s Approach to Payment Data Compliance and Access with Atlan
abstract: Supporting a payment network reaching over 21 million users and processing $28B in annual payments, Affirm’s data team powers business growth while ensuring sensitive data is secure and compliant. With Snowflake at the core of their modern data stack, Affirm’s team is now extending the value of this data to hundreds of producer and consumer teams. Join Ankit Singh of Affirm and Sharif Karmally of Atlan to learn their no-compromise approach, supporting both carefully cataloged data for regulatory compliance and auditing and data products that act as a single source of truth and ownership for their assets, resolving questions about data in minutes, rather than days or weeks.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Ankit Singh combines deep experience in data processing with a contextual awareness of the BNPL industry to help drive technical decision making at Affirm. A part of Affirm's Data Storage & Services (DSS) technical leadership team. Technical background in OLAP and OLTP storage infrastructure platforms and Data Engineering services. Focused on building and optimizing the critical data systems that power Affirm's financial products. Data Storage & Services (DSS) manages all data related operational and analytical infrastructure needs at Affirm. Storage systems including OLTP and OLAP, batch processing capabilities, and enterprise data governance.

With 15+ years of data-driven leadership, Sharif has evolved from running analytics-powered business units at Adobe, to managing teams of data analysts at Asana, to now leading product & corporate marketing at Atlan. Atlan was recently named a Leader in the Forrester Wave for EDCs in 2024, and a Visionary in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for D&A Governance in 2025. Atlan serves hundreds of leading enterprises including GM, FOX, Unilever, Nasdaq, and HubSpot. Having experienced first-hand how data can either accelerate business outcomes or paralyze a company, Sharif is thrilled to help enterprises find, trust, and govern AI-ready data at scale.

title: A Seismic Shift: The Largest Fintech Migration in History
abstract: Join this session to learn how PayPal optimized customer experience and exceeded SLAs at scale by modernizing its data infrastructure and analytics. Discover how the company consolidated disparate data sources into Snowflake, leveraging Iceberg for future platform flexibility, and centralized back-office analytics and ETL processes for efficiency. See how it delivers near real-time insights and reconciliation to clients, architects applications for global scalability and explores future developments such as ML for business outcome prediction and generative AI for dynamic network adaptation, positioning itself at the forefront of hyperscale fintech.

speakers.bio: Cory is a Principal Engineer at PayPal where he works on critical systems that pay customers and reporting infrastructure to help everyone make sense of data. A rustacean at heart, he wishes he could use more Rust in Snowflake.

Venkata Kalyan Sanaboyina is a seasoned Engineering Leader with extensive experience building and scaling products. His expertise spans Commerce and payments, platform monetization, and strategic management. He has a strong background in leading the tools and frameworks and development of sophisticated billing and pricing platforms and currently serves as the head of monetization engineering for PayPal large enterprise platform. Kalyan brings a wealth of knowledge in driving revenue growth and optimizing platform monetization strategies.

title: Best Practices for Protecting Against Credential Theft and Data Exfiltration
abstract: Join us to learn more about network and authentication best practices and how to use Trust Center to continuously audit and monitor your organization's security health. This hands-on lab will guide you through the latest best practices to harden your Snowflake accounts against data exfiltration risks. You'll also learn how to use Snowflake authentication enhancements — some which will be mandatory — and how to improve your migration strategy via customer success stories.

speakers.bio: Luis Leon is the Product Manager for Identity and Access Management at Snowflake. Prior to Snowflake, Luis worked for multiple years in the Identity space, driving access control (RBAC) solutions and Enterprise Single Sign-On experiences at Microsoft Entra ID. His experience includes developing and managing solutions for Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) and Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC), with a focus on creating secure and efficient access for users and applications.

20+ years experience in Networking, Crypto, and Security in startups to large enterprises. I drive cross-functional teams towards a unified product vision, helping expand Snowflake's adoption with a shared destiny model.

title: Build and Deploy Data Agents with Snowflake Cortex AI
abstract: In this hands-on lab, you’ll build and deploy AI-powered data agents using Snowflake Cortex agents. Learn how to create agents that automate complex, multi-step operations by orchestrating structured and unstructured data retrieval with Cortex Analyst and Cortex Search. Explore best practices for integrating AI into enterprise workflows, managing governance, and improving decision-making with real-time insights. By the end of the session, you’ll have a fully functional agent that can process requests, retrieve relevant data, and generate intelligent responses — all securely within Snowflake.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Carlos Carrero is a Lead Architect for AI/ML Partners at Snowflake. He works with all Partners world wide defining new products and innovative solutions using Machine Learning and Generative AI. Brings more than 27 years of experience working in different roles as support, consulting, sales engineering, product management and technical leadership.

With experience in big data, data science, and machine learning Dash Desai is able to apply 18+ years of full-stack, hands-on software and data engineering skills to help build solutions that solve business problems and surface trends that shape markets in new ways than imagined before. As a Senior Lead Developer Advocate at Snowflake, he is passionate about evaluating new ideas, trends, and helping articulate how technology can address a given business problem. Dash Desai has worked for global enterprises and in agile environments–for tech startups in the Bay Area in varying verticals, such as VoIP, Social Gaming, Digital Health, NoSQL database, and Data Cloud platforms.

title: Build Intelligent Apps with Microsoft Copilot Studio and Snowflake Cortex Agents
abstract: Do you see the potential of AI Copilots for your organization but struggle to translate that potential into action due to disparate data sources? In this hands-on lab, you’ll learn how to build an intelligent chatbot and talk with your data using Snowflake Cortex Agents and CoPilot Apps to empower your organization to interact with their Snowflake data with natural language. Extend the insights from your Snowflake data for all users with Cortex Agents and Microsoft Copilot. You’ll develop an intelligent app that can provide insights from both structured and unstructured data in Snowflake using Microsoft Co-Pilot and Cortex Agents.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Matt is a Partner Engineer at Snowflake specializing in integrating the Snowflake platform with Microsoft for AI use cases. He has 11 years of experience in Data Science and Machine Learning roles both in consulting and with industry organizations. Matt has worked for companies such as Deloitte and Slalom as a Data Science Consultant and as an AI Architect and Engineer with Microsoft and Snowflake. Matt has experience developing and deploying AI and ML models across many different organizations in areas such as marketing, sales, operations, clinical and finance as well as advising in consultative roles.

Shankar Narayanan SGS is a Principal Architect at Microsoft leading up the forefront of innovation between Microsoft and Snowflake. He is a Snowflake Data Superhero and the co-author of The Ultimate Guide to Snowpark", a definitive resource on building scalable AI and data applications using Snowpark. He is also the co-creator of the Snowflake AI Toolkit.

title: Cortex AI and Streamlit: A Perfect Match for Data Analysts
abstract: For more than 40 years, Centene has operated government-sponsored healthcare programs that reduce costs and improve medical outcomes. We developed a Streamlit app with Cortex to allow analysts to ask natural language questions of our curated data sets. This capability increases the analyst productivity by allowing knowledge workers to ask questions with Snowflake without needing SQL expertise. The system is powered by Centene’s golden queries and knowledge, providing accurate and reliable insights through Cortex.

speakers.bio: Maia is a Cloud Platform Engineer at Centene, where she’s been working since she graduated college in 2022. She enjoys partnering with other software engineers to deliver meaningful automation, data science, and other technology solutions. She recently worked to combine Cortex AI with Streamlit through a process of prototyping, iterative testing, and leveraging real-world data. Her work is especially meaningful in the healthcare space, where better, faster data insights can directly improve people's lives and streamline operations. Maia is passionate about making real-world impacts through innovative solutions that make a real difference in people’s lives.

Dan Wynne is a Senior Manager of Cloud Platform Engineering at Centene with 19+ years of experience in cloud and data platforms. He leads multiple teams, specializing in Snowflake to build secure, standardized, and auditable data platforms for diverse customer teams. A CISSP-certified leader, Dan focuses on security, compliance, and governance, ensuring a one-size-fits-all approach that drives efficiency and scalability. His expertise enables organizations to unlock insights and drive data-driven decisions while maintaining strict enterprise standards.

title: Executive Industry Booth Tours - Retail & Consumer Goods
abstract: Join an exclusive, guided tour of the Snowflake AI Data Cloud and its premier partner ecosystem, tailored for leaders in Retail & Consumer Goods. This immersive experience will take you through five curated stops that showcase the art of the possible—real-world solutions, cutting-edge AI use cases, and data-driven innovations transforming your industry today. Gain direct access to experts, see industry-leading technology in action, and leave with actionable inspiration to accelerate your data strategy. Space is extremely limited—registration is first come, first served. Please note that this experience includes walking between stops; comfortable footwear is recommended. Please note that the Executive Booth Tours are reserved for customers only—we kindly ask our partners not to register.

times.room: Vertical Village - Retail & Consumer Goods Booth

speakers.bio: Jim Warner has over 20 years of experience building large-scale systems at companies like Amazon and Oracle and as head of engineering at four venture-funded startups. Previous to Snowflake, he was co-founder and CTO of Upwave, an ad measurement platform measuring brand lift and reach across digital, CTV, and linear TV. Jim is also an inventor with over 20 issued U.S. patents as well as an author of seven peer-reviewed papers. He earned his B.S. in Computer Science and Applied Math from Johns Hopkins University, graduating with university and departmental honors.

title: Executive Networking Snow Hour: FSI, HCLS and Public Sector
abstract: Join a curated group of leaders across financial services, healthcare, life sciences and the public sector for a high-value hour of networking and insight sharing. This is your opportunity to connect with peers facing similar data, compliance and AI transformation challenges in highly regulated industries.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

title: Flip a Lengthy Data Process on Its Head with TransUnion and Snowflake
abstract: For data teams urged to “do this faster” on a regular basis, join us! The notoriously slow process to access and link credit data isn’t as seamless or speedy as coworkers think — until now. You can access unlimited TransUnion credit data on demand, and a linking app to match it to your first- and third-party data in a pseudonymized manner, all via Snowflake, with TruIQ Data Enrichment. Hear how it works and how teams used it to launch campaigns in half the time and run analyses in two days versus two weeks.

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)
Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Katie Cate is a Director of Solutions Consulting at TransUnion supporting credit risk and analytic solutions. She has over 25 years of experience consulting with top financial services clients. Currently, she consults with customers on the benefits of trended credit and alternative data, as well as the optimal implementation of credit risk decisioning and enabling lenders to create and utilize best-in-class analytic capabilities.

Matt joined TransUnion in 2021 and currently serves as Director of Product of TruIQ Data Access Solutions under Global Credit Risk Solutions. Here he focuses on making credit data easily accessible and usable to data scientists. Prior to TU Matt was responsible for product strategy in data engineering at Enova International, powering the fintech's analytics platforms, including Snowflake. Before that he was Vice President of Product at eSight Energy, a global sustainability and energy management SaaS and consultancy provider. He has a passion for the product management craft and problem solving, and he received his B.S. in industrial engineering from the University of Illinois at Chicago.

title: From Data Integration to Innovation: Centralizing Data from Every Source
abstract: Organizations struggle with data spread across disparate systems, limiting innovation and efficiency. By centralizing data into a single source of truth, businesses unlock the full potential of their data, driving analytics, AI/ML advancements and strategic decision-making. Fivetran’s Connector SDK accelerates this by building custom connectors for unique data sources, ensuring no data is left behind. Join us to explore how data centralization and Connector SDK simplify data integration.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: An automation enabler at Fivetran, known for turning complex challenges into streamlined solutions using Python, Fivetran, the Connector SDK, and HVR. With a passion for scalable systems and developer enablement, Elijah builds tools that empower teams to move faster and smarter without the manual grind.

title: GROUP BY Japan
abstract: Meet fellow attendees at the Japan networking meetup, where peers from across Japan gather to forge new connections. Take this opportunity to meet the Japan community leaders, share insights and broaden your professional network!

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

title: Machine Learning at Capital One Software and Exploring Snowflake Model Registry
abstract: In this session, we’ll take a look at how Capital One Software deploys machine learning models to leverage Snowflake for scalable predictive analytics. It will also provide a glimpse into how we’re exploring Snowflake ML to take advantage of the Snowflake Model Registry to increase potential and velocity.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Formerly a neuroscience researcher, I traded the lab bench for the IDE to focus on driving commercial outcomes with data. Previously, I led a team of data scientists and engineers at a financial services consultancy. As an AI/ML Architect at Snowflake, I now lead AI and ML implementations for our largest financial services clients. I am particularly interested in coupling technology to business outcomes, and I love the creative process of making that happen.

title: Modernizing Massive File System Infrastructure at Morgan Stanley
abstract: Take a deep dive into modernization at Morgan Stanley, enabled by Snowflake, for one of the financial sector's largest distributed file systems — a critical infrastructure managing 500 billion files across 30PB of storage. We tackled the challenge of understanding usage patterns across trillions of file accesses, analyzing 100 million software releases, and identified millions of unused releases. This talk will address the challenges in operational resilience and managing large-scale infrastructure transformation in regulated industries.

speakers.bio: Boris Serebrinskiy leads modernization efforts in Data and Analytics platforms. He specializes in Big Data, compute, networking, and other aspects of data architecture. In 2019 he was promoted to Distinguished Engineer. Boris holds M.S. in Nuclear Physics and Engineering from Odessa Polytechnic University, Ukraine, with a specialization in construction, physics and computer automation of nuclear power plants. He enjoys being part of volunteering efforts that focus on helping non-profit organizations and schools to improve their technology platforms, especially data and analytics related. He is also a huge fan of all things related to aerospace and renewable energy.

title: Revolution in Data: The Journey to Data Mesh
abstract: Discover how Snowflake’s cutting-edge technology is transforming analytics applications of a world-leading logistics company from an on-premise solution to a cloud-based, data mesh ecosystem. See how one organization, with the support of Capgemini, is taking charge of the future of data architecture. This session is a must-attend for those interested in the practicalities and strategic planning involved in large-scale data platform modernization. It will cover: Client’s data mesh platform Large-scale integration database migration Project organization and cultural shift Technical solutions and architectural patterns A reskilling academy

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: As the Delivery Architect Director at Capgemini Insights & Data Poland, I specialize in data platforms and cloud-based data analytics. With extensive experience as a developer and architect across the telco, automotive, and logistics sectors, I have successfully led strategic projects and managed remote teams in Germany, India, and beyond. Fluent in both German and English, I excel in direct client engagements as a lead architect. Additionally, I serve as the Chief Technology Officer at Capgemini Insights & Data Poland.

title: Scale AI-Powered Contextual Ad Targeting: Kargo on Snowflake Cortex
abstract: In 2024, Kargo innovated its contextual ad targeting with a scalable solution for 6M+ premium inventory slots. As the industry shifts toward AI-driven contextual targeting, Kargo harnesses Snowflake Cortex’s AI-powered cohorting and embeddings engine to translate simple, natural-language audience descriptions into bespoke segments. By keeping all data within Snowflake, Kargo streamlined workflows, enhanced targeting precision and reduced reliance on external tools. Today, this built-in contextual solution stands as a key differentiator, delivering measurable lifts in engagement and ROI. Join us to hear how we’re reshaping the future of AI-powered contextual advertising.

speakers.bio: Andy's deep data experience spans ad-tech platforms from mobile and digital publishing, DSP's, and his current role at Kargo. He leads a global team of data scientists, analysts, and data engineers driving Kargo’s auction, audience, and outcomes products and service lines. Scaling cross-channel, CTV, dynamic product ads, and cross-device advertising. Specializing in auction optimizations, enhancing outcomes optimizers, and using NLP, CV, & LLMs across their systems, including product recommendations.

Andrew Underwood leads a Data Science team at Kargo, building scalable ML solutions for advertising — from video recommendation systems to AI-powered audience targeting. He focuses on delivering production-ready models and experimentation frameworks using platforms like Snowflake to drive data-driven product development.

title: Scaling Data Science: Build & Deploy with Snowflake Cortex, Snowflake Notebooks
abstract: Frontdoor, a leading provider of home service plans, is transforming how homeowners maintain and repair their homes through AI-driven solutions. Learn how they transitioned data science and analytics developers from decentralized environments to Snowflake Notebooks at scale. Join this session to explore key development patterns supporting gen AI, classic ML, GraphRAG and Graph Analytics, enabling efficient model development and deployment. Throughout this transition, Frontdoor prioritized data gravity, consolidating computational workloads within the Snowflake ecosystem to maximize performance and efficiency.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Kishore Aradhya is a senior technology leader with over twenty-five years of experience developing and scaling technology organizations, from startups to Fortune 500 companies. He currently leads Data and AI initiatives at Frontdoor Inc., a tech-enabled leader in home warranty services. He has held senior Data/Analytics leadership positions at Stanley Black & Decker, Bose, Adobe, edX/MIT, Staples, and various other startups. He also serves as adjunct faculty at Northeastern University, teaching graduate classes in Data Engineering and Knowledge Graphs, serving on the CDO Magazine Editorial Board, and held Industry/Product Advisory roles at early-stage startups.
I am Nikilesh Urella, currently working as Machine Learning Engineer at Frontdoor. I specialize in Computer Vision & Machine Learning technologies.

title: Shaping the Future of Capital Markets with AI-Driven Data Innovation
abstract: Discover how strategic migration from traditional data infrastructure to cutting-edge architectures revolutionized our product development. By embracing cloud-native technologies and DevOps, we turned data limitations into innovation opportunities. Explore how AI-driven data intelligence is shaping the future of capital markets, unlocking predictive insights, automation and new revenue streams. Learn key lessons that accelerated our data product strategy and AI-powered market innovation.

speakers.bio: Over 25 years' experience in Capital Markets and Financial Services in Industry, Consulting, and Software Product roles focused on data management and analytic initiatives. Currently driving strategy for FIS Investment Data Platform.

A seasoned data leader with extensive experience transforming data organizations into scalable, modern teams. He brings deep expertise in Financial Services and strong background knowledge and experience in database administration and business intelligence to help Hakkoda clients build data-driven strategies that enhance efficiency, innovation, and business value.

title: The ROI of Data Modeling: Speaking to the C-Suite Using Business Metrics
abstract: Data engineers value documentation, governance and best practices but often struggle to convey their financial impact. Lacking fluency in business language and metrics, they find it challenging to demonstrate the ROI of data modeling and governance. This talk uses real-world examples to highlight how these practices improve data quality and team efficiency while linking them to business metrics that resonate with stakeholders and the finance committee.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Serge Gershkovich has nearly two decades of experience in data architecture and business intelligence. He is currently Head of Product at SqlDBM, a Snowflake Data Superhero, and author of the bestselling Data Modeling with Snowflake.

title: We have an Optimization Score – Now what?
abstract: At Fidelity Investments, we have a refrain: Snowflake spend is not bad, but unoptimized spend is not good. What does this mean when we have deployed multiple Snowflake accounts with petabytes of data, numerous end user applications, tens of thousands of users and billions of queries? It means we needed to answer many questions, such as: What does it mean to be optimized? Who is accountable and/or responsible for optimization? And how do you optimize in a large and changing landscape? Join us to learn best practices for building a culture of optimization.

speakers.bio: Balaram Keshri is Vice President, Data Architecture at Fidelity Investments, focused on Enterprise Data Analytics Platform, Snowflake, Data collaboration, Platform capabilities, Security, FINOPS, Cost optimization, Disaster recovery and Business continuity. Over 20 years of experience as Information Management Professional, specializes in various Database and Middleware Architecture, Design, Implementation and Process Automation.

title: Zelis Healthcare: Making Smarter Analysts Using AI and Snowflake Cortex
abstract: Navigating today's complex data landscape demands not only powerful tools but also intelligent, AI-driven solutions that adapt and enhance user engagement. Zelis Healthcare's presentation will showcase an innovative use case that enables its finance team utilizing Cortex AI to revolutionize the way data interacts with analysts across reports and dashboards. Zelis will explore the architecture, discuss the technical challenges and breakthroughs, and demonstrate the significant impact on reducing time to insight and enhance decision-making processes. Join this session to discover how this dynamic, AI driven approach can be scaled to empower users.

speakers.bio: Lakshman Pindikur, with over 28 years in IT and Data, serves as the Senior Director of Zelis Data Cloud and BI at Zelis. His current focus is on leveraging Snowflake's capabilities to enhance data-driven strategies and improve decision-making processes within the company. Holding a Bachelor of Engineering and several Agile and leadership certifications, Lakshman’s leadership at Zelis has been pivotal in transforming the organization’s approach to modernize Data & BI platforms. Based in the NYC area, he continues to drive innovation and excellence in Data Platform & Analytics.

Peter Prina is the Vice President of Data and Software Engineering at Zelis, where he leads initiatives to harness data for optimizing healthcare payments and pricing. He has extensive experience in driving data and analytics strategies across health plans, hospital groups, and integrated healthcare systems. Peter has successfully executed strategic priorities to integrate health data across healthcare, including the development of shared clinical, claims, health plan data capabilities with partners.

title: Feature Feast: Building a Data Buffet for Models
abstract: Fifth Third Bank transformed its machine learning operations using a feature store built with Snowflake and dbt Cloud. The result? Improved model governance, reduced risk and faster product innovation. In this session, learn how the company defined features, automated pipelines and delivered real-time and historical feature views. You’ll walk away with strategies to centralize feature data, boost data science team performance and accelerate business outcomes.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Katie Long is the Lead Product Owner for the Data Science Enablement Squad at Fifth Third Bank, where she plays a pivotal role in driving the bank's MLOps and Data Science initiatives. With a strong Product Mindset and extensive banking experience, Katie collaborates closely with Fifth Third's Data Science Group, Enterprise Data, and Platform teams to articulate, prioritize, and deliver valuable features for the bank. She graduated from the University of Cincinnati in 2020 with a Bachelor of Business Administration and a Certificate in Business Law.

Shanna has a diverse background spanning across Consumer Banking and Technology. With over 15 years of experience in the Consumer Banking, she has a deep understanding of customer needs, as well as the operational and regulatory challenges faced by the industry. She is currently using her expertise to lead Insights & Customer Data in Fifth Third Bank’s Enterprise Data Office using data to drive best-in-class outcomes. Shanna received her bachelor’s degree in international relations from Michigan State University and her MBA from Oakland University. Despite not growing up in the IT world, her unique perspective and expertise have allowed her to bridge the gap between business and technology.

title: From Slow to Pro: Optimizing Iceberg Tables for Peak Query Performance
abstract: Apache Iceberg is the foundation for an open, interoperable lakehouse, but getting the best performance from all its data is hard. In this session, you’ll learn how adaptive Iceberg optimizations in Qlik Open Lakehouse significantly simplifies the ingestion and management of high-volume, frequently changing real-time data from complex sources. We’ll unbox the adaptive engine to understand key techniques it uses to deliver unmatched query performance for Apache Iceberg tables in Snowflake.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Jason is a seasoned solutions architect with over 20 years of experience in building scalable data and analytics platforms. At Qlik, he helps enterprise clients modernize legacy data environments through Lakehouse architectures that unify batch and streaming data, support open formats including Iceberg, and enable high-scale, low-latency workloads. His expertise spans ELT and ETL pipeline orchestration, compute optimization, and platform observability.

title: How Flatiron Health and UCHealth Share Data to Reduce Onboarding Time by 70%
abstract: Discover how Flatiron Health and UCHealth transformed their clinical data exchange by switching from a traditional integration to bi-directional Snowflake Private Listings with Terraform code to automate share configuration. This approach reduced onboarding time by over 70% - from months to just weeks, significantly lowering long-term maintenance efforts, improving reliability, and enabling rapid cross-cloud collaboration on insights from data on more than 250k cancer patients.

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)

Kevin is a Staff Data Analytics Engineering Manager specializing in developing, maintaining, and monitoring sophisticated data integrations with leading Academic Medical Centers. His work empowers researchers by providing high-quality, research-grade datasets aimed at improving and extending lives by leveraging insights gained from the experiences of every individual with cancer.

title: How Fundrise Replaced Calendar-Based Campaigns with AI Decisioning Agents
abstract: With 400,000+ investors and growing, and an increasing number of financial products on offer, Fundrise needed a better way to deliver high-quality lifecycle marketing campaigns that are personalized, convert at higher rates and don’t slow the company down as its business gets more complex. Learn how Lindsay Kaplan, Senior Director of Lifecycle Marketing at Fundrise, replaced 1:many segmentation and calendar-based email sends with AI decisioning agents that autonomously test, optimize and deliver 1:1 personalized messages at scale — driving rapid revenue growth and team efficiency.

With 12+ years of experience in B2C startups across retail, e-commerce, subscription services, and FinTech, she specializes in building lifecycle programs that drive growth, retention, and customer loyalty. A passionate digital marketing leader, Lindsay blends creativity with analytics to craft impactful campaigns that resonate with audiences and deliver business results.

Co-Founder of Hightouch. Hightouch is the leading Composable CDP and AI Decisioning platform for marketing and personalization. Find insights, run campaigns, and build AI agents with all of your Snowflake data.

title: No Risk, All Reward: Verisk's Blueprint for Embedded Analytics
abstract: Discover how Verisk turns data into dollars! Learn their blueprint for app monetization, leveraging the power of Snowflake to fuel their applications. We'll unveil how ThoughtSpot Embedded Analytics provides a seamless, agentic experience for end users, transforming risk assessment into pure reward. See how this integration drives revenue and delivers unparalleled insights, making data work harder for you.

speakers.bio: Matt Huntsman leads the Data Analytic Solutions team for Verisk's Claims organization, where he focuses on delivering impactful, customer-facing ThoughtSpot solutions embedded directly within Claims products. With over 16 years of expertise in data and analytics, Matt guides multiple teams of data engineering, BI, and analytics, empowering them to innovate and help customers unlock critical insights. He partners closely with executive stakeholders, aligning data strategy with key product and business goals to build and deliver advanced analytical solutions across SaaS offerings.

title: Paramount's Always-On Attribution: Measuring Real-World Outcomes at Scale
abstract: In advertising, measurement is everything. Last year, Paramount set out to measure real-world outcomes for every linear and digital ad campaign through Always-On Attribution, a continuous measurement capability built on Snowflake. By rearchitecting our data warehouse from a complex tech-stack into a single Snowflake-powered hub, we enabled seamless Data Shares with partners like iSpot, EDO, and Mastercard, and powered Snowflake dashboards for swift analysis. Centralized warehousing and robust ETLs via Snowflake unlocked rapid development and scalability. Join us for a conversation all about execution - because in the world of advertising, measurement isn’t just a metric, it’s a mandate.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Swathi Chandrasekaran is a Director of Product at Paramount Global with over five years of experience in AdTech and product management. She leads product strategy and development for Vantage—Paramount’s Convergent Planning, Optimization, and Reporting product—along with Paramount’s Always-On Attribution product, Convergent Optimization capabilities, and the data warehouse. She graduated from Columbia University with an MS in Management Science and Engineering. In her free time, she loves reading, writing, and solving puzzles, and cryptic crosswords.

title: Practitioners at Work: Accelerate Data and AI Innovation with Live Demos
abstract: Struggling with lengthy, costly development cycles or complex, difficult-to-manage tech stacks? Imagine cutting dependencies, automating workflows, and reducing costs by 50%, without adding complexity. In this hands-on session, we’ll show you how a modern innovation platform can: Eliminate bottlenecks by orchestrating your AI-ready data products Enable data product composability through data pipeline modularization Deliver data and AI use cases faster

speakers.bio: Suraj is a Senior Sales Engineer with a strong background in software development, security, and auditing. A former full stack developer, he has worked in larger corporations as well as small startups. A passionate mentor, he enjoys coaching teams and fostering innovation. At Calibo, Suraj helps organizations accelerate development and maximize their impact by understanding their problems, and then applying Calibo properly to effectively and efficiently implement a solution.

Technology leader with strong technology/solutions architecture and software development experience. 30+ years of experience building and implementing technical strategies, architecture, and quality technology solutions for Government, B2B, and B2C organizations ranging from 100 to 3000 people. Holder of several patents and a diverse background that encompasses hands-on software engineering, software engineering management, and technology business management within SaaS, Government, e-commerce, and Telecom enterprises.

title: Precision Input, Perfect Output: The Key to a High-Performing RAG Chatbot
abstract: Quality input data is crucial for RAG chatbots to deliver accurate and informative responses. Refining input data such as splitting the raw text, adding meta data, and leveraging filters to find relevant references can significantly enhance chatbot performance. By improving the data, chatbots can better understand user queries, retrieve more relevant information, and generate more accurate responses. Prioritizing input data refinement, developers can unlock their RAG chatbot's full potential.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Katy Haynie is a Senior Solutions Architect at Snowflake, based in the United States, specializing in the AI and ML. She has been pivotal in driving the adoption and implementation of advanced AI and machine learning projects using Snowflake's AI/ML capabilities. By leveraging Snowflake feature suites such as Cortex, Snowpark, and Streamlit, Katy fosters the development and deployment of scalable and impactful AI solutions.

Ian Mathieson is the Director of Data Systems and Application Development, leading the data engineering practice to ensure critical, high quality data is available to end-users for driving business value. Ian fosters innovation in data systems and application development, leveraging modern tools like Snowflake, Kafka, and Azure to create impactful and scalable solutions.

title: Snowflake Account Management and Customer Onboarding at Scale
abstract: Explore strategies for managing the provisioning and decommissioning of hundreds of Snowflake accounts, ensuring efficient setup and teardown processes across a large-scale environment. Learn best practices for configuring authentication and access controls to maintain account security and compliance. Additionally, learn how Adobe seamlessly integrates with Snowflake accounts owned by their customers, enabling secure data sharing and enhanced collaboration across organizational boundaries.

speakers.bio: A seasoned professional with 14 years of industry experience, specializing in programming, distributed systems, and databases, he is currently working at Adobe on the Adobe Campaign product, leveraging Snowflake to power large-scale batch processing.

title: The NBA: Data Teams Delivering ROI in the Age of AI with Hex and Snowflake
abstract: If you're a data leader, you've probably been asked: How do we measure our ROI? If you're seeking an answer ... it's complicated. But some are succeeding. With a new approach to team, measurement and tooling, the NBA has evolved their data org from a cost center to a strategic business partner. We'll explore how the NBA and other Hex customers are revolutionizing the data field, evolving their understanding of AI, and taking a practical approach that delivers business value. This session will provide honest, actionable insights including how the NBA identifies future stars, forecasts fan engagement and even uses in-game events to understand viewership and make real-time decisions.

speakers.bio: Erik leads the Data Science team at the National Basketball Association, where he oversees the league's fan data platform strategy and builds AI-driven tools for business intelligence. Trained as an experimental and computational neuroscientist, Erik brings a scientific approach to understanding and engaging NBA fans at scale.

Barry is the CEO and co-founder of Hex, the world’s most popular platform for collaborative data analysis. Prior to Hex, Barry led deployment and product teams at Palantir, working on solutions to data problems all over the world. He was then at TrialSpark/Formation, where he helped stand up their clinical trial platform. He has a B.S. from Northwestern University, where he focused on network science.

title: The Path to High Data ROI: From Pipeline Automation to Managed AI Agents
abstract: A major shift is happening in the data space and it’s not just AI. Handling the explosive growth of data while managing costs — all without compromising performance — is pushing leaders to revaluate Data ROI. In this session, we explore strategies to minimize total cost of ownership for data integration and to create additional value with data. Learn how to deliver intelligent systems that are better connected with automated data pipelines and how to put this data to use using managed AI agents.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: After working as an in-house data engineer, solution architect at a product company and later on as a data analytics consultant, Ariel has established himself as a data and analytics expert with a blend of strategic, product management, marketing, sales and technical skills. Ariel is driven by designing best-in-class data analytics solutions and achieving customer success.

title: Unlocking Telco Network Operations with Agentic rApps and Snowflake Cortex AI
abstract: This session showcases how Snowflake Cortex AI and multi-agent frameworks like Schema Agent enhance Ericsson’s Intelligent Automation Platform (EIAP) for network management. AI-driven rApps — anomaly detectors, KPI analyzers, RCA agents — use LLMs, RAG and Cortex Analyst to automate troubleshooting. This reduces MTTR, enables intent-driven automation, and paves the way for self-optimizing telecom networks.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: AI, Data, and Cloud leader with 20+ years delivering transformative solutions for Tier-1 telcos and enterprises. Specializes in leveraging data, ML, Gen AI, and Agentic AI to enable automation, optimization, monetization, and evolution of telco networks into fully autonomous networks. Successfully led multimillion-dollar AI/cloud initiatives globally and incubated high-impact innovations leveraging Snowflake, AWS, Azure, and GCP.

title: Architecting SaaS for Scale: Why and How to Build on Snowflake
abstract: A reliable, performant, secure and scalable data layer is critical for SaaS applications — but managing and scaling it remains a major challenge. Snowflake is uniquely architected to address many of the challenges of SaaS applications. In this session, we’ll explore the key challenges of building SaaS applications, uncover opportunities for optimization, and demonstrate how Snowflake enables scalable, efficient and secure architectures.

speakers.bio: Brian has been in the data and analytics industry for over 20 years. He started his career doing applied research in mathematics and computer science at the National Security Agency. Since then he has had various technical and product roles in government and companies specializing in data warehousing, NoSQL, and analytics. Brian is currently Global Field CTO focusing on data applications.

Product Manager working on Snowflake's application platform. Focusing on the different type of app deployments in the Snowflake platform like Managed, Connected and Native Apps. Previously worked on large scale application platform products for Meta (Infra and Ads) and Microsoft (serverless computing with Azure Functions and PaaS with Azure App Service). Excited to see what companies & developers can build at Snowflake.

title: Best Practices: Scalable, Secure and Cost-Optimized Orchestration
abstract: Data Engineers need flexible, efficient and secure orchestration to simplify data pipelines without the burden of optimizing compute for price and performance. This session introduces the latest Serverless Tasks capabilities that dynamically scale compute based on workload characteristics, optimizing for both performance and cost. Learn best practices to eliminate operational overhead, maximize resource efficiency and streamline your data workflows — all without managing infrastructure. Join us to explore how serverless orchestration is redefining data engineering.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Engineering Manager of Snowflake's Orchestration team, working on Snowflake Tasks.

Expert in Snowflake Data Cloud, Big data and advance analytics. Primarily focusing on Data Science, Data Engineering and Data Lake workloads. Led and worked with several technical and corporate teams, from developers to CTOs/CIOs, to build cost-effective data solutions using public/hybrid cloud infrastructure. Delivered and responsible for overseeing the design, implementation, and management of large-scale data infrastructure.

title: Build a Next-Gen Open Security and Observability Iceberg Data Lake
abstract: Create a completely open observability and security data lake without worrying about storage, storage costs or scalability with Snowflake and Iceberg. In this session we will show how you can use the OpenTelemetry ecosystem of connectors and plug those natively into Snowflake to receive logs, traces and metrics. You'll learn how to build an observability and security data lake using Snowflake to create and manage Iceberg tables in your own object storage.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
