speakers.bio: Kevin Keller is a seasoned technology professional with a deep expertise in data engineering, AI/ML, observability, and security automation. He has made significant contributions to the field through his innovative approaches in regards to integrating OpenTelemetry data into Snowflake as well as automating security incident alerting to downstream systems from Snowflake natively. He also wrote extensively on the topic of data type and format type preserving encryption of data on the column level. Additionally he also made significant contributions in the field of connecting Snowflake securely to on-premise or behind firewall infrastructures.

Jake is a Principal Architect heading Snowflake's Cybersecurity Data Cloud. At Snowflake, Jake's mission is to evangelize and enable the implementation of modern security analytics and engineering. title: CarMax’s Move to Snowflake: 450+ Pipelines, 80+ TB, 1,200+ Reports in 18 Months
abstract: Join this session to learn how CarMax planned and performed its enterprise data warehouse modernization to Snowflake, which involved more than 450 data pipelines, a data transfer of more than 80 terabytes, and more than 1,200 business intelligence report conversions within 18 months — all while minimizing disruption to end users and applications. The team at CarMax used SnowConvert and partnered with Snowflake Professional Services to accelerate their migration, and they are now keeping the momentum going postmigration with user training through a Snowflake Guild, proactive cost management and exploration of Snowflake AI capabilities.

title: Delivering Self-Service Data for AI Scale on Snowflake
abstract: AI initiatives stall when data teams can’t meet the demand for ad hoc, self-service data. Pipelines are not agile enough for AI agents, BI tools or business users needing data immediately. Promethium enables the data teams to generate instant, contextual data products (data answers) to meet this demand, empowering data teams for AI-scale collaboration with the business. We will demo how to build data answers on Snowflake, extending Cortex AI with additional context to ensure accuracy and relevance.

We will share insights on their data mesh architecture, representation of their business units in the Internal Marketplace, best practices for curation of data assets, lessons learned, and more!

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: As a Principal Product Owner at Flix, a leading global travel-tech company, I am dedicated to empowering a growing decentralized data organization. Collaborating with a team of data experts, we build central data services following the principles of Data Mesh to ensure the right data reaches the right people at the right time.

title: How PepsiCo Turns Data into Business Assets with Embedded AI-Powered Analytics
abstract: Analytical apps today must turbocharge digital experiences with embedded insights at every customer touchpoint and accelerate value realization. Hear from PepsiCo on how they have modernized their data and analytics strategy to enable a data-driven culture, drive business agility, optimize operations and delight customers. Learn how they leverage ThoughtSpot’s natural language search and embedded analytics to deliver true self-service analytics and actionable intelligence at every level.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: As an Analytics Engineering Lead within the Insights & Intelligence organization at PepsiCo, Marcus leads data engineering efforts in digital product development, with a focus on automation and full-stack analytics applications. His ability to integrate cutting-edge technology with business strategy has positioned him as an effective leader; delivering scalable, consumer-centric solutions. By aligning data-driven insights with business objectives, Marcus helps drive growth, efficiency, and innovation at PepsiCo.

title: How Q2 Protects and Shares Billions of Records with ALTR and Snowflake
abstract: With $3.5T+ in annual transactions and 4.5B+ logins, Q2 needed a secure way to share regulated financial data across 400+ institutions while ensuring isolation, compliance and performance in the cloud. This session reveals how Q2 solved this with Snowflake and ALTR’s format-preserving encryption (FPE). Discover how ALTR enables a single Snowflake instance to function as a multi-tenant banking environment, giving each institution the experience of its own secure, isolated database — without the complexity of separate deployments. Learn how Q2 redefined regulatory logging with ALTR, ensuring continuous monitoring, control and auditability at scale.

speakers.bio: An engineer by trade and coach by passion, building skillful development and IT teams has been a trademark of Lou Senko’s career. Lou brings more than 20 years of experience leading architectural design, development, implementation and integration of enterprise-wide solutions to meet global business, financial and market demands to Q2. His organization is responsible for the Availability, Performance and Quality of the solutions being delivered, and the Security and Compliance of the environments they are delivered from. This aligns Hosting, Support with Security & Compliance under a single organization.

James Beecham is a Co-founder and the CEO at ALTR, an innovator of complete data access control and security solutions. He has over a decade of experience leading technology and engineering teams in computer architecture design, database and operating system drivers, database security and data governance and privacy system design. James holds multiple software patents and a degree in Electrical and Computer Engineering.

title: How the NYSE Leverages Time-Series Analytics
abstract: Time-series data is everywhere, from IoT sensors and web logs to financial markets and beyond. With Snowflake's latest innovations, organizations can efficiently process and analyze time-series data to derive insights at low latencies. Hear from data leaders at the NYSE, a large financial firm that operates global financial exchanges and clearinghouses, about how it leverages Snowflake for its time-series use cases such as exchange analytics, order book analysis and VWAP calculations.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Jon Herrick is the Chief Product Officer for the New York Stock Exchange, a part of Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE: ICE), responsible for product development and innovation across all areas of the exchange’s business. In his role, Jon oversees the NYSE’s transactions, ETF Listings and market data businesses, driving competitive strategy and distribution.

title: How to Manage Data Quality, Security and Cost Visibility for AI
abstract: Companies need robust data management capabilities to build and deploy AI. Data needs to be easy to find, understandable and trustworthy. And it’s even more important to secure data properly from the beginning of its lifecycle, otherwise it can be at risk of exposure during training or inference. Tokenization is a highly efficient method for securing data without compromising performance. In this session, we’ll share tips for managing high-quality, well-protected data at scale that are key for accelerating AI. In addition, we’ll discuss how to integrate visibility and optimization into your compute environment to manage the hidden cost of AI – your data.

speakers.bio: Yudhish Batra is a Distinguished Engineer for Capital One Software, an enterprise B2B software business of Capital One. In his current role, he leads solution architecture for Capital One Slingshot. Yudhish has more than 18 years of software and data engineering experience. He has a Bachelor's degree in computer science, and lives in Richmond VA with his wife and two daughters. In his free time, Yudhish likes to play or watch cricket and spend time with his family.

Laura is a Director of Product Management at Capital One Software, an enterprise B2B software business of Capital One. Laura has spent the last 25 years passionately exploring and talking about all things data. As a data and analytics product leader, she has built innovative enterprise solutions that empower customers across healthcare, data management, and data security industries to make better data-driven decisions. Her expertise lies in transforming data challenges into intuitive, high-impact products that drive real-world value. Beyond building great products, Laura is known for growing and nurturing teams by leading with authenticity, humor, and optimism.

title: Questioning the Answers: LLMs Enter the Boardroom
abstract: Earnings call Q&A offers insights into executive behavior. When analysts ask about topics not covered in prepared remarks, it may indicate oversight or intentional avoidance. Using LLM vector embeddings on 192,000 earnings calls, executives were scored on: Proactiveness: How well-prepared remarks addressed analyst questions On-topic alignment: Whether responses stayed relevant Firms with on-topic executives outperformed by 390 bps/year, proactive executives by 170 bps/year, and combining both factors yielded 506 bps/year in a long/short portfolio.

speakers.job_title: Global Head of New Product Development
speakers.bio: Liam Hynes is the Global Head of New Product Development at S&P Global Market Intelligence, leading strategic innovation and advancing research across S&P's data and analytics platforms. He oversees the full solution life cycle, from proof-of-concept to commercialization. Previously, he was Head of ESG & Quant Specialists, EMEA, advising clients on quantitative modeling and climate risk. Before S&P, he co-founded Monreith Capital LLP, managing a Global Financials Hedge Fund, and held senior investment roles at Liontrust Asset Management and Occam Asset Management. Liam holds a BSc (Hons) in Mathematics from the University of Limerick and a Machine Learning certification from Stanford.

title: Streamlining Snowflake Implementation: Insights from Industry Leaders
abstract: In this session, a representative from Fortune Brands Innovations Inc. will share their experience accelerating their Snowflake implementation. They will discuss challenges; strategies to improve modeling, version control and governance; and how they streamlined workflows for faster deployment. Joining them, SqlDBM's head of product will provide insights into best practices for modeling and governance. Attendees will gain insights into strategies and lessons from their implementation.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Serge Gershkovich has nearly two decades of experience in data architecture and business intelligence. He is currently Head of Product at SqlDBM, a Snowflake Data Superhero, and author of the bestselling Data Modeling with Snowflake.

Joseph leads Data Operations and Data Modeling at Fortune Brands Innovations, driving data-driven decision-making and innovation. With significant expertise in Data Vault, Enterprise Data Warehousing, data modeling, data engineering, integration strategies, visualization, and team leadership, Joseph plays a key role in enhancing data accessibility and reliability. He provides strategic guidance on emerging technologies and ensures compliance with governance and security policies. His leadership fosters high-performing, collaborative teams while enabling the organization to maximize the value of its data assets and strengthen its data management capabilities.

Seasoned data leader in the Data field, with a focus on modern data and analytics and data science stacks. Leading data teams to deliver data products that will drive business decisions is something that I take a lot of pride in.

speakers.job_title: Head of Data, Cloud & Security Architecture
speakers.bio: As a seasoned technical leader with over 20 years of expertise in digital product delivery, I bring a hands-on approach to driving innovation in data, security, data, and cloud. As a recognized Snowflake Data Superhero, Subject Matter Expert, and Co-organizer of the Snowflake Toronto User Group. I am passionate about guiding organizations through complex data challenges and scaling high-performing engineering teams.

As one of Snowflake's original Data Superheroes, he has a deep knowledge of cloud data platforms, data modeling, and analytics technologies such as dbt Cloud, Snowflake, AWS Redshift, ThoughtSpot, and Looker. He has led multiple migrations to cloud data & advanced analytics platforms from on-premise platforms such as Cloudera and Oracle. He also know as a international thought leader on embedded analytics, data monetization, and data product management. He has a passion for data and analytics and is always looking for ways to help organizations leverage their data to drive business value.

title: The Data Townhouse: How Ramp Brings Data Personas Together with Hex & Snowflake
abstract: Ramp, the all-in-one spend management platform, was struggling to get nontechnical users empowered with data. The Data Science team wasn't able to do its best work; insights were scattered across fragmented tools and the team wasn’t able to work efficiently. Using Snowflake and Hex, Ramp built a “data townhouse” to increase data consumption and improve decision making. They provide verticalized reporting, forecasts and applied AI, increasing user engagement with data across the business. Come learn from Ian Macomber, Ramp’s head of Analytics Engineering and Data Science, about how his data team organized and executed on this ambitious project.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.job_title: Head of Analytics Engineering and Data Science
speakers.bio: Ian Macomber is the Head of Analytics Engineering and Data Science at Ramp. Ramp’s data team leverages SQL, python, and cutting edge ML/AI techniques to empower teams across the company to make informed decisions, and to save Ramp customers time and money. Prior to Ramp, Ian led data teams at Wayfair and Drizly. Ian splits time between Boston and New York with his wife, and is an avid skier, road cyclist, and Red Sox fan.

title: Opening Keynote
abstract: Join us to kick off Snowflake Summit 2025 in San Francisco! Come hear from Snowflake's CEO, Sridhar Ramaswamy, as he discusses the impact AI has had across organizations and how the Snowflake AI Data Cloud is helping to accelerate enterprise AI. He'll be joined by Lynn Martin, President of the New York Stock Exchange Group, for a conversation on how data and AI are being used to power operations and shape the future of global markets. Then, in a CEO fireside conversation, Sridhar and OpenAI Founder and CEO Sam Altman will explore what the future holds in this new AI era.

speakers.bio: Sridhar Ramaswamy currently serves as Snowflake’s CEO. After joining Snowflake in May 2023 in connection with the company’s acquisition of Neeva, the world’s first private AI powered search engine, Sridhar spearheaded Snowflake’s AI strategy as SVP of AI. Prior to joining Snowflake, Sridhar co-founded Neeva in 2019. Prior to founding Neeva, Sridhar led all of Google’s Advertising products, which included search, display and video advertising, analytics, shopping, payments, and travel. During his 15 years at Google, he was an integral part of the growth of AdWords and Google’s advertising business from $1.5 billion to over $100 billion. He also held research positions at Bell Labs, Lucent Technologies, and Bell Communications Research (Bellcore). Sridhar was a Venture Partner at Greylock Partners from October 2018 until recently, and he sits on the board of trustees at Brown University.

Sam Altman is the co-founder and CEO of OpenAI, the AI research and deployment company behind ChatGPT and Sora. Sam was president of the early-stage startup accelerator Y Combinator from 2014 to 2019. In 2015, Sam co-founded OpenAI with the mission to build general-purpose artificial intelligence that benefits all humanity.

Lynn Martin is President of NYSE Group, which includes the New York Stock Exchange, the world’s largest stock market. Martin is the 68th President of the New York Stock Exchange and the second woman to lead the exchange in more than 230-year history. The NYSE, the premier venue for capital raising, is the listing home to the world’s most influential and innovative companies. In addition to the exchange, NYSE Group includes four fully electronic equity markets, including NYSE Arca, the industry leader in ETFs, and two options exchanges. Martin is also Chair of Fixed Income & Data Services at Intercontinental Exchange, Inc. (NYSE: ICE), the parent of NYSE Group. Most recently, she was President of that business, responsible for ICE Bonds execution venues, securities pricing and analytics, reference data, indices, desktop solutions, consolidated feeds and connectivity services that cover all major asset classes. She previously served as President of ICE Data Services, COO of ICE Clear U.S., and in a number of leadership roles including CEO of NYSE Liffe U.S. and CEO of New York Portfolio Clearing. Martin began her career at IBM in its Global Services organization. Martin holds a BS in Computer Science from Manhattan College and an MA in Statistics from Columbia University. She currently sits on the Board of Directors of the Partnership for New York City, the Inner-City Scholarship Fund and the New York State Emerging Technology Advisory Board. Martin also serves on the Manhattan College Board of Trustees.

title: Basecamp Welcome Reception
abstract: Basecamp Welcome Reception

title: Best Practices in Automating/Managing Snowflake: Data Projects, Terraform, CI/CD
abstract: Discover effective strategies for managing Snowflake objects and creating DevOps CI/CD pipelines. This hands-on lab will guide you through utilizing native Snowflake Data Projects as well as the Snowflake Terraform provider to streamline the management of your Snowflake Infrastructure. We will also cover how to efficiently create and manage CI/CD pipelines with your preferred platform (GitHubActions, Azure Devops and Jenkins) to automate deployments of your managed infrastructure.

speakers.bio: Christen is an experienced data management specialist with over 10 years of expertise as a data analyst, engineer, and architect, and Snowflake is the only thing she has not broken (yet). Passionate about innovation and process improvement, Christen has successfully modernized data platforms, implemented new technologies, and identified major cost savings at companies in Healthcare and Oil and Gas. She loves sharing knowledge with others and enabling teams to choose optimal solutions. Currently a Senior Solution Engineer, Christen helps customers build innovative, cost effective solutions to business challenges in Healthcare using the Snowflake Data Cloud.

Artur, a Senior Software Engineer at Snowflake, has led the Snowflake Terraform Provider project for nearly two years, focusing on stability and official support. He is dedicated to developing simple, practical solutions and delivering robust software engineering outcomes.

title: Data Drivers Awards
abstract: The Data Drivers Awards program recognizes Snowflake customers (individuals and organizations) who are pushing boundaries, driving innovation and delivering impact within their business and industry — using the Snowflake AI Data Cloud. Join us live at the Builder’s Hub stage as we announce our global Data Drivers Award winners for 2025.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

title: How to Build a Commerce and Retail Media Network on Snowflake
abstract: Traditionally, commerce and retail media networks are built by sending first parties to external tools for activation and measurement, but this results in long build cycles, limited flexibility and vendor lock-in. Instead, bring your media directly to your data in Snowflake to build faster, be more agile and maintain full data ownership. Join Hightouch, The Trade Desk, and Snowflake and build an end-to-end offsite media network solution to curate, syndicate and measure first-party campaigns for both managed service and self-service operations.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Having worked for The Trade Desk for the past 6 years, I have technically managed accounts across inventory partnerships, data partnerships and client integrations. My main focus in my current role is entirely on retail integrations while managing a small team of retail focused Technical Account Mangers!

Ian leads AdTech products and go-to-market initiatives at Hightouch. He works with advertisers, agencies, and internal teams to ensure that every Hightouch customer makes the most of their first-party and third-party data strategies. Ian has spent ten years in paid media as a practitioner for growing ecommerce brands, a B2B growth leader, and a product manager and GM for AdTech products. His expertise includes the use of first-party audiences, third-party digital identity, data onboarding, conversion APIs, AI signals, attribution, incrementality testing, and data clean rooms.

title: Simplify and Accelerate Data Access Provisioning with Immuta and Snowflake
abstract: As demand for data accelerates, data teams face a challenge: How to securely provision data access at scale. Immuta tackles these complexities head-on, offering policy-driven workflows that automate data provisioning and help you put Snowflake data to work. In this lab, you’ll hear from industry leaders who have transformed data governance with Immuta. Then, roll up your sleeves for an interactive experience led by Immuta's field CTOs. You’ll learn how to: Craft scalable, automated data policies Swiftly handle data access requests at scale Enforce governance policies and watch them instantly take effect in Snowflake

speakers.bio: Paul serves as Principal Field CTO at Immuta, where he develops and implements scalable governance strategies for large enterprise customers within the HCLS and FSI verticals. Leveraging over 15 years of global experience, Paul has collaborated with numerous top 100 companies to align their regulatory and compliance initiatives with data scaling and security requirements. In response to the growing presence and influence of AI models, Paul has established best practices that balance data privacy concerns with secure and efficient access across organizational boundaries.

With nearly two decades of dedicated service at JPMorgan Chase, Jenson has established himself as a leading expert in analytics and reporting. His extensive experience has been instrumental in driving the firm's success through data-driven insights and strategic decision-making. As a key figure in developing analytic platforms, Jenson has played a pivotal role in creating systems integral to the operations of internal stakeholders. His innovative approach and commitment to excellence have enhanced the firm's capabilities. Jenson's expertise and leadership continue to inspire and empower teams across the organization. Beyond his passion for data, he enjoys traveling and motorcycles.

title: Platform Keynote
abstract: Snowflake executives including Snowflake Co-Founder and President of Product, Benoit Dageville, and Snowflake Executive Vice President of Product, Christian Kleinerman, along with other leaders, will unveil new innovations that make enterprise data and AI even more connected, trusted and easy. They will showcase how organizations of every size are securely bringing AI to data and application development, maximizing value while saving both time and money with Snowflake.

speakers.bio: Benoit Dageville co-founded Snowflake and currently serves as President of Products. He oversees the company’s technical strategy and leads a team of engineers, product managers, and designers who are making data easily accessible and actionable to everyone. Benoit is a leading expert in parallel execution and self-tuning database systems. Prior to founding Snowflake, Benoit was with Oracle for over 10 years as a lead architect for parallel execution in Oracle RAC and a key architect in the SQL Manageability group. Prior to Oracle, Benoit worked at Bull Information Systems. He helped define the architecture and lead database performance efforts for Bull’s parallel systems. Benoit has a PhD in Computer Science with a focus in Parallel Database Systems from Université de Paris VI and is a named inventor on more than 270 patents.

Christian Kleinerman serves as Snowflake’s EVP of Product and has been with the company since 2018. He oversees the company’s global product strategy and vision. Christian is a database expert with over 20 years of experience working with various database technologies and has more than 15 years of management and leadership experience. Most recently, Christian worked at Google leading YouTube’s infrastructure and data systems. Prior to that, he served as General Manager of the Data Warehousing product unit at Microsoft where he was responsible for a broad portfolio of products. Christian holds a BS in Industrial Engineering from Los Andes University in Colombia, and he is a named inventor on numerous Snowflake patents.

With experience in big data, data science, and machine learning Dash Desai is able to apply 18+ years of full-stack, hands-on software and data engineering skills to help build solutions that solve business problems and surface trends that shape markets in new ways than imagined before. As a Senior Lead Developer Advocate at Snowflake, he is passionate about evaluating new ideas, trends, and helping articulate how technology can address a given business problem. Dash Desai has worked for global enterprises and in agile environments–for tech startups in the Bay Area in varying verticals, such as VoIP, Social Gaming, Digital Health, NoSQL database, and Data Cloud platforms.

Amanda Kelly serves as Director of Product Experiences at Snowflake, where she oversees all of Snowflake’s product experiences. Prior to Snowflake she was Co-founder and COO of Streamlit (acquired by Snowflake). Amanda also led product and operations for Zoox (acquired by Amazon) and several Google X projects. She has an MBA from Stanford GSB and holds several industry patents.

Renee Huang is a seasoned product leader at Snowflake, where she drives the snowflake AI-SQL initiative, a core component of Snowflake’s cutting-edge unstructured data batch analytics. With a keen focus on enabling intuitive, large-scale data interactions using AI, Renee plays a pivotal role in shaping the future of AI-powered data analytics. Prior to her current role, Renee was the engineering lead for the Cortex Analyst (Text-to-SQL), where she helped pioneer natural language interfaces for data access—bridging the gap between human intent and SQL generation.

speakers.job_title: Director of Product, Snowflake Intelligence and Agents
speakers.bio: Jeff Hollan is Director of Product for Snowflake's GenAI Agentic platform and Snowflake Intelligence. Prior to joining Snowflake Jeff led the serverless and developer compute businesses for Microsoft Azure. Jeff is a well-known and frequent speaker on building cloud native applications. He has been a keynote speaker and presenter at industry conferences including KubeCon, ServerlessConf, Microsoft Build, and the Linux Foundation API Summit.

speakers.job_title: VP, Insights and Technology Excellence - Chief Data Officer
speakers.bio: Brian Dummann serves as the VP IT & Chief Data Officer at AstraZeneca since 2021, bringing extensive experience in data and technology leadership. Previous roles include VP IT & GM of Data & Analytics at Intel Corporation (2020-2021), SVP and Chief Data & Analytics Officer at McKesson (2018-2020), and SVP and Chief Technology Officer at GE (2016-2018). Additional positions held include Chief Architect & Data Leader at Emerson (2011-2016), Global IS Director at Boston Scientific (2009-2011), and Management Consultant at Pfizer (2008-2009). Dummann's career began at Medtronic as Global IT Director (1997-2008)

Julia Morrison is currently leading Data & Privacy Engineering in Global Technology at Marriott International on the CTO 's team. Julia has been with Marriott International for over 19 years in progressively expanding roles in Data Engineering, Product Management, Privacy, Analytics, Data Science, Revenue Management, all on Technology side. In recent years, Julia lead prototyping, development, and maintenance of the Modem Data Platform (MDP), Tokenization Platform, Event Hub and real-time Customer 360 systems (UGI/CIAM). Under her direction, Modern Data Platform grew from purposely built warehouses, to the single Data Platform with one stop shop for Business Analysts, Data Scientists, supporting many marketing, computational, financial, and operational business processes. As privacy regulations evolved around the globe, Julia established the GT Privacy Product & Engineering practice, responsible for supporting Global Privacy & Compliance Office and making sure our systems, data handling and retention processes are compliant with the regulations, and all relevant Privacy requirements are pulled through our systems. Julia began her career at Marriott International in Revenue Management Systems, where she developed pricing, forecasting, and optimization models and later led the team of Data Scientists who were responsible for building all the Machine Leaming models for revenue and inventory optimization. She built One Yield v2, Revenue Pricing Optimizer, award winning Total Yield/GPO, and many others, that delivered significant profit lift to the properties. Prior to joining Marriott International, Julia was a consultant with McKinsey & Company, specializing in Pricing, Analytics, Retail, and Consumer Packaged Goods. Julia holds PhD in Operations & Financial Engineering from Princeton University, and BS in Applied Physics & Mathematics from the Moscow Institute of Technology Originally from Ukraine, Julia lives in Northern Virginia with her husband and their three teen/tween kids. All three kids play ice hockey, so Julia is very active in the local youth hockey community, manages a couple of travel teams each season, and specifically helps with travel panning for their teams and ensures the teams stay in best hotels.

Brandy currently oversees Client Experience Products at Fiserv focusing on merchant solutions. She utilizes her 20+ years of expertise in financial services and technology to drive innovation and enhance client experiences. She specializes in building strategic partnerships and delivering on digital transformation to future proof technology solutions for clients and partners.

Passionate about solving complex data challenges, improving efficiency, and collaborating with the data community.

Snowflake Data Superhero, FinOps expert, & IndatAI Inc. co-founder. For 17+ years, I've empowered Fortune 500 leaders (retail, banking, green energy, media) to turn data into measurable outcomes. Specializing in Snowflake since 2019, I transform platform costs into strategic ROI, identifying 5-figure overspends and consistently reducing compute-credit burn by ~30%. My hands-on bootcamps in FinOps, SQL optimization, and advanced analytics immediately boost team performance. Passionate about AI innovation, I architect robust AI systems powered by Snowflake, enabling clients to reinvest their savings into impactful growth.

As a Data Engineering Practice Manager at Billigence, my main focus is to develop and deliver innovative business intelligence and data analytics solutions. With a specialization in data cloud technologies, data engineering and data governance, my consulting track record includes several international implementations of DWH/BI solutions in Australia, the USA, the UK, Germany, and the Czech Republic.
As a Data Engineer at EY – FSO and the founder of Snowflake Italia User Group, Sofia builds data solutions and communities with the same purpose: sharing knowledge and pushing boundaries.

He loves writing and was awarded Quora Top Writer and is also Snowflake Data Superhero for three years. Outside work, he loves reading and long distance running.

title: Best Practices for Cost-Effective Development and Deployment on Snowflake ML
abstract: In this session, two of Snowflake's most senior ML architects will deep-dive into best practices for ensuring maximally efficient and cost-effective development and deployment of ML capabilities on Snowflake. We will cover optimization techniques throughout the end-to-end ML workflow to achieve best-in-class price performance, as well as best practices for managing efficient model deployment and production pipelines. We will highlight different approaches and trade-offs as well as considerations for how to maximize performance and cost efficiency when running enterprise models on Snowflake ML.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.job_title: Principal AI/ML Architect, Applied Field Engineering
speakers.bio: Caleb is a Principal Architect for AI/ML in the Applied Field Engineering office at Snowflake. He is the global field lead for the Snowpark and Snowflake ML ecosystem. His background is in theoretical mathematics and philosophy. Prior to Snowflake, he was a practicing data scientist and machine learning engineer primarily in the defense and intelligence contracting industry. Caleb is based out of Denver, CO.
Manager of our Solution Innovation team specializing in implementing AI/ML workloads on Snowflake

title: Build Intelligent Apps with Microsoft Copilot Studio and Snowflake Cortex Agents
abstract: Do you see the potential of AI Copilots for your organization but struggle to translate that potential into action due to disparate data sources? In this hands-on lab, you’ll learn how to build an intelligent chatbot and talk with your data using Snowflake Cortex Agents and CoPilot Apps to empower your organization to interact with their Snowflake data with natural language. Extend the insights from your Snowflake data for all users with Cortex Agents and Microsoft Copilot. You’ll develop an intelligent app that can provide insights from both structured and unstructured data in Snowflake using Microsoft Co-Pilot and Cortex Agents.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Matt is a Partner Engineer at Snowflake specializing in integrating the Snowflake platform with Microsoft for AI use cases. He has 11 years of experience in Data Science and Machine Learning roles both in consulting and with industry organizations. Matt has worked for companies such as Deloitte and Slalom as a Data Science Consultant and as an AI Architect and Engineer with Microsoft and Snowflake. Matt has experience developing and deploying AI and ML models across many different organizations in areas such as marketing, sales, operations, clinical and finance as well as advising in consultative roles.

Shankar Narayanan SGS is a Principal Architect at Microsoft leading up the forefront of innovation between Microsoft and Snowflake. He is a Snowflake Data Superhero and the co-author of The Ultimate Guide to Snowpark", a definitive resource on building scalable AI and data applications using Snowpark. He is also the co-creator of the Snowflake AI Toolkit.

title: Customer Zero: How Snowflake Uses Cortex AI to Drive Business Outcomes
abstract: In this session, see how Snowflake's internal teams — Engineering, IT, Legal, HR and more — use Cortex AI to quickly build and deploy innovative AI use cases. Learn how these innovations drive productivity, cut costs, increase revenue, and most importanly, improve customer experience.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: As a value engineering leader, Mitali advises Snowflake customers on their Data and AI use cases and helps them align their Snowflake spend to their business goals to maximize their ROI. Prior to Snowflake., Mitali worked in various consulting roles across different industries with large technology vendors.

Sandeep Chandana is Sr. Director for Machine Learning (Corp) at Snowflake and leads AI/ML initiatives for corporate, business and security functions. He has extensive experience leading ML, data science and data engineering, building products that leverage ML at scale and operate near real-time.

title: Driving Toyota’s Digital Transformation with Snowflake Cortex AI and Document AI
abstract: Toyota Motor Europe is revolutionizing operations with generative AI and Document AI. This session explores how the RAG-as-a-Service (RaaS) platform enhances enterprise search, enabling users to securely access documentation via knowledge chatbots. Additionally, learn how Snowflake’s Document AI streamlines contract management by automating data ingestion and reducing manual work, improving efficiency and driving digital transformation across the organization.

speakers.bio: Anthony is a Senior Manager within the AI & Data team at Deloitte, focused on helping clients in their AI transformations. With a background in developing AI solutions himself, Anthony his main expertise lies within the AI model space and how to unlock value for the business. Prior to working at Deloitte, Anthony was working as a data science consultant at a boutique AI consultancy.

title: Effective Snowflake Native Apps: Patterns, Tips and Tricks
abstract: New capabilities and proven patterns are shaping the Snowflake Native App Framework. This session highlights best practices for app evaluation, installation and productionization — whether for internal use, partners or customers. Join us to explore key topics, including identifying ideal use cases for Snowflake Native Apps, implementing RBAC in line with organizational policies, optimizing compute and storage choices, and designing for seamless upgrades. This session is valuable for both new builders starting with Snowflake Native Apps and experienced developers looking to explore the latest enhancements.

speakers.bio: Dinesh Kulkarni is a product manager in the Native Apps team at Snowflake building Native Apps framework. Previously he shipped ML functions for forecasting, anomaly detection & Auto-Insights for understanding KPI changes. Before that at Snowflake, he built out the data pipelines story with streams, tasks & Snowpipe. Before Snowflake, he worked on ML in Google Cloud & on various developer & database services & components in Azure & MIcrosoft including SQL Server & C# language.

Scott is a Principal Software Engineer at Snowflake, leading the Native Applications Foundation team. He has an extensive career in the world of databases, including leading the development of Apple's CloudKit database platform, and architecting IBM's Big SQL database platform.

title: Executive Industry Booth Tours - Financial Services
abstract: Join an exclusive, guided tour of the Snowflake AI Data Cloud and its premier partner ecosystem, tailored for leaders in Financial Services. This immersive experience will take you through five curated stops that showcase the art of the possible—real-world solutions, cutting-edge AI use cases, and data-driven innovations transforming your industry today. Gain direct access to experts, see industry-leading technology in action, and leave with actionable inspiration to accelerate your data strategy. Space is extremely limited—registration is first come, first served. Please note that this experience includes walking between stops; comfortable footwear is recommended. Please note that the Executive Booth Tours are reserved for customers only—we kindly ask our partners not to register.

title: From AI Pilot to Enterprise-Grade Gen AI Apps with Informatica and Snowflake
abstract: Successful adoption of generative AI applications in an enterprise require a robust and trusted data foundation. In this session, discover how a joint customer leverages Informatica’s Intelligent Data Management Cloud™ (IDMC) and the Snowflake AI Data Cloud to build a modern data foundation to provide clean, trusted and governed data for their gen AI initiatives. Dive deep into Informatica’s Generative AI Blueprint for Snowflake Cortex AI, highlighting its agentic AI framework to harness metadata, business-specific semantics, and trusted data from Informatica’s Business 360 applications to create business-aware AI solutions.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Moli has led data teams for 20 years, starting in finance and now driving innovation in EdTech. She has navigated every major shift in the data landscape, from on-prem databases to cloud-native AI solutions. For the past five years, she has been pushing the limits of Snowflake, leveraging its power to transform how organizations harness data. Passionate about democratizing insights, she builds scalable, AI-driven solutions. Based in the Pacific Northwest, Moli enjoys outdoor adventures with her two boys.

Rik Tamm-Daniels currently serves as Informatica’s GVP Ecosystem Alliances and Technology responsible for driving technology innovation and go-to-market strategy with leading cloud platforms including AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud and Oracle Cloud and data and analytics leaders such as Snowflake. Rik has 20 years of experience in cloud data management, AI and analytics, big data, text analytics, NLP, data prep, data cataloging and data governance. Rik holds Bachelor’s and Master’s degrees in Computer Systems Engineering from Boston University.

title: From Data to Dialogue: Marriott's AI-Powered Analytics Revolution
abstract: Marriott International showcases its AI-powered conversational analytics solution that democratizes data across the enterprise. Using Cortex and semantic modeling, business users will have over 500 critical business key performance indicators on demand and at their fingertips through natural language queries by asking simple questions such as, "I manage the Beaver Creek Lodge. What was my RevPar last month? How does this compare with last year? What was my ADR for each period?"

speakers.bio: Scott Reed is a data professional delivering impactful solutions. For 3 years at Snowflake, he's partnered with customers on innovative data engineering, data warehousing, and data science use cases. Previously at DataRobot, Scott built model governance capabilities, focusing on trusted AI, explainability, and bias mitigation. Before DataRobot, he spent several years at Fannie Mae as a data scientist. He holds an M.S. in Applied Information Technology from George Mason University and a B.A. in International Relations from Bucknell University. On a personal note, Scott is a wine enthusiast and certified sommelier. He's also a huge Baltimore Orioles fan and goes to many games each year.

Daniel MacDonald serves as the Vice President of Data & Analytics Technology at Marriott International. Bringing 20 years of experience in data and analytics, Daniel specializes in the strategic application of technology to create robust platforms that directly drive business outcomes.

title: From Rebuild to Reliability: DIRECTV’s Data Mesh and AI Observability Journey
abstract: DIRECTV, one of the largest content distributors in the U.S., didn’t just modernize their data stack — they reimagined it. By implementing a data mesh and rearchitecting their data landscape from the ground up, they established a single source of truth to drive measurable business impact, while reducing their footprint and optimizing their costs. To ensure this new system delivered trusted, reliable data at scale, they turned to data + AI observability with Monte Carlo to drive data quality, increase stakeholder confidence, and lay the foundation for AI innovation.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Barr Moses is the CEO & Co-Founder of Monte Carlo, the data + AI observability leader, backed by Accel, GGV, Redpoint, ICONIQ Growth, Salesforce Ventures, and other top Silicon Valley investors.

Growth-minded senior executive with over two decades of experience building and leading high-performing teams that drive business agility, execution, and growth through the agile adoption of modern development frameworks, advanced analytics, and data-driven decision-making. As Chief Data Officer at DIRECTV, led the migration and transformation of legacy monolithic on-prem platforms and technology-aligned teams into a modern, cloud-based distributed data mesh architecture—featuring business-aligned teams, data products, and services.

title: How DoorDash Revolutionized Accounting with Snowflake’s AI Data Cloud
abstract: Fragmented financial data and manual accounting processes create inefficiencies, delay reporting and hinder timely, accurate decisions. Discover how DoorDash tackled this challenge by building a centralized, cloud-based data hub on Snowflake — eliminating data silos and creating a single source of truth. By standardizing workflows and automating tasks like reconciliations and reporting, the company saved thousands of hours annually. The result: real-time access to accurate data, improved collaboration, stronger audit readiness, and a scalable foundation to support future growth and agility.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Matt Graveen is a Senior Manager of Accounting Data and Systems at DoorDash, where he specializes in building and scaling data infrastructure to support the company's accounting operations. With deep expertise across tools like Snowflake, dbt, Fivetran, and Hightouch, Matt focuses on designing systems that drive accuracy, automation, and insight in financial data workflows. He brings a passion for bridging technical engineering with core accounting processes, helping to modernize and streamline how financial data powers business decisions at scale.

title: Leading Through Disruption: CIOs in the Era of Enterprise AI
abstract: As AI reshapes industries, CIOs must balance innovation with reliability. This panel explores how CIOs build robust infrastructures, align AI initiatives with business goals, and foster agile teams. Hear how top tech leaders navigate challenges and transform data into competitive advantage in the age of AI.

speakers.bio: Mike serves as the Chief Information Officer at Snowflake, overseeing global IT, data and security functions. Prior to joining Snowflake, he served as CIO of Payments at J.P. Morgan. Before joining the firm, Mike served as the Chief Executive Officer at Bakkt, a publicly traded company specializing in digital assets trading and payments. Before Bakkt, Mike was Head of Payments Engineering at PayPal, where he led the team through PayPal’s IPO in 2001, and led Google’s Payments Engineering and Wallet Engineering teams through the launch of Google Wallet in 2011. Additionally, he founded and developed two startup Payments/Commerce companies which were both acquired at maturity. Mike is also a Veteran who served four years in the United States Army.

Lutz Beck, Chief Information Officer at Daimler Truck North America (DTNA), is shaping the future of IT. By directing cultural change through digital activation, his visionary IT strategy, “Building the Intelligent Company,” guides digital transformation at DTNA, positioning the organization at the cutting edge of technology. Lutz champions IT Leadership, disruptive ideas and innovation by re-imagining the way business is done.

title: Post-Platform Keynote Networking Snow Hour
abstract: Keep the momentum going after the Platform Keynote by joining us in the lounge to unwind, digest the innovation and grab a light bite to eat. This is your moment to relax and connect — whether you're reflecting on what you just heard or want to casually chat with Snowflake leaders and peers. Come recharge, share your takeaways and continue the conversation in a laid-back, welcoming setting.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

title: Privacy-Preserving Collaboration: Where sensitive data meets business value
abstract: A practical Product-oriented guide to leveraging Snowflake's privacy-preserving Collaboration tools, including data protection policies, data clean rooms, synthetic data and more to unlock business value from sensitive data. Modern organizations are looking for ways to collaborate internally and externally on sensitive data of all kinds, while still respecting the sensitivity. Hear from Snowflake's Head of Data Collaboration Strategy on ways to get the best of both worlds.

speakers.bio: Having spent a career working with data providers and as a data provider, including as a Snowflake Marketplace launch partner, Jonathan now works with aspiring providers on their own collaboration strategies, helping them stand up their data sharing programs and data commercialization efforts. He has a Computer Science degree from MIT and a lifelong love of the Miami Heat, but also has season tickets for the Denver Nuggets (love the one you're with!)

title: Simplify and Accelerate Data Access Provisioning with Immuta and Snowflake
abstract: As demand for data accelerates, data teams face a challenge: How to securely provision data access at scale. Immuta tackles these complexities head-on, offering policy-driven workflows that automate data provisioning and help you put Snowflake data to work. In this lab, you’ll hear from industry leaders who have transformed data governance with Immuta. Then, roll up your sleeves for an interactive experience led by Immuta's field CTOs. You’ll learn how to: Craft scalable, automated data policies Swiftly handle data access requests at scale Enforce governance policies and watch them instantly take effect in Snowflake

speakers.bio: Paul serves as Principal Field CTO at Immuta, where he develops and implements scalable governance strategies for large enterprise customers within the HCLS and FSI verticals. Leveraging over 15 years of global experience, Paul has collaborated with numerous top 100 companies to align their regulatory and compliance initiatives with data scaling and security requirements. In response to the growing presence and influence of AI models, Paul has established best practices that balance data privacy concerns with secure and efficient access across organizational boundaries.

With nearly two decades of dedicated service at JPMorgan Chase, Jenson has established himself as a leading expert in analytics and reporting. His extensive experience has been instrumental in driving the firm's success through data-driven insights and strategic decision-making. As a key figure in developing analytic platforms, Jenson has played a pivotal role in creating systems integral to the operations of internal stakeholders. His innovative approach and commitment to excellence have enhanced the firm's capabilities. Jenson's expertise and leadership continue to inspire and empower teams across the organization. Beyond his passion for data, he enjoys traveling and motorcycles.

title: Solving Last Mile Analytics Using Snowflake Cortex AI and Sigma Data Apps
abstract: Top companies use Sigma and Snowflake to build interactive applications, automate workflows and take action customized to their team’s needs — all without writing any code. Join us for a hands-on lab to explore three distinct last mile analytics case studies and identify the commonalities among them. Learn how to execute machine learning models and democratize Cortex LLMs from Snowflake by leveraging scalable code-free apps in Sigma that tackle these last mile challenges. By the end of this lab, you’ll be able to drive AI adoption across your organization with customizable apps that leverage Sigma on Snowflake.

speakers.bio: Shawn Namdar has spent the past eight years in BI and data architecture, helping businesses unlock the full potential of their data. As the Snowflake Sr. Technical Alliances Manager at Sigma Computing, he specializes in integrating Sigma with Snowflake to drive seamless, no-code analytics solutions. Shawn is passionate about solving last-mile analytics challenges, empowering teams to operationalize ML models, and leveraging Snowflake Cortex LLMs within Sigma’s data apps. In this session, he’ll guide you through real-world case studies and hands-on exercises designed to accelerate AI adoption across your organization.

June of 2025 marks 7 years at Snowflake for Ben, a journey which was amazing at the start and ages like fine wine every year. He's assisted many customers with their journeys on Snowflake, ranging from scouting organizations to professional sport leagues to hedge funds to multi-national banks and insurers. In spring of 2024 he joined the Snowflake Partner Sales Engineering team, where his primary focus is the joint go-to market efforts of Snowflake and several close partners, such as Blue Yonder, Sigma, Fiserv, TransUnion and Capital One Software.

title: SQL Server to Snowflake: Harvesting AI & Digital Innovation in $70B Agriculture
abstract: Join this session to learn how Farm Credit Services of America migrated to Snowflake, including dashboards and queries, and decommissioned SQL Server. You'll also hear how Farm Credit Services of America uses Snowflake with Adobe to leverage personalized customer experiences and learn more about Snowflake's role in our $12 billion AgCountry partnership, using Document AI to process over 20 million documents, boosting efficiency and learning.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Narayanan Nair is SVP & Chief Data Officer for Farm Credit Services of America, AgCountry Farm Credit Services, and Frontier Farm Credit—a $70B agricultural financial services organization. He leads data teams spanning architecture, integration, BI, governance, document management, data operations, and AI. He has successfully launched enterprise data programs, led cloud analytics migrations, and advanced AI strategies. With leadership experience in financial and transportation sectors, Narayanan bridges business and technology to deliver actionable insights that improve operations, enhance customer experience, and drive innovation and market share growth.

title: Transformed Legacy Platforms Drive Optimal Use of Data and AI for FS
abstract: How can AI be as transformative as electricity and enable organizations to derive greater business value from their data assets? Data-derived insights drive better business decisions. To capture the opportunity, financial services (FS) companies need a modernized legacy platform that makes innovative use of AI possible. The No. 1 challenge our clients face is generating powerful analytical insights quickly to enable more dynamic responses to market opportunities. Explore how to activate your most strategic asset — data — on a modernized platform that can employ AI to deliver better personalized products and services and drive operational excellence and competitiveness.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Data, Analytics and Technology leader with vision, strategy and execution of Enterprise Data Strategy across financial, retail and healthcare industry. My purpose - Enable businesses to derive greater value from their data assets.

title: Unlock Democratized Innovation: Empower Business Teams to Innovate
abstract: Are tech stack complexity and bureaucratic processes slowing you down? Discover how a modern innovation platform with sandbox capabilities empowers business teams to develop data and digital products faster. Apply a methodology-driven approach to streamline innovation and accelerate time-to-market. Watch live demos of how teams use the Calibo Sandbox to stay ahead in a fast-paced digital world and leave with actionable strategies to implement immediately.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Suraj is a Senior Sales Engineer with a strong background in software development, security, and auditing. A former full stack developer, he has worked in larger corporations as well as small startups. A passionate mentor, he enjoys coaching teams and fostering innovation. At Calibo, Suraj helps organizations accelerate development and maximize their impact by understanding their problems, and then applying Calibo properly to effectively and efficiently implement a solution.

Scott Sandschafer, leading as the CEO of Calibo, is a visionary at the forefront of revolutionizing the digital PaaS industry. His strategic leadership aims to position Calibo as a benchmark in digital and data product development. With over a decade of technology leadership experience, including roles such as global CIO of Novartis, Scott has been instrumental in driving transformative change. Recognized for his influence in technology leadership circles, Scott, a Mechanical Engineering graduate from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, champions the integration of digital technology into business models, contributing to Calibo's innovative edge.

title: Unlock Insights with Snowflake and Deloitte for Investment Managers
abstract: Come learn more about our new solution for investment managers from Converge by Deloitte. Asset Insight Suite is a cloud-based platform that supports multiple business needs and is a set of prebuilt data services and data models to support asset management use cases. We will dive in to challenges this solution addresses, demo how it works and showcase business outcomes. Come discover how we can drive impact together.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Jib Wilkinson, a Principal at Deloitte Consulting LLP, leads Cloud and Product Innovation for Investment Management. With 25+ years of experience, he specializes in technology transformations, data modernization, and analytics for Investment Managers. Jib has led the implementation of multiple investment systems including an AUM system on Snowflake. He is the product owner for Deloitte Converge Asset Insight Suite product offering, leveraging Snowflake's Snowpark. A frequent industry speaker, he demystifies complex data concepts. Jib holds a Master's in Engineering and Information Systems, empowering investment firms in the data-driven era.

Quraish is a leader in Deloitte Consulting’s AI & Data practice with over 20 years of consulting experience in Financial Services. Specializing in Investment Management, he has led large-scale data-led transformations for diverse clients. From leading a multi-year data & platform transformation for a top 10 investment manager to innovating cutting-edge cloud-based solutions, Quraish has developed skills giving him unique perspectives on data platforms. As Co-Owner of Asset Insight Suite, he focuses on developing rapid, scalable platforms that help generate value & insights for clients. Quraish is also known for driving digital innovation and enhancing operational efficiency.

title: Value-Driven Approaches to Deliver and Adopt Data and AI Products at Scale
abstract: Research shows that 80% of data and AI investments either fail or lead to unused products. However, the main struggle is not with technology, but with organizational and human complexities. Adopting a product-centric approach is foundational to achieve success, but it also comes with many challenges at scale to get a coherent and systematic approach across tens and sometimes hundreds of data and AI products. Join us to learn pragmatic approaches to design, deliver and drive adoption of data and AI products on Snowflake, along with real-life examples on how to optimize value, risks and costs. Lead with value-driven governance!

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Nicolas is the founder & CEO of YOOI, a SaaS platform that manages Data & AI use cases to demonstrate value and ensure compliance. With 25 years in enterprise software, he's launched innovative platforms across industries. Nicolas is committed to ensuring technology drives business value. YOOI recently joined DataGalaxy to offer the first end-to-end Data & AI Governance platform, enabling organizations to really engage business teams in order to animate and accelerate their transformation.

title: What's New: Revolutionizing Data Movement with Snowflake Openflow
abstract: Learn how to bring all your data together with Snowflake Openflow to build with limitless interoperability and deployment flexibility, no matter if it's structured, unstructured, batch or streaming data. Make your integration pipelines ready for AI and the future.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Saptarshi (Sap) Mukherjee leads Product Management for Data Ingestion at Snowflake, driving innovations in enterprise application, SaaS, streaming, unstructured data, and database integrations. His team builds native, high-performance ingestion solutions to maximize Snowflake’s capabilities. With over 20 years in data analytics, integration, and AI, Sap previously held product leadership roles at Google and Microsoft, shaping cloud-scale data platforms. He holds a bachelor’s in computer science engineering and a master’s in information management.

Joe leads Snowflake's Connectivity Engineering which includes the exciting new launch of Snowflake Openflow. Prior to Snowflake Joe was the co-founder and CEO of Datavolo acquired by Snowflake in 2024 and before that a GM and VP at Cloudera and Hortonworks leading Data in Motion. That was spurred by the co-founding of Onyara which was a company set to commercialize the brand new open source project called Apache NiFi. Joe spent more than a decade at the National Security Agency where he started the project which later became Apache NiFi in 2006.

title: A Data Leader's Playbook: From Engineering Trusted Data to Agent Exploration
abstract: Trust isn’t a byproduct of AI — it’s a prerequisite. In this masterclass, learn how Novartis’s Digital Media and Marketing team applies “Marketing as Science” to build trusted data systems, accelerate campaign delivery and transform healthcare experiences. Discover how they reduced review time from weeks to days — and how trusted, controllable AI agents built with Dataiku and Snowflake are driving innovation at scale.

speakers.bio: With more than 20 years of experience in Data Engineering, Media Analytics, & Architecture, Ash has served multiple leadership roles in driving large data teams in high growth environments across Novartis, Warner Music Group, Sony Music, and iHeartRadio. His experience with Snowflake spans over 8yrs and is one who consistently utilizes it to its max potential.
As a Principal Solution Architect at Dataiku, Pat works on integrations with various data storage, compute, and AI/ML services.

title: AI and Unified Data at JPMorgan: Transforming Controls, Compliance and Risk
abstract: Join the JPMorgan Chase Wealth Management RegTech team as they showcase their innovative use of Snowflake to integrate data, analytics and AI into a unified platform for all lines of defense, including controls, risk and compliance. Discover their plans to leverage LLM capabilities through Cortex AI to deliver cutting-edge risk and regulatory insights. Learn best practices that enhance efficiency by 50%, strengthen compliance, mitigate risks and achieve substantial cost savings. Gain valuable insights into proactive risk management and staying ahead of industry trends and challenges. Don't miss this opportunity to explore the future of data-driven compliance and control.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Girija Penumarti serves as the Managing Director of JPMorgan's Asset & Wealth Management RegTech division, leveraging extensive experience in the field. As the Global Head of Technology for the Wealth Management RegTech Platform, Girija is responsible for shaping and executing the product vision, technology strategy, financial planning, execution and delivery. With a diverse skill set that includes design, architecture, engineering, product management, analytics, AI/ML, and large-scale platform execution in the financial services domain, Girija Penumarti offers invaluable insights to the industry.

Ravindra Varshney is a seasoned technology executive with over 21 years of experience in transforming enterprise and data landscapes. As the Chief Data and Analytics Lead for JPMorgan's Asset & Wealth Management RegTech division, he has been pivotal in developing advanced cloud-based analytics platforms that offer strategic insights and efficiencies to executives, business units, and risk, audit, and compliance teams. His expertise includes cloud architecture, AI/ML model development, integrating GenAI into business workflows, enterprise data strategy, and leading global engineering teams. Ravindra excels in aligning data, analytics, and AI initiatives with business goals.

With over 15 years of experience in building large-scale enterprise AI and analytics solutions, El Diawlol is a seasoned data and analytics executive. Currently serving as the Analytics Lead for JPMorgan's Asset & Wealth Management RegTech division. His expertise spans AI/ML, large language models (LLM) development, and adoption. El is passionate about leveraging data to drive business value.

title: AI-Driven Growth: Transforming Customer Experiences and Operational Efficiency
abstract: Drive tangible business results with AI. This session dives into how Infosys Topaz is helping enterprises to personalize customer journeys, optimize operations and unlock new revenue streams. Learn how to leverage AI for predictive analytics, intelligent automation and enhanced decision-making, ultimately achieving sustainable growth and a competitive advantage.

speakers.job_title: Senior Vice President & Business head -Data & Analytics Unit
speakers.bio: Sunil Senan is Senior Vice President responsible for the Data & Analytics service line at Infosys. In this role, he works closely with Infosys’s strategic clients on their data & analytics led digital transformation initiatives. He is passionate about how data & analytics is creating economic impact in the society and how enterprises and governments can engage in driving this transformation. He has written the “Data economy in Digital times” paper articulating how the new data economy presents a set of new possibilities for enterprises, governments to serve their citizens and consumers.

Executive business leader with the responsibility of overseeing a large team of 450+ individuals and leading data analytics, data science, data governance, and data management across our entire $24B global organization.

title: Automating Supply Chain Data Integration with a Snowflake Native App
abstract: Blue Yonder, a global leader in digital supply chain transformation, partnered with Hakkoda to develop a Snowflake Native App that simplifies data onboarding and integration for retailers, manufacturers and logistics providers. Initially designed to streamline data sharing via a Streamlit-based UI, the app has now evolved into a comprehensive data orchestration and AI-powered mapping tool — all within Snowflake. Join this session to learn how we built it and how you can leverage this innovation to optimize your supply chain.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: https://www.linkedin.com/in/indusrimushtam/

speakers.job_title: VP Global Head of Supply Chain & Logistics
speakers.bio: Rico Mawcinitt is a dynamic leader at the intersection of data strategy, technical innovation, and client success. With over 15 years of experience, he brings deep expertise in modern data architecture, data modeling, and cloud-native platforms like Snowflake. Rico is known for translating complex challenges into elegant solutions—whether through designing scalable data ecosystems, leading enterprise migrations, or architecting end-to-end analytics platforms. As a trusted partner to some of the world’s leading organizations, he excels at building strong relationships, aligning technical delivery with business goals, and mentoring teams to achieve lasting impact.

title: Beyond The Basics: J.Crew's AI-Powered Retail Revolution
abstract: In this session, dive into how J.Crew built a personalized retail experience by connecting its Snowflake data with AI. From dynamic email content that adapts to individual preferences to predictive upsell recommendations, we’ll share the tools that make J.Crew a leader in modernizing data-driven marketing. Learn practical lessons from J.Crew's journey including implementation challenges, unexpected wins and how the data team became a strategic business partner. Walk away with actionable insights for applying similar techniques in your organization.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Nikhil Goyal is Senior Director of Data & Martech at The J.Crew Group, where he oversees enterprise data science, data engineering, and marketing technology. With over a decade of experience, he specializes in customer analytics, ML-driven personalization, and martech architecture. Nikhil partners with cross-functional teams to drive measurement, experimentation, and omni-channel strategy, empowering executive decision-making with data. He is passionate about building high-performing teams and advancing the role of AI in marketing effectiveness and real-time personalization.

Boris is the co-founder and CEO of Census, the leading Data Activation & Reverse ETL platform that enables companies like HubSpot, Canva, and J.Crew to operationalize their warehouse data across 200+ business tools in real-time. Before Census, Boris founded Meldium, a team password manager acquired by LogMeIn in 2014, and earlier worked on developer tools for Microsoft. He's loved computers since he was 5 when his father brought home a Macintosh Plus. Soon after, he discovered the delight of programming with Hypercard. He's spent his career building tools to give individuals and teams superpowers.

title: Building Equitable Healthcare: Ending Data Disparity Through Data and Action
abstract: This panel will explore how organizations can leverage data and actionable strategies to build equitable healthcare ecosystems. Panelists will discuss how to align the healthcare ecosystem with the needs of diverse patient populations. This conversation will delve into how creating a more inclusive and equitable healthcare landscape drives accessibility and improves health outcomes for all.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries
Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)

speakers.job_title: Global Healthcare and Life Sciences CTO
speakers.bio: Murali Gandhirajan is Global Healthcare and Life Sciences CTO at Snowflake and focused on helping healthcare and life sciences organizations build next generation data and analytics platforms leveraging a broad set of Snowflake capabilities. He has more than two decades of experience in being a thought leader and trusted advisor to senior data and analytics leaders of Healthcare and Life Sciences organizations and delivering significant business value through data-driven insights.

speakers.job_title: VP, Insights and Technology Excellence - Chief Data Officer
speakers.bio: Brian Dummann serves as the VP IT & Chief Data Officer at AstraZeneca since 2021, bringing extensive experience in data and technology leadership. Previous roles include VP IT & GM of Data & Analytics at Intel Corporation (2020-2021), SVP and Chief Data & Analytics Officer at McKesson (2018-2020), and SVP and Chief Technology Officer at GE (2016-2018). Additional positions held include Chief Architect & Data Leader at Emerson (2011-2016), Global IS Director at Boston Scientific (2009-2011), and Management Consultant at Pfizer (2008-2009). Dummann's career began at Medtronic as Global IT Director (1997-2008)

Shahran Haider is a senior executive passionate about improving patient outcomes and healthcare operations through data. He serves as Deputy Chief Data Officer for NYC Health + Hospitals, the largest public health system in the U.S. His responsibilities include providing guidance for AI strategy and investments for the enterprise, leading AI Governance and ownership of the Enterprise Information Management function. Under his leadership, NYC Health + Hospitals has evolved to a modern cloud data management with enterprise capabilities for AI/ML, business intelligence, data discovery, collaboration and exploration.

title: ConocoPhillips' Data Modernization Journey from Teradata to Snowflake
abstract: Learn from ConocoPhillips' Data and Analytics Modernization Project, where Snowflake was pivotal in enhancing data capabilities and reducing legacy technology footprint. Key achievements include successful go-live releases, secure data sharing, optimized DevOps, and eliminating 65% of duplicated/deprecated data. We’ll discuss: Lessons on change management and overcoming integration challenges How we built our data model to leverage Data Sharing, Cortex AI and Streamlit How we leverage SAP, GEP, Workday, Wellview and Service Now data in a flexible SOX-compliant security model while fostering cost governance

speakers.bio: Experienced Solutions Architect with a proven track record in the Oil & Energy Industry. Specializing in Enterprise Data Warehouse data modernization and Snowflake implementation, I bring a wealth of expertise in transforming data landscapes. Adept at leveraging Big Data Analytics and Cloud Technologies to drive innovation and efficiency. Passionate about delivering scalable solutions that enhance business performance and customer satisfaction.

Paloma has extensive experience implementing data and analytics solutions. She has just completed a three-year implementation of Snowflake as part of ConocoPhillips’ Data Modernization Program. Previously, she implemented analytics strategy and solutions for HR and the Subsurface organization. Prior to ConocoPhillips, Paloma worked for Landmark Graphics, Halliburton, Petroleum Geo Services and IBM. She is originally from Spain and currently lives in Houston. Paloma has worked and studied in the UK, Colombia, Norway and USA.

title: Domo AI Solutions for Marketing
abstract: Unlock the power of AI-driven marketing with Domo AI and Snowflake AI Data Cloud. In this session, discover how leading brands are using AI to optimize campaigns, personalize customer engagement and maximize ROI. See how real-time insights and automation can transform your marketing strategy — turning data into actionable intelligence. Don’t miss this chance to explore cutting-edge AI solutions that drive smarter, faster marketing decisions!
