speakers.bio: Laura Qualey has been with Domo for over a decade, majority of that time has been spent in operations and analytics. In her current role she supports the Chief Revenue Officer in operations, strategy and analytics for Domo's partner ecosystem.

title: GXS Uses Autonomous AI Agents to Speed Data Engineering from Months to Hours
abstract: Discover how GXS, Singapore's first digital bank, transforms its data engineering SDLC through a partnership with Genesis, achieving significant gains in cost, quality, and time-to-market. By leveraging autonomous AI agents and task-specific microbots, GXS automates complex processes. The solution features Cortex-AI Agents, Cortex Search, and seamless git/JIRA integration within SPCS. This reduces the SDLC from months to hours, boosting productivity and reinforcing GXS's leadership in banking.

speakers.job_title: Head of Engineering Data, AI & Enterprise Tech
speakers.bio: Harikrishnan leads Data, AI, and Enterprise Technology at GxS Bank. He brings over 16 years of experience as a data leader across several major banking institutions.

An industry leader in enterprise data platforms, Matt led the development of business-critical analytics at Goldman Sachs for over 20 years. He later joined Snowflake to run Product Management and launched the Snowflake Data Marketplace. Now, he is bringing AI workers to enterprises to power the new AI economy.

title: How Capital One Uses Tokenization to Protect Data
abstract: Modern companies are managing more data than ever before, and the need to derive value from that data is becoming more urgent with AI. But AI adoption is often limited due to data security challenges, and adding to this complexity is the need to remain compliant with evolving regulation. At Capital One, we’ve deployed tokenization to further secure our data without compromising performance. In this session, we’ll discuss lessons learned from our tokenization journey and show how companies can tokenize the data in their Snowflake environment.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Ben Moser is a Senior Manager of Product Management at Capital One Software, an enterprise B2B software business of Capital One. Having worked at early-stage startups and large enterprises, Ben has spent the last 10 years of his career helping organizations of all sizes and industries unlock the power of their data. He has a passion for making data usable and understandable at scale. With deep expertise in data management, governance, and analytics, Ben enjoys breaking down complex problems in order to identify business opportunities and deliver the next generation of data products. Outside of work, you can find Ben running, reading, or building his own data analysis and ML Python projects.

With 20+ years building B2B software across multiple industries, including fintech and telecommunications, I’m a product leader passionate about creating secure, user-centric experiences. Over the last few years, I’ve led the buildout of a modern data tokenization solution—taking it from concept to a product trusted by data-driven enterprises. I’m driven by creativity, love solving complex problems, and thrive on turning vision into best-in-class products.

title: Powering Personalization: How T-Mobile and Deep Sync Connect Brands & Consumers
abstract: Discover how T-Mobile and Deep Sync are redefining personalized marketing through the power of Snowflake. Deep Sync, a leader in deterministic identity solutions, leverages Snowflake to manage its identity spine, covering over 97% of U.S. households with the most current and accurate attribute data available. T-Mobile is bringing to market for the first time a new data services business that introduces privacy-compliant, consent-based consumer data. Together, T-Mobile and Deep Sync are transforming how brands engage with consumers — enabling bespoke, hyperpersonalized workflows, identity-driven insights and closed-loop measurement through Snowflake’s privacy-centric infrastructure. Join this session to learn how data and identity are converging to solve today’s modern marketing challenges so consumers can rediscover what it feels like to be seen, not targeted.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Jeff is the VP of Data and AI Monetization at T-Mobile responsible for spearheading internal and external Data and AI initiatives across the organization. Jeff previously worked at Verizon where he oversaw various business units including Verizon Location Technology, Robotics, and AI & ML. Before Verizon Jeff was at Oracle where he led the teams responsible for strategy, sales, partner management, and business development of the Oracle Data Cloud’s ad tech lines of business. Jeff joined Oracle through the BlueKai acquisition where he was the Vice President of Mobile and responsible for driving the implementation and growth of the companies’ mobile data initiatives.

title: Sequencing the Student Genome: Modeling Click-Level Behaviors in Snowflake ML
abstract: Learn how the Anthology data science team used Snowflake ML to derive students’ behavioral “DNA” from click-level data and uncover common patterns to help our customers better understand their students. This presentation will cover how we experimented with feature creation, neural network autoencoding and unsupervised learning approaches. We incorporated Snowflake artifacts into our iterative development and machine learning operations framework, unblocking research on this previously underused data.

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)

speakers.bio: Allie Feras has held a range of data related roles, from integrations, to analytics consulting, to data science. She and her team derive insights from Anthology's expansive EdTech solution ecosystem to empower educational institutions and their students. As a former librarian, she has always been passionate about supporting learning. Her recent projects have focused on understanding students' engagement and success in their courses using techniques from the simple and explainable to more complex autoencoder embeddings.

title: Thomson Reuters Builds Scalable, Efficient and Secure AI Agentic Workflows
abstract: Everyone is talking about AI agents, but few have seen them in action. In this session, we’ll show how to build an AI analyst that delivers real-time insights to finance, HR, support and more. Using Snowflake Cortex Search and LLM Observability, we'll seamlessly handle RAG, text-to-SQL and reliability at scale. See how RBAC and advanced orchestration reduce time-to-insight, cut costs and empower business units while ensuring data security. Join us for a deep dive and Q&A.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: A Principal Engineer at Thomson Reuters Labs with 15 years of experience in data and analytics, now focused on pioneering AI agent development and Generative AI applications. Combines deep expertise in enterprise data architecture with cutting-edge AI technologies to build innovative solutions. Currently leading research and development in Gen AI applications and AI agents, while leveraging extensive background in data engineering to create next-generation AI Data Agents.

Ruichong "Alex" Wang is an Applied Scientist at Thomson Reuters, specializing in artificial intelligence, machine learning, and large language models (LLMs). His expertise includes developing intelligent AI systems, pioneering agentic AI technologies and agentic evaluation.

title: What's New: Managing Costs and Optimizing Performance in Snowflake
abstract: Learn how to better oversee, control and optimize the cost and performance of your Snowflake workloads through capabilities like Cost-Based Anomaly Detection, Tag-based Budgets and Performance Explorer. Join us for new product and feature overviews, best practices, and live demos

speakers.bio: Stephen Yigit-Elliott leads product management for Snowflake data warehouse workload optimization. His career has spanned various cloud and underwater platforms. Prior to joining Snowflake, Stephen was a product leader at AWS and GCP, and a nuclear submarine officer in the US Navy.

title: Accelerate Data Clean Room Deployments with GitHub and Snowflake
abstract: Snowflake Data Clean Room (DCR) APIs enable application development using secure analytics. Discover how integrating GitHub directly with Snowflake can optimize DCR deployment workflows between engineering and data science teams. This session will highlight the benefits of this integration including increased efficiency, reduced errors, seamless collaboration, and improved customer satisfaction.

speakers.bio: Innovative and forward-thinking senior director with extensive experience in architecting and implementing cutting-edge data collaboration and clean room solutions. title: AI-Powered Next Best Offer: Scaling Personalized Engagement for 50M Customers
abstract: Learn how Globe transformed customer engagement through an AI-driven Next Best Offer platform serving 50 million customers with hyper-personalized recommendations. The evolution from rules-based to context-aware AI processes 2.5B real-time offer scores, achieving 217% higher campaign take-up and 204% better retention. Join us to learn how we transformed a traditional system into a modern AI solution using Snowflake and AWS to deliver next best offers at scale.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.job_title: Sr. Director, Head of Data Architecture & Engineering and AI Data Engineering
speakers.bio: Innovative Leader Enhancing Customer Experience through (Real-Time) Big Data Solutions | Inspiring Digital Transformation for Measurable Business Impact and Customer Engagement in a Psychologically Safe Workplace.

speakers.job_title: Loyalty Enablement and Analytics Lead, Feel Valued Tribe

He currently holds the role of Loyalty Enablement and Analytics Lead at Globe Telecom in the Philippines. In this capacity, he spearheads the development of solutions driving Globe's CLM and loyalty campaigns, with a focus on delivering highly personalized customer experiences. His professional journey spans over 20 years in the field of data and analytics. Prior to joining Globe, he served as the Head of Data Analytics at Eastwest Ageas Life and held the position of Chief Technologist at DXC Technology.

abstract: In this session, Jeremy Hughes, Senior Manager of Data Engineering at Carvana, and Ian Whitestone, CEO at SELECT, will share how Carvana scaled the Snowflake data platform to thousands of users and millions of workloads processed per day, all while keeping costs stable and driving consistent ROI. They will cover cost allocation strategies, data product ROI measurement, ways to stay on top of usage trends and other rituals and best practices.

Recently, he’s been building the largest analytics platform at Carvana by implementing Snowflake and constructing enterprise-level ingestion pipelines that feed into it, ensuring that crucial data is accessible for informed decision-making. He believes in fostering a collaborative environment where his team is encouraged to explore new technologies and methodologies, keeping Carvana agile and responsive in a rapidly evolving digital landscape.

title: Data-Driven Futures: CDOs in the Age of AI
abstract: The age of AI demands a strong data foundation, and CDOs hold the key. This panel reveals how data officers lead governance, drive analytics innovation and empower entire organizations. Join experts as they discuss harnessing AI for business value, fostering data fluency and ensuring responsible growth.

With an illustrious career spanning leadership role at Western Union, Ernst & Young, Deloitte, and Accenture, Harveer has consistently excelled in helping organizations navigate technology challenges and bridge the gap between business and IT.

Anahita Tafvizi joined Snowflake in 2024 as Chief Data Analytics Officer, bringing a powerful blend of technical expertise and strategic vision to her role. She has led teams across Data Science, Strategy, and Analytics at tech giants like Instacart, Google, YouTube, and eBay, consistently aligning data initiatives with business goals to drive growth. With a deep passion for building high-impact teams, Anahita bridges technical expertise with business strategy to shape outcomes. Growth-minded senior executive with over two decades of experience building and leading high-performing teams that drive business agility, execution, and growth through the agile adoption of modern development frameworks, advanced analytics, and data-driven decision-making. As Chief Data Officer at DIRECTV, led the migration and transformation of legacy monolithic on-prem platforms and technology-aligned teams into a modern, cloud-based distributed data mesh architecture—featuring business-aligned teams, data products, and services.

title: How DraftKings Saves 200 Hours of Platform Management per Season with Snowflake
abstract: Late-night pings, cascading latencies and overprovisioning were all challenges DraftKings' data platform team used to deal with. Learn how DraftKings' new autoscaling approach leveraging Snowflake saved them 200 hours per season of monitoring and scaling. DraftKings now regularly handles volume spikes of 20x with per-minute scaling to process billions of records. The result? No more critical report SLA breaches, stale leadership dashboards or forgetting to scale back down and running up the bill.

speakers.bio: Senior Data Engineer building solutions for Draftkings data platform.
Building rock-solid data platforms for large enterprises; improving both technical and operational efficiency across orgs.

title: Mastering Data Quality in Snowflake
abstract: Ensuring the reliability and accuracy of your data is paramount. This session will explore how Snowflake empowers organizations to proactively monitor and manage data quality at scale. Learn how to automate quality checks, validate data freshness, and run custom rules across your data pipelines. We’ll share real-world examples, best practices, and new Snowflake features that streamline data quality monitoring, helping you build trust in your data and drive confident decision making. This session is perfect for data engineers, analysts and data stewards looking to elevate their data governance strategies.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Pino Suliman is a Senior Product Manager on Governance team at Snowflake, focusing on data and AI observability.

Nick Meyer is a seasoned Manager and Architect with a proven track record in designing and implementing scalable data solutions. With extensive experience leading high-performing teams, he has played a key role in transforming data architectures for organizations like Apiture, Select Sires, and Highlights for Children. Specializing in cloud-based data warehousing, ETL development, and business intelligence, he leverages platforms such as Snowflake, AWS, and Microsoft Azure to drive data-driven decision-making. Holding a Master’s in IT Management from Indiana University, he is passionate about empowering organizations through innovative data strategies and leadership.

title: Modernizing Insurance Analytics with Hub International, Snowflake and Coalesce
abstract: HUB International, the world's fifth-largest insurance consulting group, faced challenges with outdated tools and manual data handling. Partnering with DigBI Consulting, they implemented infused analytics, leveraging Snowflake for centralized data and Coalesce for transformation. In this session, Dane Bernhardt (HUB International) and Lee Derks (DigBI Consulting) will share how they streamlined ETL, cut manual labor, and revolutionized insurance consulting. Learn how their data-driven approach is reshaping the industry, reducing costs, and improving health outcomes for clients and employees.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.job_title: Director of Product Analytics, National EB

Since joining through an acquisition in 2006, he has advanced into a leadership role supporting both national analytics initiatives and client consulting. With deep expertise in self-funding, claims management, and provider strategy, he develops data-driven solutions to reduce costs and improve performance. Dane holds a BS in Economics from the Colorado School of Mines.

Lee, CEO and Co-Founder of DigBI Consulting, is a driving force in the world of data-driven decision making. With over two decades of experience in business intelligence and analytics, Lee is passionate about empowering individuals and organizations to unlock the hidden potential within their data. He founded DigBI on the principle of "Insights for Good. Results for All," a testament to his commitment to creating accessible and meaningful reports. Beyond his professional pursuits, Lee finds joy in culinary exploration, the art of cooking, the melodies of the piano, and the immersive world of flight simulation.

title: Predicting Optimal Route Paths for Autonomous Vehicles at Nokia
abstract: Autonomous vehicles must always maintain network connections with acceptable signal quality. Quality of the connection may impact ability of the vehicle to safely navigate its way through urban and suburban traffic. By combining the power of the Snowflake AI Data Cloud with Nokia's Network as Code platform, Nokia has implemented an optimal route path algorithm which predicts the relevant network KPIs to ensure that the autonomous vehicle has acceptable quality connections to the network.

speakers.bio: Aric Wang is currently a Senior AI Specialist Engineer at Snowflake. His role involves helping the world's largest Telecommunications, Media, and Entertainment Enterprises utilize Machine Learning and Generative AI technologies on the Snowflake Cloud Data Platform. A lifelong technologist, Aric has been professionally pitching, solutioning, and implementing technology for over a decade. In his spare time, Aric enjoys playing tennis, travelling, and hiking around the Pacific Northwest where he is based.

Mikko heads portfolio and architecture for Network Monetization Platform, Nokia's API business unit. Network Monetization Platform business unit was setup to build offering for global 5G and advanced network programmability and monetization, based on Nokia’s Network as Code vision, which Mikko was driving and incubating in his previous role. As a Head of Portfolio and Architecture, Mikko’s is responsible defining portfolio and technology vision & strategy and differentiating offering for Network Monetization Platform, and drive the transformation on how mobile networks are used and monetized in enabling entirely new, ecosystem created next generation experiences and enterprise applications.

abstract: High interest rates and the geopolitical landscape are leading to a scarcity of easily available capital. Additionally, there is a push for CFOs to transform their finance organizations for improved control, compliance and cost optimization. Learn how EY and Snowflake collaborate to help finance organizations gain streamlined and accurate finance data supply chains with scalable compute infrastructure, improved analytics, forecasting and reporting, while reducing the risk of regulatory and compliance errors.

Over the last 20+ year, Geoff has been working with leading financial institutions on their finance transformation, front/middle/back office transformations, business intelligence (reporting and analytics), and data management needs. He has led many client engagements ranging from process-led data strategy, tool implementation, conversion/migration efforts as well as several large scale Finance Transformations for global firms.

She has helped organizations create robust, scalable, modern data platforms that enhance consumer experiences through improved insights and timely access to information. Natasha is dedicated to delivering high-impact solutions that yield enduring benefits and unlock new avenues for growth. She leverages emerging technologies to create sustainable competitive advantages for clients. Her passion lies in fostering diverse, cross-functional, purpose-driven teams, united by a common goal: to tackle complex challenges and deliver exceptional value.

Reporting to the CTO, as the Chief Architect of T Rowe Price, Wangzhong will lead T. Rowe Price’s technology strategy and architectural direction enterprise wide. EA function will help advance our technology modernization goals of accelerating technology execution, reducing risk and costs, and enabling new business growth for the firm.

With over 20 years of experience at firms like Western Union, IBM, EY, Deloitte, and Accenture, Amit has built global digital banks, modern data platforms, advanced analytics, and led large transformations at several global financial institutions. Amit holds a degree from Duke University in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science and was recently named one of the 40 Most Influential Data Leaders in U.S. Finance by CDO Magazine.

title: The Golden Age of the Analyst: How Chime Is Driving Data Impact
abstract: Everyone is wondering how data roles will evolve in the AI era. At Chime, we're already seeing ways in which modern tools are helping our teams do more and have deeper impact — and it's changing the way we're thinking about hiring, staffing and workflows.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Turning data into insights for 12+ years and leading data teams for 6+ years. Experience in consumer tech (Chime, Uber, Facebook, food delivery at Maple) and emerging tech (autonomous driving at Zoox).

title: Unifying Data Cataloging & Security: Fortune Brands’ Success with ALTR & Alation
abstract: As Fortune Brands Innovations consolidated Snowflake across its subsidiaries, the company needed a seamless solution to integrate data cataloging with security and governance. The ALTR-Alation integration enabled precise data classification, empowering admins with better control while allowing nontechnical users to easily view what's in their data catalog and understand how it's classified, ensuring greater visibility across the organization. This session will explore how this solution — built with ALTR's APIs and Alation's SDK — enhanced FBIN's visibility, compliance and governance, and how as a Snowflake Native App it streamlines deployment for a faster, more efficient rollout.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

title: How Coinbase Developed an End-to-End ML Platform on Snowflake
abstract: Join this session to learn how Coinbase builds end-to-end ML workflows on top of Snowflake’s platform for optimal data security, governance and price performance. Using features such as Snowflake Feature Store and Snowflake Model Registry, Coinbase now automates batch and online inference on predictive ML models to quickly and accurately unban users who were initially incorrectly flagged as suspected fraud or bots, resulting in an improved user experience and increased revenue.

With 10+ years of experience across early- and late-stage companies, Tuhin brings deep expertise in experimentation, causal inference, ML, and translating complex data problems into clear business outcomes. title: How Powerex Uses Yes Energy Data to Adapt to Market Changes
abstract: Join Powerex, a leading energy provider, as it shares how Snowflake transformed its data operations, enabling seamless access and innovation. Discover how its collaboration with Yes Energy, Marketplace’s longest-tenured energy data provider, delivers real-time insights to help Powerex quickly adapt to market changes and streamline decision-making. Learn how Yes Energy’s data sharing empowers Powerex and a vast network of other customers to stay agile while ensuring governance and security.

speakers.bio: Sam Lockshin is the Product Manager of data products at Yes Energy, which deliver Yes Energy’s high quality power market data catalog to consumers through the Snowflake Marketplace. He has a passion for reliably delivering programmatic data to customers so they can confidently achieve their business goals. Sam also likes to extract value from data using cutting edge data science, machine learning and AI techniques on Snowflake. Sam’s background in geospatial and time series data analytics got him interested in energy markets.

Ian Mathieson is the Director of Data Systems and Application Development, leading the data engineering practice to ensure critical, high quality data is available to end-users for driving business value. Ian fosters innovation in data systems and application development, leveraging modern tools like Snowflake, Kafka, and Azure to create impactful and scalable solutions.

title: How to Increase ROI from AI and Data Products with Effective Governance
abstract: A strong data platform is essential for effective AI. Access to data through a catalog of data products is crucial. However, disconnect often exists between the data platform, data products, AI capabilities and business value, resulting in isolated AI efforts and qualitative assessments. Our model addresses this gap by clarifying how a “just enough” approach can help businesses focucs on essential components for specific outcomes around data products and AI capabilities. Establishing an “AI Value Office” enhances governance and links investments to measurable business value, justifying data and AI spend with clear returns.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.job_title: Head of Data, Analytics & AI Platforms

speakers.bio: Balaji Ramanujam is a Distinguished Technologist with Infosys and is a Chief Partner Architect for Snowflake @ Infosys. He has unique experience of Architecting Large Data & AI platforms to help accelerate business transformation and “Getting Enterprises Data Ready for AI”.

title: Private Equity, Powered by Data: Scaling Retail M&A at Janie and Jack and Beyond
abstract: Discover how the CIO of Janie and Jack is leading data transformation at Go Global Retail, the private equity firm reinventing modern retail. Learn how premium brands like Janie and Jack and Hatch Collection are unifying their data with Fivetran to eliminate silos, accelerate M&A integration, and fuel AI-driven decision making — building a modern, scalable and insight-driven retail portfolio from the inside out.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Seasoned Retail IT executive inspiring and influencing cross-functional global teams to resolve complex business challenges. Over 20 plus years of experience leading large scale digital transformations spanning Supply Chain, Omni, Retail Applications, Data and Analytics, and Infrastructure Modernization. Excels in collaboration and building partnership that creates a positive work culture and win-win relationships.

speakers.job_title: Sr Global Director, Partner Sales Eng
speakers.bio: Kelly Kohlleffel leads the Fivetran Global Partner Sales Engineering organization, working with a broad ecosystem of technology partners and consulting services partners on modern data product and solution approaches. He also hosts the Fivetran Data Podcast where some of the brightest minds across the data community talk about their data journey, vision, and challenges. Before Fivetran, he spent time at Hashmap and NTT DATA (data solution and service consulting), Hortonworks (in Hadoop-land), and Oracle. You can connect with Kelly on LinkedIn or follow him on Twitter.

title: Secure, Exabyte-Scale Lakehouse Analytics at Netflix with Iceberg and Snowflake
abstract: In this session, discover how Netflix utilizes Snowflake’s highly scalable and performant platform to seamlessly connect to its Iceberg REST catalog and run advanced analytics. We will explore how Netflix is leveraging Snowflake’s latest capabilities—including Iceberg REST catalog integration with auto-refresh, vended credentials, and multi-bucket support—to optimize workloads at unprecedented scale. Join us to gain insights into how these innovations drive performance, efficiency, and scalability in one of the world's largest data ecosystems.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Ankit is a member of the Big Data Compute Engines team at Netflix. Before joining Netflix, he graduated from Carnegie Mellon University and worked in the Database team at Salesforce

speakers.job_title: Engineering Senior Manager, Big Data Analytics Platform
speakers.bio: Anjali leads the Big Data Analytics Platform at Netflix that manages 1ExaByte+ Lakehouse, Iceberg table format, engines such as Spark, Trino, Druid and Snowflake and home-grown Orchestration technologies like Maestro, Dagobah and Genie. This central platform serves data analysis needs in all domains such as Member, Content, Studio, Growth, Ads, Games etc.
Working in Data Warehouse team, have been working on data security, Iceber Rest Catalog, query engine integration etc.

title: Shipt’s Cost Optimization Journey with Snowflake
abstract: As Shipt's Snowflake usage grew rapidly over the last few years, the company made aggressive cost savings goals in 2024 and was able to meet them by leveraging the latest built-in cost management capabilities that Snowflake provides. Learn how Shipt leveraged capabilities such as Warehouse and Cluster Utilization, Warehouse Events History, Per Query Cost Attribution and Cost Insights to effectively analyze, monitor and optimize Snowflake spend.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Shailendra Toutam joined Shipt 2 years ago as the Principal Data Architect in the Data Platform team. He has over 22 years of experience in leading, designing and implementing optimized and scalable Data and Analytics Solutions. Prior to joining Shipt, he held various roles in Data Engineering and Software Engineering space at Twitter and Yahoo! for 18 years. Shailendra worked in various industries and technologies including databases, big data, open source and cloud platforms.

Kelly Smunk has been the director over Shipt's data platform since 2023. She has held various roles within technology over the past 10 years. Her experience gives her a broad perspective that applies to technical implementation to meeting stakeholder needs.

title: Tech Debt Reduction and Renovation in Apache NiFi 2
abstract: Apache NiFi 2 includes many major upgrades, from Java 8 to Java 21, from AngularJS 1 to Angular 18 and from Jetty 9 to Jetty 12, among others. Apache NiFi is the foundation for Snowflake's managed integration service, providing managed and scalable pipelines for sending data to Snowflake from a variety of sources. Join a project committee member and author of the NiFi 2 roadmap for a review of the goals and implementation that went into this new version. From dependency changes to module restructuring, from code deletion to significant rewrites, this session highlights engineering strategies for extending, refining and pruning a large open source project.

speakers.bio: David Handermann is a Senior Software Engineer at Snowflake working on data pipelines. As a committer and member of the Apache NiFi Project Management Committee, he has contributed over 1000 pull requests to the project. With over 20 years of experience in information technology, he has released several open source libraries and contributed to multiple projects. Interested in all things related to information security, he has designed, developed, and maintained a number of solutions for authentication, authorization, communications security, and data protection. He maintains a software development blog at exceptionfactory.com.

title: The Agentic Enterprise: Early Wins and Lessons
abstract: AI hype feels impossible to avoid. Every person, business and function stands to benefit from a tremendous leap in productivity. In this talk, phData will cover the wins we’re already seeing and practical lessons about what challenges are faced and what lies ahead. With each story, you will learn about groundbreaking Snowflake products and apps — from Document AI to Snowpark Container Services and more — that power these wins and overcome barriers to adoption.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Andrew Evans is the Chief Architect of AI & ML at phData, with over 10 years of experience in advanced algorithms and big data, from ML research at CERN to building enterprise-grade AI systems across various industries with phData. Andrew has a proven track record of successfully deploying AI applications into production and enjoys working with customers to develop effective AI strategies. He is highly involved in the Snowflake and partner ecosystem and has written numerous blogs for phData and Snowflake. He has also given many joint presentations at other Snowflake events like Build.local, Customer Workshops, and the Demo Dare challenge on LinkedIn Live.

title: The Future of Healthcare and Life Sciences
abstract: As healthcare and life sciences organizations look to transform their business functions by leveraging AI, the ability to ingest and transform multimodal data is more critical than ever. Join the Healthcare & Life Sciences keynote at Summit and hear directly from leaders about how they are working with Snowflake to simplify their data foundations — with the FAIR and TEFCA principles at the core. Hear about best practices, design considerations and customer proof points. Learn what the latest Snowflake announcements mean for the industry. We will also provide actionable insights on how to both enable AI by architecting for interoperability and leverage AI for operational efficiency.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.job_title: Global Industry Lead - Healthcare & Life Sciences
speakers.bio: Jesse Cugliotta is the Global Industry Lead for Healthcare & Life Sciences at Snowflake. He has over 20 years of experience working with clients in the Healthcare & Life Sciences industries with a focus on driving business outcomes via data analytics & AI. He holds a graduate degree in Engineering from the University of Pennsylvania and has spent his entire career in Data & Analytics.

Experienced native data & technology leader with a history of managing solutions throughout their lifecycle, with a goal of enhancing value to business & customers. Adept in driving data transformation that impacts the business bottom-line and help people, process and technology meet their objectives

Johanna DeYoung is a visionary leader driving strategy and go-to-market for Slalom’s HCLS business. With 20 years of experience, she partners with top HCLS companies to create compassionate patient experiences, accelerate growth, and unlock the value of data and innovation. Her expertise spans commercial strategy, M&A, CX, transformation, launch, data strategy, and field force enablement. Known for her strong executive relationships and ability to anticipate industry trends, Johanna is a respected thought leader and international speaker, delivering impact across the healthcare value chain.

speakers.job_title: VP, Head of Healthcare & Life Sciences
speakers.bio: Chris Puuri has worked for over 20 years as a healthcare and life sciences solution architect empowering some of the largest payer, provider, health tech, and life sciences organizations in the world with cloud-based data and analytics solutions. Chris has overseen data modernization at dozens of organizations in the space, including Scripps Health, Keck Medicine, Advocate Health, Beth Israel Lahey, the Children's Hospital of Philadelphia, Gilead Sciences, Blue Cross Blue Shield, and the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services.

title: The Future of Public Sector: Mission Impact with the AI Data Cloud
abstract: In the era of accelerated digital transformation, the U.S. public sector stands at the crossroads of unprecedented opportunity and significant responsibility. Aligning with critical administrative priorities — such as enhancing citizen experience, improving cybersecurity posture and increasing government efficiency — requires practical, innovative solutions. Join us to explore how Snowflake's AI Data Cloud for Government & Education enables AI-driven analytics and data collaboration to deliver measurable impact on mission-critical operations. Showcasing real-world public sector use case demos, we will highlight how leading government and educational organizations can leverage AI to drive data-informed decision-making, automate processes and fundamentally transform public service delivery.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.bio: Tim Tutt is a proven technical leader with over a decade of software engineering experience focused specifically developing and deploying large scale search and discovery and data analytics solutions in both the public and private sectors. He has a strong passion for operationalizing deep technical capabilities for the benefit of non-technical business users. Tim was previously the CEO and Cofounder of Night Shift Development, Inc., which focused on helping organizations make sense of their data. Night Shift Development was acquired by Snowflake in September of 2024, where Tim now leads US Public Sector focusing on bringing theSnowflake Platform to both the Federal and SLED markets.

Alice Irvin is a former intelligence officer with over 15 years experience working in the U.S. intelligence community at CIA, DIA, Undersecretary of Defense for Intelligence, and DEA. For the last five years, she has worked at helping to bridge the gap between customers working the hardest problems and technology.

title: The Future of Telecom: Unlocking AI-Driven Intelligence and Innovation
abstract: In this session, Snowflake will guide you through the blueprint for becoming an AI-powered telco, covering how real-time data, AI models and advanced analytics are reshaping the industry. We’ll spotlight Zayo as a proof point of what’s possible. David Sedlock will join us to share how Zayo is executing on its AI transformation, the challenges it's tackled and key lessons to accelerate your own journey. The future of telecom isn’t just about speed — it’s about intelligence. With the Snowflake AI Data Cloud, operators are transforming data into decisions, networks into AI-driven ecosystems and challenges into opportunities. Join us to explore what it takes to lead in this new era.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.bio: Phil Kippen is currently Head of Industry, Telecommunications at Snowflake. Phil has over twenty years of experience working with more than two-hundred wireless & wireline Telecommunications Services Providers globally. In his current role, Phil is responsible for Telecommunications industry strategy, market & business development, solution adoption and go-to-market (GTM) programs.

David is the Chief Data Officer of Zayo Group and recognized Data and AI industry leader with hands on experience with VC funded start-ups, Fortune 10 and private equity backed companies. Zayo is the largest independent infrastructure provider in the US with over 145,000 Fiber route miles. David received Dataiku’s 2024 Frontrunner Award for the Best Showcase of Return on AI and has been cited in numerous data, telecom and business publications. In his first 2 years at Zayo, David delivered $38M+ of savings through Data and AI “Quickstrikes”, scaled his CDO team from 5 to 50+ FTEs, drove a multi-year board level $15M Data & AI program that operationalized target state capabilities to include - single data platform, data ingestion patterns, Critical Data Elements (CDEs), Business Ready Datasets (BRDs) / Gold Datasets, data product catalog, data inventory & quality frameworks. David interacts regularly with Zayo’s ELT, Board and Investors (EQT & Digital Bridge) while leading Zayo's Data Ecosystem which includes Snowflake, Dataiku, Sigma, FiveTran, Tableau, PowerBI, Azure Fabric, Global ID's, and dbt Labs. Articles: AI monetization starts with good data governance Oct 2024: Dataiku’s 2024 Frontrunner Award: Best Showcase of Return on AI Sept 2024: TMForum: Zayo unlocks a trove of data to deliver business value April 2024: Dataiku: Zayo: Driving Operational Efficiency With Analytics & AI.

title: The Honest, No BS on Data Governance: Why It’s Finally Exciting
abstract: Join Tim Gasper, Chief Product Officer, and Juan Sequeda, Principal Scientist and Head of AI Lab, for a candid discussion about the evolution of data governance. Discover how data.world's knowledge graph platform, integrated with Snowflake, transforms governance from a burden into a benefit, with organizations reporting 16x higher adoption rates and 11x faster insights. Learn practical strategies that combine innovation with real-world needs: interact directly with your catalog, automate governance tasks, and create data products that users will actively utilize — fostering enterprisewide adoption where traditional approaches have fallen short.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Tim Gasper is Chief Product Officer at data.world, home of the data catalog platform built for your AI future, and co-host of Catalog & Cocktails, an honest, no-BS, top rated podcast and live web show on enterprise data management. Tim has over 15 years of product and customer leadership experience, navigated companies through 4 successful exits, and is a writer and speaker on data, AI, and entrepreneurship.

speakers.job_title: Principal Scientist & Head of AI Lab
speakers.bio: Juan Sequeda is the Principal Scientist and Head of the AI Lab at data.world. He holds a PhD in Computer Science from The University of Texas at Austin. Juan’s research and industry work has been on the intersection of data and AI, with the goal to reliably create knowledge from inscrutable data, specifically designing and building Knowledge Graph for enterprise data and metadata management. Juan is the co-author of the book “Designing and Building Enterprise Knowledge Graph” and the co-host of Catalog and Cocktails, an honest, no-bs, non-salesy data podcast.

title: Whats New: Snowflake Intelligence and No-Code Agentic AI
abstract: Discover how to build and deploy a no-code, agentic AI platform with Snowflake Intelligence. See how organizations are bringing together structured and unstructured data to make insights accessible across their entire enterprise via an AI-driven agentic interface, all within Snowflake. Join us for a product overview, best practices discussion and live demo.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.job_title: Director of Product, Snowflake Intelligence and Agents
speakers.bio: Jeff Hollan is Director of Product for Snowflake's GenAI Agentic platform and Snowflake Intelligence. Prior to joining Snowflake Jeff led the serverless and developer compute businesses for Microsoft Azure. Jeff is a well-known and frequent speaker on building cloud native applications. He has been a keynote speaker and presenter at industry conferences including KubeCon, ServerlessConf, Microsoft Build, and the Linux Foundation API Summit.

title: Build a Real-Time Streaming Generative AI Application with AWS and Snowflake
abstract: Organizations have various streaming use cases where they need to ingest unstructured data — such as customer reviews or social media comments in text or JSON format — into a lakehouse. Before storing this data in database tables, they often want to process it in real time using generative AI capabilities, such as fraud analysis, sentiment analysis and summarization. This hands-on lab provides step-by-step guidance on building an end-to-end data pipeline using Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink, Amazon Data Firehose and Snowflake.

speakers.bio: James Sun is a Senior Partner Sales Engineer at Snowflake. He actively collaborates with strategic cloud partners like AWS, supporting product and service integrations, as well as the development of joint solutions. He has held senior technical positions at tech companies such as HP and AWS

Nithyashree Alwarsamy is a Partner Solutions Architect at Amazon Web Services, specializing in data and analytics solutions with a focus on Snowflake. With the strong background in cloud architecture and data engineering, Nithyashree works closely with Snowflake and joint customers to design scalable, secure and cost-effective data platforms on AWS. Leveraging deep expertise in modern data architectures, Nithyashree helps organizations unlock the full potential of their data by integrating Snowflake's cloud-native data platform with the breadth of AWS services.

title: Building a Data API with Snowpark Container Services
abstract: APIs play a crucial role in providing data interfaces for external services and applications. In this hands-on lab, you’ll learn how to build a public REST API serving data hosted in Snowflake. The API will use the JDBC Driver and the Snowflake Ingest SDK to send and retrieve data from Snowflake. The service will be hosted in Snowpark Container Services and accessible externally.

speakers.bio: Aran Sakzenian is a Principal Solution Engineer at Snowflake, with over 20 years of industry experience. Before Snowflake, he held various leadership and hands-on software development roles. He most recently spent 11 years at Experian Marketing Services where he co-authored Audience Engine, Experian's flagship marketing segmentation and activation platform, and re-platformed it on Snowflake. Aran holds a Computer Science degree from The University of Texas at Dallas. In his personal time, he enjoys traveling with his wife and two sons.

Brian has been in the data and analytics industry for over 20 years. He started his career doing applied research in mathematics and computer science at the National Security Agency. Since then he has had various technical and product roles in government and companies specializing in data warehousing, NoSQL, and analytics. Brian is currently Global Field CTO focusing on data applications.

title: Building Simulator Models for Multi-Outcome Decisions at Swire Coca-Cola
abstract: Join this session to explore how Swire Coca-Cola used Snowflake ML to build its Simulator System, a next-generation decision intelligence solution designed to model real-world scenarios with the most accurate, up-to-date data. Built to balance competing priorities, transform insights into actions and optimize strategy selection, it enables businesses to adapt seamlessly to changing conditions. This talk will discuss how this system delivers smarter, faster planning — providing a competitive edge in an unpredictable world.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Atul Sehgal is a data science leader with 17+ years of experience driving AI-powered solutions across Consumer Goods, Mobility, Mining, Manufacturing, Insurance, Healthcare, and Life Sciences. He has led global AI rollouts in India, China, Japan, Australia, Chile, and the USA, built high-performing teams, and developed market-driven AI solutions. Atul balances mathematical rigor with practical application, ensuring data-driven decisions translate into real business impact. He actively collaborates with the University of Utah to mentor and hire top talent in data science.

title: Extracting Insights from Complex Weather Data Using Snowflake
abstract: The Met Office, the United Kingdom's national weather service, generates over 400TB of weather data every day and faces challenges making data useful for business decision making. With growing demand for accurate weather information, transforming raw data into actionable insights is crucial. Join this session to learn how the Met Office uses Snowflake's data platform to ingest NetCDF files, deploy user-defined functions (UDFs), and create variant tables and H3 indexed data for efficient querying and analysis. Plus, see how Cortex AI in Snowflake helps extract insights using natural language prompts, giving users more value from their data.

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)
Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: As a Partnerships & Innovation Manager at the Met Office, I develop strategic collaborations to expand access to Met Office data and create impactful new products. Working within the Product Futures team, I design value propositions that address emerging market needs by integrating evolving science and technology. I manage a portfolio of partnerships, aligning capabilities with opportunities to drive innovation. I welcome discussions on how emerging technologies, science, or new products can help the Met Office enhance its impact and benefit industries worldwide.
Lead Technical Architect for Data Provisioning at the Met Office, currently wrestling with the best way to make vast amounts of complex data available and useful to customers.

title: Gaining Customer Insights and Loyalty with Generative AI
abstract: See how you can combine the power of Snowflake and ChatGPT or a Streamlit app to unify customer data and extract sentiment from structured and unstructured data. Using predictive LTV models, we will walk you through how generative AI can identify churn risks, fuel loyalty strategies and deliver real-time, AI-driven insights that personalize engagement and boost retention across channels.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Shoby Abdi is a seasoned Growth Partner specializing in Data & AI at a leading consulting firm. With a strong foundation in data-driven strategies, Shoby excels in transforming complex challenges into actionable solutions, driving measurable outcomes for clients. His approach combines traditional business acumen with forward-thinking innovation, ensuring sustainable growth and success. Committed to excellence, Shoby continues to lead initiatives that harness the power of data and AI to deliver impactful results.
Explorer and music lover at heart, Data Scientist by passion.

title: How to Boost Developer Productivity with Automated Testing for Stored Procedures
abstract: Testing Snowflake objects — particularly stored procedures — can be challenging due to data dependencies, lack of repeatability and manual processes. By automating test setup and execution and integrating them into a CI pipeline, teams can achieve faster and more thorough testing of Snowflake stored procedures, which can lead to higher-quality code and reduced risk of regressions. In this talk, Capital One Software's Director of Software Engineering Kishore Kolanu and Senior Lead Data Engineer David Ellis share how they built a reusable testing framework to solve these challenges.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: David is an Data Engineering leader who is passionate about solving developer challenges across the entire software delivery lifecycle. Since 2021, David has been with Capital One Slingshot, focusing on delivering world-class data management solutions for enterprise customers. As a leader and senior developer on the team that built Slingshot's Snowflake-powered Insights Engine, David offers unique perspectives on the ways that data can make or break the delivery of data-driven applications.

speakers.job_title: Director of Engineering for Capital One Software
speakers.bio: Kishore Kolanu is a Director of Engineering for Capital One Software, an enterprise B2B software business of Capital One. Currently, he leads the development and implementation of recommendation systems within Capital One Slingshot. Kishore holds a master's degree in computer science, and he lives in Richmond, Virginia with his wife, daughters, and son.

title: Lessons from Revolutionizing Snowflake’s Analytics with Sigma
abstract: Discover how Snowflake’s AI Data Cloud leverages Sigma to deliver unified business intelligence that drives critical decision-making. Join Anahita Tafvizi, Chief Data Analytics Officer, as she shares how Snowflake utilizes Sigma to surface real-time metrics, enabling fast, data-driven decisions in high-stakes weekly leadership reviews — and what this approach means for the future of enterprise data.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Rob Woollen is a seasoned technology executive with a background in software engineering and product management. Rob has held various leadership roles at companies such as Sigma Computing, Sutter Hill Ventures, salesforce.com, BEA Systems, and HP. Rob's expertise includes serving as Chief Technology Officer, Principal Architect, and as an Entrepreneur in Residence. Rob is a co-founder of Sigma Computing and has a degree in Computer Science from Princeton University.

Anahita Tafvizi joined Snowflake in 2024 as Chief Data Analytics Officer, bringing a powerful blend of technical expertise and strategic vision to her role. She has led teams across Data Science, Strategy, and Analytics at tech giants like Instacart, Google, YouTube, and eBay, consistently aligning data initiatives with business goals to drive growth. Her early career involved academic research positions at Harvard and MIT as well as consulting at the Boston Consulting Group. With a deep passion for building high-impact teams, Anahita bridges technical expertise with business strategy to shape outcomes. Her appointment underscores Snowflake’s commitment to advancing data analytics and AI.

title: Orchestrating Data Analytics Workloads with SQL and Python
abstract: Expand your Snowflake workflows by combining the power of SQL with Python. In this hands-on lab, you’ll learn to write Python UDFs, DataFrames and Procedures and integrate them seamlessly into your SQL queries, transforming how you approach complex data processing. We’ll also cover practical strategies for monitoring and troubleshooting Python code. Whether you’re a data analyst or BI engineer, this session will give you the tools to level up your skills and unlock new possibilities with Snowflake.

speakers.bio: Doris Lee leads Python and data science experience across Snowflake's products. Previously, Doris was CEO and co-founder of Ponder (acquired by Snowflake in 2023), the company behind the open-source project Modin. Doris received her Ph.D. from the UC Berkeley RISE Lab and School of Information in 2021, where she developed open-source tools that help data scientists explore and understand their data. She received Forbes 30 under 30 for Enterprise Technology in 2023.

Jason drives key initiatives for the Snowpark developer platform, enhancing its capabilities and user experience. Feel free to ask him about anything pertaining to Snowpark, Python, UDFs, observability, DevOps, etc.

title: Why Snowflake Wins: A Strategic Comparison with Databricks and Microsoft Fabric
abstract: The modern data stack is crowded with ambitious claims — unified analytics, AI readiness, cost efficiency and seamless interoperability. As enterprises search for a platform that can do it all, three names continually surface: Snowflake, Databricks and Microsoft Fabric. But when it comes to delivering at scale — across workloads, clouds, teams and partners — Snowflake stands apart. This session provides a strategic, technical and economic comparison of the platforms across several dimensions including cost, interoperability, data engineering, governance and AI. We’ll demonstrate why Snowflake is uniquely positioned to be the core of the modern enterprise data and AI platform.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: James Dinkel is the president and CEO of Squadron Data and is considered one of the world’s foremost experts in data lakes and warehouses. James possesses deep technical skills in all things Hadoop (past life) and Snowflake (present life). James often provides companies very useful and expert guidance on navigating the data landscape, particularly with comparison of products from Snowflake, Fabric and Databricks. Previously, James was the Director of Network Data and Analytics at Sprint, managing 250 technical resources and over $90M/year of budget. As the executive overseeing Network Data and Analytics, James was the thought leader in the big data space for the company.

title: Breaking Down Data Silos: Okta’s Journey to Scalable, Governed Analytics
abstract: Okta transformed its data strategy by moving from fragmented, ad hoc processes to a governed, scalable architecture with Snowflake and dbt Cloud. This session explores how Okta accelerated development, improved continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD), and empowered teams with self-service analytics — reducing costs, increasing data transparency and delivering trusted insights faster. Learn how standardizing workflows with dbt Cloud unlocked agility, efficiency and businesswide collaboration.

speakers.bio: Pooja Crahen is a Principal Analytics Engineer at Okta, with a strong focus on designing scalable data models, enabling technical teams and powering seamless decision-making across executive leadership. Passionate about bridging data and business outcomes, she led the implementation of dbt Cloud and Okta's transition to the modern data stack. Previously, as a Director of Analytics Engineering at Pluralsight, she built and led high-performing teams to help drive strategy and execution. As a 2x Tableau Visionary, she is a recognized expert in analytics and visualization. Her work has made an appearance on UNICEF, United Nations and several news organization world-wide.

title: Democratizing Data at LegalZoom Through AI-Powered Business Intelligence
abstract: Learn how LegalZoom transformed the role of their data team by leveraging Snowflake alongside ThoughtSpot's Agentic Analytics Platform to produce tangible business impact through data and insights democratization. This session will detail best practices around platform implementation and how to drive internal adoption, empowering business stakeholders to conduct their own data analysis for informed decision-making.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.job_title: VP of Data and Platform Engineering

title: From Data to Action: A Unified Approach with Salesforce
abstract: Discover how Salesforce Data Cloud, Agentforce and Tableau work together to power automation, AI and analytics. Learn how a unified data strategy can drive smarter agents, real-time insights and better business outcomes.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: With over 20 years of leadership experience, Rajkumar is a Product leader for Salesforce Data Cloud—the active data platform that empowers teams to engage customers with relevant insights and contextual data at every touchpoint. He leads key capabilities including Calculated Insights, Data Actions, SQL/Query, and Zero Copy. Rajkumar is passionate about understanding customer needs and delivering impactful, insight-driven experiences.

title: Future of Manufacturing: IT, OT & AI Convergence
abstract: Discover how Snowflake is shaping the future of manufacturing with insights from industry leaders at Honeywell, ConocoPhillips, and Penske. Learn how AI and integration of IT and OT data in Snowflake is driving breakthrough performance in supply chains, enhancing shop floor productivity and improving product quality and service. Explore key strategies for building scalable, data-driven manufacturing systems that enable faster, smarter decision-making.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.bio: Tim Long is the Global Head of Manufacturing in Snowflake's go-to-market team where he leads Manufacturing customers through data and AI transformations while fostering industry partnerships to expedite customer success. Before joining Snowflake, Tim was the Head of Data and Analytics at adidas North America and Head of Analytics and Data at Micron Technology, where he led the global manufacturing data environment to Snowflake in just four months. Tim holds a Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering from the University of Wyoming and a Master's degree in Business Analytics from NYU Stern.

Vishwa Ram is the Vice President of Data Science and Analytics at Penske, where he plays a pivotal role in shaping the company’s data-driven strategies. Under his leadership, Penske has achieved significant milestones in digital transformation, including the development and launch of a cutting-edge cloud-based analytics platform and several AI powered applications. Vishwa has also spearheaded initiatives to digitize the customer experience, enhancing operational efficiency and customer engagement. Vishwa resides in the Seattle, WA area with his wife, their middle school-aged son, and their beloved dog.

speakers.job_title: Vice-President IT & CIO,  Enterprise Data & Analytics
speakers.bio: Ravi Sankar is Vice President IT & CIO, Enterprise Data & Analytics. Ravi is leading the Enterprise Data Strategy for Honeywell. He plays a critical role in driving Enterprise Business Transformation by leading Data Management & Governance, Enterprise Data Warehouse & Analytics and AI & Automation. Over the last 4 years, Ravi and his team have built a modern Enterprise Data Warehouse, running on Snowflake, as well as building industry-leading AI/Gen AI capabilities to support Honeywell’s diverse businesses.

title: GROUP BY Women on Snowflake
abstract: Join us at the Women on Snowflake meetup, where women and allies come together to network and inspire each other. In the tech environment, we recognize that women are often underrepresented due to various factors. This significant gap needs to be addressed. Come by to celebrate achievements, share experiences and foster meaningful connections with women in the industry!

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Jyoti Pathak is a distinguished Data and AI expert, honored as a Snowflake Data SuperHero and Data Analytics Professional of the Year. As Founder and CEO of Clipeum Techonlogy LLC, she leads a top Data and AI advisory firm and part of Snwoflake Partner Network . She also a certified Confidence and Leadership Coach for high-performing professionals. Jyoti serves on the boards of the City of Chandler Cultural Foundation and the Indo American Foundation. A lifelong learner, she is pursuing her private pilot license. Her passion for growth and empowerment inspires many.

title: Modernizing Enterprise Search with AI in Cortex Search
abstract: Enterprise search has long been a challenging domain — subjective search relevance, complex tuning options, limited lexical search, and hard-to-manage indexing and infrastructure. However, with advancements in AI, the search landscape is shifting. A new type of search stack is emerging — hybrid search, semantic reranking and eval-driven quality tuning augmented by LLMs. This session covers Snowflake's modern, AI-forward approach to the traditional enterprise search problem. We'll walk through a demo leveraging Cortex Search, LLMs for synthetic query generation and relevancy judging, auto-tuning, and more to quickly build a high-performance, high-quality AI search stack with just a few clicks.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Product Manager at Snowflake working on Cortex Search

title: Public Sector Businesses: Increase Team Collaboration Using Data Sharing
abstract: Connecting Through the Data Sharing Hub - The Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT) has made significant strides in enhancing data sharing and collaboration with organizations, including the Texas A&M Transportation Institute (TTI), through its innovative Enterprise data platform. The Snowflake platform serves as a pivotal tool enabling seamless data exchange and fostering cooperative efforts across various entities.

speakers.bio: Mark Prindle is a Sr. Data Architect in the Information Technology Division at the Texas Department of Transportation. He possesses extensive expertise in designing and implementing data pipelines and data warehouses. With over 30 years of experience in data management, Mark has previously held positions at the Texas Department of Family and Protective Services and the Texas Comptroller of Public Accounts, where he successfully developed data strategies that emphasize data governance principles, including data quality, security, and compliance.

Suresh Sundararajan leads Data Management and Analytics teams in the Texas Department of Transportation, Information Technology Division. Suresh’s team works on building enterprise data management and analytics platform at TxDOT that servers as a single point of truth of data across divisions and districts. In addition to Data Management efforts, Suresh and team is heavily involved in defining enterprise data governance policies and data strategies for TxDOT. Suresh, having served in various positions with specialization in data management, is a strong advocate of data democratization with strong emphasis on data security. He strives hard to empower customers with data driven insights.

title: The Future of Financial Services: Architecting for Data & AI ROI with Snowflake
abstract: Navigating an AI-powered, data-driven financial services future can be challenging, particularly as the industry faces greater pressure to demonstrate tangible returns on their AI investments. Join the Financial Services keynote at Summit and hear directly from industry leaders about their partnerships with Snowflake, the business and technology challenges they’re looking to solve and the key use cases they’re implementing. And learn what the latest Snowflake announcements mean for the industry. Whether you’re a business executive, a technology leader or a Snowflake user, this session will provide actionable insights on how to architect for data and AI ROI.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.bio: Rinesh Patel is the Global Head of Financial Service at Snowflake where he is responsible for developing the financial service value proposition and supporting our customers' transition to an enterprise data & AI strategy in cloud. Rinesh has been in the financial services industry for over 20 years in a variety of banking, consulting and business leadership roles with his most recent experience as Global Head of Cloud Platform at London Stock Exchange Company where he established and led a commercially-minded platform team to develop a modern cloud data platform to deliver new data solutions. Rinesh is based in London where he lives with his wife and two daughters.

Billy Cummins is a senior technology leader with nearly 20 years of experience in designing data warehouses, data platforms, and building the enthusiastic teams to deliver on the enterprise's goals. His passion at work starts with applying good technology to solve real business problems. Starting in Oil & Gas supporting enterprise data through Business Intelligence built on endless tools and approaches, Billy led his first implementation of Snowflake in 2018 to replace Hadoop and hasn't looked back since. He has spent the last 6 years in Financial Services supporting all areas of the business with data solutions.

Mark Kubik is a Senior Vice President of Data and Analytics at Global Payments Inc. Mark built and launched Global Payments’ cloud native data lake house in 2019 and now maturing it to and Open Lakehouse Architecture by 2026. He is responsible for data management and delivery, data platform, business insights, and data as a service products that serve internal partners and millions of external customers worldwide. Mark has 24+ years experience in Financial Services and 34+ years in the data space which has earned Mark the reputation as a data specialist and authoritarian in data management, analytics, quality, and governance

speakers.job_title: Chief Product Officer Data and Analytics
speakers.bio: In this role, he is responsible for designing and delivering the AI and Data platforms for JP Morgan Chase. The includes governance, data discovery, management and entitlement, model development and model serving across generative and traditional AI. He is responsible for Fusion, the firm’s cloud-native data solution for institutional investors that provides end-to-end data management, analytics, and reporting across the investment lifecycle. Prior to joining JPMorganChase in 2021, Francis spent 25 years at Bloomberg, where, in his most recent role as Head of Enterprise Solutions, he was responsible for product, commercials, business growth, sales, and service delivery.

Diwakar Goel, Managing Director, is the Global Head of Aladdin Data. Driving innovation, he ensures thorough data support for BlackRock and Aladdin clients, grows the expansion of data coverage into new markets and asset classes, champions the distribution of Aladdin data via Aladdin Studio, and enhances user experience and insights. With a rich background as the former Global Chief Data Officer at General Electric (GE), he led data and analytics initiatives across diverse industrial sectors. Diwakar holds a degree in Computer Engineering from Manipal Institute of Technology, India, and actively contributes to the advisory boards of tech start-ups and the MIT Sloan Customer Advisory Board.

title: The Future of Media: Enterprise Data and AI for a Privacy-First Ecosystem
abstract: Navigate the future of media with the AI Data Cloud. This session reveals how three critical trends — multimodal AI, the unified adtech/martech cloud and a centralized data gravity approach — are revolutionizing content strategies and revenue models. Discover how Snowflake empowers media leaders to deliver personalized experiences, maximize advertising ROI and streamline operations. Witness firsthand how industry pioneers leverage Snowflake and Snowflake Cortex AI to drive innovation and gain a competitive edge in the dynamic media landscape.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.bio: Kashish Gupta is the co-founder and co-CEO of Hightouch, the best in class Composable CDP now helping marketers build AI agents for personalized marketing. Kashish has a background in Machine Learning, and has been working on startups since 2018. He did his masters in ML at Penn, and he went through Y-Combinator in 2019. Kashish works across product and go-to-market at Hightouch, and enjoys working hands on with marketing teams at large consumer brands. When he’s not helping marketers with data and AI, you can find him playing squash, tennis, or golf on the weekend.

David is Snowflake's European industry expert for media and related sub-verticals. He joined Snowflake with more than 20 years’ experience, gained across the industry with senior roles at Warner Bros. Discovery, Sky, The Guardian and others.

Travis is the Head of Solutions Engineering at TwelveLabs. Biologist turned technologist, Travis has a wealth of experience across academia, industry, and startups. With deep roots in computer vision research, Travis spends his time with TwelveLabs' customers to sweat the details on how they utilize cutting edge technologies in their video workflows.

Jenny Yurko is the Vice President of Data Product Strategy at Warner Bros. Discovery supporting advanced advertising initiatives. With 14 years in the industry, Jenny specializes in orchestrating seamless integrations within data clean rooms, improving partner interoperability, and expanding identity capabilities while unlocking the full potential of data insights. Jenny’s relentless pursuit of excellence has earned her recognition as a thought leader in the industry, sought after for her expertise in the latest advancements in data technology and best practices for maximizing advertising ROI. Jenny is currently is an adjust professor at The University of Chicago teaching predictive analytics and has held previous roles with the NBA, Comcast and Samsung Ads.

title: The Future of Retail and Consumer Goods
abstract: The retail and consumer goods industries are undergoing significant transformation, driven by shifting consumer behaviors, global economic changes, supply chain disruptions and, most importantly, rapid technological innovation. This session is designed for business and technology leaders in RCG, offering them insights and strategies needed to navigate and thrive in this evolving landscape. Learn from the transformational experience of the leading global consumer goods company, Snowflake industry experts and key partners as they explore how data and AI technologies are shaping the industries' future.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.bio: Rosemary DeAragon leads the global industry team for retail, consumer, travel, and hospitality at Snowflake. Prior to Snowflake, Rosemary led global data strategy at Walmart. An important part of her role over 5 years at Walmart included driving top executive initiatives in supply chain, merchandising, product, and global data. Prior to Walmart, Rosemary headed up Amazon's transactions and risk management machine learning team.

Mahadev Konar is a seasoned engineering executive with over two decades of experience in distributed systems, data infrastructure, and open-source technologies. As Vice President of Engineering at Instacart, he leads the development of scalable platforms that power the company’s dynamic grocery delivery services. Before joining Instacart, Mahadev co-founded Hortonworks, where he was instrumental in advancing the Apache Hadoop ecosystem. His contributions include significant work on Apache ZooKeeper, Apache Hadoop, Apache YARN. His tenure at Yahoo! further solidified his expertise in managing large-scale distributed systems.

Sagar Balan is Chief Business Officer for the Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) vertical at Tredence, a leading AI and data science services firm. He partners with C-suite executives at nine of the world’s top ten CPG companies, driving digital transformation, commercial excellence, and supply chain innovation through data strategy and AI solutions. Known for insightfully integrating long-term vision with immediate business outcomes in the rapidly evolving AI landscape, Sagar consistently bridges data strategy and business goals to deliver measurable impact for global consumer brands.

title: The Golden Ticket: A Behind-the-Scenes Look at Generative AI Observability
abstract: Generative AI observability holds the potential to transform enterprise data teams. In this session, deep dive into the architecture, benefits and challenges of T. Rowe Price’s gen AI use cases for data discovery and data supply chain innovation — resulting in 50% increased efficiency. Learn how the key components of gen AI observability, including observability into unstructured data, vector databases, LLMs and the AI data stack accelerate data quality management.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.job_title: Head of Data - Global Distribution Tech
speakers.bio: Srikumar Kanthadai (Sri), Head of Data, Global Distribution Technology. Sri is responsible for formulation, innovation, and execution of Data strategy in enabling data as a key business growth driver for lines of business - Individual Investors, Institutional Intermediary, and Marketing. Sri previously worked at Toyota Motors, Experian, CoreLogic, and Credit Sesame, focusing on Automotive, Consumer, Business Credit, and Real Estate markets. Sri's expertise is in Innovation, Transformation, and M&A in the data & technology space. He owns six US patents. Sri has led several large transformation initiatives leveraging Data and AI/ML as a key differentiator, yielding top-line business growth.
Barr Moses is the CEO & Co-Founder of Monte Carlo, the data + AI observability leader, backed by Accel, GGV, Redpoint, ICONIQ Growth, Salesforce Ventures, and other top Silicon Valley investors.

title: What's New: Accelerating Data Ecosystem Migrations to Snowflake
abstract: Learn how to accelerate and automate your data ecosystem migrations with SnowConvert AI, powered by Snowflake Cortex Agents. SnowConvert AI is your free, automated solution designed to dramatically reduce the complexities, costs, and timelines associated with data warehouse, BI, and ETL migrations. It intelligently analyzes your existing code, automating code conversion, data validation, and streamlining the entire migration process. Join us for an overview of the solution, migration best practices, and live demos.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Federico Zoufaly helps Snowflake customers accelerate their adoption of the platform from his position as Director of Migrations and Modernizations @Snowflake. Federico previously served as Co-Founder of Mobilize.Net. Federico has been a leader in modernizing software applications for almost 30 years. With a technical background (Ph.D.c. from the University of Florida with a Fulbright scholarship and M.Sc. from the Technology Institute of Costa Rica) and hands-on management in real world technology projects, Federico is passionate about the intersection of the technical challenges and the business need for modernization. Federico is a fellow of the Aspen Global Leadership Network.

Venky Ganti is a member of the Snowflake product team helping customers unlock the full potential of data through the Snowflake Data+AI platform. His experience in data management and AI innovation positions him to drive impact within the Snowflake ecosystem. He co-founded Alation, pioneering the Data Catalog category as CTO, and has led innovation at leading tech companies including Google, Microsoft, and early stage startups such as Numbers Station AI. His experience spans from foundational research in data systems and search to leading product development and engineering for AI applications. He is driven by a desire to help customers solve their most complex data and AI challenges.

title: What's New: Build, Test and Deploy Products with Developer Tooling
abstract: Learn how to leverage build, test and deploy your work with Snowflake's latest developer tooling. Join us for new product and feature overviews, best practices and live demos.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Myles Borins is a Toronto based product leader with deep experience in shaping technology, innovating product, and building developer platforms. He Leads Product for Developer Platform at Snowflake where he is focused on empowering individuals building on Snowflake's AI Data Cloud. He previously held roles at GitHub, Google, and IBM. As a founder of the OpenJS Foundation, a former TSC member of the Node.js project, and former vice-president of ECMA International, Myles spent close to a decade steering the evolution of the JavaScript ecosystem. Beyond his professional endeavours, he is passionate about the open web and fostering healthy communities.
