title: What's New: Why You Should Migrate Your ML pipelines to Snowflake ML
abstract: Learn how the latest capabilities in Snowflake ML accelerate and simplify the dev to prod lifecycle. Join us for new feature overviews and a live demo showcasing our latest capabilities, including Data Science Agent.

speakers.bio: Pavan Pothukuchi is the Director of Product Management for AI at Snowflake. His teams are responsible for Snowflake ML, which is a set of capabilities that enable ML practitioners to productionize machine learning workflows end-to-end inside Snowflake. He is in addition responsible for Document AI, which is an AI product that enables business users to extract information from unstructured documents and automate business processes, and AI observability - which enables developers to build safe and trusted AI applications.

Mona Attariyan, Director of Machine Learning at Snowflake, is a visionary leader in Cloud, Data Analytics, and ML. At Redfin, she led ML Engineering, Data Engineering, and Analytics during the pandemic, driving innovation and resilience. Previously at Google, she founded the Cloud AI Natural Language team, advanced ML on unconventional hardware, and launched Project BigGene for large-scale genome analysis. Her early work included face recognition and VM management for GCE. Mona’s career reflects her passion for solving complex problems and her commitment to innovation in machine learning and data.

title: Workday’s 'Secret Sauce': Becoming AI-Native with Governed Data Products & Atlan
abstract: Workday helps more than 11,000 organizations, including 60% of the Fortune 500, manage their most important assets — their people and their money. The key to Workday’s success lies in ensuring their more than 20,000 global employees are comfortable with and capable of using data to inform their decisions and work. Join Joe DosSantos, Chief Data Officer at Workday, and Prukalpa Sankar, Co-Founder of Atlan, to learn what it takes to build AI companions and marketplaces that expand the definition of who can use data, carefully governing and building data products with Snowflake and Atlan, and delivering the rich context necessary for accurate, trustworthy insights with AI.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Prukalpa Sankar is a Co-founder of Atlan, the data and AI governance platform, trusted by visionary data teams at Fox, HubSpot, Medtronic, HelloFresh, and Autodesk. Named a Visionary in the Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ and a Leader in the Forrester Wave™, Atlan helps enterprises manage and govern their data & AI products. Previously, Prukalpa cofounded SocialCops, recognized by the World Economic Forum and the New York Times. She’s a Forbes 30u30, Fortune 40u40, and TED Speaker.

Joe DosSantos is VP, Enterprise Data and Analytics at Workday, an industry leader in HR and Finance software solutions. He is responsible for aligning cross-functional analytics, AI, and data management across the enterprise, including data governance and data ops. Prior to joining Workday, Joe was the Chief Data and Analytics Officer at Qlik, a leader in BI and Analytics software and SVP, Enterprise Information Management at TD Bank. Joe is also a veteran of Accenture, where he spent 10 years in the Electronics and High Tech Area, and EMC, where he built the first Big Data Professional Services practice

title: Building AI-Powered Educational Apps at Scale with Snowflake at Stride
abstract: Discover how Stride and phData built an enterprise-grade AI and data architecture to streamline development, enhance safety and ensure accuracy. By migrating from legacy data solutions to Snowflake’s AI Data Cloud, they leveraged Unistore and Snowpipe Streaming to create a centralized API layer, enabling real-time personalization for teachers and students. This transformation cut development time by 60% while maintaining trust, compliance and adaptability. This session is ideal for data engineers, product managers and architects looking to harness Snowflake for scalable, AI-driven solutions.

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Bradley Robb is a product manager turned enterprise AI leader with over fifteen years in the field. He currently serves as the VP of AI and Product Engineering at Stride, a leading education company. Prior to Stride, Bradley worked on the Enterprise Messaging team at Capital One and several startups. He currently resides on a small farm with his dogs, Baxter and Mayonnaise.

Andrew Evans is the Chief Architect of AI & ML at phData, with over 10 years of experience in advanced algorithms and big data, from ML research at CERN to building enterprise-grade AI systems across various industries with phData. Andrew has a proven track record of successfully deploying AI applications into production and enjoys working with customers to develop effective AI strategies. He is highly involved in the Snowflake and partner ecosystem and has written numerous blogs for phData and Snowflake. He has also given many joint presentations at other Snowflake events like Build.local, Customer Workshops, and the Demo Dare challenge on LinkedIn Live.

title: Data and AI Governance in Action: From Policy to Practice
abstract: This session focuses on the practical aspects of implementing data and AI governance within organizations. Learn how to move beyond policy documents to establish effective governance structures, integrate ethical AI practices into daily operations, and utilize tools and frameworks to ensure responsible AI development. Gain insights on transforming data and AI governance from theory to practice.

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)
Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.job_title: Data analytics and AI Consulting Head
speakers.bio: Gaurav Bhandari is an AVP at Infosys Ltd. He leads the Consulting for Data & Analytics globally and also has an additional responsibility of overseeing Data Governance, Data Privacy, and the MDM COE. Gaurav has held leadership roles working with senior executives and business users to understand their requirements, recommend best practices for process improvement, and drive technology automation. Gaurav has passion for data and collaborates with clients across various industries to harness the power of data and insights. He spends time working with customers to design and develop ‘Responsible AI systems’ that are built on the foundation of Trust, Ethics, Privacy, Compliance, and Security.

title: Frictionless dbt™: Bringing dbt™ Natively to Snowflake
abstract: Many workloads on Snowflake originate from dbt™, but operationalizing those models at scale can be challenging. Developed in conjunction with Snowflake, this DataOps.live solution makes model transformations fully native, eliminating infrastructure complexity, automating deployments and testing, and streamlining the entire process. Join us for a live demo for how DataOps.live works and comes together with continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) for Snowflake, and see how you can accelerate development and time to value.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Guy Adams is the CTO & co-founder of DataOps.live as well as a Snowflake Global #1 Data SuperHero. An experienced CTO & VP who is passionate about DataOps. He's spent 20+ years running software development organisations and now his focus is bringing the principles and business value from DevOps and CI/CD to data. Co-founder of the truedataops.org movement. Also Dad, technologist, (over) engineer, amateur inventor, skier, and mildly eccentric.

title: From Data Silos to AI-Powered Insights: Roadmaps to Modernize Public Health
abstract: State health departments are modernizing data to break silos, enable AI-driven insights and improve outcomes. Leaders from Illinois, Massachusetts and Tennessee share how they built secure, scalable platforms using Snowflake to integrate data, govern sensitive information and power real-time analytics. This panel covers actionable steps for modernization for public health agencies and healthcare organizations on similar data journeys, including platform design, data governance, AI and advanced analytics use cases and lessons learned.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.bio: Dejan Jovanov is an accomplished Senior IT leader with over 18 years of expertise driving innovation in healthcare informatics. As the inaugural Chief Data and Informatics Officer at the Illinois Department of Public Health, he leads transformative initiatives, including the state's Data Modernization Initiative (DMI), advancing data governance, interoperability, and public health innovation. Dejan’s strengths span health informatics, systems architecture, database management, and clinical data standards, with deep proficiency in ICD-10-CM, CPT, LOINC, and HL7 integration. He has successfully led teams in designing and implementing enterprise-level solutions, including the Illinois DPH Data Hub, revolutionizing the state’s data infrastructure for advanced analytics and visualization. Dejan holds a B.S. in Automatics and System Engineering and carries multiple certifications, including Mirth, Cisco CCNA, and Microsoft SQL Business Intelligence. A visionary technologist, Dejan continues to shape the future of healthcare informatics, championing data-driven strategies that improve public health outcomes.

Kevin is dedicated to helping public sector teams in the public health and healthcare sectors leverage modern data solutions. He focuses on enabling organizations to make informed, data-driven decisions using cloud-native data solutions, modern data architectures, AI, and advanced analytics . His work encompasses data governance, data architecting, business intelligence, predictive analytics, and AI. With over a decade of experience, Kevin has led cross-functional teams in developing modern data strategies and implementing enterprise data and analytics platforms, helping public agencies unlock predictive insights, improve data quality, and enhance decision-making processes.

speakers.job_title: Director of Data Modernization Implementation Strategy
speakers.bio: Kris leads a small but mighty team in data modernization efforts at the Massachusetts Department of public health. The data modernization efforts at DPH include developing strategy and data governance, overseeing the implementation of DPH's new Snowflake Enterprise Data Platform and training and enabling the DPH analytics workforce. Prior to DPH, he worked on the establishment of the MA State HIE, on Federal Data Standards related to Long Term and Post Acute Care, HIE technology innovation, global NGOs in disaster response and community health center nonprofit support for kids. Kris oddly enough still likes to climb trees in his spare time ... really tall ones.

title: How Cognizant and a Media Conglomerate Partnered Together in Evolving the Enterprise Data Ecosystem
abstract: Discover how Cognizant helped a Media conglomerate transition to Snowflake in just six months, saving millions in licensing fees. By automating 70% of the conversion process and leveraging Cognizant Ignition™ accelerators and Cognizant’s Migration Factory approach, the project modernized the data architecture, improved data accuracy and enhanced accessibility. The successful migration resulted in a robust, autoscaling data warehouse platform, faster project deliveries and significant cost savings.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Geetha is a seasoned leader in Cognizant's Data, AI & Analytics practice. She champions large deals business development for the practice and is also the North America Delivery Leader steering client service delivery for customers in the Telecom, Media, Entertainment, Education & HighTech businesses. With over 25 years of experience in technology consulting and delivery, she has a track record of successfully delivering large & complex managed services programs providing end-to-end services and solutions to enable data-driven innovation and transformation for clients.

title: Making Data Human: Democratizing Insights with Streamlit and Agentic AI
abstract: At Block (Cash App), we’re breaking down technical barriers to empower any employee to engage with data — no SQL required. In this session, we’ll share our three-phase journey to democratize data insights across the company at scale: Phase 1: Developing Streamlit dashboards that empower teams to independently make informed decisions Phase 2: Automating our analytics with Agentic AI — from question to insight — cutting analysis time by 95% Phase 3: Building integrated, scalable solutions that leverage Snowflake MCP, Codename Goose and Streamlit Make data human by letting it tell its own story.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Zach is a senior engineer on the AI & Data team at Block (Square/Cash App), where he champions AI use cases to deliver business intelligence at scale. Formerly Director of Insights at a restaurant analytics company, he combines business strategy with an engineering mindset. He’s passionate about leveraging agentic AI to automate workflows and democratize data. At Snowflake Summit 2025, he’ll demonstrate how Block is breaking down technical barriers to empower anyone in the company to talk to the data.

Matt Piekarski is a Senior Solution Engineer at Snowflake, with almost 10 years of experience in databases, migrations, and performance optimization. For the past 3 years, he's helped companies in numerous industries adopt cloud and AI technologies, driving digital transformation and unlocking greater business value through modern data solutions.

title: Mastering Iceberg and Open Lakehouse Architectures with Snowflake and Qlik
abstract: Apache Iceberg powers the new world of open high-performance lakehouses, but how does it work? In an SAP-based manufacturing demo, see how Qlik — in collaboration with Snowflake and AWS — fully uses Iceberg. Explore data pipelines integrating IoT devices, structured SAP data and other sources, all managed within Snowflake and AWS. Learn how Qlik enhances Iceberg-managed data with real-time Snowflake Cortex AI integration, Open Catalog and Bedrock Generative AI to elevate analytics and insights.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: David Freriks is a Technology Evangelist in the Technology Alliances team at Qlik. Dave’s mission is to help spread the word on the power of the Qlik Platform with Snowflake systems and build integrations with technologies related to that ecosystem. He has spent 20+ years in the business intelligence space working at Qlik, SAP, IBM, and Cognos helping launch new products to market. Dave has a background in data warehousing and a Mechanical Engineering degree from Texas Tech University. He is married with two kids, and two Australian Shepherds.

title: Powering Executive Decisions and AI at Life Time with Snowflake and Domo
abstract: Life Time empowers people to live healthy, happy lives through its portfolio of more than 175 athletic country clubs across the United States and Canada and via its complimentary Life Time Digital app. In addition to delivering the best programs and experiences through its clubs, Life Time owns and produces nearly 30 of the most iconic athletic events in the country. Life Time's partnership with Domo and Snowflake has been instrumental in driving transformation, fostering innovation, and aligning technology with business strategy. In this session, discover how Life Time leverages these platforms to empower team members, make data-driven decisions and achieve enterprise goals.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Angie Schulke is a dynamic technology leader with over 20 years of experience driving digital transformation and aligning technology with business strategy. As VP of Application Development, Data & Analytics at Life Time, she leads a team of 250+ professionals focused on innovation and operational excellence. Since joining in 2019, Angie has advanced AI, data, and digital solutions to enhance the experiences of members and team members across 175+ luxury athletic clubs, while fostering a culture of collaboration and trust that supports Life Time’s mission to help people live healthier, happier lives.

Jason Longhurst is passionate about turning complex technology into engaging stories that simplify technical topics and inspire fresh ideas. He spent most of the last decade at Domo designing enterprise-grade tools for data analysis and transformation, making intricate systems intuitive and people-friendly. Now, as Domo’s Head of Product Marketing, he blends design thinking and technical expertise to make AI and data feel approachable, exciting, and impactful.

title: Revolutionizing Industries with AI-Powered Drone Intelligence
abstract: In this joint Ericsson and Snowflake presentation, we showcase an AI-powered drone solution that leverages LiDAR, 5G and cloud AI to transform real-world operations. Our approach enables real-time capture and processing of high-resolution 3D data, seamlessly streaming insights via programmable networks. By integrating AI-driven analytics with Snowflake’s scalable data platform, we unlock new efficiencies in telecom, disaster response and industrial automation.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.bio: Fawad is a results-driven technology leader with 20+ years of experience across 50+ countries. He excels in enterprise data warehouses, big data analytics, and shared economy business models. As Global Field CTO, Fawad advises C-level executives across diverse sectors on leveraging data and technology to drive success. His expertise in data monetization, data as a service, and data products enables him to develop innovative strategies that fuel revenue growth. Fawad is passionate about sustainability and utilizes data and analytics to achieve impactful business outcomes and foster positive environmental change.

Soren Marklund, VP, Global Services, Technology Consulting and AI/Data Strategy at Ericsson, with more than 35 years of experience in the telecommunications and software industries. He has spent his career in advising Ericsson’s global services’ innovation and data transformation. Spearheading efforts of adopting advanced AI/ML digitalization for greater business transformation globally. He has held sales, operational, data, and strategy positions globally, influencing the company Digital, AI/ML and Data Strategy. He holds a strong passion for delivering industry leading customer experience through innovative customer-centric data-driven service offerings.

title: Snowflake on Snowflake: Lighting Up HR Ops with the Power of Heat Maps
abstract: The HR team developed a digital transformation process framework to assess processes using a weighted matrix and generate scores (0-100) for comparison. A heat map visualizes scores, prioritizing efforts and guiding strategic planning. This was previously a manual process that often relied on spreadsheets, limiting security and efficiency. By leveraging Snowflake and partnering with the Streamlit team, we created an app with automated heat maps and role-based access control for streamlining data tracking and planning.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Andrei is a seasoned data science leader with over a decade of technical expertise and people management experience. With a proven track record in both startups and Fortune 50 companies, Andrei has driven initiatives across revenue optimization, feature development, and strategic decision-making. Career highlights include leading Instagram Channels’ data science team at Meta, modernizing decision science for ABC Television at Disney, and spearheading insurance pricing modeling at Zenefits. Currently at Snowflake, Andrei heads data science teams for Product Experiences and Collaboration & Marketplace.

speakers.job_title: Senior Manager, HR Process and Operations
speakers.bio: Olga is an HR professional with over 15 years of international experience, enabling organizations to scale through data-driven decisions, process intelligence, and operational excellence. Certified as a Lean Six Sigma Master Black Belt and CIPD-accredited, she optimizes processes, leads digital transformations, and mentors teams. Currently driving cross-functional process improvements at Snowflake, Olga brings a continuous improvement mindset to every initiative, ensuring measurable impact and team growth. Her career spans roles in HR operations, process design, and system implementation across diverse industries. Before Snowflake Olga worked at industry leaders ServiceNow and Cloudera.

title: The Agentic Data Team: The Future of Digital Data Engineering
abstract: Explore how the rise of agentic ETL and copilots is revolutionizing data engineering. This session will delve into the transformative potential of automation in data integration, reshaping daily workflows, and empowering data teams to focus on innovation rather than routine tasks. Discover how these advancements dramatically enhance productivity, streamline pipeline development, and ensure seamless maintenance in the evolving data landscape. Hear the candid and entertaining views of Joe Reis, best-selling author of "The Fundamentals of Data Engineering."

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Joe Herbert is a Senior Solution Architect at Matillion, at the forefront of the go-to-market strategy for their new Maia agentic AI and virtual data engineers. With a rich background in applying data science to solve real-world challenges, including optimizing operations as a data scientist at Edinburgh Airport, Joe brings a unique perspective on the evolution of data engineering. He is passionate about empowering organizations to leverage autonomous AI agents to drive business value and innovation.
Matt is Director of Product at Matillion, working to bring productivity to Data Engineering teams.

Joe Reis, a "recovering data scientist" with 20 years in the data industry, is the co-author of the best-selling O'Reilly book, "Fundamentals of Data Engineering." He’s also the instructor for the wildly popular Data Engineering Professional Certificate on Coursera, in partnership with DeepLearning.ai and AWS. Joe’s extensive experience encompasses data engineering, data architecture, machine learning, and more. He regularly keynotes major data conferences globally, advises and invests in innovative data product companies, writes at Practical Data Modeling and his personal blog, and hosts the popular data podcasts "The Monday Morning Data Chat" and "The Joe Reis Show."

title: The Healthcare and Life Sciences Executive Panel
abstract: Get the inside track. Join this off-the-record and completely candid conversation with leaders across the healthcare and life sciences industry about how they are driving data, technology and organizational transformation within their companies. This session will explore the data and AI investment priorities at their organizations, the key business and technology use cases they’re enabling, the direction the industry is headed and key learnings from their Snowflake partnerships.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.bio: David Haney is the Chief Data Officer at Cambia Health Solutions, where he leads the company's efforts in leveraging data and artificial intelligence to enhance healthcare experiences for over 3.4 million members across four Western states. Before joining Cambia, David held several senior leadership roles in prominent organizations, including Global Senior Director of Consumer Knowledge at Nike, and positions at Visa, Yahoo!, and Hewlett Packard Enterprise. He holds a M.S. in Predictive Analytics from Northwestern University and has been a frequent speaker at industry conferences, sharing his insights on data strategy, AI, and digital transformation.

Brooke resides in Bentonville, AR and after spending time at EMC and ServiceNow, she has been leading various sales teams across Snowflake for the last 6 years. She is excited to now lead the US Strategics Sales Teams for HCLS and loves helping Snowflake's customers drive tremendous value by transforming their data strategy with the help of Snowflake's partner ecosystem.

speakers.job_title: EDO & Vice President, Strategic Data Products

speakers.bio: Nick McKenzie is a seasoned technology leader with almost 15 years of experience in partner management, product management, and digital healthcare transformation. Currently serving as VP of Product Management in the Insights BU at Inovalon, he spearheads the development of a data portfolio that solves a wide range of identified data use cases and verticals. Throughout his career, Nick has been instrumental in integrating cloud-native technologies, APIs, and clinical information systems into healthcare infrastructures. His work often focuses on leveraging AI to drive massive data decision making scale in ecosystems in the clinical, financial, research and actuarial sectors of healthcare.

speakers.job_title: Chief of Analytics and Data Management
speakers.bio: Jeff Morrison is a healthcare technology leader passionate about innovating with data to enhance decision-making and customer interactions. At Northeast Georgia Health System, he drives operational efficiency by transforming complex datasets into actionable insights that improve patient and provider experiences. With a keen eye for detail, Jeff proactively solves business and technical challenges, streamlining workflows and developing scalable solutions. His expertise in data-driven innovation empowers organizations to navigate the complexities of modern healthcare and optimize performance through smarter, more efficient use of information.

title: What's New: Deploy Accurate Conversational Apps in Cortex AI with AI Data Agents
abstract: Learn how to build and deploy accurate conversational apps and bring AI-powered data agents into production with ease and trust across your enterprise. Join us for new product insights, feature overviews, best practices and live demos.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: As a Product Manager on the Snowflake Cortex AI team, Harshal Pimpalkhute leads initiatives across LLM inference and Agentic AI. With a deep understanding of cloud services and software engineering he is passionate about bringing AI innovations to customers.

title: "Ask me Anything" with Product Leadership - Platform, Apps & Collaboration
abstract: Join us for an exclusive Ask Me Anything (AMA) session with Snowflake’s top product leaders, where no topic is off-limits. This interactive conversation offers a rare opportunity to engage directly with the minds shaping Snowflake’s strategy and innovation in Apps, Collaboration & Platform. Whether you're looking for insights into product vision, best practices, or upcoming capabilities, this is your chance to go straight to the source.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Artin Avanes joined Snowflake in 2015 as its first-ever product manager, building out the broader team and ecosystem from the ground up. As Head of Core Data Platform at Snowflake, he is now responsible for building and scaling product management teams responsible for Snowflake core products, including SQL analytics, data warehousing, database security, privacy and data governance, multi-cloud and metadata-driven features, and cloud infrastructure. Prior to Snowflake, Artin helped launch multiple features across the Microsoft Azure platform. He holds both an M.S. in Database and Information Systems, and a PhD in Computer Science. Artin has also spent time doing research at major universities, including Stanford and Humboldt University in Berlin.

Prasanna is Senior Director of Product Management at Snowflake, where she’s responsible for Collaboration on the Data Cloud, the Snowflake Marketplace and Snowflake Horizon. She has been an exec at venture-backed startups including Jetsetter (acquired by Tripadvisor), MongoDB and Founder/CEO at SmartyPal where she was profiled in Inc. Magazine. She was a VC at Draper Fisher Jurvetson, and worked on product roles at Microsoft and Comcast. Prasanna has an MBA from the Wharton School, as a Palmer Scholar, a Masters in Computer Science from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign as a Siebel Scholar and a Bachelors in Computer Science from BITS, Pilani, India.

title: AI in Retail: Personalization and Efficiency Across Channels
abstract: Retail is evolving with AI and shifting consumer expectations. This panel explores how top retailers are transforming customer experiences across online and offline channels, leveraging social commerce and streamlining supply chains with data-driven insights. Experts will discuss AI’s impact on consumer interactions, predictive analytics, dynamic pricing and operational efficiency. Join us to discover how AI and data are redefining retail, enabling seamless, personalized and highly efficient shopping experiences.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.bio: Paul has worked within the Retail industry for over 30 years. In his early career Paul spent nearly 20 years at Sainsburys. In the past 19 years Paul has led the Retail GTM industry practice at Snowflake in EMEA to help retailers apply intelligence to their data with the Snowflake Retail AI Data Cloud.

With more than 20 years of experience in the advertising effectiveness industry, Georgi is passionate about helping consumer brands drive advertising sales. In his current role, he forges strategic partnerships with media platforms, data providers, DSPs, data management platforms, and all components of the media ecosystem to support better advertising. Prior to NCS, Georgi was SVP Media Analytics at Kantar, where he led audience targeting and measurement, brand effectiveness, and strategic partnerships. His team developed the next generation of global audience, targeting, and measurement solutions. Before Kantar, Georgi worked at Nielsen where he designed targeting and measurement studies.

Michael Doll is the Vice President of Data for Guitar Center. As the VP of Data at GC, the nation's largest musical instrument retailer, Mike leads enterprise-wide data strategy across infra, BI, analytics and ML. Mike has over 15 years of experience in data, analytics and strategy, with a track record of building high performing data functions at companies across multiple industries such as edtech, staffing marketplace and fintech. Mike grew up in Chicago and then the University of Chicago where he was an all-conference hurdler, graduating with a degree in Mathematics. He resides in the San Francisco Bay Area with his wife and three children and enjoys gardening, chess and poker.

Ian Dewar likes to call himself an accidental marketer. Currently the Sr. Director for Global Strategy at Anthropologie, Ian has grown to this role by continually putting the customer experience first. Blending consumer behavior and data analytics to best understand what customers want and how to get there has been the central theme of his whole career. Ian is responsible for the Consumer Insights, Global Analytics and Loyalty teams at Anthropologie and works to blend the art and science of bringing marketing and product innovation to life for their customers.

title: Automate and Scale Role-Based Access Control with Snowflake, Okta and Terraform
abstract: This session explores a scalable and automated approach to managing role-based access control (RBAC) in Snowflake using Okta and Terraform. By integrating Okta’s identity management with Terraform’s infrastructure-as-code principles, organizations can streamline user provisioning, enforce least-privilege access, and ensure consistent role assignments tied to functional groups. The discussion will cover best practices for automating access control, aligning with organizational policies, and using declarative workflows to enhance security, scalability and operational efficiency.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Kamal is the Principal Architect of ResMed's global data platform, where he spearheaded the Snowflake project six years ago. Today, Snowflake is a cornerstone of ResMed's data infrastructure. With over 30 years of experience in data management, Kamal's expertise spans multiple industries and technologies, including Oracle Corporation, Teradata, Deloitte, and the Australian Government. He has been an integral part of ResMed's team for over 12 years, driving innovation in solution development, data migration, and team leadership. Based in Sydney, Australia, Kamal continues to shape ResMed's data strategy and architecture.

Shiv Girijashankar is a Sr Manager and Architect specializing in Snowflake, Data Catalog, Data Governance, Privacy, and Data Security. With over 18 years of experience, Shiv has been instrumental in designing and implementing scalable, secure, and compliant data architectures. His expertise lies in building robust data governance frameworks, ensuring privacy compliance, and optimizing enterprise-wide cloud data strategies. Passionate about innovation in cloud data management, Shiv is dedicated to helping organizations harness the full potential of their data while maintaining security and compliance.

title: From Concept to Marketplace: Building a Data Quality Snowflake Native App
abstract: Discover how Ataccama transformed an idea into a marketplace-ready Data Quality app using the Snowflake Native App Framework. This session offers a behind-the-scenes look at the development journey, covering key aspects such as leveraging Snowflake UDFs, Snowpark Container Services and overcoming deployment challenges. Gain best practices for building robust Snowflake Native Apps and effectively navigating the Snowflake ecosystem.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Marek is the Field CTO at Ataccama, owning the company’s Snowflake technical innovation and integration, including the development of Ataccama’s Native App. He drives technical strategy, working closely with clients, engineering teams, and product managers. Specializing in data quality, MDM, ETL, data warehouses, and Big Data, Marek has led projects since the early days of Hadoop. With expertise in full-stack JavaScript, PHP, Java, Perl, Python, Ruby, and C, he has extensive experience with RDBMS and Hadoop clusters. Marek blends strategic leadership with hands-on technical expertise to deliver innovative data solutions.

title: From Sidelines to Scoreboards: Winning with Data and AI on Snowflake
abstract: The modern sports landscape is a data-driven arena, where competitive advantage hinges on the ability to extract actionable insights from a deluge of data. This session will delve into the transformative power of data and AI for sports. We will explore how leading organizations are leveraging Snowflake's platform to revolutionize everything from fan engagement and player performance to revenue optimization and operational efficiency.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.bio: Madeleine Want is the VP of Data at Fanatics Betting & Gaming, where she leads data science, analytics, and engineering to drive product and business strategy in the sports gaming industry. She is the co-author of Precisely, a book about precision systems and data-informed decision-making. Previously, she held product leadership roles at Audible, Axel Springer, and Index Exchange, building data-driven products across media and technology. Madeleine holds a master’s degree in public policy and economics from Columbia University and brings a cross-disciplinary perspective grounded in philosophy, economics, and real-world experimentation.

Prior to Snowflake, Michelene was a strategic/technical BD executive at Amazon Music. Michelene has also served as the VP and Head of BD/Partnerships for Bleacher Report under Warner Bros. Discovery. Michelene joined WBD from Yahoo! Sports where she served as the Global Head of BD & Strategic Partnerships and closed the first ever NFL rights deal to distribute a live game exclusively via streaming, and the first ever deal to distribute personalized highlights from a major US sports league (NBA) leveraging AI. Michelene holds a Bilingual MBA from IESE Business School in Spain. She received a Bachelor of Arts in Economics and Communications from the University of California, San Diego.

Jeremi T. Gorman served as president of worldwide advertising for Netflix, Inc., until October 2023. Prior to becoming president of worldwide advertising, Mrs. Gorman was chief business officer at Snap Inc. and prior to that, held various positions at Amazon.com, Inc. including as global head of enterprise advertising sales. Earlier in her career, she held advertising and marketing roles at Yahoo!, Variety and Monster.com. In January 2025, Mrs. Gorman joined Fanatics to lead their advertising business. She also serves as a senior advisor to various companies and is an active angel investor. Mrs. Gorman is also a founding investor in Angel city Football Club and serves on the board of Women for Women International, as well as GameSquare Holdings, inc. and Revitate. Mrs. Gorman holds a bachelor’s degree from the University of California, Los Angeles and an MBA from Pennsylvania State University.

title: How AT&T Combines Generative Models with Knowledge Graphs for Better AI Accuracy
abstract: Generative AI is taking the world by storm. What happens when it meets its structured, fact-driven soulmate, the relational knowledge graph? We explore how combining GenAI with knowledge graphs creates a dynamic duo: AI that's not just creative, but also accurate, explainable, and deeply informed. We'll dive into real-world use cases, from chatbots to BI. Expect insights, practical applications, and a few AI-generated jokes (with fact-checking by our knowledge graph, of course!)

speakers.bio: Farbod Tavakkoli is a Data Scientist at AT&T, specializing in fine-tuning and pretraining small language models, knowledge graph construction, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines. His work has enhanced data-driven decision-making across high-stakes applications, including text-to-SQL pipelines and knowledge-driven insights. Farbod combines academic rigor with hands-on expertise in advanced NLP, predictive modeling, and ontology development.

Nikolaos Vasiloglou serves as the Vice President of Research-ML at RelationalAI, where his work focuses on advancing machine learning technologies. His career has been centered around the development of ML software and leading data science projects in sectors such as retail, online advertising, and security. He is actively involved in the machine learning community, contributing as an author, reviewer, and organizer within various forums including ICLR, ICML, NeurIPS, UAI, MLconf, KGC, and IEEE S&P. At RelationalAI, Nikolaos leads research efforts that investigate the integration of Language Models with Knowledge Graphs, aiming to enhance the company's capabilities in AI and data processing.

title: How to Obtain Data-Driven Revenue Growth for Automotive Retailers
abstract: Join industry leaders from Rohrman Auto Group and Tealium to learn how Tealium's real-time customer data collection enhances Snowflake data by enabling near-instantaneous ingestion of interaction data, allowing automotive retailers to recapture ownership of their customer data, build a complete customer view and deliver 1:1 personalization across all touchpoints. This is a can't-miss session all attendees will want to join regardless of their industry!

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Strategic executive with a track record of success at Tealium, MuleSoft, New Relic, and Boomi. Board advisor and angel investor known for blending strategic problem-solving with a people-first approach. Skilled in generative AI, global partnerships, enterprise sales, and go-to-market strategy. Over two decades of data collection, enrichment, master data management, and analytics at scale for the most highly regulated industries on the planet. Proven ability to drive growth, build high-performing teams, and navigate complex challenges to drive value.

As the leader of Rohrman Automotive Group, my focus has been on Driving Relationships and Providing Awesome Experiences. Under my guidance, our team has grown to 20 dealerships with more than 1,500 team members, achieving consistent growth across Wisconsin, Illinois, and Indiana through strategic business development and a commitment to enhancing the dealership experience. We have cultivated a culture that emphasizes our core values of Transparency, Innovation, Personableness, and Servant-Mindedness. These efforts align with my mission to position Rohrman Automotive Group as a leader in the automotive industry.

title: Implementing Automated CI/CD for Snowflake with dbt and GitHub Actions
abstract: Learn how to streamline CI/CD for Snowflake using dbt, GitHub Actions and Snowflake workflows. This session will cover setting up a CI/CD pipeline, automating dbt model testing, version control and deployments. Attendees will discover how to manage branch-based development and enforce data quality checks. By the end, participants will be equipped to deploy scalable, automated data workflows that support modern DataOps principles.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Vinoth Nageshwaran is a seasoned Data Engineering Leader with over 16 years of industry experience, specializing in the design and implementation of scalable data architectures. Vinoth has played a pivotal role in transforming data infrastructure for leading organizations, driving critical projects in cloud migration, data integration, and AI-driven automation. He is an accomplished technical reviewer, keynote speaker, and mentor, having presented at international conferences, judged global hackathons, and reviewed IEEE and other industry-recognized publications. Vinoth holds a fellowship from IICSPA and is a professional member of IEEE and ACM.

title: Inside Snowflake Cortex AI: Gen AI and Enterprise Intelligence Capabilities
abstract: Join Snowflake’s Head of AI Products for a tour of Snowflake’s enterprise-AI vision, recent innovations and roadmap. You’ll see how Snowflake Intelligence, automatic Semantic Model generation and specialized AI tools work together inside Snowflake’s secure platform, turning multimodal data into transformational impact for enterprise customers without infrastructure headaches. Walk away knowing how these integrated capabilities give business users, data analysts and engineers the tools, speed and governance required to deploy AI into production.

speakers.bio: Baris is Snowflake’s Head of AI where he is responsible for leading AI and machine learning initiatives, and driving Snowflake’s overarching AI product roadmap and strategy. He is passionate about working on cutting-edge AI and leveraging the technology to solve real world problems for customers. Baris joined Snowflake following its acquisition of nxyz in August 2023, where he served as the co-founder and CEO. Previously, Baris spent over a decade at Google across various product leadership roles, with a focus on developing consumer AI products. Baris holds a M.S. in Electrical Engineering from Cornell University and an MBA from Stanford.

title: Secure Data Provisioning at Scale in the Agentic Era
abstract: As AI, internal data marketplaces and self-service access become more popular, data teams must rethink how they securely govern and provision data at scale. Success depends on provisioning data in a way that balances security, compliance and innovation, and promotes data-driven decision making when decision makers are AI agents. In this session, we'll discuss how you can: Launch and manage an effective data marketplace Secure your AI initiatives Scale your data governors through agentic AI Join us to learn how to navigate the complexities of modern data environments, and start putting your data to work faster.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Matt Vogt is a seasoned technology professional with over two decades of diverse experience in the tech industry, currently serving as the Vice President of Global Solution Architecture at Immuta. His expertise lies in bridging business objectives with technical requirements, focusing on data privacy, governance, and data access within Data Science, AI, ML, and advanced analytics. Matt's current focus is building diverse technical data teams that are helping organizations develop strategies for security and governance to address challenges in accessing sensitive or regulated data.

title: The Connected Enterprise: Micron’s Journey to a Unified Data Platform
abstract: Discover Micron Technology's six-year journey to build a unified data platform on Snowflake, integrating manufacturing, supply chain and business data. Learn about its mesh architecture, centralizing security and frameworks, all while supporting decentralized development. Understand how Apache NiFi facilitated data movement into Snowflake. Gain insights from Micron’s data leaders on best practices and benefits of data consolidation.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Tony Money is a seasoned expert in the field of artificial intelligence and data analytics, currently serving as the Director of AI Solution Architecture for the Smart Manufacturing and AI (SMAI) organization at Micron. With a robust background in AI and a passion for leveraging data to drive innovative solutions, Tony has been instrumental in shaping the strategic direction of Data and AI initiatives within the organization. In his role, Tony leads a talented team of Solution Architects, Platform Engineers, Data Scientists and Machine Learning Engineers, focusing on the development and implementation of cutting-edge AI solutions that address complex business challenges.

title: The Financial Services C-Suite Panel
abstract: Join an off-the-record and candid panel discussion with financial services C-suite executives on where they see AI investment priorities, data and AI design considerations and strategic use cases being implemented across the industry. Moderated by Rinesh Patel, Snowflake’s Global Head of Financial Services, this session will explore the evolving ethos toward AI and what it means for the future of the industry.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.bio: A transformation-driven c-level executive with more than 20 years of experience directing teams overseeing development and launch of challenging and innovative data projects and programs for industry-leading organizations across the U.S. I am an industry expert in data governance and have served as chief data officer for 3 large-cap financial services firms building teams and instilling a fundamental understanding, level of comfort, and empowerment in cross-functional data and analytics initiatives resulting in several significant achievements over the years, including implementation of cloud-based enterprise capabilities that drove both revenue and efficiency outcomes.

Avinash Naik is President & Chief Information Officer at Bajaj Allianz General Insurance.He comes with rich experience in running large Technology operations, Digital Transformation,& Innovation programs across multiple geographies.He spent over a decade at Infosys Limited,where he played multiple leadership roles across Delivery Management,Consulting,Program Management,Enterprise Architecture for Fortune 100 companies.Before joining Bajaj Allianz General Insurance,he was part of the Group Corporate Strategy team at Bajaj Finserv where he was responsible for driving the Digital &Innovation agenda across Group companies.He has been recognized as one of the“Top 30 Tech Leaders”by Forbes India.

speakers.job_title: CDO, Consumer and Small Business Banking
speakers.bio: Harveer Singh is a highly accomplished technology executive renowned for his expertise in driving Digital Transformation and Platform Enablement within the financial services industry. With an illustrious career spanning leadership role at Western Union, Ernst & Young, Deloitte, and Accenture, Harveer has consistently excelled in helping organizations navigate technology challenges and bridge the gap between business and IT.

title: Train, Deploy, Repeat: Building Advanced Pipelines with Snowflake ML
abstract: Deploying machine learning models into production typically involves automated CI/CD pipelines. After experimentation, data scientists commit finalized code, initiating pipelines that replicate development steps on production data for consistency and scalability. This session introduces how Snowflake ML is designed to simplify ML workflow deployment by enabling remote execution directly from orchestration engines, including Snowflake Tasks. Come to this session to learn more about how you can productionize your end-to-end ML pipelines within Snowflake and configure them to trigger model retraining runs and visualize and monitor task execution graphs.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Vinay is a product manager on the ML Platform team. He's currently focused on enabling production-grade ML powered by Snowflake's Container Runtime. Prior to Snowflake he spent many years in the AI/ML platforms space leading product initiatives and teams across small, medium, and large enterprises.

David is a Senior Software Engineer on the Machine Learning Platform team working on foundational components to enable efficient, scalable machine learning workflows in Snowflake. Prior to Snowflake, he worked at Microsoft on Conversation Transcription and Text Summarization in Azure AI.

title: Unlocking the Future of Data Orchestration: Introducing Apache Airflow 3
abstract: Airflow 3 transforms data orchestration with three game-changing capabilities: Intuitive experience: Revamped UI, DAG versioning and integrated backfills streamline pipeline management Enterprise security: Task isolation architecture eliminates direct database connections, reducing exposure risks Boundless flexibility: Execute tasks anywhere — from edge to hybrid-cloud to GPU workloads — with remote execution Discover how these innovations are already revolutionizing how forward-thinking teams operationalize mission-critical data workflows.

speakers.bio: Kenten manages the Developer Relations team at Astronomer and has a background in field engineering, data engineering, and consulting. She has first-hand experience adopting and running Airflow as a consultant, and is passionate about helping other data engineers scale and get the most out of their orchestration experience. When she isn't working with data she's typically outside trail running or skiing.

title: What's New: Faster Insights with Snowflake Standard Warehouse - Gen2 and Adaptive Compute
abstract: Learn how Snowflake is innovating to provide faster warehouses with Standward Warehouse- Gen2, while making compute easier to use with better price/performance through Snowflake Adaptive Compute. Join us for new product and feature overviews, best practices and live demos.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: PD Dutta is a product manager at Snowflake responsible for virtual warehouses, which is the primary compute model for running customer workloads. Prior to joining Snowflake, PD held product management roles at Microsoft Azure, AWS and NetApp.
Rudi Leibbrandt is a Principal Product Manager at Snowflake, and is focused on the core Snowflake query platform, and loves talking about SQL, performance, and data types.

title: Best Practices for Optimizing Your Snowflake SQL Workloads
abstract: Learn best practices in performance optimization for your Snowflake SQL workloads. We will review our latest features and guide you through best practices in the optimization lifecycle — from monitoring key performance indicators to understanding trends, diagnosing issues and identifying optimization options — ending with implementing optimizations such as Automatic Clustering, Search Optimization Service, Query Acceleration Service and other techniques. Leave this session equipped with practical tools and proven strategies you can implement when you return from Summit, giving you the confidence that you and your team are maximizing value from Snowflake.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Nick is the technical lead for the SQL Workload Optimization team. His focus is helping Snowflake users understand when there are opportunities to improve performance or cost, from changes to individual queries to enabling advanced data and compute features like Clustering or QAS. Prior to Snowflake, Nick was a Principal Engineer at Amazon.

Stephen Yigit-Elliott leads product management for Snowflake data warehouse workload optimization. His career has spanned various cloud and underwater platforms. Prior to joining Snowflake, Stephen was a product leader at AWS and GCP, and a nuclear submarine officer in the US Navy.

title: Beyond Chatbots: Predicting Customer Intent
abstract: Call centers handle massive call volumes daily, with complex workflows, high operational costs and the challenge of maintaining service quality. Traditional approaches rely on human agents and rule-based automation, leading to inefficiencies. By leveraging generative AI and machine learning, we developed a solution using Snowflake Cortex AI to predict customer intent, automate responses and optimize call handling. This "Menu of One" approach enhances efficiency, reduces costs and sets a new standard for customer service operations.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.bio: Fawad is a results-driven technology leader with 20+ years of experience across 50+ countries. He excels in enterprise data warehouses, big data analytics, and shared economy business models. As Global Field CTO, Fawad advises C-level executives across diverse sectors on leveraging data and technology to drive success. His expertise in data monetization, data as a service, and data products enables him to develop innovative strategies that fuel revenue growth. Fawad is passionate about sustainability and utilizes data and analytics to achieve impactful business outcomes and foster positive environmental change.

title: End-to-End Data Engineering with Python in Snowflake
abstract: Modern data engineering leverages Python to build robust, scalable, end-to-end workflows. In this talk, we will cover how Snowflake offers you a flexible development environment for developing Python data pipelines, performing transformation at scale, orchestrating and deploying your pipelines at scale. Topics we’ll cover include: Ingest: Data source APIs, Snowflake file-to-read and ingest data of any format when files arrive, with sources outside Snowflake Develop: Packaging (artifact repo), Python runtimes, IDE (Notebook, vscode) Transform: Snowpark pandas, UDFs, UDAFs Deploy: Tasks, Notebook scheduling

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Bhanu Prakash is a Senior Product Manager at Snowflake, responsible for leading the product roadmap and strategy for the Snowpark platform. Prior to joining Snowflake, he worked for Microsoft as a Principal PM, responsible for big data services including Azure HDInsight, and Azure Databricks.

Qinyi Ding is a Product Manager at Snowflake, where he leads product efforts for Snowpark, focusing on improving the developer experience for DataFrame-based data engineering workflows. With a strong foundation in data engineering and data warehousing, his goal is to empower data engineers to build scalable, performant pipelines on Snowflake. In addition to his role at Snowflake, Qinyi also serves as a Teaching Fellow at the Harvard Business Analytics Program.

title: Executive Networking Snow Hour: Media, Retail, Manufacturing and Telecom
abstract: Meet fellow decision-makers from media, retail, manufacturing and telecom in this focused executive session. Discuss how data and AI are reshaping customer experience, operational agility and monetization strategies in industries that are rapidly converging and evolving.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

title: How Snowflake Powers the Patient Journey from Diagnosis to Care Management
abstract: Uncover the power of the network effect and a connected AI Data Cloud. This session brings to life how healthcare and life sciences organizations are collaborating with partners to enable business-critical use cases, including patient 360, clinical trial matching, drug supply chain optimization and post-clinical-trial care management. Hear from industry-leading organizations on how they’re architecting around data interoperability, secure data sharing and new AI-powered capabilities to deliver a more efficient, personalized patient journey.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.job_title: Global Healthcare and Life Sciences CTO
speakers.bio: Murali Gandhirajan is Global Healthcare and Life Sciences CTO at Snowflake and focused on helping healthcare and life sciences organizations build next generation data and analytics platforms leveraging a broad set of Snowflake capabilities. He has more than two decades of experience in being a thought leader and trusted advisor to senior data and analytics leaders of Healthcare and Life Sciences organizations and delivering significant business value through data-driven insights.

Pushwaz is the Senior Medical Director at Blue Shield of California responsible for health transformation projects. Previously, he was a Chief Medical Information Officer at Providence St Joseph Health and also had roles at IT consulting, software development, and business process outsourcing organizations. He is an attending physician in hospital medicine and an author of a book on applied digital health.

GTM product leader and author on the forefront of AI and digital health. Served on COVID-19 vaccine task force for the state of California resulting in an estimated 20,000 saved lives and prevention of 1.5M COVID-19 cases. Shipped key ML products at Figure Eight, leading up to a $300M acquisition by Appen. Currently oversees the technology products and platforms at Blue Shield of California, which are accessed by 6M members. In her 2021 book “Real World AI: A Practical Guide for Responsible Machine Learning,” Alyssa shares advice and best practices on building ethical, scalable AI applications.

speakers.job_title: Global Head of Digital Strategy and Operations
speakers.bio: Dimitrije is responsible for Sanofi's stance on digital transformation and ecosystems in the field of digital, data and AI, as well as overseeing strategy and transformation. He manages the digital portfolio and the operations of the organization. Within Sanofi, he established the AI CoE (Center of Excellence) in Toronto, which experienced remarkable growth, reaching a team size of 80 members within a year.

Karen Jenkins is a dynamic, customer-focused executive with 15+ years in SaaS workflow software. She drives revenue and growth by making strategic improvements to the overall customer experience from product roadmap through delivery. Fueled by a keen technology acumen, Karen thrives on simplifying complex problems into indispensable solutions for business users.

title: Is Your Data Ready for the Future? Delivering Trustworthy Data for AI
abstract: A company with a ready data strategy is a company ready for AI. The successful implementation of innovative technologies, such as AI, is closely correlated with the strength of an organization’s data foundation. However, data management can be complex, with challenges around data sprawl, quality and governance hindering progress. For visionaries who imagine more out of their data, this session will cover the best ways to overcome this and manage and deliver trustworthy data for AI.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Tony Giordano leads IBM Consulting’s Global Data Services Practice. He has led enterprise-scale, multi-cloud data platform deployments in the financial services, life sciences, retail, automotive sectors and more. He has extensive international experience in England, France, Belgium, Australia, China, Thailand, Turkey, and Japan. He has specialized in multi-use case data architectures, agile-based data methodologies, data organization operating models, and information governance.
Victor has been a Snowflake user and evangelist since 2018 - and now helps clients get the most out of the platform every day.

title: Leveraging Amazon S3 Tables with Snowflake: Enhancing Data Access and Analytics
abstract: Discover how to effectively utilize Amazon S3 Tables in conjunction with Snowflake to streamline your data analytics workflow. In this session, topics will focus on the practical integration of Amazon S3 Tables and Snowflake, demonstrating how to unlock new possibilities in your approach to data management. Learn how to create a more efficient and flexible data environment that leverages the strengths of both Amazon S3 and Snowflake. Whether you're a data engineer, analyst or architect, this session will provide valuable insights to enhance your cross-platform data strategy and maximize the potential of your cloud data infrastructure.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Nidhi Gupta is a Sr. Partner Solution Architect at Amazon Web Services (AWS). She spends her day working with customers and partners, solving architectural challenges. She is passionate about data integration and orchestration, serverless and big data processing, generative ai and machine learning (ML). Nidhi has extensive experience leading the architecture design and production release and deployments for data workloads.

title: Leveraging Data Mesh on Snowflake for Finance Transformation
abstract: Eaton Corporation is undergoing a significant transformation by migrating its data, analytics and AI capabilities to Snowflake, covering eight business functions/domains. This strategic move, guided by data mesh principles, aims to enhance decentralized data management, domain-oriented data ownership, and overall data accessibility. Concurrently, Eaton is implementing a finance transformation, utilizing a central finance alternative approach in Snowflake through a data products approach, to modernize financial operations and improve decision-making. Join this session to discover how these initiatives are driving efficiency, scalability and innovation across the organization.

speakers.bio: Rupesh is a Technology Fellow in AI & Engineering operating portfolio. He is the practice lead and CTO for the Snowflake channel and helps clients with data and analytics modernization on Snowflake. He also leads the Center of Excellence and Snowflake Modernization Factory to deliver value to clients while lowering the total cost of ownership with minimal risk to delivery.

Shilpi Reddy is a Principal Architect at Eaton, where she has been instrumental in implementing a data mesh architecture within Snowflake to drive data transformation. She is leading initiatives to implement governance across domains in the data mesh and data products, align data definitions across various business domains, empower cross-functional teams to make informed, value-creating decisions. Shilpi's proven approach unites policy, process, and people, fostering a culture of data-driven decision-making and operational efficiency.

title: Omnichannel Personalization: Elevating Engagement with Attentive and Snowflake
abstract: URBN’s iconic brands, including Urban Outfitters and Anthropologie, have long set the standard for customer-centric shopping experiences. Now, using Attentive and Snowflake, URBN is taking AI-powered personalization to the next level. This session explores how optimized ML models enhance predictions, driving higher-performing SMS and email marketing. Learn how URBN unlocks richer insights, deeper customer connections and more impactful marketing strategies — redefining personalization one intelligent, data-driven interaction at a time.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

title: Quiz Question Analytics Pipeline with External API Data Source Enrichment
abstract: The University of Florida utilizes a cloud-based learning platform to deliver quizzes to students. Individual student responses are only available via API to the vendor's cloud. The university wanted to gather these responses and blend them with existing data. A Snowflake pipeline to extract and blend data was created to meet the need.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Andrew McDonald is 22 year data engineer at the University of Florida in Gainesville, Florida. He graduated from the University of Florida with a bachelor’s degree in Computer Engineering. Several productivity awards for process improvement and a superior accomplishment award for customer service distinguish his service to the university. He has presented at the PeopleSoft Higher Education User's Group sharing his experiences in student information systems. Now he is senior data engineer for UF’s learning ecosystem Canvas and provides LMS data and visualizations for research projects and the university learning analytics initiative.

title: Responsible AI in the Public Sector: Innovation, Governance and Global Insights
abstract: As AI adoption accelerates, public sector leaders must balance innovation with accountability. This panel explores how governments are shaping national AI strategies, ensuring transparency and enabling safe, responsible adoption through modern data governance. Panelists will share insights on overcoming challenges, aligning with ethical standards and driving public trust. Discover how public institutions are turning responsible AI principles into real-world outcomes — from policy to implementation.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.bio: Glenn Powell serves as Snowflake’s Global Public Sector Industry leader where he focuses on data-enabled solutions tailored for Public Sector organisations. He supports global government, educational and utilities ecosystems to enable them to make better informed and more impactful decisions to support their purpose. His three decades of experience span Policy, Regulatory and Service Delivery agencies across all levels of Government.

A graduate in Aeronautics & Astronautics from Southampton University and with previous experience in the Royal Navy Fleet Air Arm, Colin has over 15 years experience in the defence industry. On leaving the military, Colin worked for Leonardo Helicopters on the build line for EH101, Lynx and Apache, and then leading the design, development and implementation of advanced mission training systems. Following this, Colin was seconded into Niteworks (a UK government research and decision support agency) as the project lead for the Aviation Simulation project – a key component to the UK MoD’s training transformation initiative. He also established the first EDA Helicopter Tactics Training system at RAF Linton-on-Ouse. Since leaving Leonardo, Colin has grown and led a number of defence technology companies, supporting their growth in the UK and Europe. He now heads up Mission Decisions, a startup that is supporting the exploitation of data and AI for military training.

Winston Chang is an expert in data driven organizational transformation, AI/ML, and innovation in public sector ecosystems. His over two decades of work encompasses start ups, IT modernizations, fashion branding, AI/ML/Blockchain prototyping, structured finance, military service, and more. Winston volunteers his time with the NIST MEP Advisory Board and the Eisenhower Fellowship network. His engagement in both organizations supports global bridge building and strengthening US economic drivers. Winston graduated from the United State Military Academy and holds a personal mission to help government and educational institutions leverage data for maximum societal impact.

title: Scaling Ski Resort Visitor Forecasting at Alterra with Snowflake ML
abstract: Accurate visitor forecasting is essential for optimizing resort operations and enhancing the guest experience. In this session, we’ll explore how Alterra Mountain Company, which operates 19 premier ski destinations across North America, quickly transitioned from manual forecasting methods to scalable ML-driven insights with Snowflake ML. Alterra will discuss how it leverages Snowflake’s native ML capabilities to build scalable daily forecasting models to manage short-term and seasonal forecasts, enabling data-driven decision-making and strategic planning.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Eli is a Data Scientist at Alterra Mountain Company, where he focuses on creating data visualization dashboards and building predictive models to drive business decisions in the ski industry. Eli's career path has evolved from brand strategy at ad agencies like R/GA and MullenLowe to ecological/botanical research in academia to data-driven decision making.
Marie Coolsaet is a data scientist on the Solution Innovation Team at Snowflake. In her role, she builds AI/ML solutions on Snowflake to solve customer business problems.

title: Stop Ignoring 80% of Your Data: Tap into Unstructured Data in a GenAI World
abstract: In today’s GenAI-driven landscape over 80% of enterprise data is unstructured and largely unchecked. As a result, organizations risk missing out on valuable insights, expose themselves to compliance violations, and undermine the effectiveness of GenAI investments. This session outlines a two-part strategy: (1) identifying and correcting issues to ensure clean, GenAI-ready datasets, and (2) extracting actionable insights to fuel strategic decision-making. Using Anomalo’s newly released Unstructured Data Monitoring product, enterprises can now transform hidden, unstructured data into trusted, high-impact assets that power scalable, responsible, and intelligent AI applications.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Vicky Andonova is GM of Generative AI at Anomalo, a leading data quality monitoring company backed by Norwest Venture Partners, Foundation Capital, and Two Sigma Ventures. A founding team member, Vicky spent six years pioneering Anomalo’s machine learning initiatives, transforming advanced AI into actionable insights that help enterprises trust their data. She now leads a team bringing innovative generative AI products to market and building a first-in-class data quality monitoring solution for unstructured data. Previously at Instacart, she built the experimentation platform and led efforts to improve delivery quality. She holds a BE from Columbia University.

title: Unlock Innovation Using Real-Time Data with Snowflake Openflow
abstract: In today's high-velocity data landscape, real-time streaming is the key to competitive advantage. Discover how Snowflake revolutionizes streaming data integration from popular streaming platforms including Apache Kafka and Amazon Kinesis. Learn best practices about streaming ingestion in Snowflake, and experience seamless ingestion, powerful in-flight transformations and custom flows that turn real-time data into immediate business value.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Ankit Gupta leads Product Management for Streaming and Metadata Connectors at Snowflake. He specializes in streaming technologies and is passionate about creating seamless experiences for organizations to process petabyte-scale data in near real-time for unlocking immediate business value. Prior to Snowflake, he led product for Amazon MSK's connectivity and integrations products including MSK Connect, MSK Replicator, and AWS Glue Schema Registry. Ankit holds an MBA from UCLA Anderson and a B.Tech from Delhi Technological University.

speakers.job_title: Principal Data Platform Architect, Applied Field Engineering
speakers.bio: Keith is a Principal Architect on the Applied Field Engineering team at Snowflake, specializing in Data Engineering. Before joining Snowflake, Keith led the Data Engineering practice at an analytics consultancy, co-founded a tech startup, and held various consulting and software development roles. He holds an MBA from Georgia Tech. Residing in St. Petersburg, Florida with his wife and two children, Keith enjoys boating, fishing, camping, and traveling.

title: Unlock Seamless Business Insights with Snowflake and Microsoft Power Platform
abstract: Discover how Snowflake and Microsoft Dataverse enable seamless, bidirectional data interoperability — empowering Power Apps developers to build low-code/no-code business applications without copying data. Learn how to effortlessly integrate Microsoft Power Platform — including Dynamics 365 applications — with Snowflake to unlock real-time enterprise insights. See firsthand how AI-powered analytics can drive smarter decisions and accelerate innovation tailored to your unique business needs.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Technical Architect with over 15 years of experience in designing and implementing scalable software solutions. Significant Achievements: - Led architecture for Industry first Zero-Copy integration enabling seamless data access between Snowflake and Salesforce, PowerApps Integration and SAP Business Data Cloud.

Sanjay is a Principal Product Manager at Snowflake currently focused on making it easy for customers to integrate their unstructured data from a variety of sources with Snowflake AI Data Cloud to boost their productivity and accelerate actions from insights. Sanjay brings over 30 years of product & engineering experience in individual contributor and executive leadership roles spanning startups to Fortune 50 companies. Previously worked at MSCI building Data & AI platforms for the financial investment industry, at Salesforce building data platforms for Salesforce Marketing Cloud, and at Microsoft building Azure SQL Database, SQL Server, and Data Developer and Manageability platforms.

title: Yes or No? LLMs Tackle Unstructured Data for Actionable Insight
abstract: Unstructured text — like customer surveys, policy documents and open-ended feedback — can be a goldmine of insights, but extracting and operationalizing that intelligence has traditionally been a challenge. We'll explore how LLMs are revolutionizing the data engineering pipeline by translating “messy” text into concise, actionable answers. By transforming yes/no questions into actionable data points, LLMs empower businesses to surface trends, drive BI reporting, and fuel predictive modeling with meaningful categorical information. See how our customers eliminated thousands of hours of tedious manual data processing in fields such as insurance and finance.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Julian's team lead Matillion's research into how AI can transform Data Engineering. This has lead to several ground breaking product features including our natural language UI options and the ability to work with unstructured data to systematically extract useful knowledge and actionable insight.

title: AI-Driven Processing of Manufacturing Documents and Data with Document AI
abstract: Leverage Document AI and Snowflake Cortex AI to transform unstructured data into actionable insights. Our solution automates vital information extraction from diverse PDF formats, reducing manual effort. Custom-trained models ensure efficient data extraction, while robust pre-processing validates documents. Enhanced metadata management tracks configurations and accuracy. A Streamlit interface enables real-time monitoring and exception handling, optimizing data ingestion workflows for manufacturing.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Scott Deering is a seasoned IT professional and Solution Principal Architect at Johnson Controls, specializing in Data and Analytics to drive innovative technology solutions for building efficiency. With over 25 years of experience in IT including roles as Architect and Digital and Consulting Account Managing Director at NTT DATA, he has developed expertise in Data Architecture, Solutions Architecture, and Business Intelligence. Passionate about AI, Scott leverages machine learning to optimize operations. His leadership has earned accolades, including the 2022 NTT DATA Innovation Award for Best-in-Class Data Analytics Program and a nomination for the Johnson Controls Chairman Award in 2023.

Nikolai Scholz is the Lead Product Manager for Document AI at Snowflake, responsible for designing and developing the product. Previously he held several product leadership roles in the marketing and travel industries in a career spanning 13+ years with heavy focus in AI, privacy & identity management, and sustainability. Nikolai comes from a data science background, and has an extensive experience in applying data in an enterprise environment at scale with the use of AI models.
