title: How Goldman Sachs architected an enterprise-grade open lakehouse
abstract: Financial institutions rely on data for decision-making, but simply centralizing it for analytics is just the tip of the iceberg. The true challenges lie in effective data storage, quality assurance, access control, seamless sharing and consumption, and timely notifications for downstream processes. Our Legend Lakehouse solution directly addresses these complexities. Join us for a discussion about how Goldman Sachs utilizes Apache Iceberg and Snowflake, Legend data models, functions and SDLC to build a secure and highly governed open lakehouse.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Abhishek Narang leads Vendor Data Engineering and Data Modeling efforts globally at Goldman Sachs, responsible for helping businesses across the firm identify, license, prototype, and use data to meet their client and business objectives. He is also responsible for co-creating firm's cloud data strategy, partnering with divisional Engineering teams and implementing solutions to migrate existing data to the cloud. Abhishek leads firmwide efforts to provide rapid access to cloud-based vendor data repositories. In addition, he manages the Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) Beacon team, which focuses on identifying and curating ESG data for multiple businesses across the firm

Tim is head of the Data Design and Curation team within Data Engineering. His team works across Goldman Sachs to help others model their data, to adopt our Legend data platform, and to select appropriate vendor technologies. He has been at the firm for 24 years and worked on many key evolutions of our data and platform. His latest project is bringing standard data to the Legend Lakehouse platform for broad use across Goldman Sachs.

Pierre is a Managing Director at Goldman Sachs and the Lead Architect of the recently open sourced Data Management Platform Legend. In addition, he manages Goldman’s Data Platform team which is responsible for developing data-centric and user-focused solutions to power the firm’s Data Strategy.

title: How Marriott Transformed Partner Collaboration with Snowflake Data Clean Rooms
abstract: Discover how Marriott International revolutionizes partner collaboration using Snowflake's Data Clean Room technology, demonstrated through their partnership with Ritz Carlton Yacht Collection. Learn how they implemented their first Data Clean Room for secure data sharing and activation while protecting customer privacy. The session covers implementation challenges, ROI benefits, and future opportunities in attribution analysis and lookalike modeling. Get insights on scaling across partners and leveraging Data Clean Rooms for secure collaboration.

speakers.bio: Leads Digital and Marketing Data & Analytics Products at Marriott. Specializes in Enterprise Data Strategy, Executive Stakeholder Engagement, Strategic Product Management, Partner Data Relationships, Digital Media & Marketing.

Daniel MacDonald serves as the Vice President of Data & Analytics Technology at Marriott International. Bringing 20 years of experience in data and analytics, Daniel specializes in the strategic application of technology to create robust platforms that directly drive business outcomes.

title: Learn What’s New for Taking Your SQL Developer Experience to the Next Level
abstract: Do you use SQL to derive insights? This session will highlight several features in Snowflake that make programming in SQL a breeze. Discover a range of powerful features including: Leveraging your favorite higher-order functions to easily process arrays and objects Writing efficient SQL stored procedures with ease with new features such as Asynchronous child job support Easily debugging queries with newly exposed bind variables and more to streamline productivity In addition, we'll showcase all these in our completely revamped Worksheets UI experience, now featuring split panes, an intuitive chart builder with drill-downs, in-line stats and more!

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

PM for SQL worksheets in Snowsight

title: New Opportunities in the Consumer Goods Industry with AI and Accessible Data
abstract: The consumer goods industry is unlocking new opportunities with AI and technological advancements. This session will explore emerging trends shaping the market and how brands are leveraging data-driven strategies to power growth, efficiency and consumer engagement. The leading experts will provide industry insights, followed by a discussion with a consumer goods executive on harnessing AI and data analytics to transform a company. Join us to discover how leading companies stay agile, competitive and future-ready in a rapidly evolving market.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.job_title: Industry Principal - Retail and Consumer Goods
speakers.bio: Freddy is part of Snowflake’s Retail & Consumer Goods Industry team and serves as the Global Head of Consumer Goods. He has spent the past 20 years driving business outcomes through data and analytics for large consumer brands and retailers across the globe. Freddy was most recently at Circana (formerly IRi), a leading data and analytics company where he served as VP of Commercial Strategy and Enablement for the Supply Chain Center of Excellence. Prior experience includes leading analytics teams at PepsiCo, ConAgra Foods, and SABMiller. Freddy holds a MS in Chemical Engineering from the University of Utah and a MBA in Marketing from Indiana University

James Martin is a seasoned data scientist and sales engineering leader with over two decades of experience in the consumer goods industry. James began his career at dunnhumby in London, specializing in personalization and loyalty data-based collaboration programs. Currently, he leads Sales Engineering globally at NielsenIQ, consulting with clients on the latest NIQ technology and addressing business issues as diverse as personalization, supply chain, and market measurement. Now based in the Bay Area, James is passionate about guiding clients on how to practically apply new technology, such as GenAI developments, to grow their business through customer-centric decisions.

Tom is a Vice President of Industry Strategy at Blue Yonder where he focuses on the Consumer Packaged Goods and Grocery industries in helping customers and prospects unlock value across their enterprise. Tom has over 20 years of experience in supply chain technology spanning category management, supply chain planning, integrated business planning, supply chain execution & visibility, sales planning, trade promotion planning, and retail execution.

title: Powering Insights with Data Collaboration: Honeywell Drives Business Excellence
abstract: Honeywell built a data hub on Snowflake with Marketplace data, enabling seamless enterprise sharing and robust disaster recovery. AI-driven summarization of unstructured data provides actionable insights, accelerating decisions at scale. Learn how Honeywell uses Snowflake’s collaboration and AI to drive agility and informed strategy globally.

speakers.job_title: Sr. Director - Data, Analytics and Predictive Insights
speakers.bio: Zubair leads Honeywell’s Data, Analytics and AI delivering data engineering, modeling, consumption and predictive analytics capabilities. Zubair has over 25 years of industry and consulting experience ranging from large scale digital transformation projects, ERP deployments, Automation, Data, Analytics and Decision Intelligence projects.

title: The Financial Services Data, Apps and AI Guide
abstract: Powering business-critical financial services use cases requires access to and seamless interoperability of state-of-the-art, best-of-breed data, services and AI models. This session will bring to life Snowflake’s AI Data Cloud for Financial Services. Hear how industry-leading organizations are tapping into the ecosystem to drive both operational efficiencies and business outcomes. Learn about new capabilities, new partnerships and new data and AI use cases being powered on Snowflake today.

Session Tracks: The AI Data Cloud for Industries

speakers.bio: As Head of Envestnet Data Solutions, Jeremi leads a dynamic team of data analysts and scientists dedicated to pioneering data-driven business and decision intelligence for Wealth Enterprises, Advisors, RIAs, and Asset Managers. Jeremi is responsible for a vast portfolio of wealth data & analytic solutions, including Envestnet’s Wealth Data Platform, Insights Engine, Enterprise/Advisor Analytics, Manager Analytics, and RIA Analytics.

Rinesh Patel is the Global Head of Financial Service at Snowflake where he is responsible for developing the financial service value proposition and supporting our customers' transition to an enterprise data & AI strategy in cloud. Rinesh has been in the financial services industry for over 20 years in a variety of banking, consulting and business leadership roles with his most recent experience as Global Head of Cloud Platform at London Stock Exchange Company where he established and led a commercially-minded platform team to develop a modern cloud data platform to deliver new data solutions. Rinesh is based in London where he lives with his wife and two daughters.

Patrick Starling is a Senior Vice President and Senior Director of Product Management at FactSet, leading the company's Cloud, GenAI Data Platform, and Partnership product ecosystems. With 19 years at FactSet, Patrick started as a Consultant working with customers across FactSet’s full suite of products and was instrumental in establishing our Strategic Customer Success, Sales Engineering and Solutions Architecture functions. He holds a MSc. in Information Technology from the UMass Lowell as well as dual Bachelor's degrees in Economics and Theatre Arts from Virginia Tech. Patrick lives in Boston and spends his spare time chasing after his 2 year old, snowboarding, hiking and cooking.

C.J. Jaskoll is Chief Technology Officer at Russell Investments, where he is leading a global tech transformation focused on AI, data, and modernization. He’s building a scalable operating model with an engineering hub in Mumbai and reshaping client, sales, and marketing platforms to drive personalization, security, and efficiency. Prior to Russell, he held senior tech roles at Neuberger Berman, Jennison Associates, and JPMorgan Asset Management.

Rowe Price. His areas of expertise span digital transformation, technology modernization, cloud migration, data and analytics, large-scale change initiatives, and investment front office and distribution technologies. He currently leads the Enterprise Data Architecture practice at Vanguard.

title: Uncertainty to Confidence: Yamaha Motor’s Financial Risk Strategy with Sigma
abstract: Learn how Yamaha Motor Finance shifted its corporate mindset from start-up to enterprise after significant pandemic-induced growth. Scaling their tech stack, they ditched spreadsheets for Snowflake and added Sigma to achieve unified business intelligence and democratized data access. Looking ahead, you’ll hear how the introduction of Sigma Data Apps will transform their workflows and enable their financial planning and analysis teams to go beyond canned reports and dashboards to achieve their continued growth targets.

speakers.bio: Anand is a senior data management, analytics, and technology thought leader with a demonstrated ability to help transform data into a powerful business asset. For more than 14 years, he has served in various roles across Yamaha, helping democratize data and championing the optimal tech stack to drive application and business growth. Anand is a proven leader who enables and collaborates within enterprises to achieve “Kando” in everything they do.

Kevin Duck is the Chief Risk and Strategy Officer for Yamaha Financial Services. His current responsibilities include Credit Risk Management, Strategic Analysis, Credit Decision Support, and Data Management. Kevin has over 20 years of combined manufacturing, retail, and financial services experience, including prior roles at Michelin North America, Capital One, The Home Depot, and CarMax Auto Finance. Kevin received his undergraduate degree in Electrical Engineering from Clemson University and his MBA from The Fuqua School of Business at Duke University. Kevin resides in the metro Atlanta area with his wife and three kids. In his spare time, he enjoys watching and playing sports.

title: Hands-on Lab: Time Series Analytics and LLM powered Chatbot with Pricing Data
abstract: This hands-on lab explores several Time Series features using FactSet Tick Data from Snowflake Marketplace, including TIME_SLICE, ASOF_JOIN and RANGE BETWEEN for insights into trade data. Learn how to build an AI-powered chatbot to help drive additional insights from the data.

speakers.bio: Software Developer and Data Architect with over 12 years of experience in enterprise-level data applications and data ecosystems. As part of the Innovation Team at Snowflake, Carlos architects and builds data applications using Python and Streamlit. Carlos has an extensive background in database administration, DevOps management, and cloud architecture.

title: Snowflake Data Integration 101
abstract: In this hands-on session, join our instructors to get experience with Snowflake's global data integration service, which offers seamless connectivity between diverse data sources and destinations. With hundreds of built-in processors and connectors, it handles structured and unstructured data, extending Snowflake's ease of use, security and governance across your entire data journey from sources to analytics to AI workloads. Join this hands-on lab to learn to build data ingestion from PostgreSQL and Apache Kafka topics into Snowflake, handling schema evolution, routing logic and data transformations on the fly.

speakers.bio: David leads the Americas Data Engineering specialists for Snowflake, focused on helping customers understand the value and benefits of Snowflake for data ingestion, transformation, and lakehouse workloads.

Sean is a Data Platform Architect, Applied Field Engineering at Snowflake, bringing over 25 years of deep expertise in data engineering, data warehousing, and business intelligence. His extensive experience spans the full data lifecycle, from building scalable data pipelines and architecting robust data warehouses to delivering actionable insights through BI solutions. Now at Snowflake, Sean empowers organizations to leverage the full potential of the Data Cloud by simplifying their data pipeline initiatives.

title: Unlock Insights from Unstructured Data with Snowflake Cortex AI
abstract: This hands-on lab (HOL: AI209) introduces participants to Snowflake Cortex AI's powerful capabilities for extracting insights from unstructured data. The lab features and you will build a comprehensive Streamlit application that demonstrates multiple AI functionalities accessible directly within the Snowflake ecosystem. Participants will explore a suite of practical AI tools including: Multi-Modal Analysis: Processing images to extract structured information about content (animals, environments, etc.) and transcribing audio files into text Text Processing: Translation between 11 languages, sentiment analysis of customer interactions, text summarization, and classification of content into predefined categories Generative AI: Leveraging foundation models like Claude, Llama, Mistral, and Snowflake Arctic to generate customer emails, determine next best actions, and answer questions based on provided data The Streamlit application demonstrates how to seamlessly integrate these AI capabilities into workflows by connecting to Snowflake, managing file uploads to stages, and processing various data types. Through this interactive experience, participants will gain hands-on experience with everyday AI tools that can transform unstructured data into actionable business insights without requiring specialized ML expertise.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Sean Morris is a Senior Solutions Engineer with extensive experience in data engineering, AI/ML, and data applications. For the past four years, he has worked at Snowflake, helping clients design and implement scalable data solutions. Prior to working at Snowflake, Sean was a customer for three years, gaining firsthand experience implementing and managing the Snowflake platform.
5+ year Snowflake Principal Solution Engineer focusing on Generative AI and Data Science.

title: ESRI and Snowflake: A Geospatial Hands-on Lab
abstract: This hands-on lab explores the integration of ESRI and Snowflake for scalable geospatial analytics. Participants will learn how to seamlessly ingest, store and analyze spatial data using Snowflake’s geospatial functions and ESRI’s powerful GIS tools. Through practical exercises, attendees will gain experience in processing spatial datasets, running geospatial queries and visualizing results, unlocking new insights for data-driven decision-making.

speakers.bio: Fawad is a results-driven technology leader with 20+ years of experience across 50+ countries. As Global Field CTO, Fawad advises C-level executives across diverse sectors on leveraging data and technology to drive success. His expertise in data monetization, data as a service, and data products enables him to develop innovative strategies that fuel revenue growth. Fawad is passionate about sustainability and utilizes data and analytics to achieve impactful business outcomes and foster positive environmental change.

An experienced hands-on architect and team leader with strong problem-solving skills. Experienced in various vertical sector and a willingness to learn more about the business and develop competitive business solutions, I bring to you, the knowledge & experience gained from multiple assignments.

After working in 17 years as a Sales Engineer in a variety of companies including Snowflake, Becky has taken the step to join the Solution Innovation team, where she aims to bring visions of the future to life with Snowflake. Her Special interest is end to end advanced analytics.

title: From Clicks to Cortex: Build AI-Ready Data Pipelines on Snowflake in a Snap
abstract: Want to try the latest Snowflake features, but don’t have the time to do it? Find out how to accelerate dev cycles by 10x and create anything in Snowflake in a few clicks using Coalesce’s data transformation platform and your favorite features like Snowflake Cortex and more. Please bring your laptop and create a Snowflake trial account before the event if you don't have a Snowflake account already.

speakers.bio: Josh Hall is a data engineer and product marketer with a passion for helping others unlock the power of data and AI. He creates video content that breaks down complex technologies, writes blogs that distill technical concepts, and teaches data practitioners how to build first class data products. With over half a decade of data consulting experience and years working in data products, Josh is now focused on helping others accelerate their AI and data journeys. Whether it’s leveraging machine learning for smarter decision-making or building scalable data pipelines, he’s dedicated to making AI and data accessible, practical, and impactful.

title: HOL for Mastering Snowflake Optimization Techniques for Query Performance Tuning
abstract: Embark on an immersive journey to master Snowflake optimization techniques for enhanced query performance and workload efficiency. This lab covers innovations such as Automatic Clustering, Materialized View, Query Acceleration Service, Search Optimization Service and Query Join Skewness Optimization to achieve optimal performance and minimize costs. In this lab, you’ll explore techniques for monitoring and tuning, optimize queries and workloads for maximum efficiency and savings, apply best practices in real-world scenarios, identify techniques for varied workload patterns and use AI for improved query monitoring. Join us to elevate your query performance and tuning skills.

speakers.bio: Senior Principal Software Engineer and Tech Lead in the Global Technical Support Engineering Team, 23 years of working experience in the Database and Data Cloud including 14 years on a traditional database and 9 years on Snowflake, with both hands-on development and customer-facing experience, seasonal technical skills specific to database schema design, query performance tuning and optimization, virtual warehouse workload tuning/design, database migration, ELT/ETL process, Role-Based Access Control expertise, and across-cloud/across-region data and account replication, etc.

speakers.job_title: Principal Cloud Support Engineer - Technical Lead
speakers.bio: Eric started as a software engineer, both frontend and backend, for about 10 years and landed in the SQL technical support field over the last 9 years, specialising in Hive, Impala, and now Snowflake. At Snowflake, his duties include helping support engineers troubleshoot complicated SQL performance issues, overseeing the APAC and global technical support team, developing tools and processes to improve the efficiencies of the support operations, as well as enhancing stronger relationships between the support team with the Snowflake internal teams and external customers.

title: SnowPro Certification (Wednesday)
abstract: Stand out in the data community by earning your SnowPro Certification on-site at Summit, with flexible scheduling and exam dates offered June 4 and 5. Certifications take approximately two hours.

title: Builders Keynote
abstract: Snowflake invites attendees to experience its latest product innovations firsthand, featuring live demos and in-depth technical sessions led by Snowflake users and experts. Discover how data practitioners are building, deploying and scaling AI applications on the Snowflake AI Data Cloud today.

speakers.bio: Danica began her career as a software engineer and pivoted to developer relations, focussing primarily on open source technologies under the Apache Software Foundation umbrella such as Apache Kafka and Apache Flink. She now leads the open source advocacy efforts at Snowflake, supporting Apache Iceberg and Apache Polaris (incubating). She can be found on X (Bluesky and Mastodon), talking about tech, plants, and baking @TheDanicaFine.

speakers.job_title: Director of Product, Snowflake Intelligence and Agents
speakers.bio: Jeff Hollan is Director of Product for Snowflake's GenAI Agentic platform and Snowflake Intelligence. Prior to joining Snowflake Jeff led the serverless and developer compute businesses for Microsoft Azure. Jeff is a well-known and frequent speaker on building cloud native applications. He has been a keynote speaker and presenter at industry conferences including KubeCon, ServerlessConf, Microsoft Build, and the Linux Foundation API Summit.

Matt Luizzi leads Analytics at WHOOP, driving innovation at the intersection of data, technology, and business strategy. Under his leadership, WHOOP has advanced its analytics capabilities, from optimizing data infrastructure in Snowflake to pioneering AI-driven solutions like an LLM-powered chat interface for real-time insights. Passionate about pushing the boundaries of what’s possible, Matt is committed to keeping WHOOP at the forefront of analytics and data science, shaping the future of how organizations leverage data for smarter decision-making.

Nithya Rangarajan brings over 21 years of experience spanning engineering, product management, and her current role as a Senior Solutions Architect in the Professional Services group at Snowflake. Throughout her career, she has remained dedicated to enterprise information management, from implementing comprehensive Enterprise Data Warehouses to developing custom frameworks for delivering data-centric products. Nithya has been with Snowflake for the past 4 years, where she focuses on helping customers and partners leverage the full potential of the Snowflake platform to drive success.

Yusuf Ozuysal leads engineering at Snowflake Cortex AI, where he applies his extensive expertise in mobile development and artificial intelligence to advance data-driven solutions. After earning his PhD in Electrical Engineering from Stanford University, Yusuf built an impressive career spanning innovation-focused roles. As Head of Mobile at Neeva, he led LLM search initiatives and open-source mobile browser development using SwiftUI and Jetpack Compose. During his nearly decade-long tenure at Google, Yusuf played a key role in launching Chrome for Android, helping grow it from zero to over one billion users. His technical specialties include Swift, Java, Kotlin, machine learning, and optimization. Throughout his career, Yusuf has demonstrated a talent for scaling products and pioneering new technologies that enhance user experiences.

title: Atlan: How Metadata and Governance Take AI from Pilot to Production-Ready
abstract: While nearly 90% of leaders expect AI investments to drive revenue growth in the next three years, as little as 12% of AI pilot projects transition into production. The consequences of this chasm between pilot and production are existential, and enterprises that bridge this gap to become AI-native will have their data team to thank. Join Varun Banka and Prukalpa Sankar, Co-Founders of Atlan, to learn how governance and metadata management are evolving to help leading enterprise data teams to become AI-native, fast-becoming the most critical part of the modern data stack by offering the context and trust needed to finally make AI production-ready.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Prukalpa Sankar is a Co-founder of Atlan, the data and AI governance platform, trusted by visionary data teams at Fox, HubSpot, Medtronic, HelloFresh, and Autodesk. Named a Visionary in the Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ and a Leader in the Forrester Wave™, Atlan helps enterprises manage and govern their data & AI products. Previously, Prukalpa cofounded SocialCops, recognized by the World Economic Forum and the New York Times. She’s a Forbes 30u30, Fortune 40u40, and TED Speaker.

title: Best Practices for Monitoring and Auditing Production Models in Snowflake ML
abstract: Monitoring your models in production is critical for ensuring that models behave as expected and business outcomes are safeguarded when performance degrades. Join this session to learn how to use Snowflake's ML Observability to track key model quality metrics (such as performance or drift) over time, with alerts in place when things go awry. This talk will also show how our native ML lineage feature allows you to quickly understand the entire journey of a model for quick workflow audits and debugging.

speakers.bio: Avinash is a Product Manager for the ML Observability at Snowflake. Avinash has spent 14+ years in the data science space, starting as a data scientist to leading data science teams to building products for data scientists

title: Building an AI-Powered Nielsen Analytics Bot: Snowflake + Teams Integration
abstract: Discover how we integrated Snowflake Cortex AI, Microsoft Teams and AWS to create a Nielsen data analysis bot that revolutionizes market insights accessibility. Using Mistral's LLM, we process natural language queries to generate real-time market share analysis. This serverless solution combines DynamoDB for conversational memory and optimized SQL in Snowflake, enabling business users to obtain critical insights without technical knowledge. We'll present the architecture, the challenges we overcame, and benefits achieved, focusing on data efficiency and accessibility.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Data Science Leader and Analytics Engineering professional with a Master's degree in Analytics and current MBA candidate at EAN University. As Head of Advanced Analytics at Alpina Foods, specializes in transforming complex business challenges into scalable data solutions through innovative technologies. Core expertise in artificial intelligence, large language models, and cloud architecture, focusing on enterprise-scale solutions that democratize data access. Proven track record implementing advanced analytics platforms combining cloud technologies (AWS, Snowflake), machine learning, and natural language processing to deliver actionable business insights.

Electronic Engineer with a Master's degree in Data Science and over six years of experience in data analytics, machine learning, and cloud architecture. Currently works as a Data Scientist and Data Engineer at Alpina, leading AI-driven solutions and data automation processes. Expertise in database management, data pipeline development, and ML model deployment, leveraging AWS (Lambda, API Gateway, DynamoDB) and Snowflake Cortex. Specializes in cloud workload optimization and AI model integration into enterprise tools like Microsoft Teams, enhancing real-time decision-making and automation

title: Building a Scalable AI Platform: Jedify Unlocks Value with Snowflake & Cortex AI
abstract: Discover how Jedify built its AI-driven insights platform as a Snowflake Native App, empowering businesses to unify contextual and noncontextual data for smarter decision-making with AI-powered analytics. This session explores how Jedify leverages Snowflake Cortex to enhance AI capabilities, streamline data processes and simplify machine learning adoption. Learn how partnering with Snowflake helps startups unlock new opportunities, thrive in a rapidly evolving digital landscape, and hear firsthand from Netlify about how this collaboration has shifted its paradigm with specific results and outcomes.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Assaf Henkin is the visionary CEO and Co-Founder of Jedify, where he’s revolutionizing business strategies with AI-powered data insights. A seasoned entrepreneur, Assaf previously built Sproutt Insurance from the ground up, serving as Co-Founder, President, and COO. At Amobee by Singtel, he led innovation as Chief Insights Officer and SVP of Brand Intelligence. As the driving force behind Kontera’s acquisition by Singtel, Assaf’s expertise in scaling transformative tech companies has made him a sought-after leader in the industry. His journey is a testament to his ability to turn bold ideas into market-leading success.

Akia is passionate about developing technical teams that drive data-driven transformations. Her career focuses on maximizing business value and aligning strategies around data ownership, integrity, and lineage. Akia excels at creating clarity in chaos, leveraging emerging technologies, and fostering a data-first culture. She has worked at HubSpot, a B2B automation leader, and led data analytics, engineering, and science teams at Criteo and Dell/EMC. Currently, Akia leads the data and GTM systems organization at Netlify, a company known for building a platform developers love, further driving data-driven innovation.

title: Data and AI as an Equalizing Force: Tackling Global Challenges Head On
abstract: This session explores real-world AI applications tackling global challenges in climate, healthcare and education. We'll discuss the ethical implications of AI deployment, focusing on fairness, transparency and inclusivity. Our goal is to shape a future where AI drives equitable and sustainable progress for all, not just a privileged few. We'll examine how to design and implement AI solutions that create meaningful, widespread benefits. This conversation goes beyond technology, aiming to forge a path where AI becomes a force for universal good and measurable impact.

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)
Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Karen Beaune currently serves as the VP of EMEA Marketing at Snowflake, bringing over two decades of marketing experience and a wealth of expertise to the role. She also leads the End Data Disparity movement as Chair of the Expert Panel. From her early days fuelling regional marketing at Genesys Conferencing, Karen has been dedicated to uncovering people's strengths and fostering a team culture grounded in mutual support and acceptance of differences. Karen is passionate about building teams and developing talents in fast-growing organizations like Polycom and Palo Alto Networks.

Benoit Dageville co-founded Snowflake and currently serves as President of Products. He oversees the company’s technical strategy and leads a team of engineers, product managers, and designers who are making data easily accessible and actionable to everyone. Benoit is a leading expert in parallel execution and self-tuning database systems. Prior to founding Snowflake, Benoit was with Oracle for over 10 years as a lead architect for parallel execution in Oracle RAC and a key architect in the SQL Manageability group. Prior to Oracle, Benoit worked at Bull Information Systems. He helped define the architecture and lead database performance efforts for Bull’s parallel systems. Benoit has a PhD in Computer Science with a focus in Parallel Database Systems from Université de Paris VI and is a named inventor on more than 270 patents.

title: Domo AI Solutions for Retail
abstract: In today’s fast-paced retail landscape, AI-driven insights are essential for staying competitive. Join us to discover how Domo AI leverages Snowflake AI Data Cloud to transform retail operations — optimizing inventory, enhancing customer experiences and driving real-time decision-making. Learn how leading retailers are using Domo AI to turn data into action, improving efficiency and profitability. Don’t miss this opportunity to see AI-powered retail solutions in action!

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Laura Qualey has been with Domo for over a decade, majority of that time has been spent in operations and analytics. In her current role she supports the Chief Revenue Officer in operations, strategy and analytics for Domo's partner ecosystem.

title: Executive Industry Booth Tours - Telecom
abstract: Join an exclusive, guided tour of the Snowflake AI Data Cloud and its premier partner ecosystem, tailored for leaders in Telecom. This immersive experience will take you through five curated stops that showcase the art of the possible—real-world solutions, cutting-edge AI use cases, and data-driven innovations transforming your industry today. Gain direct access to experts, see industry-leading technology in action, and leave with actionable inspiration to accelerate your data strategy. Space is extremely limited—registration is first come, first served. Please note that this experience includes walking between stops; comfortable footwear is recommended. Please note that the Executive Booth Tours are reserved for customers only—we kindly ask our partners not to register.

speakers.bio: Jon Penrose is the Telecommunication Industry Principal for EMEA at Snowflake, where he helps CSPs and NEPs develop data and cloud strategies, define use cases, and create frameworks for sustainable competitive advantage. Prior to Snowflake, Jon was a Data and Analytics Strategist at AWS, a Strategic Consultant at Ab Initio Software, and led Teradata’s Telecommunications Consulting Practice, focusing on advanced analytics to drive business value through enhanced network and customer intelligence for telco clients.

speakers.job_title: Principal Architect, Global Telco, Applied Field Engineering Office

title: GROUP BY APJ
abstract: Join us at the APJ (India, Asia-Pacific, Australia & New Zealand) meetup, where attendees from across the region gather to build new connections. Come by to meet local community leaders, share ideas and expand your professional network!

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: I’m a Data in The New evangelist and technology leader helping enterprises unlock the full potential of their data with Snowflake. As a certified Snowflake Data Superhero, I architect and scale future-ready, multi-cloud platforms, combining deep business insight with hands-on expertise in enterprise data strategy. With a strong focus on innovation, I work at the intersection of data and AI—leveraging Snowflake Cortex and GenAI capabilities to power next-gen applications, intelligent automation, and hyper-personalized experiences. A speaker, author, and patent holder, I’m passionate about shaping the future of data by enabling people, platforms, and ideas to thrive.

Results-driven marketing professional with 7+ years of experience leading end-to-end digital marketing programs across brand awareness, demand generation, sales pipeline acceleration, field marketing and developer community engagement. Proven ability to take ownership, set priorities and execute strategies that deliver measurable impact. Adept at cross-functional and cross-regional collaboration, with a strong focus on stakeholder alignment and team leadership. Known for being inquisitive, proactive, detail-oriented and continuously focused on learning and adding value in fast-paced environments.

Sandip Pani is a seasoned data architect and community leader based in Bengaluru, India, with nearly two decades of hands-on experience across diverse data platforms. He is widely recognized for his deep expertise in data architecture and analytics, as well as his active leadership within the global Snowflake community. As a Snowflake Data Superhero and the Bangalore Chapter Leader, Sandip is passionate about enabling data-driven transformation and fostering innovation. He actively contributes to the Snowflake forums, helping users around the world solve complex data challenges and stay ahead in the fast-evolving data landscape.

As Head of Snowflake Tech Consulting at LTIMindtree, I focus on helping enterprises maximize the value of Snowflake’s AI Data Cloud. With 21+ years of experience in data architecture, AI, and cloud data platforms, I specialize in delivering transformative digital strategies tailored to complex business challenges. My core strengths lie in Cloud Data Modernization, Architecture Consulting, Cloud Data Engineering, and Data Governance—enabling secure, scalable, and high-performance data ecosystems. I’m passionate about driving data-centric cultures, helping organizations modernize their platforms and unlock advanced analytics.

title: Hands-on Lab: Data Discovery, Sharing and Management Using Internal Marketplace
abstract: Join this lab to gain hands-on experience with sharing, discovering and managing data products on the Snowflake Internal Marketplace. You will create data provider profiles to define different teams, publish data products as organizational listings with rich meta data, and configure discoverability and accessibility. You will also exercise request-and-approval workflows, as well as the governance and consumption of data products within and across Snowflake accounts.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Matthias is a member of the Snowflake Field CTO Office and serves as a trusted advisor to Snowflake clients and partners worldwide. He has more than 25 years of experience in the design, engineering, and deployment of database and analytics technology. Matthias focuses on Enterprise Data Architecture, Data Collaboration, Data Governance, and he leads the Data Mesh field strategy at Snowflake. Prior to joining Snowflake Matthias worked for 21 years at IBM in the US and across Europe.

Active since 2001 as a data expert, focusing on delivering value through Data and Analytics solutions. Worked in roles in sales, development, implementation, and support, culminating in my position as a Principal Data Cloud Architect at Snowflake. Primary focus is to help businesses transform their data strategies into actionable insights, guiding organizations through the process of crafting strategic data roadmaps and architecting frameworks for data collaboration, ensuring seamless information flow and utilization in a governed manner. Industry experience spans Retail/Wholesale Banking, Insurance, Oil & Gas, Utilities, Energy, Public Sector, Telecommunication, and IT.

title: Hands-On Workshop: Enterprise Data Modeling for Snowflake Success
abstract: In today’s data-driven landscape, organizations must ensure their Snowflake environments are structured for efficiency, scalability and governance. This hands-on workshop will guide enterprise teams through best practices for data modeling within Snowflake, leveraging SqlDBM’s powerful platform to streamline collaboration, enforce modeling standards and accelerate cloud data initiatives. Learn practical experience in designing and managing enterprise-grade data models that align with Snowflake’s best practices, ensuring consistency, performance and maintainability across their data ecosystem.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Hazal Sener is an Enterprise Data Modeling Expert at SqlDBM with a background in Business Intelligence and Data Warehousing. Her experience in building data transformation pipelines and optimizing SQL queries led her to specialize in data modeling, helping organizations design efficient and scalable data architectures. At SqlDBM, Hazal helps teams make the most of the platform. She works closely with organizations to refine their data modeling strategies, streamline workflows, and simplify complex processes. Through hands-on guidance and best practices, she helps teams integrate data modeling seamlessly into their workflows, ensuring scalability and long-term success.

Eric Ruiz is a Sales Engineer at SqlDBM and has previous experience in Data Engineering, Data Modeling, Data Cataloging and Data Integration. He has a proven track record of helping organizations with everything data and best practices. As a Sales Engineer, Eric collaborates with organizations to refine their data modeling strategies and data modeling frameworks. He is deeply passionate about Data Modeling, promoting Data Governance, and fostering effective Data Practices.

title: How Capital One Uses Apache Iceberg™ to Optimize Loading and Storage Costs
abstract: Open table formats empower companies to bring compute to their data while maintaining a single copy of data. At Capital One, we use Iceberg Tables to optimize ingestion and storage costs in Snowflake without compromising data integrity or performance. This enables us to expand how we leverage Snowflake's capabilities for diverse workloads. In this session, we'll share how Iceberg helps us democratize compute choices along with key learnings from migrating datasets from native Snowflake tables.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Temesgen (Teme) Meheret is a Director of Software Engineering at Capital One in the Enteprise Data Storage and Consumption Team. In his role, Teme leads the engineering team responsible for optimal usage and risk management of Snowflake. Prior to Capital One, Teme served as the VP of Engineering for the Data & Intelligence group at Amobee/Videology. His interests include the convergence and consolidation of large-scale data and software platforms using proven architectural patterns and methodologies.

title: How Data Governance Unlocks Scale, Efficiency and Trust at Zscaler
abstract: Managing data at scale and efficiency requires a delicate balance of governance and collaboration. Learn how Zscaler’s enterprise data team built its governance framework using dbt Cloud and Snowflake to automate best practices, optimize SQL, and improve data quality across thousands of dbt models and hundreds of business dashboards. Discover how CI/CD processes, lineage tracking and dashboard health scoring can enhance trust and empower teams to develop and govern models faster.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Ra serves as the Head of Advanced Data Engineering and Analytics at Zscaler, leading the architecture, strategy, and execution of the Enterprise Data Platform. With a focus on scalability, security, and business alignment, he drives the development of robust data pipelines, transformation processes, and seamless integration of diverse data sources to empower innovation across the organization. As a forward thinking leader, he also champions the operationalization of machine learning workflows and the adoption of Generative AI to unlock advanced analytics, automate processes, and drive strategic impact

title: How to Build a Commerce and Retail Media Network on Snowflake
abstract: Traditionally, commerce and retail media networks are built by sending first parties to external tools for activation and measurement, but this results in long build cycles, limited flexibility and vendor lock-in. Instead, bring your media directly to your data in Snowflake to build faster, be more agile and maintain full data ownership. Join Hightouch, The Trade Desk, and Snowflake and build an end-to-end offsite media network solution to curate, syndicate and measure first-party campaigns for both managed service and self-service operations.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Having worked for The Trade Desk for the past 6 years, I have technically managed accounts across inventory partnerships, data partnerships and client integrations. My main focus in my current role is entirely on retail integrations while managing a small team of retail focused Technical Account Mangers!

Ian leads AdTech products and go-to-market initiatives at Hightouch. He works with advertisers, agencies, and internal teams to ensure that every Hightouch customer makes the most of their first-party and third-party data strategies. Ian has spent ten years in paid media as a practitioner for growing ecommerce brands, a B2B growth leader, and a product manager and GM for AdTech products. His expertise includes the use of first-party audiences, third-party digital identity, data onboarding, conversion APIs, AI signals, attribution, incrementality testing, and data clean rooms.

title: How to Get >90% Text-SQL Accuracy with Cortex Analyst Semantic Models
abstract: Unlock the power of your structured data with Cortex Analyst, Snowflake’s AI-driven text-to-SQL feature. This session will demonstrate how enterprises can set up semantic models to query databases using natural language with >90% accuracy. We'll start with what it takes to set up Analyst, and end with a demo of Analyst running on a publicly available dataset.

speakers.bio: Abhinav Vadrevu is a seasoned product manager with expertise in AI and data infrastructure. As the product manager for Snowflake's Cortex Analyst, he focuses on creating AI-driven solutions for enterprises to get insights from their data. With a strong track record working on GitHub Copilot and Grammarly AI, Abhinav has launched enterprise AI features and scalable products, driving significant user adoption. In this session, he will share how to achieve over 90% accuracy in text-to-SQL conversions using Semantic Models, with a live demo showcasing practical applications.

Okhtay Azarmanesh is a Principal AI Architect at Snowflake, contributing to the forefront of AI development with a focus on conversational assistants. His career highlights include previous roles as Staff ML Engineer at Lacework and Senior Machine Learning Engineer at Kinship, where he focused on machine learning platform development and pet health insights. At Lyra Health, he applied his data science skills to improve mental healthcare access. He holds a PhD from Penn State University in electrical engineering, where he also worked as a Research Associate and Instructor.

title: Inside the Open Source Snowflake AI Toolkit: AI Innovation in the Data Cloud
abstract: Bringing AI innovation to the data cloud, the Snowflake AI Toolkit is a game-changing, open source accelerator designed to fast-track AI adoption in the Snowflake AI Data Cloud. Built on Snowflake, this tool kit empowers developers to explore Snowflake Cortex AI, prototype AI-driven applications and build advanced AI pipelines such as RAG and fine-tuning — all within Snowflake! The tool kit runs as a Snowflake Native App and integrates with TruLens and the new Cortex Agents. Join us for a look at how we built it, the challenges we tackled and how to use it to supercharge your AI workflow. Get ready for live demos, real-world use cases and a glimpse into the future of AI on Snowflake!

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Shankar Narayanan SGS is a Principal Architect at Microsoft leading up the forefront of innovation between Microsoft and Snowflake. He is a Snowflake Data Superhero and the co-author of The Ultimate Guide to Snowpark", a definitive resource on building scalable AI and data applications using Snowpark. He is also the co-creator of the Snowflake AI Toolkit.

title: Leading the Change: CDAO's Journey with Snowflake for AI Synergy at a Large Bank
abstract: Moving from fragmented data landscapes to a state of true AI synergy is a significant undertaking for any large bank. In this candid panel discussion, the Chief Data and AI Officer of a leading financial institution will share practical insights and lessons learned from their journey leveraging Snowflake. Explore the key implementation strategies, best practices and technological considerations involved in building a unified data foundation that empowers AI initiatives across various business units.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.job_title: Chief Data, AI and Analytics Officer
speakers.bio: Tom Mazzaferro is the Chief Data, AI and Analytics Officer for Truist Financial Corporation. Tom has been named the Top 100 Most Influential Leaders in Data for the last 3 years and named Top 10 leaders in AI and Big Data by CIO Business World. Tom has also received the fintech of the year award in payments, AI and Fraud for both 2020 and 2021 and recently was name Top CDAO to watch out for in 2024. Previously, Tom held multiple roles at HSBC, most recently as U.S. Chief Data Officer, and multiple executive roles across risk, technology, data, and finance at JPMorgan Chase & Co. Mazzaferro earned an MBA from The Ohio State University and his undergraduate degree at Villanova University.

speakers.job_title: Sr. Vice President, Data, Analytics & AI
speakers.bio: Naveen Sharma is a Sr. Vice President of Cognizant’s AI & Analytics business. In this role, he is responsible for leading the growth of Cognizant’s Data, AI, and Analytics portfolio through thought leadership, innovation, pre-sales, offering development, and portfolio management. As the go-to-market leader for Cognizant's AI offerings, he was recognized as one of the Top 25 leaders in AI by The Consulting Report in 2024 Naveen oversees our 35,000+ associates in the AIA practice, collaborates with clients, partners, and industry analysts to advance client and Cognizant objectives.

title: Lunch (W)
abstract: Lunch (W)

title: Multi-Tenant SaaS on Snowflake: Lower Costs, Launch Faster, Add Revenue
abstract: Managing a multi-tenant SaaS with a complex data stack (lake, warehouse, online transaction processing) and struggling with high cost of goods sold (COGS)? Explore how SaaS providers are consolidating hundreds of instances onto a single Snowflake platform to unlock new revenue within the Snowflake Data Cloud. Join this session by the Snowflake Enterprise Architecture Office for an in-depth look at the architecture and features behind this transformation

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Amit, at Snowflake Data Architecture Office, is focused on improving global data accessibility within and across organizations. He has developed designs to activate data products located in open and proprietary engines, across region, across cloud providers, and deliver tangible ROI for Fortune 500 companies.

title: Roche's Journey with Snowflake: The Successes, Challenges and Lessons Learned
abstract: Join this session to explore how Roche is tackling the pharmaceutical industry's challenge of diminishing R&D returns by adopting a Data Mesh operating model. Discover how cross-domain collaboration, a reuse mindset and Snowflake-powered self-serve data infrastructure are driving a data-driven future in big pharma, with a focus on delivering value to patients.

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)
Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Meet Pierre Fischer—a dynamic Data and Analytics maestro with an intriguing journey from high-energy physics to semiconductors, and now, life sciences! With his adept skills in digital transformation and IT strategies, he's been making quantum waves at Roche since 2013. Pierre's career started with a PhD in High Energy Physics from Université Louis Pasteur, dabling in Monte Carlo simulations. He then engineered software for electron microscope data analysis in the semiconductor industry. Now at Roche, he leads Data Platforms with a passion. Fluent in French and English (and some comical German), he's also into Quantum Computing. When he’s not deep in data, Pierre mentors future tech wizards!

title: Streamlining SCD: Building Cost-Effective Data Pipelines with Dynamic Tables
abstract: Discover how Snowflake Dynamic Tables revolutionize the implementation of slowly changing dimensions (SCDs). Learn to create automated, self-maintaining data pipelines that eliminate complex ETL processes and reduce maintenance overhead. This session demonstrates practical examples of building Type 1 and 2 SCDs using Dynamic Tables, showcasing their cost-efficiency and performance benefits while ensuring data accuracy and historical tracking.

speakers.bio: Senior Software Engineer with a long-lasting passion on databases, distributed systems, storage and performance. Working on Snowflake Dynamic Tables with a strong focus on anything performance-related.

title: The Exec AI Playbook: Strategy, Innovation and Impact for Enterprises
abstract: Enterprise AI success requires cross-functional collaboration. In this fireside chat, senior executives from technology, data and innovation will explore how AI is being embedded into enterprise strategies, optimizing costs and ensuring governance. Gain insights into the evolving role of AI in the C-suite, the challenges of adoption, and how leadership teams can future-proof their organizations for AI-driven transformation.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.job_title: AI Innovation, Head of Hub US

speakers.bio: Baris is Snowflake’s Head of AI where he is responsible for leading AI and machine learning initiatives, and driving Snowflake’s overarching AI product roadmap and strategy. He is passionate about working on cutting-edge AI and leveraging the technology to solve real world problems for customers. Baris joined Snowflake following its acquisition of nxyz in August 2023, where he served as the co-founder and CEO. Previously, Baris spent over a decade at Google across various product leadership roles, with a focus on developing consumer AI products. Baris holds a M.S. in Electrical Engineering from Cornell University and an MBA from Stanford.

title: The Invisible Work: How Reducing Friction Transformed HubSpot’s Data Practice
abstract: Behind every dashboard, there’s a tangle of duplicated work, siloed tools and missed insights. In this session, HubSpot shares how their data team tackled the invisible work slowing them down, not by adding more tools, but by rethinking how their stack supported collaboration, exploration and trust. Learn how they streamlined workflows, empowered analysts of all skill levels, and made analytics a shared capability across the organization.

speakers.job_title: Sr. Mgr, Head of Product & Partner Analytics Engineering

title: SQL Optimization Powered by Generative AI for Smarter and Faster Insights

This session explores how generative AI can revolutionize SQL optimization by automating query enhancements and recommending improvements with perfect accuracy. We’ll feature a native application for Snowflake, developed using Snowpark Container Services, Streamlit and gen AI, capable of optimizing thousands of queries in minutes and identifying dynamic inefficiencies such as resource contention, overprovisioning and incorrect clustering for cost and performance.
