Quick access to Snowflake platform and ecosystem information is essential, but filtering relevant content from knowledge articles, product docs, Jiras and support cases can be challenging. Snowflake Cortex Search simplifies this by enabling efficient content discovery when data is in Snowflake. This session explores how Snowflake Support leverages Cortex Search for content retrieval and RAG in B2B customer service.

speakers.bio: Adam Warrington is Senior Director of Customer Experience Engineering at Snowflake, focused on building applications that enable Snowflake to more effectively support its customers and enable their success with the product. Over the last few years Adam has focused heavily on building AI solutions, improving Snowflake’s ability to more quickly find the root cause of customer reported issues, and enabling Snowflake customers to more quickly self-answer their questions. Prior to joining Snowflake Adam spent a decade focused on building support systems that spanned from case management systems, advanced telemetry systems, proactive support capabilities, and intelligent search platforms.

Rohan Gupta is committed to transforming support for Snowflake customers and internal support engineers. He specializes in developing AI powered solutions that enhance Snowflake’s ability to rapidly identify the root cause of customer-reported issues while empowering customers to find answers independently through self-service resources.

title: What's New: Eliminate Silos with Snowflake Horizon Catalog & Internal Marketplace
abstract: Learn how to drive collaboration acorss your buisness ecosytem with the latest from Snowflake Horizon Catalog, including External Data Discovery, to effortlessly find data both within Snowflake and from external systems. See demos of the latest Internal Marketplace updates, including Organization Profiles and Request for Access, to streamline and accelerate the sharing of data, apps, and AI. Finally, learn how Iceberg Data Sharing and the Egress Cost Optimizer can increase interoperability and data availability while keeping your data sharing costs down.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Sanjay is a Principal Product Manager at Snowflake, overseeing Snowflake’s collaboration and Snowflake Marketplace platform. Sanjay has been at Snowflake since 2022 and is focused on providing a simple, scalable, and easy-to-use data-sharing and collaboration platform that enables the democratization of access to data, data services, and applications across the Data Cloud. Before Snowflake, Sanjay built new products and led teams at large organizations, including AWS and Conviva. He has also served as an executive at venture-backed startups as the VP of Products.

Riccardo Muti is a Principal Product Manager at Snowflake focusing on search & discovery capabilities in Horizon Catalog and Snowflake Marketplace. He has over 15 years’ experience building business intelligence and database platforms. Prior to Snowflake, he held product management roles at Google working on Looker Studio and BigQuery, and at Microsoft working on Power BI and SQL Server Reporting Services.

title: What's New: Managing Costs and Optimizing Performance in Snowflake
abstract: Learn how to better oversee, control and optimize the cost and performance of your Snowflake workloads through capabilities like Cost-Based Anomaly Detection, Tag-based Budgets and Performance Explorer. Join us for new product and feature overviews, best practices, and live demos

speakers.bio: Stephen Yigit-Elliott leads product management for Snowflake data warehouse workload optimization. His career has spanned various cloud and underwater platforms. Prior to joining Snowflake, Stephen was a product leader at AWS and GCP, and a nuclear submarine officer in the US Navy.

title: What's New: Why You Should Migrate Your ML pipelines to Snowflake ML
abstract: Learn how the latest capabilities in Snowflake ML accelerate and simplify the dev to prod lifecycle. Join us for new feature overviews and a live demo showcasing our latest capabilities, including Data Science Agent.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Pavan Pothukuchi is the Director of Product Management for AI at Snowflake. His teams are responsible for Snowflake ML, which is a set of capabilities that enable ML practitioners to productionize machine learning workflows end-to-end inside Snowflake. He is in addition responsible for Document AI, which is an AI product that enables business users to extract information from unstructured documents and automate business processes, and AI observability - which enables developers to build safe and trusted AI applications.

Mona Attariyan, Director of Machine Learning at Snowflake, is a visionary leader in Cloud, Data Analytics, and ML. At Redfin, she led ML Engineering, Data Engineering, and Analytics during the pandemic, driving innovation and resilience. Previously at Google, she founded the Cloud AI Natural Language team, advanced ML on unconventional hardware, and launched Project BigGene for large-scale genome analysis. Her early work included face recognition and VM management for GCE. Mona’s career reflects her passion for solving complex problems and her commitment to innovation in machine learning and data.

title: Build an LLM-Powered Geospatial Route Optimizer as a Snowflake Native App
abstract: Join this session to explore the key concept and learn how to build a simulated LLM-powered route optimization app using Streamlit and Carto’s Overture maps for delivery planning. The app leverages Cortex LLMs to filter maps, generate customer samples and optimize routes. The process is documented in a Lab notebook, resulting in a dynamic visualization within Streamlit in Snowflake. The backend integrates SPCS and a Docker image of an open-source route optimization service to generate routes from real data. The app will be published as a Snowflake Native App.

speakers.bio: After working in 17 years as a Sales Engineer in a variety of companies including Snowflake, Becky has taken the step to join the Solution Innovation team, where she aims to bring visions of the future to life with Snowflake. Her Special interest is end to end advanced analytics.

title: Continuous Quality: Test-Driven Development in Snowflake
abstract: A strong testing framework is essential for ensuring stability and efficiency in software development, and databases are no exception. At Tenable, we've developed a Snowflake database testing framework that seamlessly integrates with our CI/CD pipeline. This automated, reliable solution has been proven to enhance the robustness of our database testing, ensuring continuous quality and faster delivery.

speakers.bio: Angela is a Senior Database Engineer at Tenable, specializing in Snowflake database development, performance optimization, and data quality to drive efficiency. With 19 years in the database industry, she thrives on the constant challenges the field offers and values the opportunities to continuously learn and expand her skill set.
Director of Engineering at Tenable building and supporting a data platform for our customers on Snowflake

title: Don’t Just Build Models — Operationalize Them
abstract: In this session, learn more about Snowflake’s end-to-end ML platform and how it supports creating production-scale deployments and operationalization of your models. We will dive into MLOps essentials and Snowflake’s capabilities for model management and model serving. You can easily bring models trained externally, or train them at scale using Snowflake ML, and then securely and scalably deploy them for inference with our flexible serving environments. At the end, we’ll bring this to life with a real-life scenario of managing production models, running continuous evaluations to train and deploy champion-challenger models.

speakers.bio: Pradeep leads the ML Ops and Model Serving teams in Snowflake. Previously, Pradeep has worked in Meta where he led distributed training infrastructure using PyTorch and before that, he designed both enterprise and consumer products in start ups.

title: Empowering Field Service Operations with Agentic AI on Snowflake
abstract: Today, field service operations often face challenges stemming from disconnected data, limited real-time information, and difficulty accessing the right expertise at the point of service. Technicians may struggle to diagnose complex issues, leading to repeat visits, extended downtime and frustrated customers. This session will delve into how we can move beyond these limitations by creating a truly "connected technician" experience, leveraging the power of agentic AI within the unified data environment of Snowflake.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Technology leader with 22+ years of industry experience, blending thought leadership with hands-on expertise in Agentic AI, Generative AI, AI & Analytics, Data Engineering, Data Platform Modernization and Digital Transformation. Known for architecting and deploying innovative solutions for global organizations and effectively communicating strategic roadmaps to drive business outcomes.

A seasoned technology leader with over two decades of industry experience, blending thought leadership, technical mastery, and practical expertise in architecting, designing, building, and deploying cutting-edge Agentic AI, Generative AI, AI & Analytics, Data Engineering, and Cloud solutions for global clients across diverse sectors. Collaborates with executive leadership, business heads, and IT professionals to assess organizational landscapes, develop strategic roadmaps for AI, Analytics, Generative AI, Data, and Cloud initiatives, driving business outcomes and growth.

title: How Booking.com Is Architecting Scalable Python Frameworks for the AI Data Cloud
abstract: Discover how the Booking.com Data DevEx team scales data platforms with Snowflake by integrating PySpark, Iceberg, and an S3‐based architecture. Leveraging an in‐house PySpark SDK, we process hundreds of terabytes daily while synchronizing catalogs, optimizing S3 reads, and ensuring high data quality and performance. Gain practical insights into overcoming technical challenges in large‐scale platform development, migration, and operational excellence, with actionable insights.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Dimitar Nedev is a Principal Data Engineer at Booking.com with over a decade of experience designing and implementing scalable, (cloud-based) enterprise data solutions. His work encompasses architecting robust data platforms, developing scalable data pipelines, applying advanced data modeling techniques, and deploying practical machine learning solutions—grounded in both industry practice and academic research. Beyond his role at Booking.com, he has contributed to major projects across e-commerce, logistics, marketing attribution and cloud infrastructure.

Dylan Goldsborough is a Software Engineer at Booking.com, where he is a contributor to the big data platform. He develops intelligent data access layers that seamlessly handle compute and storage contexts, enabling users to focus on insights rather than infrastructure. Prior to working on data warehouses at Booking.com, he spent several years in e-commerce automating and optimizing the performance of physical warehouses.

title: How Boston Children's Hospital Unlocks Sensitive Data's Power with Governance
abstract: Boston Children’s Hospital is improving children’s health worldwide by unlocking the full potential of data. With diverse and sensitive healthcare data, strong data governance is essential for ensuring quality, compliance and accessibility. This session explores BCH’s governance strategies, supported by Snowflake and Alation, that drive data trust, adoption and usability — laying the foundation for analytics, AI and better patient outcomes.

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)

speakers.bio: Oliver brings over 20 years of data & technology experience to his clients. He has spent his career deriving value from data, masterminding strategic programs across multiple industries that delivered new data insights and capabilities, and addressed important business challenges. A key differentiator is his focus on people, pushing the development of client teams to improve their skills and to grow through collaboration. ​Oliver has wide ranging experience in creating Data Governance programs across a range of industries including Financial Service, Telecoms, Public Sector and Healthcare.

speakers.job_title: Sr. Director, Enterprise Data, Analytics & Reporting
speakers.bio: Jared Hawkins is the Senior Director of Enterprise Data, Analytics & Reporting at Boston Children’s Hospital and a faculty member at Harvard Medical School. A proven leader in both industry and academia, he has led technology, product, and data teams across multiple startups. Now leading data at BCH, he drives enterprise data governance, analytics, and reporting to advance operational efficiency, research, and patient care while shaping the hospital’s future in data and AI.

title: How Invesco Optimizes Snowflake Spend for Major Savings
abstract: Invesco, a major asset management firm, is witnessing substantial growth in Snowflake usage, which is driving significant business value — but at one point, this also led to unhealthy consumption levels. Learn how Invesco collaborated with Snowflake to implement various optimizations, including warehouse consolidation, warehouse right-sizing and best practices for Automatic Clustering and Materialized View. These measures have not only resulted in substantial savings but also led to avoiding significant future costs.

speakers.job_title: Global Head of Enterprise Data and Analytics
speakers.bio: A seasoned data executive in the financial services sector, Raghu brings extensive expertise building and operating enterprise ready data platforms and patterns, operationalizing data capabilities, and strategy execution of large-scale data modernization programs. Passionate about harnessing the power of data, Raghu uses innovation and collaboration to drive strategic decision-making and operational alpha. Raghu holds an MBA in Finance from LSU, and Masters in Computer Science from University of Louisiana.

Mark is highly motivated and skilled IT professional with broad industry experience operating on a global scale with over 25 years of experience. He leads the Data Platform Team at Invesco whose focus is building out and enabling data platform capabilities in the cloud. Mark specializes in leadership, management, data strategy, data architecture ,solution architecture, design and data delivery. He enjoys the challenges faced with the ever-evolving technology landscape.

title: How Podium Conducts Qualitative Churn Analysis with Cortex LLMs
abstract: Discover how Podium harnesses Snowflake Cortex LLMs to automate qualitative churn analysis at scale. Learn how the company transforms raw customer interactions into actionable insights — uncovering pain points and churn drivers with ease. This session explores how Cortex LLMs simplify text analysis, making it a cost-effective, seamless solution for extracting deep customer intelligence. Don’t just track churn — understand it.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Collin manages the Analytics Engineering team at Podium - a SaaS company enabling local businesses to get more leads and make more money. He has ten years of analytics experience comprised of several years in the Litigation Consulting industry focusing on difference-in-difference regression models and consumer fraud cases as well as five years architecting data model structures and migrating to dbt and Snowflake at Podium. With an academic background in Economics, Collin enjoys exploring causal relationships in analytics as well as the more mechanical side of working with data and streamlining business tasks.

Nancy Linford Gallo leads a team of analysts and data scientists at Podium, a SaaS company helping local businesses grow. Her team focuses on internal analytics for Post-Sales operations and leverages Snowflake’s LLM tooling to gain insights from customer interactions. They also develop custom reports for enterprise clients. Previously, she worked in economic consulting, analyzing litigation cases across finance, retail, and healthcare. Nancy holds a Bachelor's in Economics and Art History from Brigham Young University. In her free time, she enjoys painting and spending time with her husband and two children.

title: Meet the PostGres Team
abstract: Join us at the Group By () Lounge for an exclusive, informal meetup with the Postgres Team! Whether you’re a longtime PostgreSQL user or just getting started, this is your chance to: • Meet the engineers and advocates behind Postgres • Ask questions, share feedback, and learn about what’s next • Connect with fellow data enthusiasts and open-source contributors No slides. No stage. Just good conversations, insights, and community vibes. Grab your lunch and pull up a chair—we’ll see you there!

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

title: Powering Tomorrow's Customer Experiences with Cortex Agents
abstract: The web is on the verge of a major shift. Agentic applications will redefine how customers engage with digital experiences — delivering highly personalized, relevant interactions. In this talk, Snowplow CTO Yali Sassoon explores how Snowplow Signals enables agents to perceive users through short- and long-term memory, and how Snowflake Cortex Agents can help leverage this long-term memory using Snowflake's connected data platform.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Yali Sassoon is Co-founder and Chief Technology Officer at Snowplow Analytics. Yali is principally focused on empowering data teams to meet the challenges they face around data delivery, meaning, quality and governance, with a particular focus on AI applications and use cases.

title: Shifting Gears: How Alation Accelerated O'Reilly Auto Parts' Snowflake Journey
abstract: Discover how O'Reilly Auto Parts, a leading automotive aftermarket retailer, uses Alation's data intelligence platform to enhance trust in their Snowflake data and reduce cost. This session will explore O'Reilly's use of Alation to catalog critical data assets, view end-to-end lineage, and implement strong governance policies. By leveraging metadata to certify data assets, O'Reilly fostered trust in their data, enabling faster and more confident decisions. Alation is also helping O'Reilly reduce cloud cost by cataloging the most efficient queries and reducing API calls to systems that are billed on consumption. Learn how your organization can achieve similar results.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Dwayne Foresee is a results-oriented IT leader with 30 years of management experience and a career spanning 40 years in Information Technology. He has a history of building and leading high-performing teams to implement complex data solutions, including data governance programs, MDM solutions, and large-scale data warehouses. Dwayne's experience includes overseeing the successful implementation of Alation Data Catalog at O'Reilly Auto Parts that is directly supporting their strategic migration to Snowflake. His insights into leveraging data governance to facilitate cloud data warehousing transitions are particularly relevant to today's data-driven organizations.

title: Snowflake on Snowflake: AI-Powered Recommendations for B2B Marketing with Kumo
abstract: AI is revolutionizing how companies approach marketing. However, most of the attention is focused on how AI can be used for text and image generation - ignoring the tremendous value that exists in structured data. In this session we will share learnings about how Snowflake has harnessed the power of AI and Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to develop targeted marketing campaigns to recommend new features to our customers using Kumo’s Snowflake Native App.

speakers.bio: Matt leads Snowflake's GTM Modeling and Experimentation team responsible for all Marketing and Sales related AI/ML models at Snowflake. He has over ten years of experience using analytics to support B2B sales and marketing teams. He holds a Master’s in Data Science from Northwestern University and a Bachelor’s of Science in Business Administration from Bryant University.

title: Sony Interactive Entertainment Accelerates Cloud Gaming with Snowflake
abstract: Cloud gaming revolutionizes gaming by hosting and rendering games on remote cloud servers, eliminating the need for downloads or high-end hardware. Players stream games across various devices, relying on stable internet connections. A key challenge is optimizing the state of streaming, encompassing factors like traffic, error rates and server performance. PlayStation leverages Snowflake as its centralized data warehouse. We also leverage Snowpipe, views and stored procedures to ingest and process near real-time streaming data. Snowflake’s robust architecture enables data-driven insights, improving player experience and highlighting cloud gaming’s growing role in the industry.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Eric Ji is a Sr Business Analyst at Sony Interactive Entertainment, bringing 10+ years of expertise in big data, data engineering and analytics. With a deep understanding of Snowflake’s Data Cloud, he has involved in building a high-performance data solutions for cloud gaming for Sony Interactive Entertainment. Based on the most recent information, Eric will share the streamming data flow solution at Sony Interactive Entertainment and also the insights on data analysis with the newly lauched game streamming device.

title: Toyota Motor Europe's Petabyte-Scale Connected Car Journey
abstract: Discover how Toyota Motor Europe revolutionized their connected car data management using Snowflake. This session reveals how TME successfully integrated petabyte-scale Apache Iceberg datasets from AWS with Snowflake, while maintaining stringent European regulatory compliance. Learn practical insights on architecting a modern data stack that combines AWS Glue & Snowflake to break down data silos, enhance vehicle quality, and elevate customer service through secure cross-regional collaboration.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Tomer Steinberg is a Principal Solutions Engineer at Snowflake with experience in the data industry since 2005. He partners with automotive and manufacturing organizations to unlock the value of their data—bringing together information across the enterprise and its ecosystem. Tomer helps design smart, trusted, and governed data and AI solutions using scalable architectures to accelerate productivity and innovation.

Jeroen Wijnants is an experienced consultant providing contractual services to Toyota Motor Europe with a strong focus on data platforms and reporting solutions. Over the last two years, he has shifted his focus to working with Snowflake, leveraging its advanced capabilities to enhance data management and analytics. Jeroen's expertise in modern data warehousing solutions, combined with his extensive background in traditional business intelligence, makes him a valuable asset in driving data-driven decision-making and fostering innovation.

title: Transforming Southern California Edison's EDW: Hana/Hadoop to Snowflake
abstract: Join this session to learn about the Hana/Hadoop to Snowflake (H2S) program, which is modernizing Southern California Edison's (SCE) enterprise data warehouse (EDW) infrastructure by migrating from on-premises SAP HANA and Hadoop systems to Snowflake. Discover how this transformation is driving significant performance improvements, cost savings and enhanced agility to better align with business needs. The team at SCE will share insights about the multiyear program, which involves migrating 44 applications and over 200 terabytes of production data, and how this shift is shaping SCE’s future in the cloud.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.job_title: Senior Advisor - Information and Integration Architecture
speakers.bio: Rajesh Dorairaj is focused, and delivery-oriented technical professional with 20+ years of experience in information technologies focusing on Development and Architecture (Application, Data Architecture) with a proven track record of transforming IT to leverage technologies and tools to achieve strategic and business objectives. Experience in architecting, solutioning and hands-on development to achieve cost efficient, result oriented applications. Successfully lead and architected solution for simple to complex programs, inclusive of software development, process, and methodology. Has proven experience in latest architect frameworks, tools, standards and patterns.

Sundeep Dakarapu is a Solution Architect in Data & Analytics specialized in GRID Modernization. With expertise in building data solutions on cloud platforms, he plays a pivotal role in transforming utility operations. His responsibilities include designing and implementing scalable, secure, and resilient IT architectures tailored to modern utility needs. He leverages cutting-edge cloud technologies to optimize data integration, real-time analytics, and automation for improved grid performance. By combining his technical acumen with deep utility domain knowledge, Sundeep also ensures that solutions comply with strategic business goals.

title: What's New: Advanced Analytics with Cortex AISQL, Native Semantic Views, and More
abstract: Master AI-powered analytics in Snowflake. Learn how to leverage Snowflake's powerful AI SQL functions for advanced analytics on unstructured, time-series, and geospatial data. We'll also demonstrate how to use Semantic Views to bridge the gap between data and business understanding.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Josh Klahr is the Head of Analytics Product Management at Snowflake, where he is responsible for driving the core database product roadmap and strategy. Josh has over 25 years of experience working with data and analytics. Prior to joining Snowflake, Josh was VP of product management for Splunk, where he was responsible for the Splunk Enterprise and Splunk Cloud product lines. He has also spent time in product management roles at Yahoo!, Pivotal Software, Greenplum, and AtScale.

Renee Huang is a seasoned product leader at Snowflake, where she drives the snowflake AI-SQL initiative, a core component of Snowflake’s cutting-edge unstructured data batch analytics. With a keen focus on enabling intuitive, large-scale data interactions using AI, Renee plays a pivotal role in shaping the future of AI-powered data analytics. Prior to her current role, Renee was the engineering lead for the Cortex Analyst (Text-to-SQL), where she helped pioneer natural language interfaces for data access—bridging the gap between human intent and SQL generation.

title: Wired for AI: The W5H of Snowflake's Managed Integration Service
abstract: From AI to analytics, right outcomes start with right data. In this session, discover how Snowflake’s managed integration service powers seamless, multi-modal data integration — from batch to real-time, structured to unstructured — all within the Snowflake platform. Using the W5H lens, we’ll unpack the who, what, when, where, why and how of Snowflake’s managed integration service — and how it unlocks ETL for AI at scale.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: As Head of Snowflake Tech Consulting at LTIMindtree, I focus on helping enterprises maximize the value of Snowflake’s AI Data Cloud. With 21+ years of experience in data architecture, AI, and cloud data platforms, I specialize in delivering transformative digital strategies tailored to complex business challenges. My core strengths lie in Cloud Data Modernization, Architecture Consulting, Cloud Data Engineering, and Data Governance—enabling secure, scalable, and high-performance data ecosystems. I’m passionate about driving data-centric cultures, helping organizations modernize their platforms and unlock advanced analytics.

Prathamesh comes with a wealth of experience in the data space across engineering and analytics. He currently works as a Senior Data Cloud Architect supporting customers and partners on all things Snowflake.

title: Build a Real-Time Streaming Generative AI Application with AWS and Snowflake
abstract: Organizations have various streaming use cases where they need to ingest unstructured data — such as customer reviews or social media comments in text or JSON format — into a lakehouse. Before storing this data in database tables, they often want to process it in real time using generative AI capabilities, such as fraud analysis, sentiment analysis and summarization. This hands-on lab provides step-by-step guidance on building an end-to-end data pipeline using Amazon Bedrock, Amazon Managed Service for Apache Flink, Amazon Data Firehose and Snowflake.

speakers.bio: James Sun is a Senior Partner Sales Engineer at Snowflake. He actively collaborates with strategic cloud partners like AWS, supporting product and service integrations, as well as the development of joint solutions. He has held senior technical positions at tech companies such as HP and AWS

Nithyashree Alwarsamy is a Partner Solutions Architect at Amazon Web Services, specializing in data and analytics solutions with a focus on Snowflake. With the strong background in cloud architecture and data engineering, Nithyashree works closely with Snowflake and joint customers to design scalable, secure and cost-effective data platforms on AWS. Leveraging deep expertise in modern data architectures, Nithyashree helps organizations unlock the full potential of their data by integrating Snowflake's cloud-native data platform with the breadth of AWS services.

title: Build a Streaming Data Pipeline in Python
abstract: In this hands-on lab, you'll build a production-ready streaming data pipeline using Python and Snowflake's new Rowset API. Learn how to efficiently ingest real-time data streams into Snowflake, create automated aggregations, and visualize insights through an interactive Streamlit application. You'll walk away with practical experience in modern data engineering techniques and a functional end-to-end pipeline that can handle continuous data flows at scale.

speakers.bio: With over 20 years of experience in engineering and leadership, Brad has designed and built countless applications leveraging huge datasets at high concurrency. At Twilio, Brad helped to deliver more than 3B emails on peak days and stored over 3B contacts for customers to drive marketing campaigns. Previous to Twilio, he designed and built solutions for SendGrid, Under Armour, MapMyFitness, Garmin, and others. Brad is recognized both within Twilio as well as externally in the industry as an expert in columnar/kv datastores and developing highly-scalable/efficient architectures around them

speakers.job_title: Principal Data Platform Architect, Applied Field Engineering
speakers.bio: Keith is a Principal Architect on the Applied Field Engineering team at Snowflake, specializing in Data Engineering. Before joining Snowflake, Keith led the Data Engineering practice at an analytics consultancy, co-founded a tech startup, and held various consulting and software development roles. He holds an MBA from Georgia Tech. Residing in St. Petersburg, Florida with his wife and two children, Keith enjoys boating, fishing, camping, and traveling.

title: Building Cortex Agents on Snowflake: Why it Matters and Best Practices
abstract: Snowflake recently launched new capabilities to help organizations build data agents connected to enterprise data environment. This session explores why Snowflake makes it easy to iterate, monitor and observe agentic AI systems. Learn associated best practices related to RBAC integration in agentic workflows and applications. The session will also focus on change control and environment management and the software development lifecycle within the AI agents develoment lifecycle.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Chase Ginther is Snowflake’s Principal Applied Field Engineer, AI. He helps Snowflake’s largest customers with their strategic AI and ML projects. He is also responsible for collecting and triaging feedback from our customers to influence Snowflake’s AI/ML roadmap & strategic priorities. Prior to joining Snowflake, Chase was a Manager for AI Platforms within Deloitte’s AI Center of Excellence

title: Data Modernization by Industry: Quantifying Value
abstract: Value determination and the latest Snowflake capabilities are utilized by KPMG to deliver innovative data product solutions that bring business and IT together. Join us for a speed round of industry use cases and client stories, demonstrating how data products shorten time to insight, increase productivity and expand margins, finally delivering on the promise of business value.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Danielle is a Principal in KPMG Advisory, specializing in Data Modernization & she is an offerings leader for Enterprise Data Strategy, Data Products, Data Marketplaces, Data Value Chain, and Chief Data Office Advisory, positioning data and analytics services with clients and expanding data-related alliances. A seasoned enterprise technology executive, ex-CDO and data integration strategist with 20+ years of experience, she focuses on application development, holistic data management, next-gen analytics, enterprise architecture, AI/ML innovation, and cloud modernization. Danielle delivers client engagements across all sectors and leads the Snowflake Alliance at KPMG US.

title: Executive Industry Booth Tours - Marketing
abstract: Join an exclusive, guided tour of the Snowflake AI Data Cloud and its premier partner ecosystem, tailored for leaders in Marketing. This immersive experience will take you through five curated stops that showcase the art of the possible—real-world solutions, cutting-edge AI use cases, and data-driven innovations transforming your industry today. Gain direct access to experts, see industry-leading technology in action, and leave with actionable inspiration to accelerate your data strategy. Space is extremely limited—registration is first come, first served. Please note that this experience includes walking between stops; comfortable footwear is recommended. Please note that the Executive Booth Tours are reserved for customers only—we kindly ask our partners not to register.

speakers.job_title: Principal Architect, Customer Technical Advisor, Marketing and Advertising
speakers.bio: Luke is the Principal Industry Architect for Marketing at Snowflake, helping brands use Snowflake to power the core of their marketing data and AI needs. He has a decade of experience in customer engagement and communication platforms, working with enterprise customers on cross-channel communication strategy and automation. Before this role at Snowflake, he helped build the martech and adtech ecosystem on top of Snowflake as a Partner SE. Prior to joining Snowflake, he led the solutions engineering team at MessageGears, where he helped household brands make data platforms, like Snowflake, the centerpiece of their martech stack.

title: Marketing Frequency and Cadence Insights: Finding the Perfect Balance
abstract: Striking the right balance in marketing frequency is crucial — too many messages can lead to churn, while too few may weaken engagement. The Braze Instant Insights (BII) application helps brands fine-tune their messaging cadence using AI-powered analytics, ensuring optimal engagement without overwhelming customers. This session will include: Identifying the ideal cadence for sustained customer engagement Preventing over-communication, reducing churn risk Optimizing marketing strategies in real time with data-driven insights Join us to discover how data-backed frequency insights can transform customer engagement!

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Jon Hyman is the cofounder and chief technology officer of Braze, customer engagement platform that delivers messaging experiences across push, email, in-app, and more. He leads the charge for building the platform’s technical systems and infrastructure as well as overseeing the company’s technical operations and engineering team. Prior to Braze, Jon served as lead engineer for the Core Technology group at Bridgewater Associates, the world’s largest hedge fund. There, he managed a team that maintained 80+ software assets and was responsible for the security and stability of critical systems.

title: Rethink Data Strategy with AI: How Genpact is Pioneering a New Operating Model
abstract: As organizations scale, they face critical choices in modernizing data strategies and operations. This session explores the synergy between human expertise and agentic AI, addressing key considerations like governance, automation and scalability. Learn how Genpact uses the Snowflake AI Data Cloud to optimize decisions and scale effectively with a new, AI-enhanced operating model.

speakers.job_title: SVP Data & AI Growth Lead Hi-Tech, MFG

title: Revolutionize Engineering with AI: Edmund Optics' Journey to Enhanced Efficiency
abstract: Edmund Optics faced significant inefficiencies with its team of 50 technical support engineers. Despite advanced science backgrounds, the engineers spent an average of 25 minutes per inquiry, sifting through unstructured documents to address repetitive, low-value technical queries. Leveraging Matillion’s speed of development, integration with Snowflake and cutting-edge AI capabilities, they transformed their operations, lowered response times by 40%, and saved >100 engineering hours a month. Learn how Edmund Optics used Matillion’s powerful integration and AI tools to improve scalability, efficiency and support quality, revolutionizing the way their team handles technical inquiries.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Mark leads the Matillion revenue organisation spanning sales, partner, marketing and field engineering roles. He is responsible for supporting Matillion's customers drive for data productivity, realising the full value of their data assets for analytics and AI use cases
Full stack analytics engineer, specializing in end to end cloud-based ELT solutions and dashboard creation.

title: Solving Last Mile Analytics Using Snowflake Cortex AI and Sigma Data Apps
abstract: Top companies use Sigma and Snowflake to build interactive applications, automate workflows and take action customized to their team’s needs — all without writing any code. Join us for a hands-on lab to explore three distinct last mile analytics case studies and identify the commonalities among them. Learn how to execute machine learning models and democratize Cortex LLMs from Snowflake by leveraging scalable code-free apps in Sigma that tackle these last mile challenges. By the end of this lab, you’ll be able to drive AI adoption across your organization with customizable apps that leverage Sigma on Snowflake.

speakers.bio: Shawn Namdar has spent the past eight years in BI and data architecture, helping businesses unlock the full potential of their data. As the Snowflake Sr. Technical Alliances Manager at Sigma Computing, he specializes in integrating Sigma with Snowflake to drive seamless, no-code analytics solutions. Shawn is passionate about solving last-mile analytics challenges, empowering teams to operationalize ML models, and leveraging Snowflake Cortex LLMs within Sigma’s data apps. In this session, he’ll guide you through real-world case studies and hands-on exercises designed to accelerate AI adoption across your organization.

June of 2025 marks 7 years at Snowflake for Ben, a journey which was amazing at the start and ages like fine wine every year. He's assisted many customers with their journeys on Snowflake, ranging from scouting organizations to professional sport leagues to hedge funds to multi-national banks and insurers. In spring of 2024 he joined the Snowflake Partner Sales Engineering team, where his primary focus is the joint go-to market efforts of Snowflake and several close partners, such as Blue Yonder, Sigma, Fiserv, TransUnion and Capital One Software.

title: The Snowflake Executive View: The Playbook to Accelerating Success in the Data Cloud
abstract: Cloud adoption isn’t just a technology shift, it’s a transformation in how organizations operate, innovate, and compete. In this candid, interactive session, Snowflake executives open the playbook on what it really takes to unlock value from the cloud. From overcoming organizational inertia to building an AI-ready data foundation, the conversation will explore both the strategic and cultural elements required to drive long-term impact. Come ready to challenge, compare, and contribute.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Madhan Arumugam Ramakrishnan is an accomplished technology executive with extensive experience in product management and cloud solutions. Currently serving as the Vice President of Product at Snowflake, Madhan has previously held prominent roles at Microsoft, including Corporate Vice President for Microsoft Cloud for ISVs & AI, Vice President of Microsoft Azure, and Partner/General Manager for Azure Application Platform & Azure ISV Solutions. Earlier in the career, Madhan gained valuable experience as a Senior Software Engineer at Intel Corp., as well as roles as a Graduate Research Assistant at The University of Georgia and a Software Engineer Intern at Intel Corporation. Madhan holds a Master of Science in Computer Science from The University of Georgia and a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science & Engineering from the College of Engineering, Guindy.

title: Unified, Scalable, Agile: How T-Mobile Reinvented Its Data Workflow for 50% Faster Insights and 20% Savings
abstract: Imagine delivering critical business insights in half the time, with a data workflow so streamlined it practically manages itself. That was the vision for T-Mobile's credit risk team, grappling with a complex data ecosystem spanning Oracle, Databricks, Azure, Salesforce and SQL Server. Faced with the limitations of their initial Azure Data Factory, Databricks and stored procedure setup, they partnered with Tredence to forge a new path. The result? A modern data engine powered by Azure Data Factory for efficient ingestion, dbt for rapid transformations and GitLab for seamless version control. This strategic shift didn't just unlock 50% faster development and 20% cost savings through optimized Snowflake utilization; it fostered a unified, collaborative environment with automated documentation and crystal-clear data lineage. Peek into their future as they build a semantic layer and model registry and use Snowflake Cortex Analyst to empower self-service anomaly detection.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: I’m the Senior Manager of the Credit Data & Infrastructure team at T-Mobile with 20+ years of experience in data warehousing and reporting for senior leadership and portfolio management. As the Credit data SME and security ambassador, I lead responses to data incidents, legal inquiries, and compliance issues. I also oversee data security and ensure adherence to regulatory standards. As the gatekeeper of all Credit data and systems, I manage the department’s technology stack and ensure alignment with organizational goals.

title: Apache Iceberg™ Generalization with Snowflake in Multi-Data Catalog Environments
abstract: The increasing complexity of data management in multi-data catalog environments, challenges due to data silos, inconsistent schema management and interpretability across diverse platforms requires innovate solutions for seamless data integration and accessibility. Barclays is strategically utilising Snowflake for Iceberg generalisation to enhance data unification, governance and accessibility in this multi catalog world.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: As an experienced cloud and data engineer, I specialize in designing, developing, and implementing robust and scalable solutions to complex data-related challenges. Leveraging modern software engineering practices and cloud-based technologies, I excel at designing and building efficient and reliable data pipelines, data processing workflows, data lakes and data storage systems.

I'm an Engineering Lead at Barclays , overseeing the Data engineering functions for a central data platform. With deep experience in data and ML engineering, I specialises in building scalable data architectures, optimising platform agnostic data and ML pipelines and enable data driven decision making at scale. In today's rapidly evolving data landscape, I advocate for best practices for data and MLOps, ensuring teams can harness the full potential of data while maintaining flexibility and scalability.

title: Architecting on Snowflake: Tenable’s Approach to Building Secure Applications
abstract: In this session, we’ll explore how Tenable leveraged the Snowflake platform to deploy its application, addressing critical business challenges and technical requirements. We’ll discuss key design choices, the high-level architecture, and how Snowflake’s capabilities enabled scalability, security and efficiency. Through an in-depth conversation, we’ll uncover why Tenable built its solution the way it did, providing insights into best practices for architecting applications on Snowflake. Whether you’re building your own application or looking to optimize an existing one, this session will offer valuable takeaways on design, implementation and real-world impact.

speakers.bio: Brian has been in the data and analytics industry for over 20 years. He started his career doing applied research in mathematics and computer science at the National Security Agency. Since then he has had various technical and product roles in government and companies specializing in data warehousing, NoSQL, and analytics. Brian is currently Global Field CTO focusing on data applications.

Jason Merrick is the Senior Vice President of Product at Tenable, where he leads the strategy and direction for the Tenable One product portfolio. With a focus on innovation and cybersecurity, he drives the development of solutions that help protect organizations worldwide. Jason previously held senior roles at Forcepoint, where he led third-party product integrations, and at Oracle, where he oversaw identity and security for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. His career also includes leadership positions at Symantec, Symplified, and Teros.

title: Becoming AI-First: A Transformation Story with Yum! and Domo
abstract: Yum! Brands is building the world's most loved, most trusted and fastest-growing restaurant brands in partnership with the best franchise operators in the business. They are the parent company for the iconic brands KFC, Taco Bell, Pizza Hut and Habit Burger. In this session, you will learn how Domo and Snowflake help Yum! Brands connect and access the data it needs to support HR, sales, operations and other business use cases with up-to-date insights by combining Snowflake’s scalable data warehousing with Domo’s intuitive analytics and visualization tools. This enables Yum! Brands to make more informed and timely business decisions across its brands and franchise operators.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Mike Forster is VP of AI & Data Strategy at Yum! Brands which is the owner of Taco Bell, KFC, Pizza Hut & The Habit Burger & Grill. Mike is responsible for Yum! Brands AI & Data Strategy and Marketing Technologies.

Jason Longhurst is passionate about turning complex technology into engaging stories that simplify technical topics and inspire fresh ideas. He spent most of the last decade at Domo designing enterprise-grade tools for data analysis and transformation, making intricate systems intuitive and people-friendly. Now, as Domo’s Head of Product Marketing, he blends design thinking and technical expertise to make AI and data feel approachable, exciting, and impactful.

title: Best Practices for Protecting Sensitive Data and Simplifying Compliance
abstract: As sensitive data continues to rapidly proliferate across businesses, staying ahead of privacy and compliance requirements like HIPAA, PCI-DSS, CCPA and GDPR is not just a regulatory necessity – it's a strategic priority. Join us to discover how Snowflake's out-of-the-box capabilities, such as sensitive data monitoring, can effortlessly detect and provide a comprehensive view of your sensitive data with just a few clicks. Also hear firsthand from a Snowflake customer about their journey in building a comprehensive data governance foundation, empowering their data teams while maintaining centralized oversight — and ultimately improving the experience for their customers.

speakers.bio: Ankit Gupta works as a Product Manager on the Data Governance team. Prior to joining Snowflake, Ankit held a product management role at Microsoft, where he worked on various products ranging from Azure, IoT, and Data Governance & Compliance.

Gaurav Kumar is a Lakehouse Principal Engineer with over a decade of experience in the technology sector, emphasizing data engineering and platform solutions. His career began as a Linux Engineer in 2011, allowing him to establish a solid foundation in system architecture and administration. In 2017, Gaurav transitioned to a Data Platform Engineer role, enhancing his expertise with industry leaders, including AWS, Teradata, and Bank of America. This pivotal shift allowed him to develop scalable data solutions and drive significant data management strategies. His work has consistently focused on harnessing the power of data to meet complex business challenges.

title: Deepening BlackRock Client Relationships with Snowflake Cortex Search
abstract: As a market leader, BlackRock has grown deep relationships with our clients by offering services spanning the whole portfolio and asset management lifecycle. To manage those relationships at scale, BlackRock has developed Client Intelligence, a suite of AI capabilities powered by Snowflake Cortex Search. Bringing together structured and unstructured data, public and internal sources, in a secure a scalable platform, provides an unlock for how BlackRock can better serve our clients' needs.

speakers.bio: Ira Sigman, VP, is a Sr. Software Engineer at BlackRock.
Chris Cook, VP, is a Sr. Data Scientist at BlackRock

title: Don't Vibe Check Your LLMs! Observability & Evaluations for Gen AI Applications
abstract: To gain trust in Generative AI applications, a robust observability framework is needed. In this session, we will demonstrate how users can instrument an AI application with Trulens, an open source observability framework, to trace each component of their application and use LLM-as-a-Judge evaluations to identify and improve points of failure. Attendees will learn about best practices in enabling observability in their AI workflows and common feedback functions used for evaluation. We will also explore using the observability framework as a mechanism to compare application versions to determine which version is best fit for production use.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Elliott is a Senior AI/ML Architect on the Applied Field Engineering team at Snowflake. He joined Snowflake in May of 2023 as part of the TruEra acquisition. Elliott has spent much of his career focusing on explainability and observability for ML and GenAI applications. Outside of work you can find Elliott enjoying the beautiful mountains in his home state of Colorado!
Anubhav Mishra is a Product Manager on Snowflake Cortex AI and spends his time to design product experiences to address business challenges using generative AI.

title: From Natural Language to Python: Advanced AI Data Analysis in Snowflake
abstract: This talk will cover how Cboe Global Markets uses Snowflake Cortex AI to analyze data. Hunter Treschl, manager of Cboe's AI Center of Excellence, will describe the overall architecture of their tool that contains AI-generated data dictionaries, SQL generation, python generation and code execution. The session will highlight some of the "gotchas" Cboe discovered developing the tool, as well as share best practices and a Snowflake-first approach to developing the company's data analysis tool.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Hunter Treschl leads Cboe's AI Center of Excellence, where he drives the development of innovative AI tools and shapes the company's broader AI strategy. In addition, he is responsible for developing an AI-first culture and driving adoption across the organization through education initiatives and partnerships.

title: How Danaher Uses Snowflake and Tamr’s AI to Master Data Across 15+ Businesses
abstract: Danaher is unifying customer data across more than 15 operating companies to power enterprise AI applications. Daniel Bruckner, Co-Founder and Chief Technology Officer at Tamr, and Thomas Hill, Senior Director, Digital AI and Commercial Enablement, at Danaher, dive into how Snowflake and Tamr’s AI-native MDM work together to master messy, siloed data into enriched Golden Records with unique Tamr IDs and 360-degree views. This results in better insights, customer experiences, sales and marketing opportunities, compliance and governance.

For over a decade, he’s advanced AI-based Master Data Management, keeping Tamr at the cutting edge of enterprise data solutions. Before Tamr, Dan was a software engineer at MIT, working with Turing Award winner Michael Stonebraker on machine-driven data integration. Earlier, he was a particle physicist at CERN’s ATLAS collaboration. Dan holds a Master’s in Computer Science from UC Berkeley and an AB in Physics from the University of Chicago.

Thomas Hill is a senior digital transformation leader at Danaher, driving global initiatives at the intersection of Commercial AI, customer data, and enterprise growth. He leads programs that unify customer data and enable AI-driven sales and marketing use cases. At Danaher, he helped deploy a modern data stack using Tamr and Snowflake to build a dynamic golden record of customer truth. Passionate about turning fragmented data into business advantage, Thomas joins Tamr at Snowflake Summit 2025 to share real-world lessons and strategies from Danaher’s journey toward Commercial AI maturity.

title: Lidl's Journey with Real-Time ERP, Snowflake and Apache Iceberg™
abstract: Lidl, Europe’s largest retailer, operates in 32 countries with 10,000+ stores and a single ERP system. Recently, we redesigned our ERP analytics platform for near-real-time data, cost-efficiency, and flexibility. This talk shares how Snowflake and Apache Iceberg transformed our data infrastructure. Show how we tackled challenges in managing pipelines creating CDC-based Iceberg tables, data freshness, scalability, schema evolution, data quality, and cost-effective analytics querying in Snowflake.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: I began my career as a software engineer before transitioning to the data engineering, where I have been thriving for the past decade. My experience spans various industries, including banking, insurance, retail, and technology. I have expanded my expertise to encompass computer vision, data engineering, and ML.

title: Measuring Commercial Impact for Data-Driven Decisions at Canva with Snowflake
abstract: Discover how Canva’s data team unified commercial impact estimation for experiments and initiatives to enable data-driven decision-making. This session explores how Canva leveraged Snowflake’s data warehouse, Snowpark and Streamlit to make a powerful tool for simplifying insight extraction and empowering stakeholders. Learn about the app’s architecture, challenges and lessons, and uncover strategies to boost insight efficiency and accelerate development with Snowflake’s advanced capabilities.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

His expertise includes data modeling, machine learning, optimizing pipelines, and transforming workflows into scalable, reusable tools. Recently, Jun has focused on automating and enhancing the process of commercial impact measurement to drive efficiency and innovation across the business.

title: Power Declarative Pipelines with Dynamic Tables and Apache Iceberg™
abstract: Snowflake’s Dynamic Iceberg Tables redefine data pipeline development by offering the only platform-native, declarative approach to writing open Apache Iceberg tables—ensuring interoperability and no vendor lock-in. Simply define a SQL transformation and target lag, and Snowflake handles processing, optimization, and table writes, eliminating infrastructure management. This session will showcase how Dynamic Iceberg Tables enable seamless pipeline chaining, full visibility into execution, and near-zero operational overhead. Attendees will learn how Snowflake simplifies Iceberg adoption while delivering the flexibility of open storage with the ease of a fully managed cloud engine.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Jason has extensive experience in data analytics, having worked at Snowflake, Dremio, and Teradata. He led the writing and co-authored Apache Iceberg: The Definitive Guide, published by O’Reilly. He is passionate about making customers and individuals successful and self-sufficient. abstract: In today’s fast-paced data landscape, businesses need real-time insights powered by transactional data. CDC streaming has emerged as a critical technology to ensure that operational databases seamlessly feed analytics and AI applications without disrupting core business operations. Join us in this session as we unveil Snowflake's new OLTP database CDC streaming connectors, purpose-built to bridge transactional data with Snowflake in real time.

He also drives the UX strategy for Openflow - Snowflake’s next-generation data integration platform, making complex data pipelines intuitive and accessible for data engineers. With a strong technical foundation and a focus on solving real-world data challenges, Jakub is helping shape the future of cloud-native data movement.

Gain practical insights to overcome challenges, ensuring scalability, reliability and success in your data-driven ML journey.

Simon has worked in the field of Advanced Analytics, Data Warehousing, Big Data and Data Science for over 30 years, helping organisations to make the transition to data-driven decision making.

title: Visual AI: Using Images and Videos to Improve Quality, Productivity and More
abstract: Computers are increasingly able to "see" the world. This session will demonstrate multiple Snowflake-native visual AI solutions from industry leader LandingAI. Topics include object detection, object tracking, visual question answering, advanced document extraction and more. Marianne Halloran, data science manager at GenMark, will share a 2024 project to develop and implement an automated system to improve the detection of defects in a microdispensing process using a visual AI platform.

speakers.bio: My background spans bioengineering, electrical engineering, computational modeling, and AI – always focusing in transforming complex data into actionable insights that drive innovation. As a Data Science Manager at GenMark Diagnostics (a Roche company), I focus on developing AI-driven solutions for process optimization, manufacturing, and product innovation. My expertise bridges machine learning, statistical modeling, and image analysis, applying cutting-edge AI to biological and industrial datasets. I thrive at the intersection of data science, automation, and business strategy, ensuring that insights are not just technically sound but also aligned with operational goals.

Erica Gilbert is an experienced Customer Success Engineer at LandingAI, bringing 10+ years of expertise in Enterprise SaaS and Deep Learning startups. She specializes in applying computer vision to business solutions, with skills spanning Computer Vision, Deep Learning, NLP, and Cloud Computing. abstract: Learn how to accelerate and automate migrations with SnowConvert AI, featuring data ecosystem migration agents powered by Snowflake Cortex AI. SnowConvert AI is your free, automated solution designed to dramatically reduce the complexities, costs, and timelines associated with data warehouse and BI migrations. It intelligently analyzes your existing code, automating code conversion, data validation, and streamlining the entire migration process. Join us for an overview of the solution, migration best practices, and live demos.

abstract: Minutes of data downtime cost millions, yet many teams remain reactive. In this session, see how you can overcome these challenges using an automated observability platform for thousands of pipelines. You’ll learn to: Slash detection times (more than 70%) Prevent high-impact outages with confidence Deliver reliable AI foundations Uncover blind spots with proactive anomaly detection Join us for a technical deep dive and walk away ready to transform your data operations.

You’ll learn how to: Empower domains with decentralized data discovery and transformation Embed governance and compliance into data workflows without creating bottlenecks Enable engineers, analysts and governance teams to collaborate seamlessly with self-service data infrastructure

abstract: Whether you're looking to expand your Snowflake knowledge and skills or open new career opportunities, being able to demonstrate your expertise through SnowPro Certification will help you stand out in the data community and strengthen your career profile. Learn about the resources available for the SnowPro certification and discuss strategies for acing the exam.

title: How Kraft Heinz Optimizes Snowflake Across the Enterprise with AI
abstract: Optimizing Snowflake for an enterprise involves configuring and managing Snowflake to achieve ideal performance and efficiency for large-scale data analysis by optimizing query execution, data storage and warehouse usage. See how the AI-driven solution that Kraft Heinz developed provides an enterprise with visibility on utilization and levers causing changes in utilization, the identification of optimization opportunities and a 360-degree view of each opportunity with confirmation on the related savings realized.

speakers.bio: Karen Blomberg, Lead, Analytic Platforms, The KraftHeinz Company. Ensuring reliable service (availability, performance and cost) from all the data warehousing, data operations, and self-serving analytics platforms that Kraft Heinz operates (Snowflake, SAP HANA, etc.)

Sudhir Nune, Sr. Technology Architect with over 15 years of experience in data and analytics technologies. He brings in the snowflake implementation expertise where he led multiple implementations for Infosys clients. His approach to technology architecture is deeply rooted in driving business outcomes, excels in designing robust solutions which are scalable, sustainable and cost optimized.

title: How Pfizer Saved 30% by Consolidating Snowflake Warehouses
abstract: As compute warehouses become more commonly used, they become more cost efficient. At Pfizer, business domains at first had their own compute warehouses, and distinct functions (such as ETL, reporting, etc.) within each domain had their own dedicated warehouses. Pfizer has since switched to one set of common warehouses, one of each size and a larger value for MAX_CLUSTERS so that the warehouse handles many concurrent queries. Learn how Pfizer has seen cost savings of 30% without any performance degradation by using this approach.

speakers.bio: Steve Ring​ serves as Director, Enterprise Analytics Platforms​ at Pfizer.

title: Next-Gen Data Science: Redefining Data Science with Posit and Snowflake
abstract: The world of data science is evolving, and so are the tools analysts use. Positron, a next-gen integrated development environment (IDE), and Posit’s Native App integrations with Snowflake enable seamless, multi-language workflows with minimal admin overhead. This session explores how Posit and Snowflake empower Python and R users to work efficiently in their preferred environment. Join us to see how these innovations expand the Posit ecosystem, enhance Snowflake and streamline data workflows.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: James is a Senior Product Manager at Posit, where he focuses on helping Posit commercial products seamlessly integrate into cloud platforms and environments. He has a background in statistics and data science and finds any excuse he can to write R code and ride his bike, although usually not at the same time.

title: Pragmatic Multi-Engine Data Mesh with Snowflake and dbt
abstract: dbt recently announced a multi-engine data mesh strategy which is appealing to many. In this session, we will discuss embracing a pragmatic approach to multi-engine landscapes. For edge cases, Snowflake can govern additional engines via Snowpark Container Services (SPCS). We will demo a dbt project executing models across Snowflake warehouses and Dynamic Tables alongside DuckDB in SPCS as an example of an external engine. Results will be stored in Parquet/Iceberg, governed by Snowflake Materials.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: I’m Dmytro, a Principal Architect at Snowflake, specializing in data engineering and generative AI. I love tackling complex data challenges and am always excited to collaborate.

title: Rethinking Data Management with Apache Iceberg™ and Mixed Compute
abstract: We propose and demonstrate an architecture that swaps compute engines by workload size. Using Apache Iceberg™ as a central store, end users can query small-to-moderate data locally with DuckDB in the browser but automatically switch to Snowflake for large-scale queries. We address predicate and projection pushdown, local caching and SQL transpilling. This mixed-engine approach is flexible, reduces cloud costs and scales effortlessly.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Matthew Mullins is CTO and co-founder of Coginiti, the collaborative data operations platform. He has over a decade of experience in data engineering and architecture, leading global teams to accelerate insights. He'd always rather be outside fly fishing.

title: The Snowflake Executive View: How AI, Data and Culture Power Our Growth Strategy
abstract: Go inside the AI-powered playbook of one of the fastest-growing enterprise software companies with Snowflake CMO Denise Persson and VP of Growth Hillary Carpio. Under Denise’s leadership, Snowflake has not only transformed its marketing function but has redefined how AI, data and technology fuel business strategy at scale. In this session, Denise and Hillary will share lessons from building an AI-driven growth engine, forging deep alignment between marketing and sales, and using data and AI to drive smarter customer engagement, faster decision-making and sustained market leadership.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Denise serves as Snowflake’s Chief Marketing Officer and has been with the company since 2016. Denise oversees worldwide marketing initiatives, and has played a pivotal role in driving growth and scaling Snowflake's revenue from $1 million to over $2 billion. Denise has over 25 years of technology marketing experience at high-growth companies. Prior to joining Snowflake, she served as CMO for Apigee, an API platform company that went public in 2015 and Google acquired in 2016. She began her career at collaboration software company Genesys, where she built and led a global marketing organization. Denise also helped lead Genesys through its expansion to become a successful IPO and acquired company. Denise holds a BA in Business Administration and Economics from Stockholm University, and holds an MBA from Georgetown University.

title: Top 5 Data Rules of AI Gurus: Are You Following Them?
abstract: Does your AI actually deliver? In the high-stakes game of AI, the right data strategy is your ultimate cheat code. Join data and AI leaders from industry-leading companies and hear them share their secrets on how they’ve built scalable, high-quality data foundations that make or break AI success. From smarter governance for trusted data to unlocking real-time insights via self-service, these are pivotal to AI success you can’t afford to miss. Ready to play the game differently?

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Deepak serves as VP -Enterprise AI at LTIMindtree. With 20+ years of experience, Deepak manages AI business at LTIMindtree – West Coast, USA. He is involved in strategizing & delivering data and AI initiatives to achieve business outcomes and empower innovative solutions.

Andrew leads national online fundraising, digital communications, and web innovation. He drives strategic growth through technology, ensuring engaging donor experiences and seamless digital systems across all levels of the organization.

title: Unlock Hidden Savings with Warehouse Optimization (WHOA) for Snowflake
abstract: Are your Snowflake credits working smarter — or harder? Misconfigurations, bad queries and idle compute waste budget fast. CDW’s Warehouse Optimization and Analysis (WHOA) helps maximize cost efficiency, performance and insights — without live agents or intrusive monitoring. Learn to spot waste, optimize usage and see real results — and do better things with your credits!

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Dan Csoke brings 15+ years of experience in Business Analytics, Data Integration, and Data Warehousing. His primary areas of focus are on scalable cross-industry/cross-functional full life cycle data and analytics solutions. From traditional on-prem solutions to cloud data architectures, Dan is well versed in working with clients to ensure customer satisfaction while delivering value. Dan has led and grown delivery teams that partner with customers by ensuring effective and successful deployments of solutions. He provides team leadership and strategic direction for a multitude of industries.

Jay Brophy is a distinguished data architect, data engineer, and data scientist with over 20 years of experience in creating and leveraging analytics platforms for AI, data science, data engineering, and analytics and enabling $1B in ROI. His mission is to drive business value and product innovation through data-driven solutions that enable low-friction, high-volume data ingest for producers and off-the-shelf data products for consumers.

title: Unlocking the New Era of Smart Travel with Booking.com and Immuta
abstract: Booking.com has confidently accelerated their AI vision with a solid and smart data governance foundation, built on Snowflake and Immuta. We will discuss Booking.com's AI goals, how agentic AI is reshaping the traveler experience, and the importance of scalable data governance in making it happen. We’ll also discuss how Booking.com’s data marketplace allows them to find, share and provision instant data access, allowing data owners to share data at scale to achieve a truly connected customer experience.

speakers.bio: Jay Stricks has been a product leader in news, advertising and marketing, e-commerce, and now travel. He is currently a group product manager at Booking.com focused on data warehousing, data modeling, data development and transformation, and reporting.

Abhro currently leads product development for multiple foundational products like distributed compute powering (reverse) ETL / ELT batch and streaming workloads, discovery marketplace (catalog/lineage), IAM, Privacy powering Booking.com's next generation Data & AI platform. With a background in engineering, over the last 15 years Abhro has had the opportunity to essay multiple IC and leadership roles with AWS, the BBVA Group and with global players in the capital markets, BFSI, manufacturing and now travel space, building distributed data & ML products addressing real world challenges.

Mo joined Immuta in May 2021 and is responsible for running engineering and product management. He previously started and sold his own company in the developer tool space. Mo got his career started when he built and sold Codeship (a SaaS CI/CD product). He grew it to over 2,800 customers, and sold it to CloudBees in 2018. At CloudBees, the leading DevOps company, Mo ran Corporate Business Development where he was responsible for making acquisitions, and he also served as the General Manager for their cloud/SaaS business. Immuta helps organizations automate data access provisioning at enterprise scale, enabling both humans and AI to use data safely without delays.

title: Using Governance to Ensure Trusted Data Leads to Business Value in Pharma
abstract: Novartis is transforming its data analytics platform with Snowflake’s scalable architecture, enabling business teams to access trusted, curated data. As the first pharma company to earn EDM Council Platinum Certification, Novartis integrates MDM, metadata management and federated governance to support HCP clustering, AI-driven analytics and commercial insights. Discover how a well-managed Snowflake ecosystem fuels business value and enables strategic decision-making at scale.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Kamal is a thought leader & change agent driving data transformation for a Digital & AI ready future across the Novartis Commercial data landscape for the key global markets. He spends his time developing, defining & activating the key data management capabilities across People, Process, Technology & Data. Championing the adoption, embedding & practice of good Enterprise Data Management principles, defining the connected technology stack to enable self-service & enabling a low-friction data enablement ecosystem at scale.

title: What's New: Revolutionizing Data Movement with Snowflake Openflow
abstract: Learn how to bring all your data together with Snowflake Openflow to build with limitless interoperability and deployment flexibility, no matter if it's structured, unstructured, batch or streaming data. Make your integration pipelines ready for AI and the future.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
