speakers.bio: Saptarshi (Sap) Mukherjee leads Product Management for Data Ingestion at Snowflake, driving innovations in enterprise application, SaaS, streaming, unstructured data, and database integrations. His team builds native, high-performance ingestion solutions to maximize Snowflake’s capabilities. With over 20 years in data analytics, integration, and AI, Sap previously held product leadership roles at Google and Microsoft, shaping cloud-scale data platforms. He holds a bachelor’s in computer science engineering and a master’s in information management.

Joe leads Snowflake's Connectivity Engineering which includes the exciting new launch of Snowflake Openflow. Prior to Snowflake Joe was the co-founder and CEO of Datavolo acquired by Snowflake in 2024 and before that a GM and VP at Cloudera and Hortonworks leading Data in Motion. That was spurred by the co-founding of Onyara which was a company set to commercialize the brand new open source project called Apache NiFi. Joe spent more than a decade at the National Security Agency where he started the project which later became Apache NiFi in 2006.

title: Agentic AI with Medallion Architecture in Snowflake: A Scalable Foundation
abstract: A global enterprise is advancing its autonomous intelligence strategy by implementing a medallion architecture within Snowflake to support agentic AI systems. By leveraging Snowflake’s multi-layered architecture of data, the organization enables AI agents to observe, reason and act with high-quality, real-time data. This structured data flow empowers agentic AI to perform dynamic decision-making and automated actions across customer experience, supply chain optimization and cybersecurity operations. Coupled with Snowflake features like Snowpark for embedded ML and time travel for auditability, the medallion approach forms a scalable and secure data backbone for enterprise-grade agentic AI.

speakers.bio: Benny Du is passionate about client success and working closely with channel/ecosystem partners in the overall enterprise IT space. He is currently a core member of the Center for Advanced AI (formerly known as the Generative AI/Large Language Models (LLMs) Center of Excellence) within Accenture Technology’s Global Data & AI Unit - and leads the global GenAI efforts for the Accenture Snowflake Practice. He has had a strong track record over the years of scaling business groups and offerings in the cloud, security, and enterprise data management spaces via strategic ecosystem partners.

speakers.job_title: Associate Director - Enterprise Data and Analytics
speakers.bio: Experienced Technology professional with depth in functional knowledge. Over 15+ years' experience in a global business environment. Led cross-functional teams in Americas, Europe and Asia across IT and Finance. Proficient in leading global team that includes both employees and vendors. Experienced transformational and strategic program leader.

Salim Shaikh is a leader in Accenture Consulting Data & AI practice. With over 20 years of experience, Salim has helped clients expand their global Data/Analytical and more recently, AI/Gen AI capabilities by building advanced data ecosystems that integrate business, technology, and innovation.

title: AI for BI Without the BS
abstract: Stuck on a treadmill of endless data requests? Omni and Snowflake Cortex let your users answer their own questions with a little help from AI. No magic, no miracles — just smart tooling that gets the semantics right to deliver clear, actionable insights. This talk will provide a blueprint for keeping pace with AI while freeing up your team to focus on what really matters.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Jamie is the co-founder and president at Omni, a data analytics platform for everyone in an organization. Prior to Omni, he was an early executive at Looker leading the product management team. Prior to Looker, Jamie was a General Partner and early-stage technology investor, at Redpoint Ventures where he championed Looker’s Series A investment and served as a board observer. Jamie began his career as a data scientist at Google, working in Ads, and as a part of the APM program, working on YouTube Search and Algorithmic Discovery. Jamie has always been passionate about data and wrote his college senior thesis on predicting movie box office returns.

title: Building Secure, Modern Applications: Architecture and Security Best Practices
abstract: This session dives into building secure, modern data applications on Snowflake, focusing on robust security measures. Attendees will explore proven architectural patterns that leverage Snowflake’s security model, including role-based access control (RBAC), row-level security, secure views and dynamic data masking. Learn strategies for designing multi-tenant architectures with strict data isolation, optimizing performance while maintaining security, and monitoring data access. We’ll also cover implementing data governance frameworks for compliance. Leave with practical insights and best practices to build secure, scalable, high-performance applications on Snowflake

speakers.bio: Nagesh Cherukuri is based in the San Francisco-Bay Area and is the Principal Architect in the Applied Field Engineering at Snowflake, responsible for driving application workloads on Snowflake and helping customers design solutions for their data applications. He has held several leadership roles in a career spanning 20 years, with his most recent experience as Principal Architect at AWS, where he help devise data strategies to develop modern data solutions on public cloud for strategic accounts. Nagesh is an expert in leading enterprise data and analytics, holds an MS degree in Computer Information Science with a specialization in Security from Boston University.

title: Executive Industry Booth Tours - Media & Entertainment
abstract: Join an exclusive, guided tour of the Snowflake AI Data Cloud and its premier partner ecosystem, tailored for leaders in Media & Entertainment. This immersive experience will take you through five curated stops that showcase the art of the possible—real-world solutions, cutting-edge AI use cases, and data-driven innovations transforming your industry today. Gain direct access to experts, see industry-leading technology in action, and leave with actionable inspiration to accelerate your data strategy. Space is extremely limited—registration is first come, first served. Please note that this experience includes walking between stops; comfortable footwear is recommended. Please note that the Executive Booth Tours are reserved for customers only—we kindly ask our partners not to register.

speakers.bio: David is an Industry Principal in the Snowflake Media / Advertising Practice where his purview is adtech / martech. More specifically, he owns the GTM and strategy associated with host of partners including third party data providers, hygiene providers, identity providers, activation platforms, analytics technologies, and measurement service partners. He previously worked on the Snowflake Marketplace Team where he owned the media / advertising vertical GTM and strategy. He has worked in data and identity over the past 15 years.

title: How Carvana Implements FinOps Best Practices in Snowflake
abstract: Join the value engineering and professional services teams for practical guidance on implementing FinOps best practices with Snowflake. You will also hear from our customer Carvana on how easy it is to implement the FinOps pillars of visibility, control and optimization in Snowflake through dashboards and reports to track consumption, cost drivers and unit economics; cost governance strategies such as workload prioritization, resource allocation and user tracking; and optimization initiatives such as right-sizing warehouses, enhancing query performance and leveraging automation. Equip yourself to implement FinOps in Snowflake for better cost management and enhanced time to value.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Shanthi Subramanian is a Consulting Manager and Senior Solutions Architect at Snowflake, where she partners with Data Leaders implement modern data solutions in their organizations. A SnowPro Subject Matter Expert, she specializes in data warehousing and cloud deployments. With deep expertise in the Data Cloud, Shanthi guides organizations in optimizing their data strategies and maximizing value from their Snowflake investments.

Jeremy is a seasoned professional with nearly 20 years of experience in data engineering, analytics, and platform architecture. Carvana Snowflake usage is in the 99th percentile for efficient operations across all Snowflakes customers. Recently, he’s been building the largest analytics platform at Carvana by implementing Snowflake and constructing enterprise-level ingestion pipelines that feed into it, ensuring that crucial data is accessible for informed decision-making. He believes in fostering a collaborative environment where his team is encouraged to explore new technologies and methodologies, keeping Carvana agile and responsive in a rapidly evolving digital landscape.

title: How Scene+ Speeds Up Data Science with Snowflake ML
abstract: In this session, you’ll hear from Scene+, a large customer loyalty program in Canada, about how it uses Snowflake ML to streamline and improve its ML workloads, eliminating cross-platform data movement and reducing project timelines and costs. With end-to-end model workflows built and deployed entirely in Snowflake, Scene+ cut time to production on feature engineering, training and inferencing by over 60% for more than 30 models.

speakers.bio: Ranjeeta Pegu is a Senior Solution Architect at Snowflake with 20 years of experience in AI/ML, Data Science, ETL, BI, and Data Warehousing. She specializes in enabling customers to build scalable, data-driven solutions using Snowflake’s platform. With a Data Science certification from Harvard Extension School, she is focused on advancing AI/ML and helping organizations maximize the value of their data.

Chris Kuusela is a seasoned leader in Data Science and Machine Learning, currently serving as Director of Data Science at Scene+. With experience at Bell, RBC, and Scene+, he specializes in analytics and machine learning development. Chris holds a degree in Applied Mathematics from the University of Guelph.

title: Latest and Greatest with Orchestration in Snowflake with Blue Yonder
abstract: Join us to explore the cutting-edge Snowflake Tasks features that are transforming how organizations orchestrate their data pipelines. You will learn practical insights into using Tasks for effective data pipeline orchestration. Get inspired by Blue Yonder’s success story that showcases how using Tasks has led to significant cost savings and data pipeline performance optimizations. Learn how these innovative solutions can help you elevate your data game, drive business transformation, and revolutionize your approach to stay ahead in an increasingly data-driven world!

speakers.bio: Manuela is a Senior Product Manager leading Snowflake Tasks and Orchestration aimed at making it easier for users to cost-effectively and reliably schedule and monitor data pipelines on Snowflake. She is passionate about delivering seamless, end-to-end platform products accelerating the productivity of data professionals. Manuela brings 20 years of experience building large scale data and ML platforms, and distributed data integration systems.

Is a Senior Director at Blue Yonder, leading cloud transformation and platform engineering initiatives. With deep expertise in AWS, Azure, DevOps, and scalable enterprise solutions, he has successfully modernized OMS, inventory, and fulfillment systems across major companies like Nike, GameStop, and now is contributing to WMS transformation at Blue Yonder. Passionate about automation, security, and operational excellence, he drives high-performing teams and strategic decision-making to deliver cost-efficient, resilient platforms. Known for mentorship, clear communication, and innovation, they excel at aligning technology with business goals.

title: Legacy to Snowflake: How to Avoid Failing Your Data Warehouse Migration
abstract: Migrating a legacy data warehouse to Snowflake should be a predictable task. However, after participating in numerous projects, common failure patterns have emerged. In this session, we’ll explore typical pitfalls when moving to the Snowflake AI Data Cloud and offer recommendations for avoiding them. We’ll cover mistakes at every stage of the process, from technical details to end-user involvement and everything in between — code conversion (using SnowConvert!), data migration, deployment, optimization, testing and project management.

speakers.bio: Federico Zoufaly helps Snowflake customers accelerate their adoption of the platform from his position as Director of Migrations and Modernizations @Snowflake. Federico previously served as Co-Founder of Mobilize.Net. Federico has been a leader in modernizing software applications for almost 30 years. With a technical background (Ph.D.c. from the University of Florida with a Fulbright scholarship and M.Sc. from the Technology Institute of Costa Rica) and hands-on management in real world technology projects, Federico is passionate about the intersection of the technical challenges and the business need for modernization. Federico is a fellow of the Aspen Global Leadership Network.

4+ years leading Migrations for Snowflake PS Previously a Snowflake customer - selected the technology after a bake-off with Databricks, fell in love with Snowflake and decided to join the company 35+ year IT Professional with a special focus on data and data related technologies Former CIO, CTO, VP, and Director with progressive experiences spanning startups and the Fortune 100. Vertical Industry experience in Healthcare & Life Sciences, Financial Services, Oil & Gas, Manufacturing, Telecom, and Retail. Resides in Raleigh, NC

title: Predict, Personalize, and Perform: AI-Powered Customer Intelligence
abstract: As consumer preferences shift rapidly, winning brands anticipate needs and exceed expectations. Optimove’s agentic AI-powered predictive cross-channel marketing platform helps businesses make smarter decisions, optimize marketing and create seamless data-driven customer journeys. This session explores key considerations for implementing AI-driven personalization and looks at how Optimove leverages Snowflake to power its data customer experiences.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Asaf leads a team dedicated to helping brands harness AI-driven marketing solutions to drive customer engagement and business growth. With nearly a decade at Optimove, Asaf has advanced through multiple roles, from Solutions Engineer to Director, shaping the company’s approach to technical solutions and client success. Asaf brings extensive expertise in martech, data-driven personalization, and CRM strategy. His background in technical project management and engineering enables him to bridge the gap between technology and marketing, ensuring brands maximize their use of Optimove’s platform. Prior to Optimove, he held roles in business operations and engineering, at HP and Fanzone.

title: Retail Analytics Reimagined: Aptos and BlueCloud’s Snowflake Solution
abstract: Disjointed back-end systems were limiting the analytics experience for Aptos ONE customers. To modernize analytics and deliver critical insights, Aptos partnered with BlueCloud to build a high-performing cloud data warehouse using Snowflake and Coalesce. In just three months, this robust data foundation transformed Aptos ONE—enhancing scalability, streamlining analytics, and bolstering customer confidence in a world-class product. Attendees will gain actionable insights, proven best practices, and a roadmap to accelerate their own data modernization journeys, empowering them to unlock the full potential of retail analytics.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Bill Tennant is a technology leader with over 15-years of experience in delivering positive business outcomes through data management, analytics, and advanced data related initiatives at organizations ranging from startups to Fortune 10. At BlueCloud, Bill leads overall alignment of our global teams to drive mutual successes and overall company growth. Tennant is currently the CRO of America's Growth Partner 2024 - BlueCloud. He is a 2023 Tampa Bay 40-under-40 Honoree; 2023 CRN Next-Gen Solution Provider Leader; multi-year Fast 50 award recipient, and has helped BlueCloud to achieve unprecedented growth and scale as a leading Snowflake partner.

John Carney is a retail tech expert with 20+ years of experience driving innovation (360Commerce, Oracle, Starmount, Infor). He has a proven background in using cutting-edge technology to further business objectives both inside and outside the brick-and-mortar store environment.

title: Silos to Symphony: Spotify's Journey with Data Lineage at Scale
abstract: Modern data ecosystems face increasing complexity with the proliferation of AI models, metrics and event streams. This sesion explores how Spotify tackled this challenge by implementing a comprehensive data lineage framework that serves as the foundation for tracking multiple dimensions of our data ecosystem. We'll share our journey demonstrating how data lineage can be extended beyond traditional use cases to encompass AI model lineage, metrics lineage and event lineage.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Dhanush Shetty is a Product Manager at Spotify, leading Data Management initiatives supporting the platform's 600M+ global listeners. He focuses on building robust data infrastructure and architecture that powers Spotify's extensive data ecosystem. Prior to Spotify, Dan brought his data expertise to Intuit, where he managed enterprise data solutions for one of the world's largest fintech companies

title: Snowpark Bound: Penske’s Smooth Migration from Databricks
abstract: Unlock optimized performance and cost efficiency! Explore Penske’s successful journey converting Databricks workloads to process 50% faster in Snowpark. This real-world case study will equip you with key technical paradigms and keen insights you’ll need to achieve a frictionless migration for your organization. Deliver a more streamlined and performant data engineering environment that also translates into tangible financial savings through reduced infrastructure costs and optimized compute.

speakers.bio: Data Engineering solution architect with Snowflake PS with a strong background in building scalable data processing pipelines and systems. With expertise in Apache Spark, Scala, Python, Snowflake and Cloud technologies have working experience on a wide range of projects involving large-scale data processing, real-time analytics, and cloud-based data infrastructure. Have implemented optimizing data engineering workflows, ensuring reliability, performance, and cost-efficiency.

Strategic Leader and Enterprise Architect experienced at leveraging disparate technologies and diverse collaborative teams to build solid, secure, integrated Business Solutions that deliver real Business Value. 25+ years delivering innovative application and data solutions across numerous industries and leveraging a wide range of cloud platforms and technology stacks – including (of course) Snowflake. Regularly advises senior business leaders around data solutions. Deep experience with data architecture, data security, data governance, data quality, data migration, and modern data engineering. Particularly adept at enabling diverse business application systems to “play nice together.”

title: The Customer 360 Playbook: How to Build, Activate and Personalize at Scale
abstract: A unified customer 360 is the key to delivering personalized, data-driven experiences — but building one is easier said than done. In this session, we’ll show you how to break down data silos, unify and enrich customer profiles, govern data and activate it at scale using Twilio Segment and Snowflake. Learn how Segment streamlines data collection and identity resolution into Snowflake — and how those rich customer profiles can then be activated to power impactful, revenue-generating campaigns.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Sean is a data enthusiast with 10+ years of experience at Cloudera, Fivetran, and various data startups. At Twilio Segment, he architects data warehouse activation strategies, helping customers personalize communications across channels to reach the right person at the right time. Sean loves partnering with customers to solve their unique data challenges and unlock actionable insights.

title: Transforming ModelOps: How Cox Automotive Scaled AI with Dataiku
abstract: Cox Automotive needed to accelerate AI model development and improve collaboration between data science and engineering teams. In this session, learn how Dataiku, integrated with Snowflake, helped Cox Automotive reduce model deployment time by 70%, streamline ModelOps and drive innovation. Discover how the combination of Snowflake’s data platform and Dataiku’s AI capabilities enabled Cox Automotive to transform operations and deliver faster, scalable AI across products and services.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: David Mitchell is Cox Automotive Engineering Sr Director. An accomplished and recognized technology leader with hands-on leadership experience with data platforms, cloud technology, analytics, ModelOps, AI, data warehousing, application development, software engineering, data engineering, system implementations, and process improvement.

title: Unleashing Manufacturing's Digital Thread
abstract: The digital transformation of manufacturing enterprises faces fragmented and unconnected data landscapes. A modern data platform enables a standardized model across all domains with their assets, processes and systems to establish a digital thread. Advanced AI, generative AI to agentic AI enable the creation of actionable insights to improve operational efficiency and gain competitive advantages. We show how this unified approach resolves key challenges in manufacturing digitalization.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.job_title: Lead Alliance Partner Snowflake (AI+Data Specialist Director)
speakers.bio: Christian is a specialist director from Deloitte Consulting in Germany, who supports clients across various industries §tor for cloud analytics transformation and data management as solution architect and subject matter experts. He brings in long-lasting experiences for advisory and implementation services, also in collaboration with Deloitte's ecosystem & alliance partners. As Lead Alliance Partner for Snowflake he is responsible for the collaboration in Germany and drives the value proposition for our joint clients in that region and for our Snowflake Deloitte EMEA network.

title: Unlocking the Power of FanGraph: Unifying Sports Data for Deeper Insights
abstract: Fanatics is laser-focused on relentlessly enhancing the fan experience across the entire ecosystem. Sports fandom is multifaceted. It extends beyond merchandise sales and into adjacent sport businesses such as collectibles, online betting and events, and so does our data infrastructure. This session will showcase how we're building FanGraph: a comprehensive 360-degree identity graph powered by Snowflake cloud technology and AI, which enables enterprise-level analytics, precision segmentation, personalized recommendations, cross-sell and strategic monetization opportunities. Join us to explore the future of data and AI in sports!

speakers.bio: Daniel is an experienced builder of 0 > 1 products that deliver customer impact and increase the bottom line across e-comm, supply chain, real estate, precision agriculture and AR/VR. Most recently at Fanatics, he has been focused on building data monetization business and leveraging massive amounts of data to build unique data and AI-based products that empower business users across all Fanatics businesses.

Somebi Eleh is an Engineering Manager for Data & Advanced Analytics at Fanatics. With previous experience at SeaWorld Parks & Entertainment, McKinsey & Company, and Deloitte, she has led data strategy initiatives across retail, entertainment, consulting, and public sector. A University of Ottawa graduate with backgrounds in Engineering/Computing and Biotechnology, she combines technical expertise with business acumen to transform complex data into actionable insights for strategic decision-making.

title: What's New Session: Build a Better Enterprise Lakehouse with Native Support for Apache Iceberg Tables™
abstract: Rethink how you build open, connected, and governed data lakehouses: integrate any Iceberg REST compatible catalog to Snowflake to securely read from and write to any Iceberg table with Catalog Linked Databases. Unlock insights and AI from semi-structured data with support for VARIANT data types. And enjoy enterprise-grade security with Snowflake's managed service for Apache Polaris™, Snowflake Open Catalog.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Ron Ortloff is a Product Manager on the Data Lake team at Snowflake. His primary area of focus is on Apache Iceberg integration with Snowflake. He is passionate about open source and the freedom of choice it provides customers. Prior to joining Snowflake, Ron worked for Microsoft where he held various roles from solution architect to engineering manager where he led building large, complex big data solutions.

Ashwin Kamath leads the product efforts for Snowflake Open Catalog. Before Snowflake, Ashwin held product management roles at Google and Microsoft, where he worked on observability, streaming analytics technologies and developer experience. Ashwin holds a master’s degree in computer science from University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign.

title: Best Practices for Cost Management in Snowflake
abstract: Learn about the latest and greatest cost management capabilities in Snowflake to optimize your spend and get the most bang for your buck. This talk will walk through capabilities and best practices that you can use today.

speakers.bio: 4.5 years in Snowflake Professional Services. Working with many large customers in both FSI and Public Sector. Over 40 Years of database consulting experience.

title: Change Takes More Than A Megaphone: How To Build Culture & Drive Transformation
abstract: Establishing a data and AI-driven culture doesn’t happen overnight. And it won’t happen with just a vocal leader, no matter how big the megaphone. Real buy-in requires a show rather than tell approach. When people see and hear real results, real change typically follows. Better yet, participation gives them a stake in the game. Join this session to hear how Snowflake’s customers communicate the value of data and AI to develop a data culture and drive truly transformative change.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Joined Toyota in 2001, I have spent 20 years in R&D designing cars. Geek with passion for Technology and Data. Leading Toyota Motor Europe's Data Mesh initiative. My mission: To make the company future-proof in Data and AI.

As Principal Data Strategist at Snowflake, Jennifer advises customers on data strategy and best practices in building world-class data organizations. Prior to Snowflake, Jennifer spent over a decade as a Forrester Analyst, held management positions in tech sales and marketing, designed urban policy programs in E. Europe and Russia, and taught math as a Peace Corps volunteer in Central Africa. She earned a Ph.D. and an M.A. from Stanford University and a B.A. in econometrics from the University of Virginia.

speakers.job_title: VP Head of AI Innovation and Incubation

title: Coke One North America Shatters SAP Data Silos to Drive Insights, Cut Data Costs
abstract: SAP data shouldn’t be siloed — it should drive your business forward. Coke One North America (CONA), a technology services provider for the North American Coca-Cola bottling business, overcame the challenge of integrating SAP data to unlock real-time insights and create a more cost-effective data architecture. Join this session to hear how they enabled self-service analytics and optimized operations with Fivetran and Snowflake — giving 30,000 users seamless access to the data they need to drive smarter decisions.

speakers.bio: David has almost 20 years of experience in IT and professional services, including 15 years in the Coca-Cola system. While spending most of his career in Information Security, he has recently held a leadership role in Data and Analytics owning the Data Warehouse for the North America Coca-Cola Bottlers (on Snowflake). He and his team focus on data product delivery, data model certification, data management and maintenance governance, and delivery insights to the Coca-Cola Bottling business.

Mark Van de Wiel is the Field CTO at Fivetran, where he helps shape the company’s technical vision and guides enterprise customers in optimizing their data integration strategies. He brings deep expertise in data replication, real-time analytics, AI readiness, and cloud infrastructure. Mark joined Fivetran in 2021 through the acquisition of HVR, where he led U.S. operations and played a key role in scaling the business. His prior experience includes technical leadership roles at Oracle, Actian, and GoldenGate Software. Today, he continues to be a driving force behind Fivetran’s efforts to simplify and modernize data movement for global enterprises.

As BI Product Director, I lead the charge in transforming data into actionable insights that drive strategic decisions and operational excellence at CONA Services. I spearhead setting the vision and guiding principles for data warehousing and analytics solutions. I ensure that our projects align with the Bottlers strategic goals and deliver measurable value. My team and I work on developing and implementing analytics solutions that enhance customer engagement and operational efficiency for our Bottlers. We assist our Bottler's ability to harness the power of data, driving growth and innovation through strategic insights and effective data management.

title: Delivering a Cloud-Based Enterprise Data Platform at Scale
abstract: Ageas, a leading UK personal lines insurer offering motor and home insurance, partnered with EY to migrate its data from legacy systems to Snowflake's AI Data Cloud. This session will detail the migration journey and highlight key learnings.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Director in EY's AI & Data competency based in London. Focusing on large cloud-based data platform implementations in UK Insurance Market.

For over 18 years, I've been at the forefront of designing and implementing data and analytic transformations that drive strategic outcomes for organisations across multiple industries. A track record of delivering high-value data services and a proven ability to collaborate across diverse stakeholders and build inclusive teams that achieve tangible business results. Global experience spanning Central Europe, North America, and APAC.

Paul Davies is the Head of Data Platforms for Ageas UK for the last 13 years, a personal lines insurer. I am an experienced data leader with over 9+ years of leadership with a proven track record in Data migrations to the cloud, decommissioning of legacy data platforms, driving transformative data insights for all business areas. Skilled in working with the business to accelerate platform adoption and embed data governance and ownership. My hobbies include watching rugby and socializing with my friends, whilst also spending time with my wife and two children as we look to travel the world with most recent adventures being St Lucia and Finland, so hot then cold.

title: Demystifying Generative AI in Snowflake: A Live Prompt Engineering Demo
abstract: Unlock the full potential of generative AI in Snowflake with hands-on prompt engineering! In this live demo, we'll build and refine prompts from scratch, showcasing how to harness LLM-powered features like Cortex Analyst, Document AI and Cortex functions. Attendees will see real-time development, practical use cases and best practices to get the most out of Snowflake’s AI capabilities — whether for extracting insights, automating workflows or enhancing data pipelines with AI-driven intelligence.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: David is a Principal Data Architect at National Grid, and a Snowflake Data Superhero since 2021. With experience across industries like gaming, finance, and energy, they thrive on solving data challenges. They enjoy dogs, games, puzzles, and math.

title: Empower Business Decisions with Data Products and Snowflake Internal Marketplace
abstract: In this session, We will enable CDAO's Enterprise Data Strategy by exploring the full lifecycle from the concept of data products—packaged, readily consumable data assets—to their distribution through Snowflake's internal marketplace. We will drive home 5 key points within this data product lifecycle: 1. Simplified data access for business users 2. Improved data discoverability and understanding 3. Increased data utilization and adoption 4. Accelerated time to insight 5.Metrics & Considerations

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: As a strategic executive with extensive data & analytics experience building large global teams, I drive operational excellence, a holistic forward-looking Data and AI strategy and decisive execution to create value for key stakeholders of our data community via enabling agile delivery of data capabilities and products. I bring proven expertise leading large, complex global transformation initiatives by leveraging data, technology, and analyzing risk and reward to strengthen competitive position and achieve operating plans. I am currently heading up Snowflake's Data Cloud Architecture Practice for Financial Services | Retail & CPG Industries and help execute client's AI Data strategy.

Matt Harris is a Principal Solutions Engineer at Snowflake, where he helps global wealth‑management firms unlock new revenue and risk‑management opportunities through Snowflake. A Data Scientist and Quantitative Anthropologist by training, Matt draws on 15+ years of experience in AI, machine learning, and data governance to design collaborative data strategies from investment research, trading, to client reporting. His work has fueled digital transformations in financial services and the Public Sector. Matt teaches machine learning and public‑policy analytics as adjunct faculty at the University of Pennsylvania, inspiring the next generation of data‑driven urban and analytics leaders.

title: Empowering Industrial IoT Use Cases with Snowflake
abstract: This session will explore why and how Snowflake's unique capabilities are crucial to enable, accelerate and implement industrial IoT use cases like root cause analysis of asset failure, predictive maintenance and quality management. The session will explain the use of specific time series capabilities (e.g. asof joins, CORR & MATCH function), built-in Cortex ML functions (like anomaly detection and forecasting) and LLMs leveraging RAG to accelerate use cases for manufacturing customers.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Michael is EMEA's Field CTO for Manufacturing at Snowflake. He brings 20 years of experience in the area of data and cloud in the manufacturing space and served in various management positions at companies like Siemens, Teradata and AWS. Michael has been responsible for delivery organizations in various industrial fields himself and consulted major EMEA companies in their journeys leveraging cloud, analytics and GenAI to transform their businesses. Michael studied International Management in Bamberg/Germany and Paris, holds a doctorate degree in international management as well as certifications from Snowflake and AWS.

title: Enterprise AI Data Agents: Automating Bronze Layer to Snowflake dbt Pipelines
abstract: Experience the future of data engineering with enterprise AI data agents in this immersive demo. See firsthand how intelligent Genesis agents autonomously discover and extract data from Databricks' bronze layer, leverage dbt for efficient transformation, and deliver it seamlessly into Snowflake’s secure, performant gold layer. Driven by cutting-edge agentic automation, Genesis streamlines complex data pipelines, minimizes manual overhead and proactively monitors workflows — all running natively within Snowflake. This session is ideal for data engineers and AI specialists eager to optimize efficiency, accuracy and unlock unmatched productivity.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: An industry leader in enterprise data platforms, Matt led the development of business-critical analytics at Goldman Sachs for over 20 years. He later joined Snowflake to run Product Management and launched the Snowflake Data Marketplace. Now, he is bringing AI workers to enterprises to power the new AI economy.

title: Go Beyond Passwords with New Snowflake Identity and Access Capabilities
abstract: Snowflake will block single-factor password sign-ins starting in November 2025. To make this easy, we’ve added new identity and access capabilities such as support for new MFA methods – including passkey and authenticator apps – native support for CSP workload identity, programmatic access tokens (PAT), and many more. We are also introducing a range of identity products that will help with collaboration following the least-privileged principle, including restricted caller’s right and user-based access models. Join us to see how these new products come together and how you can improve your authentication, authorization and access approach with them.

speakers.bio: Anoosh leads security product management at Snowflake. He is accountable for security of production, and also security features offered to customers. Prior to that, he was the lead PM for Google Cloud workload zero trust portfolio. Before that, he was leading the Windows identity and access management team at Microsoft delivering first-in-class enterprise-ready face recognition technology to market and also leading Microsoft engagement at FIDO. Anoosh received his Ph.D. from University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign in the area of computer security

Eric Woroshow is a Principal Software Engineer in Snowflake's Database Security group. In his 15 years of writing software professionally he has had the privilege of working at multiple leading organizations, including NVIDIA, Google and Stripe.

Greg's professional experience spans over 20 year across multiple industries, with the last 5 years at Roche. A seasoned Project Manager with a track record of delivering data and analytics solutions involving multiple technologies like Oracle, SQL Server, Redshift and Snowflake. In his current role as a Product Manager, Greg leads the Snowflake team, where his mission is to develop and sustain a secure, scalable and efficient global data platform to harness the power of Roche data assets.

title: How Fiserv Is Building a Financial Data Network on Snowflake
abstract: What started as a means to federate development and share data across the enterprise turned into a productized data ecosystem for Fiserv’s customers. Hear from Marc Rind, Fiserv's Chief Data Technology Officer, about the data journey Fiserv has taken for the company and for their customers with Snowflake.

title: How Pacific Life Is Embracing Snowflake in a Highly Regulated Environment
abstract: Pacific Life, a 150-year-old mutual company with a strong brand, continuously balances modernization with trust. For the past five years, Snowflake has served as its backbone, supporting data management, data science and gen AI initiatives. As Snowflake expands its offerings, Pacific Life strategically integrates offerings like Snowpark, Streamlit, Dynamic Tables and Cortex into its ecosystem. This session will explore Pacific Life’s journey in adopting these innovations within a highly regulated environment.

title: Journey from Legacy Data Warehouse to a Columnar and Metadata-Driven Snowflake
abstract: At Carlyle, we transitioned from a legacy on-prem data warehouse to a fully metadata-driven columnar data warehouse on Snowflake. This session covers challenges, best practices for data pipelines, and frameworks for ingestion, transformation and auditing. We’ll explore Snowflake Secure Data Sharing, Dynamic Tables, and scalable compute and real-world solutions to streamline workflows, manage complexity and boost performance. Hexaware has been a key partner in this modernization journey.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: As Hexaware's Vice President of Data & AI, Prashant Dahalkar spearheads the Data & AI Presales, Solutions, Delivery & Consulting divisions. With a dynamic blend of business acumen and technological prowess, Prashant is dedicated to transforming data into a strategic asset that drives business excellence and competitive advantage. Prashant's influence extends beyond Hexaware, as he is a trusted advisor to Chief Data Officers (CDOs) and data leaders worldwide, as he guides them in navigating the complexities of modern data landscapes. Outside the boardroom, Prashant's passion for badminton and yoga reflects his holistic approach to leadership and life, fostering balance and well-being.

Experienced architect with a demonstrated history of working in the alternative asset management industry with broad experience across the data, security, infrastructure and enterprise architecture domains.

title: Next-Generation Data Operations and Insights with Cortex Analyst and Slack
abstract: In this session, meet Athena, Mariana and our AI data agent, Aida. Learn how teams can enrich every step of the data delivery pipeline, from raw data wrangling to executive reporting and data-driven decision making. Join Salesforce's Slack Business Analytics Data Engineering team exploring insightful, agentic data products, utilizing Snowflake Cortex Analyst and Slack to delight data practitioners and business leaders alike with actionable insights meeting them right in the flow of their work.

speakers.bio: Apun Hiran is a tech enthusiast and seasoned leader with over a decade of experience wrangling data at Slack, Dropbox, and Yahoo. He specializes in turning chaos into clarity—transforming complex data into actionable insights and building scalable solutions that drive efficiency, revenue, and the occasional “aha!” moment. A firm believer that great data teams run on innovation (and coffee), Apun has led engineers, analysts, and scientists in crafting rock-solid data governance frameworks and uncovering game-changing insights. Passionate about revolutionizing data management, he’s on a mission to make data not just useful, but truly impactful.

title: Optimizing Local Commerce with Geospatial Analytics: Wolt and Honeycomb Maps
abstract: Wolt shares how they built a state-of-the-art internal location data platform by combining Snowflake’s native geospatial functions with the Honeycomb Native App for interactive map visualizations. This platform enables team members around the world to see the selection that’s available to customers in each area and easily identify areas that should be prioritized for improvements. By leveraging the Snowflake Native App framework, data always loads quickly, is up to date, and offers built-in authentication.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Carston Hernke is the founder of Honeycomb Maps, an interactive map dashboard tool powered by Snowflake. With a background in data engineering and analytics, Carston has led geospatial initiatives at TIER Mobility and consulted for Fortune 500 corporations. A passionate advocate for making geospatial data accessible to all through intuitive design, Carston combines technical skill with a strategy mindset. He holds a degree in Management Information Systems from the University of Minnesota.

Sharat is the Head of Selection Analytics at Wolt (DoorDash International). He's responsible for enabling the global sales organization with insights and tooling to acquire the best merchants in all 30+ Wolt markets. He's also particularly interested in the application of spatial analytics to identify growth opportunities and drive business outcomes.

title: Transforming Healthcare Through Data Democratization at NYC Health and Hospitals
abstract: Join us to explore the journey of America’s largest public hospital system toward data democratization, leading to enhanced decision-making and organizational agility. Decentralized teams at 11 acute care hospitals generated more dashboards and drove greater business impact than centralized teams. Attendees will learn about the evolving role of Data Champions, the Data Literacy Program, the curation of structured data assets and ways to ensure access to Snowflake's modern data and AI platform.

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)
Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Shahran Haider is a senior executive passionate about improving patient outcomes and healthcare operations through data. He serves as Deputy Chief Data Officer for NYC Health + Hospitals, the largest public health system in the U.S. His responsibilities include providing guidance for AI strategy and investments for the enterprise, leading AI Governance and ownership of the Enterprise Information Management function. Under his leadership, NYC Health + Hospitals has evolved to a modern cloud data management with enterprise capabilities for AI/ML, business intelligence, data discovery, collaboration and exploration.

speakers.job_title: Sr. Director, Data and AI Strategic Enablement and Operations
speakers.bio: Bharti Sharma is the Senior Director of Data & AI Strategic Enablement & Operations at NYC Health + Hospitals, where she leads the data democratization efforts and drives the adoption of Data & AI technologies to improve healthcare outcomes. She previously led the COVID-19 Intelligence Team, providing critical data insights that informed decision-making for leadership, regulatory authorities, and the Mayor’s office during the pandemic. With a strong background in population health informatics and quality care, Bharti has successfully leveraged data analytics to optimize clinical and operational performance across large healthcare organizations.

title: What's New: Snowpark Container Services
abstract: Learn how to deploy the best-in-class OSS models in Snowpark Container Services for AI-driven insights in Snowflake. Join us for new feature overviews, best practices, and a demo featuring an advanced agentic use case.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Muzz Imam is Product Lead for Snowpark Container Services (including Native Apps). Muzz has been in the tech industry for over 15 years. He has a Masters degree in Computer Science specializing in Artificial Intelligence from University of Southern California and holds a Masters of Business Administration (MBA) from University of Washington. He specializes in Containers, Compute and AI/ML. Previously he was the Product Manager for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) for a number of years before joining Snowflake to solve some advanced compute problems for AI, ML and Data Cloud.

speakers.job_title: Sr. Global Manager, Solutions Engineering - SI Partners
speakers.bio: Senior leader on the Partner Solution Engineering team primarily focused on System Integrators (SI) function and responsible for driving AI/ML and DE/DL initiatives.

title: AI Chatbots: Scaling from Prototype to Products with Snowflake Cortex LLMs
abstract: Embark on a journey through the development of AI chatbots, from initial prototype to robust, scalable and secured products deployed across multiple factories in different countries. This session highlights the integration of Cortex LLMs and Cortex Search, leveraging multilingual capabilities and integration to other systems to create seamless user experiences for a global user base. Discover how an idea born in business has scaled within the organization through a citizen developer approach.

speakers.bio: After studies in industrial engineering I started a role as a Low Code Consultant (Mendix) at Accenture. After one year on several projects I joined Siemens Energy as a Solution Architect. Within Siemens Energy I've lead several digitization initiatives and developed multiple Mendix Applications. After 2 years as a Solution Architect, I've moved to another role closer to manufacturing business as a Product Owner. Now being responsible for several AI/ML projects in Snowflake and leading the intiative Factory Data Cloud.

Responsible for digitalization on the implementation of a new power transformer factory in the US. Development and implementation of digitalization roadmap, digital tools landscape, cyber security program, Factory Digital Twin Simulation and innovative & data driven initiatives.

title: AI Won’t Work Without It: Minimum Viable Data Governance (MVDG) for Success
abstract: AI is only as smart as the data it learns from. Without trusted, governed, accessible data, AI fails. Enter Minimum Viable Data Governance (MVDG) — the foundation of AI success. This session covers why AI fails without MVDG, how Snowflake enables scalable, automated governance, and real-world examples. Learn how lightweight governance removes friction and fuels AI — not a checkbox, but a necessity. MVDG ASAP!

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Christopher Marcolis is a dynamic technology leader with a deep passion for innovation and the transformative potential of Data & AI. As a steward of technology teams, he excels at guiding the creation of solutions that align cutting-edge technologies with strategic business goals. Christopher specializes in empowering organizations to become Data & AI-ready, leveraging his expertise in data management, governance, and advanced analytics to help clients navigate the complexities of AI adoption.

Ben Castleton is the Data Quality Principal for CDW's Data and Analytics Practice. Along with leaders Rex Washburn and Paul Zajdel, Ben helped coin the term Minimum Viable Data Governance. He has been helping clients and industry groups understand its benefits for the past year. Ben has spent over 15 years in data and analytics, from healthcare and finance to tech and AI startups. Ben was one of the cofounders of the team at Great Expectations Labs, Inc. That experience gave him insights into hundreds of companies implementing data quality profiling, monitoring, and reporting at enterprise scale. Ben's pragmatic approach to data quality focuses on making data valuable for stakeholders.

title: Best Practices for Accelerating the Data Pipeline Development Lifecycle
abstract: Building resilient data transformation pipelines requires more than just writing code — it demands a structured lifecycle that ensures reliability, performance and maintainability. This session explores best practices for managing the data pipeline development lifecycle in Snowflake, from creation in a rich IDE to robust testing, monitoring and debugging. We’ll cover strategies for version control, data quality testing, leveraging telemetry for proactive monitoring, and using UI-driven tools for faster debugging. Attendees will gain actionable insights into streamlining development workflows, reducing downtime and ensuring data integrity across the pipeline.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Building tools for Data Engineers with the main focus on Data Pipelines and Observability. Passionate about programming languages, parsers, and their application to data transformations.
Architect on Snowflake’s Data Pipelines team, focusing on Dynamic Tables, Tasks, and Streams. Exuberant about stream processing, databases, programming languages, and distributed systems.

title: Bring SAP Data into Snowflake Seamlessly with SnapLogic
abstract: If your SAP and Snowflake data remain disconnected, you’re missing a key piece. Enriching your data is crucial to drive better decisions, power smarter AI, and ensure good data in, good AI out. However, integrating data can be challenging — manual processes are slow, integrations are complex and silos persist. SnapLogic makes it easy. With pre-built SAP connectors and automated pipelines, you can quickly bring SAP data into Snowflake — no coding, no headaches. Join us to learn how companies use SnapLogic to integrate SAP data and uncover best practices.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Atalia is a multi-dimensional thought leader specializing in AI/ML, automation, and digital transformation. As Head of Data and AI Practice for North America at Customertimes, Atalia supports businesses in driving innovation and leveraging data and AI to accelerate their strategic growth.

title: Causal AI Hotel Forecasting at Scale with 95% Accuracy at Cloudbeds
abstract: Productionizing ML models doesn't have to be hard. In this talk, see how Snowflake ML makes it easy to securely and scalably deploy models for inference and manage them in production. We'll discuss how Cloudbeds chose Snowflake ML to process over 4 billion data points per hour to drive demand forecasts across 20,000 hotels with XGBoost-based models as part of its proprietary causal AI framework, resulting in 95% forecast accuracy. Cloudbeds seamlessly deploys models with Snowflake Model Registry for inference. The result is scalable causal AI forecasts that adjust room rates and drive coordinated marketing actions based on predicted demand as part of the Cloudbeds Intelligence commercial platform.

speakers.bio: Amit Popat, Head of Machine Learning at Cloudbeds, is a University of Cambridge Mathematician and distinguished machine-learning expert with a remarkable career spanning senior roles at Valtech and London Town Group before founding his AI consultancy. He pioneered advanced AI analytics and marketing solutions for FTSE-250 companies and global giants like easyJet, Shell Energy, and Herbert Smith Freehills.

title: How NatWest Saved $600,000 Through Proactive Cost Optimization of Snowflake
abstract: At NatWest Group, rising Snowflake costs led to a shift from reactive to proactive cost optimization. Initially focused on cost spikes, NatWest realized users lacked the tools to effectively manage costs; by partnering with domain teams, NatWest improved cost visibility with a "cost per query" feature, resulting in $600,000 in savings. Learn key techniques from NatWest on how a proactive, cross-functional approach can unlock significant savings and efficiency.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Over the last couple of decades, I have specialised in helping clients—primarily in the financial industry—unlock the full potential of their data assets and technology investments. My expertise lies in addressing business process inefficiencies, developing strategic data roadmaps, and implementing transformative data solutions, with particular focus on the Snowflake Data Cloud. I am committed to building robust data foundations that not only optimize operations but also enable AI capabilities and foster long-term business growth.

Gaurav Kumar is a Lakehouse Principal Engineer with over a decade of experience in the technology sector, emphasizing data engineering and platform solutions. His career began as a Linux Engineer in 2011, allowing him to establish a solid foundation in system architecture and administration. In 2017, Gaurav transitioned to a Data Platform Engineer role, enhancing his expertise with industry leaders, including AWS, Teradata, and Bank of America. This pivotal shift allowed him to develop scalable data solutions and drive significant data management strategies. His work has consistently focused on harnessing the power of data to meet complex business challenges.

title: How to Build Data Pipelines for Apache Iceberg™ with Dynamic Tables
abstract: Learn how to build data pipelines on Iceberg with Dynamic Tables. This talk will demonstrate how to build an end-to-end pipeline that ingests the data into Snowflake, processing it with Dynamic tables both on Snowflake managed Iceberg data lakes and externally managed data lakes, and then run an Apache Spark job on the golden data.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Mat is an engineer on the Dynamic Tables team, specializing in Data Pipelines, Iceberg, and Incremental Processing.

Péter is a Senior Software Engineer at Snowflake, working on scalable streaming infrastructure like Streams and Dynamic Tables. Earlier in his career, he worked on Microsoft SQL Server and Fabric (formerly Synapse Data Warehouse), focusing on scaling the storage engine and metadata for OLAP workloads. He has a strong background in data infrastructure and enjoys working on query engines for modern data analytics.

title: Mastering CI/CD for Snowflake Native Apps: Build, Automate, Deploy
abstract: Maxa, a leader in AI-powered financial analytics, specializes in helping businesses optimize performance through data-driven insights. Hear from Maxa on enterprise-grade CI/CD best practices to streamline Snowflake Native App development and deployment. Learn how to design efficient CI/CD pipelines, implement automated testing, and leverage advanced versioning techniques between Git and Snowflake. Discover automated deployment strategies using Snowflake CLI, optimize release management with app release Channels, and enhance observability through Snowflake Trail.

speakers.bio: Sebastien Coutu, an innovative technology leader with over 20 years of experience, excels at transforming complex business challenges into elegant technical solutions. With a career spanning startups to large corporations, including software vendors, integrators, and service providers, he combines strategic vision with deep technical expertise. His mastery of infrastructure, analytics, and data platforms enables him to architect cutting-edge solutions using Kubernetes, data processing services, cloud infrastructure, and automation to deliver secure, enterprise-grade systems.

Frédéric L’Anglais has been working in the data industry for 25 years and currently serves as Senior Manager, Solution Engineering at Snowflake. With a Master’s degree in Software Engineering, he is a 2024 Snowflake Data Superhero, SnowPro Advanced Certified, and a Subject Matter Expert (SME). Frédéric is passionate about leveraging cloud, AI, and data-driven innovation to solve complex business challenges and help organizations make the most of their data.

title: Scaling AI-Driven Data and Analytics at Workday
abstract: Scaling data teams in the era of AI is no small feat. Workday embarked on a major transformation, replacing up to 15 technologies to build an AI-first data ecosystem, with Sigma as the last mile for analytics. They have also embedded Sigma into their analytics development process, leveraging it for rapid prototyping. With a focus on both scale and velocity, the enterprise data team streamlined operations, centralized analytics, and empowered decision-making across the organization. Join Workday’s Senior Director of Data Products and Senior Director of Data Portfolio & Strategy to learn how large organizations can drive AI transformation at both speed and scale.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: As the Senior Director of Data Strategy & Portfolio at Workday, Farhad Choudhury has deep expertise in Data Strategy, Portfolio Management and Technology. Passionate about leveraging technology to solve real-world business challenges, he has a proven track record of delivering results while leading high-performing global teams. Farhad specializes in driving large-scale corporate transformation initiatives, where technology serves as a key enabler. Before joining Workday, Farhad held leadership roles on Data & Analytics teams across at SGWS, Tapestry (Coach) and Accenture. He holds an MBA from the NYU Stern School of Business.

Nick Kouvaris is the Senior Director of Data Products, Analytics, and Business Intelligence at Workday and a founding member of the company’s centralized data and analytics function. He helped scale the team to approximately 40 people within a year and rebuilt the single source of truth for ARR and revenue retention. Passionate about AI, Nick drives prescriptive and predictive analytics to enhance customer retention, operational performance, and data-driven decision-making. Nick spent nearly six years at Lockheed Martin as an FP&A analyst and eight years at Silver Spring Networks, where he oversaw corporate and product finance. He holds an MBA from Santa Clara University.

Jake leads the Data Platform and Demo Engineering teams at Sigma, where his teams focus on building robust data products and data applications to help enable Sigma, and Sigma customers, to run on Sigma. Jake has been in the data space for more than 10 years—working at everything from small startups, to fortune 500 companies, to consultancies.

title: Scaling Real-Time Data Pipelines for AI-Powered Lead Optimization
abstract: In today’s fast-paced B2B landscape, real-time data is crucial for driving lead engagement and optimising sales. This session explores how Canva’s marketing tech stack leverages Snowflake’s Streams, Tasks, and Dynamic Tables for seamless workflows, while Cortex AI delivers precise lead scores and flags. Discover how this approach enhances lead prioritisation, and scales data solutions, tackling the challenges of real-time pipelines to meet Canva’s growing data demands.

speakers.bio: Hi there, I’ve spent the past four years at Canva building scalable data solutions that power low-latency and AI-driven use cases. My work focuses on designing efficient data pipelines, optimising analytics infrastructure, and ensuring privacy and consent controls are embedded at every step. I’m passionate about making complex data systems simple and intuitive, always thinking about how users interact with. tldr: I love breaking patterns and trying new things to test out what could be possible.

title: Snowflake Cortex: Energizing Healthcare Payers for Better Care
abstract: CareFirst and phData show how Snowflake Cortex AI is transforming healthcare payor operations by streamlining access to imperative member and provider data. Traditionally, customer service associates navigate multiple systems to assist callers. With Cortex Analyst, they can swiftly access claims, benefits and enrollment insights in one place to make critical and informed health decisions. Learn how this innovative technology empowers healthcare payors to deliver easier, smarter, more efficient and personalized support.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Jonathan is a software developer, data scientist, database and SQL expert, creative thinker, and natural leader at phData.

Manan has more than 25 years of experience in the software development field and leading teams. Manan’s skills include expertise in Digital Product Development, Architecture, and Technical Management. Manan has a unique Entrepreneurial approach to align Technologies to Business Opportunities and has a proven track record of delivering multi-million dollar projects providing Innovation and Automation in Healthcare. Manan is well versed in Cloud, Snowflake, Salesforce.com, Data warehouse, AI, Machine Learning and Java/J2EE Technologies.

title: Snowflake Openflow: A Technical Deep Dive
abstract: With Snowflake's integration service, Snowflake is providing enterprises with open, extensible and secure data integration capabilities that enable real-time, scalable and bi-directional data movement. Powered by Apache NiFi, this service offers customers the option to use a self-contained data plane in Snowpark Container Services, as well as the ability to run BYOC data planes in the customer’s cloud VPC. We will dive into the platform architecture in this technical session.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Sam joined Snowflake as a Senior PM with the Datavolo acquisition, where he served as CTO and led Product and Field Engineering teams. Prior to that Sam was at Google Cloud and led Customer Engineering teams supporting ISVs on GCP in the areas of integration, data analytics, and ML. Sam has been close to data engineering and data architecture for his entire career and focuses on helping customers solve tough challenges to support continued innovation to inform better analytics, forecasting, and decision-making.

title: Transforming Dynamic Yield by Mastercard: Migrating Legacy Systems to Snowflake
abstract: Join us to learn how we transformed Dynamic Yield, a subsidiary of Mastercard, and its recommendation engine by migrating from legacy systems such as HBase, Flink and Spark to Snowflake. See how Snowflake powered statistical and ML algorithms along with reporting pipelines, driving enhanced performance, scalability and simplicity. This session will cover key challenges, lessons learned and the measurable impact of our migration journey.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Yotam Feldman is a Machine Learning Engineer at Dynamic Yield by Mastercard, specializing in building and optimizing recommendation systems. With extensive experience in real-time serving and low-latency architectures, Yotam develops scalable ML solutions that enhance personalization. A proficient Python developer, he has worked extensively with agentic workflows, leveraging LLM agents to drive intelligent and adaptive recommendation systems. His expertise includes integrating Snowflake for data preprocessing in training pipelines and real-time applications, ensuring efficient and high-performance ML deployments.

Benny Greenberg has over a decade of cloud computing experience spanning frontend, backend, and machine learning. He leads the ML engineering team at DynamicYield by Mastercard, building scalable machine learning solutions with a core focus on recommendation systems. His expertise lies in developing AI-driven solutions that process massive datasets for personalized digital experiences. Passionate about bridging research and real-world applications, Benny ensures ML models drive tangible business impact. When not exploring AI, he enjoys biking and surfing.

title: Whats New: Snowflake Intelligence and No-Code Agentic AI
abstract: Discover how to build and deploy a no-code, agentic AI platform with Snowflake Intelligence. See how organizations are bringing together structured and unstructured data to make insights accessible across their entire enterprise via an AI-driven agentic interface, all within Snowflake. Join us for a product overview, best practices discussion and live demo.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.job_title: Director of Product, Snowflake Intelligence and Agents
speakers.bio: Jeff Hollan is Director of Product for Snowflake's GenAI Agentic platform and Snowflake Intelligence. Prior to joining Snowflake Jeff led the serverless and developer compute businesses for Microsoft Azure. Jeff is a well-known and frequent speaker on building cloud native applications. He has been a keynote speaker and presenter at industry conferences including KubeCon, ServerlessConf, Microsoft Build, and the Linux Foundation API Summit.

title: What the Spec?! New Features in Apache Iceberg™ Table Format V3
abstract: Snowflake and Apache Iceberg™ go great together, but did you know things can get even better? With Table Format V3, engineers at Snowflake and in the community have come together to bring some previously vendor-exclusive features into the open! In this session, we’ll look at an overview of the new features in V3. Variants, geometric types, row lineage and more are all available now and portable between engines. Come and learn about how engine interoperability just got even better!

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Russell Spitzer received his Ph.D from UCSF in 2013 after performing a lot of comparisons of protein binding sites. Following that, he became deeply invested in distributed computing and involved in several Apache projects. While working at Datastax he was a key contributor to the DataStax Spark-Cassandra Connector and also worked on many Apache projects. After leaving Datastax he worked at Apple growing the then nascent Apache Iceberg project where he worked on data file management and advancing the table format. Currently Russell working on OSS software at Snowflake and is a PMC member of the Apache Iceberg project, and PPMC member of the Apache Polaris (Incubating) project.

Danica began her career as a software engineer and pivoted to developer relations, focussing primarily on open source technologies under the Apache Software Foundation umbrella such as Apache Kafka and Apache Flink. She now leads the open source advocacy efforts at Snowflake, supporting Apache Iceberg and Apache Polaris (incubating). She can be found on X (Bluesky and Mastodon), talking about tech, plants, and baking @TheDanicaFine.

title: AI for All: How Snowflake and Universities are Shaping Global Education
abstract: In a rapidly evolving AI-driven world, equitable access to education is paramount. The Snowflake Academia program, through Snowflake's "One Million Minds, One Platform" initiative, democratizes AI and data education. This session highlights how Snowflake empowers university students worldwide with a free, hands-on data analytics curriculum, AI labs and industry-recognized certifications. Featuring testimonials from pioneering educators in the United States and Austria, it showcases real-world impact stories. Join us to see how Snowflake Academia breaks barriers, enabling educators to integrate Snowflake into classrooms and offering students practical skills to thrive in the global job market!

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Benjamin Gigerl is a Researcher at the University of Graz, specializing in AI, AutoML, and the Smart Regulation of low-code / no-code development platforms. His work focuses on enabling domain experts to build high-quality AI Solutions. Previously, he served as a Global Data Scientist at Siemens Energy, driving digital transformation across 30+ production sites. Benjamin published in IEEE, presented at Gartner D&A Summit and teaches AI & Data Literacy to students. Beyond academia, he’s an avid golfer who enjoys exploring courses worldwide.

speakers.job_title: Technical Director, Head of Global Academia
speakers.bio: Seeling is an experienced software professional specializing in distributed data management systems and big data technologies. As Head of Snowflake's Academia program, she leads efforts to democratize AI education for university students worldwide. With deep expertise in educating and enabling users to leverage technology effectively, Seeling is also passionate about mentoring and contributing to non-profit education initiatives, empowering youth and women with hands-on technical career development skills. She holds a Bachelor's and Master's degree in Computer Science from Florida Atlantic University.

Jim Caccia is a seasoned data professional with over a decade of experience working with Snowflake. He currently teaches at Northern Illinois University's School of Business, specializing in Information Systems. Jim lead the certifying process to allow NIU to bring both Snowflake and AWS Academy programs into the curriculum. In addition to his academic role, Jim worked as a Senior Partner Engineer at Snowflake for 4 years, collaborating with top Solution Integrators. While at Snowflake, he earned Snowflake Core, Engineering, and Architect certifications. Prior to Snowflake, Jim held technical and leadership positions where he successfully led Snowflake migration initiatives.

title: Applying DevOps Principles in Data Warehouse Creation
abstract: This session dives deep into the practical techniques Nordea employed to build a data warehouse using Snowflake and Airflow. Discover how to prioritize delivery pipelines, implement build-in quality through practices like test-driven development and trunk-based development, and automate the core layer using Data Vault 2.0 features like stored procedures and Snowflake scripting. We'll also explore how to leverage the "ASYNC" command and build a presentation layer with DYNAMIC tables. Join us for actionable insights and techniques to accelerate and optimize your own data warehouse creation process.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Biography: 1999-2005: The University Of Vilnius, master degree of Management Information Systems 2000-2005: Lead Web Application Developer 2005-2012: Data Warehouse Developer at Swedbank Lithuania 2012-2016: Data Warehousing Consultant at Teradata Sweden. 2016- now: Chief IT Developer at Nordea bank Sweden.

title: Composable in Action: Real-World Architectures from 3 Leading Brands
abstract: Composable isn’t the future, it’s the standard. But execution is everything. In this session, we’ll walk through how technical teams at Autodesk, Docusign and The Globe and Mail are designing and deploying modern customer data architectures with Uniphore while operationalizing their Snowflake AI Data Cloud. Learn how these enterprises built scalable, secure pipelines for real-time marketing activation and are working toward the ultimate goal of composability — activating directly from Snowflake and eliminating data duplication. Plus, hear how Uniphore is upleveling the audiencing and activation process with AI. Join us if you're looking to operationalize your AI Data Cloud investments and make your data truly activation-ready.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Kalyan Tummala is Vice President of Product Marketing at Uniphore, where he shapes the go‑to‑market strategy for the company’s enterprise AI platform. With more than 15 years in B2B infrastructure, data, and SaaS, he has launched and scaled category‑defining products including VMware vSAN, Qualtrics Design XM, and the real‑time streaming platform Grainite. Before Uniphore, Kalyan served as Head of Marketing at Grainite after earlier product‑focused roles at VMware, Altera, and Intel.

title: Deploying Data Pipelines with Snowflake and dbt Labs
abstract: Are you an analytics engineer looking to improve your architecture and DevOps practices? Have you been experimenting with new features like Snowflake Dynamic Tables or is your organization considering new architectural approaches? In this lab, you'll learn: Developer-centric tips for discovering and accessing data products across Snowflake Marketplace, ad-hoc uploads and connecting via Snowpark external access Alternatives to lambda architecture for streaming data sources with Dynamic Tables New materialization options with Dynamic Tables and Apache Iceberg

title: Executive Industry Booth Tours - Manufacturing
abstract: Join an exclusive, guided tour of the Snowflake AI Data Cloud and its premier partner ecosystem, tailored for leaders in Manufacturing. This immersive experience will take you through five curated stops that showcase the art of the possible—real-world solutions, cutting-edge AI use cases, and data-driven innovations transforming your industry today. Gain direct access to experts, see industry-leading technology in action, and leave with actionable inspiration to accelerate your data strategy. Space is extremely limited—registration is first come, first served. Please note that this experience includes walking between stops; comfortable footwear is recommended. Please note that the Executive Booth Tours are reserved for customers only—we kindly ask our partners not to register.

abstract: Connect with EMEA-based executives navigating AI adoption, data strategy and innovation at scale. This exclusive networking hour is designed to foster dialogue among leaders managing complex regional dynamics while pursuing global impact.
