speakers.bio: Global sales enablement leader with proven track record and 15+ years of passion for Data, Analytics, & AI. Extensive experience in assessing business needs and translating them into targeted and personalized field enablement programs for thousands of global Microsoft sellers (Data and AI Specialists, Technical Specialists, Global Black Belts and Cloud Solution Architects). Delivered $300M+ ROI per year over the last 3 yrs. through the creation of technical content including cross solution 'product differentiators', industry scenario DREAM demos, deployable DREAM Demos in a Box (DDiBs) / POC accelerators and cloud workshops for sellers & partners: https://aka.ms/msdreams.

title: Deep Dive: Security and Permissions for Snowflake Native Apps
abstract: Snowflake Native Apps revolutionize security by bringing app logic to the data, eliminating the need to move data elsewhere for processing. This ensures superior security and governance while keeping the data under the customer’s control. This session covers how Snowflake Native Apps deliver both security and advanced functionality, including the security sandbox, RBAC, App Specification, Restricted Caller’s Rights and controlled role grants. Designed for developers, security teams and decision-makers, this deep dive offers key insights for building, reviewing, buying or maintaining Snowflake Native Apps. RBAC knowledge is helpful but not required.

speakers.bio: Sandy Gao is the Technical Lead for the security pillar in Snowflake Native Apps team. He is a software architect, with a wide range of technical skills, and experience leading designs of complex software systems, focusing on simplicity, consistency, and performance.

Scott is a Principal Software Engineer at Snowflake, leading the Native Applications Foundation team. He has an extensive career in the world of databases, including leading the development of Apple's CloudKit database platform, and architecting IBM's Big SQL database platform.

title: Dev Day Opening
abstract: Join us as Snowflake CEO Sridhar Ramaswamy delivers opening remarks.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Sridhar Ramaswamy currently serves as Snowflake’s CEO. After joining Snowflake in May 2023 in connection with the company’s acquisition of Neeva, the world’s first private AI powered search engine, Sridhar spearheaded Snowflake’s AI strategy as SVP of AI. Prior to joining Snowflake, Sridhar co-founded Neeva in 2019. Prior to founding Neeva, Sridhar led all of Google’s Advertising products, which included search, display and video advertising, analytics, shopping, payments, and travel. During his 15 years at Google, he was an integral part of the growth of AdWords and Google’s advertising business from $1.5 billion to over $100 billion. He also held research positions at Bell Labs, Lucent Technologies, and Bell Communications Research (Bellcore). Sridhar was a Venture Partner at Greylock Partners from October 2018 until recently, and he sits on the board of trustees at Brown University.

title: Digital Twins Reimagined: A Knowledge Graph AI Approach to Network Analysis
abstract: Leveraging a digital twin of our physical network infrastructure, we’ve transformed our ability to identify network vulnerabilities, optimize equipment placement and plan for demand fluctuations. This enables network engineers to run complex what-if scenarios, proactively identifying bottlenecks and analyzing failure impacts while maintaining data within Snowflake. This eliminates data movement and reduces significant TCO while enabling unprecedented network analysis capabilities. Future opportunities have been identified around fraud and revenue assurance; see this press release - https://finance.yahoo.com/news/relationalai-knowledge-graph-coprocessor-generally-170000497.html

speakers.bio: I'm a tech leader working at the intersection of data, AI, cloud, and software, currently leading AI engineering at Spark New Zealand. I've built and led products and teams across startups and enterprises, always pushing the boundaries of what's possible. I care about creating environments where people can solve hard problems, move fast, and grow. I love turning complex ideas into real solutions - from neuromorphic research in my PhD to working in exciting startups to building a SaaS platform - Kapello, a digital twin of marketing that brings data, decision systems, and automation together for real-time experimentation, I have explored the art of possible.

Spark is one of APJ's most successful Snowflake customers and being the proud winner of the 2023 Data Driver Award. Anshuman leads the team responsible for Analytics, AI, Data Engineering, Commercialisation and Data Science.

Marco is Vice President and Global Head of GTM at RelationalAI where he leads the Sales and Delivery. Before joining RelationalAII he was a Junior Partner at QuantumBlack, the analytics arm of McKinsey, leading QuantumBlack work in TMT (High-Tech, Media and Telco). Prior to QuantumBlack, he was a Deployment Strategist at Palantir where he led the commercial growth of the company in Europe. Marco holds a DPhil in Computer Science from the University of Oxford.

title: How AmorePacific Gets Insights 53x Faster with a 30% Cost Reduction on Snowflake
abstract: AmorePacific is a trailblazer in Korean beauty that exemplifies leadership in data strategy. By empowering real-time segmentation of 10 million customers, AmorePacific has achieved insights 53x faster, facilitating seamless omnichannel integration while reducing costs by 30%. Learn how this transformative journey toward a customer-centric data platform offers a blueprint for using data to drive growth.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: I've been working as a digital innovation director at the Korea's biggest cosmetic company, Amorepacific, which runs its business in +15 countries. My work scope varies from e-commerce related platforms to AI for the industry. Managing big data analytics platform is essential to achieve goals in those innovative areas. Our company has been with Snowflake since 2022 to overcome the efficiency and speed limitation in other cloud-based data platforms.

title: How NBCUniversal Designs High-Performance Data Systems in Snowflake
abstract: Materialization is a powerful tool — but overuse can hurt performance, drive up costs and complicate pipelines. This session explores strategies that NBCUniversal uses to design high-performance data systems in Snowflake while minimizing unnecessary materialization. Learn query planning and execution fundamentals using a single physical table to power multiple virtual schemas, and learn how to build pipelines with nonmaterialized views and UDTFs. You’ll also learn practical criteria for deciding when materialization is necessary and how to efficiently materialize streaming data. Attendees will leave with actionable insights to keep their Snowflake implementations lean and fast.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Matt Braymer-Hayes is a Senior Software Engineer, Content Delivery at NBCUniversal. In his role, he focuses on delivery decisioning and analytics to stream Peacock's series, movies, live events, and more to viewers. Prior to NBCUniversal, Matt worked for Lytics, a customer data platform, where he helped operate and scale their real-time identity resolution database over six years of ever-increasing load. He previously served as co-organizer of PDXData, a data science and engineering Meetup in Portland, Oregon and as technical editor for Two Scoops of Django, a guide for the Python web framework.

title: Open Source Convos with JB Onofré and Russell Spitzer
abstract: Join us for an open and engaging conversation on the future of open source at Snowflake. Hosted in the GROUP BY Lounge, this session brings together Snowflake’s open source leaders — Russell, JB and Yufei — to share updates, perspectives and behind-the-scenes stories from projects such as Apache Iceberg, Streamlit and Polaris. Whether you’re a contributor, a curious developer or just someone who wants to know how open source fits into the Snowflake ecosystem, this is your chance to ask questions, get inspired and connect. Coffee, community and candid insights — don’t miss it!

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

title: Optimize storage costs using Storage Lifecycle Policies
abstract: Discover how to leverage Snowflake's Storage Lifecycle Policies to automatically manage data archival and deletion based on custom rules. Learn to reduce storage costs by moving aging data to lower-cost tiers while maintaining accessibility for compliance reasons. This session covers policy creation, monitoring, and best practices with hands-on examples to help you implement effective data lifecycle management strategies.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Wendy Liu is currently working on the Storage Lifecycle Policy within the Snowflake Storage team.

Mayank is a Senior Software Engineer at Snowflake, deeply involved in the engineering of scalable and resilient storage systems capable of managing exabytes of data. As part of Snowflake's Storage Platform team, he architects and implements solutions for large-scale data management. Earlier in his career, Mayank honed his skills as a Senior Software Engineer at Microsoft, where he worked on developing a Reinforcement learning as a service on Azure and improving the scalability of the services.

title: Real-Time Insights at Scale: OLTP Database CDC Streaming with Snowflake Openflow
abstract: In today’s fast-paced data landscape, businesses need real-time insights powered by transactional data. CDC streaming has emerged as a critical technology to ensure that operational databases seamlessly feed analytics and AI applications without disrupting core business operations. Join us in this session as we unveil Snowflake's new OLTP database CDC streaming connectors, purpose-built to bridge transactional data with Snowflake in real time.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Jakub Puchalski is a Senior Product Manager at Snowflake, leading the development of real-time CDC database connectors that power seamless and scalable data ingestion into the Data Cloud. He also drives the UX strategy for Openflow - Snowflake’s next-generation data integration platform, making complex data pipelines intuitive and accessible for data engineers. With a strong technical foundation and a focus on solving real-world data challenges, Jakub is helping shape the future of cloud-native data movement.

title: Removing Barriers and Improving the Experience of At-Risk Oncology Patients
abstract: Florida Cancer Specialists (FCS) has leveraged the breadth of Snowflake's Date Warehouse functionality to unlock siloed data to identify its most at-risk oncology patients. With a vision of developing patient cohorts and increasing care coordination, FCS teamed up with a market leader to incorporate hospitalization data. After implementing principal illness navigation (PIN) services, FCS saw a 40% reduction in ER visits for qualified, high-risk patients, which was shared at the White House.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.company: Florida Cancer Specialists & Research Institute, LLC
speakers.bio: Norma Jean Carlile is an accomplished Lead Business Analyst at Florida Cancer Specialists and Research Institute. With 10 years of experience in Oncology, she consistently strives to transform complex oncology-related business problems into scalable analytical solutions. With the combined efforts of Norma Jean and the FCS Enterprise Analytics Team, led by Franklin German, FCS has successfully integrated an array of innovative technologies that is accelerating our Florida community-based oncology practice’s mission and advancing patient care faster than ever before.

speakers.company: Florida Cancer Specialists and Research Institute
speakers.bio: Sr Director Enterprise Analytics For Florida Cancer Specialists and Research Institute. In this role, he is the Solutions Data Architect, Cloud Healthcare Analytics, Integration, Data Governance, Business Intelligence , Analytics and Data Warehousing. Experienced and passionate enterprise data architect and healthcare data management professional with over 25 years experience leading implementation and support of Enterprise Data Architecture, Cloud Analytics, Business Intelligence, Data Governance and Data Warehouse projects:

title: Saving Lives with Snowflake: AI-Powered Predictive Healthcare & Risk Mitigation
abstract: Since 2001, more than 120,000 veterans have tragically died by suicide. Face the Fight — a coalition founded by USAA, Reach Resilience and the Humana Foundation — is leveraging Snowflake’s advanced data engineering and AI capabilities to provide real-time risk insights for organizations serving veterans. This AI-driven initiative enables rapid, life-saving interventions in minutes, helping prevent suicides and reduce risk. Learn how robust data architecture, advanced analytics and predictive AI applications are transforming healthcare, optimizing costs and improving outcomes for those who need it most.

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)

speakers.bio: I'm the Chief Technology Officer at a healthtech startup, where I combine my obsession for troubleshooting with a mission to transform healthcare through technology. My journey spans both Fortune 500 companies and tech startups, giving me a rare view of how to scale while staying nimble. I've built teams from scratch, led multimillion-dollar operations, and learned that the best innovation happens when you challenge existing paradigms. Teaching is woven into everything I do—from mentoring engineering teams to helping organizations embrace change.

Kirk earned his master's degree in data science from the University of North Carolina at Charlotte and has been working in the field since 2019. He has helped companies across various industries use Snowflake to build and scale machine learning models and AI capabilities over a wide range of use cases .

title: Scalable Data Governance Across Regions and Business Units with Medtronic
abstract: Medtronic has a global implementation of Snowflake across multiple regions. This session will cover how Medtronic implemented role-based access controls, masking and row-access policies at scale for its finance and sales data products. Learn best practices for optimizing row-access policies at scale for thousands of users across use cases and business units with varying levels of security requirements.

speakers.bio: Senior Solutions Architect at Snowflake

30+ year data professional with experience in a variety of industries (MedTech, Travel, Telcom, etc). Currently at Medtronic and responsible for the Data Platforms that support reporting, analytics, AI, and integrations.

title: What's New: Build, Test and Deploy Products with Developer Tooling
abstract: Learn how to leverage build, test and deploy your work with Snowflake's latest developer tooling. Join us for new product and feature overviews, best practices and live demos.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Myles Borins is a Toronto based product leader with deep experience in shaping technology, innovating product, and building developer platforms. He Leads Product for Developer Platform at Snowflake where he is focused on empowering individuals building on Snowflake's AI Data Cloud. He previously held roles at GitHub, Google, and IBM. As a founder of the OpenJS Foundation, a former TSC member of the Node.js project, and former vice-president of ECMA International, Myles spent close to a decade steering the evolution of the JavaScript ecosystem. Beyond his professional endeavours, he is passionate about the open web and fostering healthy communities.

title: Will They Buy? Building ML Models to Crack the Code of Customer Conversions
abstract: In this lab, you'll build a model to predict purchase likelihood by analyzing relationships between performance metrics, product differentiation and customer reviews. This lab will guide you through the following: 1) Use generative AI to process feedback (sentiment analysis, review quality, retailer interactions), 2) aggregate unstructured features with structured web analytics features (performance metrics, user interactions, reviews), 3) train custom models, 4) analyze feature importance (technical performance, product quality, customer experience), and 5) create stakeholder visualization. By the end, you’ll know best practices for predicting purchasing likelihood at your company.

speakers.bio: I'm a developer advocate at Snowflake who gets excited about helping developers do cool things with AI and data in the cloud. After a decade of building developer communities at Hume AI, Bill.com, and Clover Network, I found my happy place bridging the gap between complex tech and the developers who want to use it. Think of me as a technical matchmaker – connecting developers with the right tools and watching the magic happen. When I'm not geeking out about AI, you'll find me spending time with my son and collecting new hobbies (because why stick to just one?).
Allie Fero is a Senior Software Engineer at Snowflake on the Machine Learning Platform team, focused on enabling users to bring their custom AI workloads to Snowflake with ease and performance.

title: Enabling Everyone to Build with AI
abstract: With amazing AI building blocks such as large models as well as AI-assisted coding, it is now possible to build prototypes and applications faster than ever before. Andrew shares some of the building blocks that are newly available, why this is a good time for everyone to build with AI and best practices for doing so.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.job_title: Founder & Executive Chairman of LandingAI, Founder of DeepLearning.AI, Chairman & Co-Founder of Coursera

title: AI Knowledge Graph Innovation Drives Life Sciences Media Planning and Strategy
abstract: Transforming Snowflake data into an AI-enabled Knowledge Graph to analyze anonymized cross-channel media-to-patient journeys, enabling natural language exploration of pathway analytics, node clustering, and similarity algorithms. Leveraging RelationalAI's Snowflake native application, the solution introduces compliant Digital Twin simulation to reduce campaign planning costs. Moving beyond aggregate reporting to improve their annual media spend.

speakers.bio: With more than 20 years of experience in Data Engineering, Media Analytics, & Architecture, Ash has served multiple leadership roles in driving large data teams in high growth environments across Novartis, Warner Music Group, Sony Music, and iHeartRadio. His experience with Snowflake spans over 8yrs and is one who consistently utilizes it to its max potential.

Max De Marzi is addicted to graphs. He spent 8 years at Neo4j, made the switch to AWS Neptune and is now with working with graph at Relational AI. He is a blogger and an open source contributor, both activities which stem from passion: teaching people about graphs. He is always open to talk graphs, always learning, and nothing thrills him more than finding easy graph solutions to hard relational problems. He has been helping people get to the "graph epiphany" for over a decade. He is an avid graph database modeler, leveraging his knowledge of mechanical sympathy and experience to deliver dozens of graph uses cases over the years.

title: Best Practices for Right-Sizing Your Apache Parquet™ Files for Apache Iceberg™
abstract: In this session we present TPC-DS benchmarking results on Iceberg tables with varying sizes of Parquet files, to help you optimize your lake house. We also show how we generate the files and analyzed the results.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Christoph is a Snowflake Field CTO, focusing on data engineering and data lakes. He has experienced the industry as a customer, partner and vendor. In his previous role as Director of Engineering for a video streaming service, he procured Snowflake as the center piece of his data platform and thus became Snowflake’s customer number eight in Germany. Christoph believes that we make better decisions with data and creates data platforms to bring that ability to as many people as possible. He has a keen interested in the interplay of technology and the workflows of engineering organisations.

I am a Solutions Consultant at Snowflake, serving as an RSA for three global organizations. In this role, I guide them through their Snowflake implementation journey while helping establish best practices. With a strong background in data and AI/ML, I provide tailored solutions and actionable insights to address diverse business needs.

title: Competing at the Rate of Learning with AI
abstract: Explore how Stanley Black & Decker is leveraging AI to turn data into decisions in minutes not days, embedding intelligence across operations to enhance agility, efficiency and decision-making. Join Prakash Holalkere, Sr Director, Enterprise Architecture & Strategy, Data & AI at Stanley Black & Decker, and Paul Blankley, co-founder and CTO at Zenlytic, as they share their insights on how utilizing AI-driven insights and automation is accelerating innovation, optimizing its supply chain and improving customer experiences.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Paul is the co-founder / CTO of Zenlytic, an intelligent analytics platform that built Zoë, an AI data coworker so anyone can ask questions in natural language and get reliable insights, fast. He's worked in data for 10+ years and is passionate about all things data and AI. He has a master's from Harvard in Data Science, and he lives in Denver, CO, where he spends his free time snowboarding, running, and rock climbing.

Prakash is an Enterprise Architect for Data & AI at Stanley Black & Decker (SBD), a global leader in tools, storage, & industrial solutions. He leads enterprise-wide strategy for data architecture, governance, observability, and generative AI across various tech stacks such as Snowflake, Azure OpenAI and Google Vertex AI, and guiding build-vs-buy decisions for emerging technologies. Prior SBD, Prakash spent over 15 years in business and data consulting roles at SAP, Infosys, and Accenture. At Accenture, he was a Managing Director in the Applied Intelligence practice leading the augmented insights 150+ person group. He is based in San Diego, California.

title: Enforcing Domain-Specific Boundaries for RAG Applications Using Policy Alignment
abstract: Enterprise applications typically block sensitive topics like hate, violence or misinformation, but different departments may have unique policies — e.g., HR might allow finance queries, while IT doesn't. Without proper guardrails, AI applications risk responding to out-of-scope queries, leading to compliance issues. This session introduces a policy alignment framework for AI apps to enforce boundaries and prevent misuse. Learn how to implement app-specific policies seamlessly in Snowflake, using SPCS for containerized deployments, Model Registry for versioned policy enforcement, and compute pools for scaling and securing AI workloads.

speakers.bio: Aditya is a Senior ML Scientist at Snowflake specializing in securing and scaling Gen-AI applications for enterprise use. His current work includes detecting AI-specific threats and enforcing policy alignment in RAG-based applications to ensure compliance and relevance. His primary interests revolve around addressing the complexities of scaling machine learning (ML) systems, real-time monitoring of ML systems, assessing model/system performance, as well as the development, deployment, and ranking of ML models and results. He also has experience in anomaly detection, NLP, time series forecasting, and real-time bidding (RTB) for ads.
Devika Baddani is an ML Scientist at Snowflake, working in the areas of AI Security, adversarial robustness, prompt integrity and responsible AI

title: Extracting Actionable Electronic Health Record Insights with Cortex AI
abstract: We will present a flexible data processing and display framework that enables extracting any intelligent insight desired from our EHR using AI. The backend features a recomposable pipeline builder, allowing users to select patient populations, attach additional records, filter out irrelevant patients, and most importantly, achieve intelligent processing with AI. This system works in harmony with the front end, providing quick access to trustworthy, traceable, AI-generated insights.

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)

speakers.job_title: Director of Data Science and AI
speakers.bio: Rob is a data science, machine learning, and AI leader with a background in engineering and analytics. He began his career designing nuclear submarines for the US Navy, developing a passion for solving unique and complex problems with data. Rob has extensive experience in applying data science, machine learning, and AI in highly regulated industries, such as defense and healthcare. Rob heads the Artificial Intelligence Center of Excellence at Corewell Health, a centralized unit overseeing the ethical and effective adoption of AI across the organization. He is a strong advocate for transforming healthcare for the better with data science and AI.

Dr. Kyle Jero is an innovator, problem solver, data scientist, and developer specializing in generative AI. After earning a PhD in Particle Astrophysics, he has held data science positions in weather instrumentation, healthcare provider data, bicycle manufacturing, and has now returned to healthcare at Corewell Health. At Corewell Health, Kyle provides technical leadership on AI across initiatives in the care, coverage, and corporate spaces. He is an advocate for using technology to help people and believes AI offers an opportunity to do more with less.

title: How ServiceNow Powers Cybersecurity Analytics with Snowflake
abstract: ServiceNow struggled with the challenge of managing fragmented data across multiple systems, inefficient processes and a costly legacy analytics system. But after adopting Snowflake, the security organization transformed how it collects, centralizes and leverages its data to drive smarter, faster decisions. This session offers practical guidance on using Snowflake to streamline operations, optimize costs and enhance decision-making. Join to learn how Snowflake can help transform your organization into a data-driven powerhouse.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: David Rider is a data-driven security leader at ServiceNow who has built a robust analytics function by leveraging Snowflake. Under his leadership, his security organization has transformed from having minimal visibility into key metrics and risk profiles to gaining a comprehensive understanding of security risks across the company—along with clear accountability measures. By centralizing security data in Snowflake, his team has proactively addressed gaps to prevent customer-impacting outages, identified security vulnerabilities across the organization, and accelerated NNACV in partnership with the Customer Security & Trust team.

title: Improving Your Snowflake Investment via Generative AI and Reinforcement Learning
abstract: Snowflake has transformed data storage and analysis, but as usage scales, teams struggle with performance, cost and efficiency. This talk explores how generative AI and reinforcement learning (RL) enhance Snowflake by automating warehouse optimization, improving observability and refining query performance. LLM-powered query rewriting reduces compute costs while making data more accessible. RL-driven systems dynamically scale resources, optimize queries and minimize waste. AI-driven observability provides deep insights to detect inefficiencies. Attendees will learn AI strategies to boost performance, visibility and value while reducing costs.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Prior to founding Keebo, Barzan was an Associate Professor of Computer Science at the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor. He has spent the last 15 years of his career conducting award-winning research at the intersection of machine learning and database systems, across Univ. of Michigan, MIT, UCLA and several companies that have commercialized his research.

title: NTT DOCOMO's Data Mesh Powering Japan's Largest Mobile Network
abstract: NTT DOCOMO, Japan's top telecom provider, tackles the challenge of managing mobile network data. This session highlights our Data Mesh architecture built on Snowflake, showcasing Apache Iceberg's adoption to separate storage and compute for better performance and cost efficiency. We'll explore how Snowflake's Internal Marketplace enhances data sharing and governance, meeting stringent telecom security needs. Gain insights into building scalable platforms and managing telecom data effectively.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Since joining NTT DOCOMO, Inc., I have mainly been involved in the development of big data (petabytes of data) analysis infrastructure. Currently, I am committed to transforming our internal data exchange from the current Centralized Data Lake Architecture to a Decentralized Data Architecture (Data Mesh) to enable more sophisticated and data-driven mobile network operations.

I joined NTT DOCOMO, Inc. in 2001, where I engaged in research and development as well as standardization for WCDMA, LTE, and LTE-Advanced. Since 2014, I have been involved in the research and development of big data analysis and the application of machine learning for network operations. After studying in the MIT Sloan Fellows MBA Program in 2019, I am currently responsible for the development and operation of NTT DOCOMO’s big data infrastructure, IDAP/Minaden.

title: Running Apache Iceberg™ for Financial Workloads at Affirm
abstract: Apache Iceberg unlocks the complex time travel requirements and intensive data validation needs that Affirm has as a financial company. Iceberg's write-once / read-anywhere paradigm simplifies Affirm’s governance by minimizing existing datasets — ensuring there is only ever one logical copy of a given dataset, complete with a validation history, and tag-based time travel watermarks. Join this talk to learn more about the way Affirm leverages Snowflake’s Open Catalog to power our Iceberg implementation, solving both operational and analytic challenges in the business.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Ankit Singh combines deep experience in data processing with a contextual awareness of the BNPL industry to help drive technical decision making at Affirm. A part of Affirm's Data Storage & Services (DSS) technical leadership team. Technical background in OLAP and OLTP storage infrastructure platforms and Data Engineering services. Focused on building and optimizing the critical data systems that power Affirm's financial products. Data Storage & Services (DSS) manages all data related operational and analytical infrastructure needs at Affirm. Storage systems including OLTP and OLAP, batch processing capabilities, and enterprise data governance.

title: Scaling Snowflake with Terraform: Building a Self-Service Data Platform
abstract: The key to success in data-driven management lies in empowering data owners to autonomously build infrastructure. However, organizations often face bottlenecks due to complex infrastructure setup and access management. Through two and a half years of experience with Snowflake’s Terraform provider, we have achieved the delegation of authority to data owners while maintaining governance. We will share our practical approach that improved both stability and agility, along with key insights from our journey.

speakers.bio: I joined Zucks, a division of CARTA HOLDINGS (formerly VOYAGE GROUP), as a new graduate in 2018. I began my career as an application engineer working on an advertising delivery platform, and later established my expertise as a data engineer through a project that renovated our company's reporting infrastructure. Currently at CARTA MARKETING FIRM, I leverage my data engineering knowledge to help realize comprehensive data-driven management, from business strategy planning to data infrastructure development. I continue to contribute to the technical community as a Snowflake DataSuperheroes for 2024-2025.
Joined CARTA HOLDINGS as a new graduate in 2023. Currently working as a data engineer at CARTA MARKETING FIRM. Focusing on enhancing the robustness and usability of data infrastructure.

title: So, Are We Still Doing a Semantic Layer?
abstract: The semantic layer is essential, and it must be vendor-neutral and seamlessly integrate with your existing tech stack. Data modeling may be everything, broken or completely transformed by AI — perhaps all of these are true. In this session, Pedram Navid, Head of DevRel + Marketing at Dagster Labs, and Colin Zima, CEO at Omni, will have a candid discussion about the state of the semantic layer — diving into what we should keep, what we should throw out, and what we should bet on for the future of data modeling.

speakers.bio: Pedram Navid leads the marketing and developer relations team at Dagster, helping build the future of data orchestration. He has an extensive background in data science and data engineering, having worked at both large enterprises and fast-growing startups. Outside of data engineering, Pedram spends his time birdwatching, cooking, and enjoying time with his twin girls.

Colin Zima is the co-founder and CEO of Omni, bringing together the best aspects of everything in the BI landscape into one tool. Prior to starting Omni, Colin was Chief Analytics Officer and VP of Product at Looker, which was acquired by Google for $2.6b, a data leader at HotelTonight, a statistician at Google, and a credit structurer at UBS.

title: Building an Agentic Future
abstract: Hear from leading faculty from UC Berkeley and Carnegie Mellon about their vision for an agentic future informed by their journeys from fundamental research to widely used open source projects and innovative startups. Learn about the latest innovations with coding agents, data agents, agent safety and the interplay of reasoning models and agents. Hear also about their experiences working with developer communities through their online courses and open source projects.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Anupam Datta joined Snowflake as part of the acquisition of TruEra where he served as Co-Founder, President, and Chief Scientist from 2019-2024. Datta was on the faculty at Carnegie Mellon University from 2007-2022, most recently as a tenured Professor of Electrical & Computer Engineering and Computer Science. Datta's current research focuses on Trustworthy AI, spanning evaluation, explainability, fairness, and adversarial robustness of ML models and GenAI applications. These research results have had a significant impact on products at TruEra and Snowflake. His past research included work in privacy and security. Datta obtained Ph.D. (2005) and M.S. (2002) degrees from Stanford University and a B.Tech. (2000) from IIT Kharagpur, all in Computer Science.

speakers.job_title: Associate Professor at Carnegie Mellon University and CEO at Swan AI
speakers.bio: Matt Fredrikson is an Associate Professor at Carnegie Mellon University's School of Computer Science, where his research focuses on achieving safety and security objectives in systems that rely on artificial intelligence components. He has worked on methods for uncovering vulnerabilities and privacy breaches in deep learning models, and developing methods to defend them against adversarial threats in real-world deployments. His work on these problems has earned several best paper awards awards at security and privacy conferences, and the USENIX Test of Time Award in 2024. Matt is also the co-founder and CEO of Gray Swan AI, a startup dedicated to providing robust assessments and safeguards for organizations deploying AI in diverse and demanding environments.

speakers.job_title: Associate Professor at Carnegie Mellon University and Chief Scientist at All Hands AI

speakers.bio: Dawn Song is a Professor in Computer Science at UC Berkeley and Co-Director of Berkeley Center for Responsible Decentralized Intelligence. Her research interest lies in AI and deep learning, security and privacy, and decentralization technology. She is the recipient of various awards including the MacArthur Fellowship, the Guggenheim Fellowship, the NSF CAREER Award, the Alfred P. Sloan Research Fellowship, the MIT Technology Review TR-35 Award, ACM SIGSAC Outstanding Innovation Award, and more than 10 Test-of-Time Awards and Best Paper Awards from top conferences in Computer Security and Deep Learning. She has been recognized as Most Influential Scholar (AMiner Award), for being the most cited scholar in computer security. She is an ACM Fellow and an IEEE Fellow. She obtained her Ph.D. degree from UC Berkeley. She is also a serial entrepreneur and has been named on the Female Founder 100 List by Inc. and Wired25 List of Innovators.

Aditya Parameswaran is an Associate Professor in Computer Science at UC Berkeley. He works in the broad area of human-centered data science, developing usable, robust, scalable, and intuitive data tooling. Multiple open-source tools developed in his group have received thousands of GitHub stars (including Modin, Lux, IPyFlow, DocETL)---and have been downloaded tens of millions of times overall across a wide spectrum of industries. Aditya has published 100+ papers in top-tier venues in data management and databases, human-computer interaction, and visualization, with multiple best paper awards. His research was commercialized as a startup, Ponder, in 2021, where he served as Co-founder and President, before its acquisition by Snowflake. Aditya has received the Alfred P. Sloan Research Fellowship, VLDB Early Career Research Contributions Award, the ARO Young Investigator Program Award, the NSF CAREER Award, the TCDE Rising Star Award, along with other recognitions. His website is at http://adityagp.net.

title: AI, Automation and Snowflake: Build Smarter Data Pipelines in Minutes
abstract: Short on time but eager to try Snowflake’s latest features? Join us for practical takeaways on building and managing your data pipelines in Snowflake without breaking a sweat. Learn how Coalesce’s data transformation platform makes it easy to build and automate data pipelines in minutes — no coding needed. You'll learn how to: Speed up data pipeline development by 10x with ready-made transformation templates Take advantage of Snowflake capabilities like Dynamic Tables, Streams & Tasks, and more Leverage AI by operationalizing the power of Snowflake Cortex Find and understand your data instantly with AI-powered data cataloging

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: John Gontarz is a seasoned Chief Data Architect at Coalesce with extensive expertise in data analytics, business intelligence, and cloud technologies. Known for his strategic vision and ability to align business objectives with technical solutions, John has established himself as an industry leader. With a distinguished career spanning roles at Coalesce.io, Snowflake, SAP America, and various consultancies, John has consistently delivered innovative solutions for enterprise clients. He specializes in building strong partnerships across diverse business sectors while implementing cutting-edge data strategies.

title: Best Practices for Boosting Your Snowflake Query Performance
abstract: Dive deep into the Snowflake query lifecycle and master techniques to boost performance. Learn actionable insights to enhance workloads by pinpointing and resolving common bottlenecks using Snowflake’s latest features for peak efficiency. Apply practical tips for troubleshooting and optimizations while gaining a detailed understanding of Snowflake’s compiler and optimizer. The expert insights and best practices shared in this session will transform your query performance!

speakers.bio: Senior Principal Software Engineer and Tech Lead in the Global Technical Support Engineering Team, 23 years of working experience in the Database and Data Cloud including 14 years on a traditional database and 9 years on Snowflake, with both hands-on development and customer-facing experience, seasonal technical skills specific to database schema design, query performance tuning and optimization, virtual warehouse workload tuning/design, database migration, ELT/ETL process, Role-Based Access Control expertise, and across-cloud/across-region data and account replication, etc.

Jiaqi Yan is a Principal Software Engineer at Snowflake Computing. He is an Architect in Snowflake’s Platform and Infrastructure Engineering group. Prior to joining Snowflake, Jiaqi worked as a Senior Member of Technical Staff for Oracle Database’s Query Engine.

title: Designing and Deploying Applications in Snowpark Container Services
abstract: Snowpark Container Services extends the Snowpark runtime in Snowflake to allow for flexible compute environments to serve a variety of use cases. This session will cover key strategies for securely deploying and scaling applications while ensuring seamless connectivity and performance. Additionally, we will explore CI/CD best practices to streamline application development, deployment and updates.

speakers.job_title: Architect, Data Apps, Field CTO Office

speakers.bio: Have been in the data industry for over 20 years helping businesses realise the value from their data

title: Easily and Cost-Efficiently Managing Data Transformation Pipelines on Snowflake
abstract: Data engineers often face the challenge of creating complex data transformation pipelines while balancing infrastructure overhead, debugging inefficiencies and cost control. Attendees will learn best practices for building scalable and cost-efficient data transformation pipelines with built-in telemetry enhancing monitoring and debugging capabilities. By leveraging automation and managed services in Snowflake, engineers can focus on delivering insights rather than maintaining infrastructure. Join us to discover how to simplify data pipeline management, optimize costs and improve reliability in modern data ecosystems.

speakers.bio: Arthur leads Snowflake’s Data Engineering Experiences team, which helps customers author, deploy, and monitor data pipelines in Snowflake.

Manuela is a Senior Product Manager leading Snowflake Tasks and Orchestration aimed at making it easier for users to cost-effectively and reliably schedule and monitor data pipelines on Snowflake. She is passionate about delivering seamless, end-to-end platform products accelerating the productivity of data professionals. Manuela brings 20 years of experience building large scale data and ML platforms, and distributed data integration systems.

title: How a Consumer Health Company Uses Snowflake to Optimize Advertising Performance
abstract: As a global leader in consumer health with iconic brands like Johnson’s Baby and Tylenol, Kenvue taps into Snowflake to fine-tune its media strategy. By integrating DMA-level media spend data and advanced machine learning models, Kenvue achieves an unprecedented level of data granularity. This enables more precise allocation of their media budget. The company has realized significant total cost of ownership (TCO) savings by reducing data processing costs and eliminating manual ETL processes through Snowflake data sharing. Moreover, the workload of data engineers has been reduced by 3x, allowing them to focus on advanced analytics and data-driven decision-making.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: As a Data Products and Analytics leader, I have 10 years of experience in various roles around marketing data across both, large CPG companies and technological start-ups. My mission is to implement the philosophy of marketing data as a high-quality, reliable, scalable, and reusable product that meets the needs of hundreds of internal customers across the global Kenvue organization.

As a Senior Data Scientist with 12 years of experience, I excel in leading data-driven initiatives and driving strategic product development. My expertise spans consumer advanced analytics, digital marketing, media, and campaign performance optimization. With a strong background in AI and machine learning, I have successfully managed cross-functional projects, delivering impactful insights and fostering innovation.

title: Lunch (TH)
abstract: Lunch (TH)

title: Mastering Customer 360: Connect, Enrich and Validate with Confidence
abstract: Learn how to unlock a true customer 360 by unifying disparate data into a single connected, customer profile with TransUnion’s native identity resolution on Snowflake. TransUnion enables businesses to gain deep insights, personalize marketing and enhance engagement on demand all while eliminating the movement of first-party data. Join us to see how leading companies are gaining deeper insights, personalizing their marketing and enhancing engagement to transform their business.

speakers.bio: Doug Feit is Vice President of Channel Management at TransUnion, he leads channel partnerships and sales alliances across TransUnion’s Marketing, Fraud and Contact Center Business. Doug has over 20 years of experience helping customers leverage data and identity-based solutions the help solve their business needs. In his role, Doug and his team lead relationships with 100+ TransUnion partners that provide complementary solutions and enable more streamlined execution of customer use cases.

Mindi Grissom is the Senior Director of Product Marketing at TransUnion, where she leads go-to-market strategy for cloud-native marketing solutions. With deep expertise and years spent in data, she helps companies activate a true customer 360 by unifying data across silos. At the intersection of data, identity, and the cloud, Mindi works to empower brands to drive smarter marketing, analytics and customer experiences at scale.

title: Meet and Greet with Lukas Biewald (Founder, Weights & Biases)
abstract: Swing by for an Ask Me Anything with Lukas Biewald, Founder of Weights & Biases and one of the leading voices in applied AI. From scaling ML teams to building tools for the frontier of AI development, Lukas brings sharp insights and honest takes. Bring your questions, ideas and curiosity — no slides, just real talk.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Lukas Biewald is the CEO and co-founder of Weights & Biases, the AI developer platform. He also co-founded Figure Eight (formerly CrowdFlower), a pioneer in the data-labeling space. Figure Eight was acquired by Appen (APX) in 2019. Lukas has dedicated his career to optimizing AI workflows, teaching AI practitioners, making artificial intelligence more accessible to all, and occasionally tinkering with robots.

title: Serving Fresh and Lightning-Fast Food to Millions of Swiggy Users with Snowflake
abstract: Swiggy, India's top food and quick commerce platform, serves 680+ cities with 200k+ restaurants and a 500k+ delivery fleet, completing 3.5B+ orders. Powering personalized recommendations, route optimization and real-time decisions for 17M+ monthly users, Swiggy uses Snowflake as its data infrastructure backbone. Discover how they achieved 20% faster query performance for seamless peak demand handling, boosting customer experience and retention. Learn about their in-house text-to-SQL gen AI solution, Hermes, leveraging Snowflake to democratize data access for nontechnical users.

speakers.bio: I am a Principal Engineer at Swiggy, leading large-scale technical initiatives and architectural strategies to build optimized, high-performance systems. With expertise in scaling platforms and solving complex engineering problems, I focus on delivering efficient, resilient solutions. I’ve driven initiatives in GenAI and machine intelligence, and play a key role in fostering an AI-driven culture. Passionate about mentoring, innovation, and collaboration, I also engage with the tech community through talks, workshops, and knowledge-sharing efforts.

title: Smarter Queries, Lower Costs: LLM-Powered Query Advisor
abstract: Discover how our Snowflake Native App leverages generative AI through Snowflake Cortex AI functions to intelligently optimize query performance. By addressing challenges such as inconsistent query quality, repeated execution on suboptimal warehouses and unnoticed query complexity, this solution empowers teams to streamline analytics workflows — no deep SQL expertise needed. This session explores how AI-driven insights and automation, powered by Cortex AI and Snowflake Procedures, deliver real-world value. Learn how organizations are achieving greater efficiency, reducing costs and scaling data operations with intelligent, self-optimizing query experiences.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Frederik has been working with Snowflake since its early days in the EMEA region. Along with his team, he's led several Snowflake greenfield and migration projects, including the successful replacement of the largest Teradata instance in Europe with Snowflake. Currently, he serves as the Lead Snowflake Architect for the Commercial Data Platform of a global pharmaceutical company.

I’m a Data in The New evangelist and technology leader helping enterprises unlock the full potential of their data with Snowflake. As a certified Snowflake Data Superhero, I architect and scale future-ready, multi-cloud platforms, combining deep business insight with hands-on expertise in enterprise data strategy. With a strong focus on innovation, I work at the intersection of data and AI—leveraging Snowflake Cortex and GenAI capabilities to power next-gen applications, intelligent automation, and hyper-personalized experiences. A speaker, author, and patent holder, I’m passionate about shaping the future of data by enabling people, platforms, and ideas to thrive.

title: Snowflake Openflow: A Technical Deep Dive
abstract: With Snowflake's integration service, Snowflake is providing enterprises with open, extensible and secure data integration capabilities that enable real-time, scalable and bi-directional data movement. Powered by Apache NiFi, this service offers customers the option to use a self-contained data plane in Snowpark Container Services, as well as the ability to run BYOC data planes in the customer’s cloud VPC. We will dive into the platform architecture in this technical session.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Sam joined Snowflake as a Senior PM with the Datavolo acquisition, where he served as CTO and led Product and Field Engineering teams. Prior to that Sam was at Google Cloud and led Customer Engineering teams supporting ISVs on GCP in the areas of integration, data analytics, and ML. Sam has been close to data engineering and data architecture for his entire career and focuses on helping customers solve tough challenges to support continued innovation to inform better analytics, forecasting, and decision-making.

title: Streamlining Cross-Engine Governance for Apache Iceberg Tables
abstract: As data lakes grow, seamless access and strong governance are crucial. However, governance for Apache Iceberg to-date has often ended up fragmented, forming inconsistencies and complex integrations. Apache Polaris simplifies governance for multi-engine architectures with a unified access control layer that integrates with Apache Spark, Trino, Snowflake and more. Join this session to learn more about how Polaris makes this possible along with demos, updates and ways to join the community.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Apache Polaris Committer Apache Iceberg PMC Apache Hadoop PMC

Scott is a Product Manager at Snowflake, focused on data lakes and data governance with Apache Iceberg and Apache Polaris (incubating). Over the past 8 years, he has had various product marketing roles at Snowflake and Tableau in Seattle and Bellevue, Washington. Scott is originally from Orlando, Florida and a University of Central Florida alumnus.

title: What's New: Faster Insights with Snowflake Standard Warehouse - Gen2 and Adaptive Compute
abstract: Learn how Snowflake is innovating to provide faster, easier to use warehouses with better price/performance through Standward Warehouse- Gen2 and Adaptive Compute. Join us for new product and feature overviews, best practices and live demos.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: PD Dutta is a product manager at Snowflake responsible for virtual warehouses, which is the primary compute model for running customer workloads. Prior to joining Snowflake, PD held product management roles at Microsoft Azure, AWS and NetApp.
Rudi Leibbrandt is a Principal Product Manager at Snowflake, and is focused on the core Snowflake query platform, and loves talking about SQL, performance, and data types.

title: 2025 Snowflake Startup Challenge Finale
abstract: Experience the 2025 Snowflake Startup Challenge Finale, held during Snowflake Dev Day 2025 in San Francisco. This annual competition draws hundreds of early-stage startups Powered by Snowflake, all vying to fuel AI innovation, gain global exposure and compete for up to $1 million in investments from Snowflake Ventures. Now in its fifth year, the Snowflake Startup Challenge — sponsored in partnership with the New York Stock Exchange — continues to spotlight and invest in the most innovative startups building on Snowflake. Join us as the top three finalists present their groundbreaking solutions to an expert panel of judges, leading to the exciting announcement of this year’s grand prize winner.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Sarah Guo is the Founder and a Partner at Conviction, an AI-native venture capital firm founded in 2022. Prior, she was a General Partner at Greylock. She has been an early investor and partner to 50+ companies including Figma, Harvey, Sierra, HeyGen, Mistral, Cognition, Stackblitz and more. Sarah is from Wisconsin, has four degrees from University of Pennsylvania, and lives in the Bay Area with her husband and three kids. She co-hosts the AI podcast "No Priors" with Elad Gil.

Ryan Green joined Snowflake in March of 2022, and launched an independent news and thought leadership network, Data Cloud Now, where he currently serves as Anchor. In this capacity Ryan engages with industry executives, thought leaders and visionaries. Formerly Ryan spent 10 years at the New York Stock Exchange. In his prior role as Senior Correspondent for the NYSE, Ryan was responsible for hosting, scripting, and co-producing IPO programing and Bell celebrations, which included interviewing CEOs, entrepreneurs, disrupters and business icons. While at the NYSE Ryan controlled Wall Street’s most visible asset, the Opening and Closing Bell.

Benoit Dageville co-founded Snowflake and currently serves as President of Products. He oversees the company’s technical strategy and leads a team of engineers, product managers, and designers who are making data easily accessible and actionable to everyone. Benoit is a leading expert in parallel execution and self-tuning database systems. Prior to founding Snowflake, Benoit was with Oracle for over 10 years as a lead architect for parallel execution in Oracle RAC and a key architect in the SQL Manageability group. Prior to Oracle, Benoit worked at Bull Information Systems. He helped define the architecture and lead database performance efforts for Bull’s parallel systems. Benoit has a PhD in Computer Science with a focus in Parallel Database Systems from Université de Paris VI and is a named inventor on more than 270 patents.

Denise serves as Snowflake’s Chief Marketing Officer and has been with the company since 2016. Denise oversees worldwide marketing initiatives, and has played a pivotal role in driving growth and scaling Snowflake's revenue from $1 million to over $2 billion. Denise has over 25 years of technology marketing experience at high-growth companies. Prior to joining Snowflake, she served as CMO for Apigee, an API platform company that went public in 2015 and Google acquired in 2016. She began her career at collaboration software company Genesys, where she built and led a global marketing organization. Denise also helped lead Genesys through its expansion to become a successful IPO and acquired company. Denise holds a BA in Business Administration and Economics from Stockholm University, and holds an MBA from Georgetown University.

Jon Herrick is the Chief Product Officer for the New York Stock Exchange, a part of Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE: ICE), responsible for product development and innovation across all areas of the exchange’s business. In his role, Jon oversees the NYSE’s transactions, ETF Listings and market data businesses, driving competitive strategy and distribution.

title: AstraZeneca's Data Engineering and Analytics Pipelines for Genomics Data
abstract: AstraZeneca’s Snowflake-powered molecular data warehouse operationalizes FAIR (findable, accessible, interoperable, reusable) principles to address challenges in molecular data management. This architecture harmonizes and governs data, enabling analytics-ready data products for ML, BI and self-service pipelines. Features like metadata management, policy-based access control (PBAC), and robust governance foster collaboration, reusability and faster insights, driving R&D innovation at scale.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Mark is a senior IT professional, leading projects through the full SDLC across sectors like utilities, government, finance, and aerospace & defense. His interests in computer science, software engineering, and life sciences led him to focus on genomics, where he is committed to optimizing architectures using modern data stack to efficiently manage the vast amounts of genomic data. Leveraging Snowflake, he has delivered Data Hubs to support researchers’ key value stream from sample collection to insight.

title: Best Practices for AI Governance and Security in Snowflake
abstract: To effectively govern your AI models end to end, you need visibility and controls across the entire data lifecycle. This includes ensuring data quality for model inputs, using data lineage to track dependencies and workflows, and enforcing safeguards on model outputs. Join this session to learn best practices for governing models with Snowflake — improving data integrity, increasing transparency into your AI pipeline, and ensuring responsible AI deployment.

speakers.job_title: Security and Governance Field CTO Office
speakers.bio: Seth has over 20 years experience in the field of Cybersecurity data governance and data privacy. He covered both the public sector, and highly regulated industry globally. In August 2020, Seth is currently working as global Field CTO, focussing on the security, security data lake, data governance, and data privacy
Pino Suliman is a Senior Product Manager on Governance team at Snowflake, focusing on data and AI observability.

title: Building Open Pipelines: Choose Your Own Adventure
abstract: This session will show customers how to build end-to-end open pipelines in Snowflake with Snowflake Open Catalog and integration service! Join us to choose from open architecture and pipeline options, and you get to decide what we build with. During the demo, we will build an integreation pipeline to ingest data from a source of your choice into an Iceberg table in Snowflake, transform it, register the tables in Open Catalog, and query the data from both Snowflake and a third-party query engine. And here's the real kicker: The audience get to pick the direction we go. Don't miss out this fun, demo session with us.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.job_title: Principal Architect, Data Engineering, Global Field CTO Office
speakers.bio: Jeremiah is a Principal Architect in the Global Field CTO office at Snowflake, leading the Data Engineering workload there. He has an extensive background in data engineering and DevOps, having spent the past 20 years focused on it as a developer, architect, dev manager, consultant leader, sales engineer, and currently Field CTO. He is a frequent speaker and blogger on data architecture and engineering.

title: Building Scalable Asynchronous Event-Driven Apps: A Framework-Agnostic Approach
abstract: This session will cover the challenges and best practices in building asynchronous event-driven applications. We will explore how Snowpipe Streaming can be leveraged to construct hybrid event-driven architectures that integrate async and sync workflows. We will also cover data loss, reconciliation, and solving the "lost ownership scenario" in microservices. Then, we will address scalability’s impact on databases, choosing between analytical and NoSQL options, multi-tenancy, cost and code optimizations, and easing engineers’ transition from batch to distributed streaming.

speakers.bio: Ram has 18 years of experience in building large-scale distributed applications with high-availability. He specializes in building event-driven applications. His favorite area is “data at scale,” whether it be insights, analytics, batch processing, streaming, or machine learning. He believes that “frameworks and languages are temporary, whereas concepts and problems are permanent.” He also specializes in building multi-tenant applications with platform based solutions.

title: ConocoPhillips’ Unified Data Platform: Convergence of IT and OT Data
abstract: ConocoPhillips has migrated all structured data from Teradata and various legacy warehouses to Snowflake. Additionally, they have migrated their time series data/metadata from millions of sensors and 7+ SCADA/Historian systems into Snowflake. This unified platform enables seamless access for analysts, engineering and operations teams across the business, supporting a wide range of use cases. By consolidating data in a single ecosystem, ConocoPhillips enhances decision-making, drives efficiency and fosters collaboration for their 12,000+ employees.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Shailender has been one of the key contributor in the migration of the data warehouse from Teradata to Snowflake at ConocoPhillips (COP). His contributions helped build one of the most cost-effective and secure Snowflake platforms within COP. With over 20 years of experience in various database technologies, Shailender has worked in multiple roles within the data management space. He was a key contributor in multiple splits and acquisitions by different companies in the data management space.

title: Get on the Path to Snowflake Certified Success
abstract: Whether you're looking to expand your Snowflake knowledge and skills or open new career opportunities, being able to demonstrate your expertise through SnowPro Certification will help you stand out in the data community and strengthen your career profile. Join our Snowflake experts as they cover how they best prepare for the Snowflake SnowPro Certification program. Learn about the resources available for the SnowPro certification and discuss strategies for acing the exam.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: For the past six years, I've been immersed in the world of Snowflake as an Education Delivery Manager and Instructor. There's nothing I find more rewarding than seeing individuals gain confidence and proficiency in Snowflake, especially through the certification process. I'm thrilled to be presenting at our annual Summit, where I'll be sharing my insights and practical advice on how you can successfully earn your Snowflake certifications.

title: How Natural Intelligence Moved from Redshift to Snowflake Without Disruption
abstract: Selecting the right data warehouse is crucial. In this session, we’ll explain why we chose Snowflake with Apache Iceberg over Redshift Severless, Dremio, and Starburst, with a focus on performance, cost, migration, and compatibility. We’ll share why & how we migrated from Redshift, AWS Glue, and Hive to a flexible Snowflake+Iceberg architecture, without workflow disruptions. We'll drill down into zero-downtime migration strategies, integration, and best practices for post-migration optimizations

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Experienced data leader and software developer with over a decade in the big data field. Specializes in data infrastructure, cloud analytics, and AI-driven automation. Passionate about coding and building scalable data platforms, and enjoy sharing insights on data modernization with the industry.

title: How to Train Thousands of ML Models Efficiently with Snowflake ML
abstract: Many-model training is a common use case across industries where there is a need to build hyperpersonalized models based on geography or customer. This type of training can be challenging to orchestrate and scale while maximizing resource utilization or trying to stay within budget. In this talk, learn how to leverage Snowflake ML’s distributed training capabilities to achieve the best price performance for many-model training from Container Runtime for Snowflake Notebooks. Join to hear how customer Les Schwab uses Snowflake ML to train and generate inferences on a large-series time series neural network to forecast a promotion’s success.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Jeffrey Geevarghese is a Senior Manager at Snowflake and leads engineering for Snowflake ML. Previously, he led several machine learning teams at Amazon Web Services (AWS). He has a graduate degree in Computer science specializing in Machine Learning from University of Massachusetts, Amherst.

I'm a data scientist with robust experience in machine learning and software development. Currently driving impact in the retail space with extensive past experience in healthcare. A lifelong learner with a passion for exploring new technologies and tackling challenging problems.

title: Revolutionizing Pharma with Data Foundations and Gen AI at Sanofi
abstract: Sanofi, a global leader in the pharma industry, has around 40 unique manufacturing and supply chain (M&S) sites. Since 2022, Sanofi has been delivering its data mesh by building single-source-of-truth GxP data based on Sanofi processes, called data foundations. With over 14 data foundations built in Snowflake, Sanofi has connected the end-to-end M&S value chain, enabling process optimization through AI at scale. The company now aims to democratize data foundations beyond experts with a copilot leveraging gen AI.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.job_title: Global Head of Data Science, M&S
speakers.bio: Yunke Xiang is a global data science leader at Sanofi, where she leads the development, deployment, and scaling of AI-driven solutions across 40+ manufacturing sites worldwide. She also helps shape Sanofi’s long-term data and AI strategy, driving digital transformation across pharma operations. Prior to Sanofi, Yunke spent 10 years at BCG, advising global clients on AI and digital strategy. She holds degrees from MIT and Peking University and is passionate about bridging technology and business to deliver real-world impact.

title: Testing Big Data in Snowflake
abstract: Isolated test environments and integration into our CI/CD pipelines is instrumental in Videoamp's ability to deliver quality cross-media measurement solutions powered by big data at scale. Learn how Videoamp leverages the power of Snowflake to enable testing their big data processing at scale.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: John Adams has been a Snowflake advocate since first becoming a user back in 2016. Lately he has found himself leading efforts to simplify the management of a multi petabyte deployment, and is finding Snowpark to be the special sauce in that recipe.

title: What's New: Build, Distribute & Monetize Scalable Apps with Ease
abstract: Learn how to seamlessly build, distribute, and deploy advanced enterprise-grade AI and data apps with efficiency. Discover built-in tools and programs that streamline commercialization for both you and your customers. Join us for new product and feature overviews, best practices, and live demos.

speakers.bio: Product Manager working on Snowflake's application platform. Focusing on the different type of app deployments in the Snowflake platform like Managed, Connected and Native Apps. Previously worked on large scale application platform products for Meta (Infra and Ads) and Microsoft (serverless computing with Azure Functions and PaaS with Azure App Service). Excited to see what companies & developers can build at Snowflake.
Eric is the product lead for the Snowflake Marketplace and Snow Public Data.

title: Best Practices for Taking Container-Based Workloads to Production with SPCS
abstract: Ready to take your container workload to production in Snowpark Container Services? Learn how to successfully run, scale, secure and troubleshoot your workloads in production, directly from our engineering team. In this session, we’ll take a deep dive into understanding various operational aspects of running and troubleshooting common container service issues such uptime and high availability, as well as tuning startup time, upgrades and scaling behavior. We'll also look at using logging and tracing, and review best practices to securely use services to process and present your data.

speakers.bio: Derek is a lead engineer on Snowpark Container Services.
Jarek is a lead engineer on Snowpark Container Services.

title: Building Agentic Applications in Snowflake
abstract: This hands-on bootcamp teaches participants to design and deploy intelligent data agents using Snowflake Cortex. These agents orchestrate multi-step operations by retrieving and reasoning over structured and unstructured data with Cortex Analyst and Cortex Search. The session covers securely integrating AI into enterprise workflows, managing governance, and driving faster, informed decisions with real-time insights. Attendees will build a functional data agent capable of processing natural language requests, retrieving enterprise data, and generating intelligent responses within Snowflake. Completion of the lab earns a custom bootcamp badge.

speakers.bio: With experience in big data, data science, and machine learning Dash Desai is able to apply 18+ years of full-stack, hands-on software and data engineering skills to help build solutions that solve business problems and surface trends that shape markets in new ways than imagined before. As a Senior Lead Developer Advocate at Snowflake, he is passionate about evaluating new ideas, trends, and helping articulate how technology can address a given business problem. Dash Desai has worked for global enterprises and in agile environments–for tech startups in the Bay Area in varying verticals, such as VoIP, Social Gaming, Digital Health, NoSQL database, and Data Cloud platforms.

Arun Agarwal is currently leading the AI product marketing initiatives at Snowflake. With prior experience at Salesforce, where he contributed to their product marketing team with a focus on data and integration, Arun brings over 15 years of diverse expertise. Holding a PhD in STEM, Arun’s achievements include co-authoring several patents and international scientific research articles. Passionate about cutting-edge technology, he is committed to driving innovation and shaping the future of the industry.

title: Colgate Data Products: Interoperability at Scale with Polaris and Iceberg
abstract: Discover how Colgate is transforming global data management with its "Colgate Data Products" initiative, powered by Apache Polaris (incubating) and Apache Iceberg™. This session explores how Colgate builds scalable, interoperable data ecosystems to drive innovation, streamline operations and enable real-time collaboration. Learn about use cases, technical insights and best practices to unlock the full potential of data for strategic growth and operational excellence.

speakers.bio: James Anderson is the Director of Data Cloud Architecture at Snowflake, focused on helping our most strategic build out their own Data Cloud vision, while maximizing the value of their Data Assets. Prior to Snowflake, James worked in the consulting space, running enterprise scale implementations of Cloud Data Platforms, including Snowflake.

Wayne, the Senior Vice President of Data Platforms, is a strategic leader who drives digital transformation through data capabilities and automation solutions. His expertise in crafting data platform strategies is in alignment with the company’s business objectives, fostering innovation and operational excellence.

title: Deploying Data Pipelines with Snowflake and dbt Labs
abstract: Are you an analytics engineer looking to improve your architecture and DevOps practices? Have you been experimenting with new features like Snowflake Dynamic Tables or is your organization considering new architectural approaches? In this lab, you'll learn: Developer-centric tips for discovering and accessing data products across Snowflake Marketplace, ad-hoc uploads and connecting via Snowpark external access Alternatives to lambda architecture for streaming data sources with Dynamic Tables New materialization options with Dynamic Tables and Apache Iceberg

speakers.bio: Luis Leon is a seasoned data and technology expert with a decade of experience in data analytics, cloud optimization, and sales engineering. He excels at bridging the gap between technical solutions and real-world problems. In his current role as a Partner Solution Architect at dbt Labs he supports technology and implementation partners across the ecosystem.

title: Developing and Deploying SaaS Globally: Powering AI-Driven Retail Insights
abstract: Discover the architecture behind NielsenIQ’s (NIQ) Discover platform, that leverages Snowflake to deliver AI-powered insights and analytics to CPG retailers and manufacturers. Learn how Discover processes point-of-sale and panel-based NIQ datasets across 84 global markets to drive strategic decision-making, uncover growth opportunities and fuel innovation. This session will explore why NIQ chose Snowflake for its scalability, efficiency and value-generating capabilities, and how the platform was designed from the ground up to support organizations of all sizes.

speakers.bio: Brian has been in the data and analytics industry for over 20 years. He started his career doing applied research in mathematics and computer science at the National Security Agency. Since then he has had various technical and product roles in government and companies specializing in data warehousing, NoSQL, and analytics. Brian is currently Global Field CTO focusing on data applications.

Vamsi is a seasoned leader with a proven track record of executing large-scale data transformation projects at NIQ. As the head of the Engineering division responsible for platform services, he recently led the technical execution and delivery of Project Moonshot. This initiative successfully deployed Discover, NIQ's flagship platform for customer analytics in the RMS (Retail Measurement Services) and CPS (Consumer Panel Services) verticals, including Omnisales and Omnishopper analytics across 84 markets. Vamsi brings extensive knowledge of NIQ's core data platform and has experience leading global, multi-cultural teams across multiple regions.

title: Hands-On Workshop: Enterprise Data Modeling for Snowflake Success
abstract: In today’s data-driven landscape, organizations must ensure their Snowflake environments are structured for efficiency, scalability and governance. This hands-on workshop will guide enterprise teams through best practices for data modeling within Snowflake, leveraging SqlDBM’s powerful platform to streamline collaboration, enforce modeling standards and accelerate cloud data initiatives. Learn practical experience in designing and managing enterprise-grade data models that align with Snowflake’s best practices, ensuring consistency, performance and maintainability across their data ecosystem.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Hazal Sener is an Enterprise Data Modeling Expert at SqlDBM with a background in Business Intelligence and Data Warehousing. Her experience in building data transformation pipelines and optimizing SQL queries led her to specialize in data modeling, helping organizations design efficient and scalable data architectures. At SqlDBM, Hazal helps teams make the most of the platform. She works closely with organizations to refine their data modeling strategies, streamline workflows, and simplify complex processes. Through hands-on guidance and best practices, she helps teams integrate data modeling seamlessly into their workflows, ensuring scalability and long-term success.

Eric Ruiz is a Sales Engineer at SqlDBM and has previous experience in Data Engineering, Data Modeling, Data Cataloging and Data Integration. He has a proven track record of helping organizations with everything data and best practices. As a Sales Engineer, Eric collaborates with organizations to refine their data modeling strategies and data modeling frameworks. He is deeply passionate about Data Modeling, promoting Data Governance, and fostering effective Data Practices.

title: How NIB Used Snowflake Cortex AI to Reduce Noise and Manual Work
abstract: Unlock Snowflake's power with Snowflake Cortex AI. We'll share real-world examples, including reducing work item backlogs through intelligent prioritization and automating client data updates from free text across multiple source systems — saving 150 FTE hours by completing tasks in under a week. Additionally, we use Streamlit as an interface for review and quality checks. Learn about our successes, challenges and practical tips for your Snowflake Cortex AI implementation.
