speakers.bio: Pip is a self-confessed data nerd who is passionate about automation, documentation (meta-data) and continually learning new things. With a developer background, she still loves opportunities to get on the tools and explore all the new things. Currently on a journey treating Data as a Product and building out roadmaps, she is enjoying meeting other technical folk and being able to share ideas at conferences.

title: Lessons Integrating Snowflake’s Next-Gen Streaming and Data Engineering platform
abstract: Nexon is always looking to incorporate the latest technologies to build games for its 25M+ active users. To process 45+ billion logs of 100TB+ per day, we leverage new features like Snowpipe Streaming REST API to ingest near real-time data, process it with Snowpark, and deliver value from our data lake with AI. Learn how Nexon engineering works with Snowflake product teams to provide feedback during the entire feature lifecycle to improve the Snowflake platform for our use case and all customers.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.job_title: Data Engineering Team Lead | Snowflake Data SuperHero
speakers.bio: After specializing in distributed file systems, I've engaged in the development and operation of a peta-byte scale data pipeline at Nexon Korea, where I standardize and maintain tens of thousands of game logs to ensure high quality. Currently, I'm leading the technical strategy for Nexon's data integration platform 'Monolake', which introduces modern data architecture to the company.
Engineer at Snowflake's Data Ingestion team, working on Snowpipe Streaming and Kafka Connector.

title: Meet and Greet with Thiago Teixeira and Amanda Kelly (Co-Founders, Streamlit)
abstract: Join us in the GROUP BY Lounge for a special opportunity to meet the co-founders of Streamlit, Thiago Teixeira and Amanda Kelly. Hear the origin story of one of the most beloved open source frameworks for data apps, learn what’s next for Streamlit, and share your own experiences and questions. Whether you’re a dedicated Streamlit user, new to the ecosystem or just curious about the journey behind building tools for developers, this is your chance to connect directly with the visionary team that started it all. Stop by for great conversation, community and inspiration!

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Amanda Kelly serves as Director of Product Experiences at Snowflake, where she oversees all of Snowflake’s product experiences. Prior to Snowflake she was Co-founder and COO of Streamlit (acquired by Snowflake). Amanda also led product and operations for Zoox (acquired by Amazon) and several Google X projects. She has an MBA from Stanford GSB and holds several industry patents.
Big nerd. Co-founder of Streamlit.

title: Nerding Out on Data: Building Reliable Profiles with Snowflake and Segment
abstract: NerdWallet needed more than just good data — they needed reliable customer profiles to drive personalized engagement. In this session, learn how they use Snowflake and Twilio Segment to clean, unify and activate data across teams, turning complex data challenges into streamlined customer experiences that convert.

speakers.bio: Kanza is a senior data engineer with a strong background in software engineering. Over the course of five years at NerdWallet, she developed and enhanced the user data platform and marketing communications platform. She later shifted her focus to architecting near real-time, high-throughput data pipelines that convert raw user financial data into valuable assets for the entire organization. Currently, Kanza plays a key role in managing the infrastructure for the Data Engineering team.
Amy Aldredge is a Staff Data Engineer at NerdWallet, focused on enabling and supporting NerdWallet's data driven operational and analytic use cases.

title: Talk to Your Data: Integrating ChatMCP in the Cimpress Data Mesh with Cortex AI
abstract: Unlock the power of conversational AI within the Cimpress Data Mesh! In this session, we’ll showcase how Cimpress integrated ChatMCP into the Cimpress Data Portal, enabling teams to interact with their data products more intuitively. Learn how Cortex Analyst enhances data accessibility, accelerates insights and aligns with a Data Mesh strategy. Join us for a live demo of the end-state functionality and discover how AI-driven interfaces are revolutionizing data interaction at Cimpress powered by Snowflake Cortex AI.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Michael Theodoulou is a Product Manager specializing in large-scale data infrastructure. At Cimpress, he leads the strategy and execution of the Cimpress Data Platform, a SaaS solution that powers 12 eCommerce businesses globally with self-service, multi-tenant, and API-first capabilities. As a data architect, Michael played a pivotal role in shaping the platform’s evolution, leveraging Snowflake, Looker, Databricks, and custom software to support diverse data strategies, including a data mesh architecture. He is passionate about building scalable platforms, enabling data-driven decision-making, and empowering teams with self-service analytics to drive business impact.

Chris Bova is the General Manager of the Cimpress Data Domain, where he leads a global team delivering shared data infrastructure, personalization capabilities, and analytics expertise to help Cimpress businesses become data-driven organizations. A former software architect, Chris played a central role in Vista’s shift from a traditional BI model to a modern data stack built on Snowflake, Looker, and Databricks. He also helped Cimpress become an early adopter of Data Mesh architecture—what the company first called its “Marketplace for Data.” Chris is passionate about building scalable platforms, enabling self-service analytics, and turning data into business outcomes.

title: The Latest and Greatest on Dynamic Tables
abstract: Come learn the latest features available in Dynamic Tables. You’ll learn about new security and governance features, expanded Iceberg support bringing the power of Dynamic Tables directly to your data lake or lakehouse, and what’s new in observability and performance optimization, among others. You will also hear from a special guest about how Dynamic Tables have delivered business outcomes.

speakers.bio: Cathy Gutman is the Manager of Boston Children's Hospital's Data Engineering team. She has worked at BCH for 15 years and her team is responsible for providing integrated clinical and operational data for its users.

Saras (Kaul) Nowak is a passionate and engaging Principal Product Manager at Snowflake, where they specialize in Data Engineering with a special focus on data transformation including Dynamic Tables. Prior to Snowflake, she led Product Management for various Amazon Web Services (AWS) including Amazon S3 and AWS Internet of Things (IoT) services. Saras is committed to driving product excellence and fostering strong customer engagement through customer empathy and always working to make lives of her customers easier.

title: Unlocking Workload Insights: Observability with Snowflake Trail
abstract: In today's data-driven landscape, visibility into workloads is essential for ensuring performance, reliability and efficiency. Snowflake Trail, Snowflake’s suite of observability tools, allows you to monitor, analyze and optimize diverse data workloads. In this session, we will explore key observability capabilities from auto-instrumentation to real-time monitoring. Through a real-world example, you'll see how Snowflake Trail enables deep observability, helping you enhance performance, troubleshoot issues and drive operational excellence.

speakers.bio: Jason drives key initiatives for the Snowpark developer platform, enhancing its capabilities and user experience. Feel free to ask him about anything pertaining to Snowpark, Python, UDFs, observability, DevOps, etc.

Vaishnavi is a Senior Product Manager at Snowflake, where she leads the Observability Framework, including Snowflake Trail. With a strong background in product management and engineering, she has previously worked at AWS and Facebook, focusing primarily on compute technologies. She holds a Master’s degree in Computer Science from Carnegie Mellon University and has a keen interest in Distributed Systems.

title: What's New: Eliminate Silos with Snowflake Horizon Catalog & Internal Marketplace
abstract: Learn how to drive collaboration acorss your buisness ecosytem with the latest from Snowflake Horizon Catalog, including External Data Discovery, to effortlessly find data both within Snowflake and from external systems. See demos of the latest Internal Marketplace updates, including Organization Profiles and Request for Access, to streamline and accelerate the sharing of data, apps, and AI. Finally, learn how Iceberg Data Sharing and the Egress Cost Optimizer can increase interoperability and data availability while keeping your data sharing costs down.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Sanjay is a Principal Product Manager at Snowflake, overseeing Snowflake’s collaboration and Snowflake Marketplace platform. Sanjay has been at Snowflake since 2022 and is focused on providing a simple, scalable, and easy-to-use data-sharing and collaboration platform that enables the democratization of access to data, data services, and applications across the Data Cloud. Before Snowflake, Sanjay built new products and led teams at large organizations, including AWS and Conviva. He has also served as an executive at venture-backed startups as the VP of Products.

Riccardo Muti is a Principal Product Manager at Snowflake focusing on search & discovery capabilities in Horizon Catalog and Snowflake Marketplace. He has over 15 years’ experience building business intelligence and database platforms. Prior to Snowflake, he held product management roles at Google working on Looker Studio and BigQuery, and at Microsoft working on Power BI and SQL Server Reporting Services.

title: What’s New: Snowflake AI Research
abstract: Discover the latest innovations from Snowflake AI Research across foundation models, agentic technologies and inference systems. Learn how Arctic Text2SQL reasoning models set a new standard in accuracy with best-in-class benchmark performance. See how we’re building intelligent enterprise agents that reason over structured and unstructured data, orchestrate tools and operate with trust. Finally, get a first look at our novel inference and training optimizations and next-generation open-source engine — designed to achieve the elusive trifecta: optimal latency, throughput and cost. Together, these advances enable capable, adaptive and efficient AI systems and applications for enterprise use.

speakers.bio: Samyam Rajbhandari currently leads the inference optimization efforts at Snowflake for reducing latency and cost. Previously, he co-led the design and development of Snowflake Arctic, an innovative foundation model achieving state-of-the-art enterprise intelligence with best-in-class cost efficiency at the time of release. Prior to Snowflake, Samyam was a co-founder and the system architect for DeepSpeed at Microsoft, where he developed transformational system technologies like ZeRO and 3D parallelism for training LLMs, and led inference optimization effort for models like Dalle.2 and Dall.E 3 models from OpenAI.

Yuxiong He is a Distinguished AI Engineer and Research Scientist at Snowflake, leading research on foundation models, agentic systems, and high-performance AI infrastructure for enterprise applications. She and her team built Arctic LLM and Arctic Text2SQL, achieving state-of-the-art accuracy, and developed Arctic’s inference and training stack with industry-leading efficiency. Their work powers Snowflake Intelligence, Cortex Agents, Search, and Inference systems. A strong advocate for open source and open research, she co-founded DeepSpeed at Microsoft, pioneering ZeRO and 3D parallelism. She has published over 100 papers and received best paper awards at SIGIR, ICDE, and WSDM.

title: EVCN on Snowflake: Agile and Intelligent Solutions for 5G Networks
abstract: EVCN on Snowflake empowers businesses to build agile, intelligent Virtual Corporate Networks on public and private 5G infrastructures. By simplifying provisioning and management for 5G-enabled PCs, enterprises can ensure seamless operations and optimized workflows. Advanced analytics and gen AI enhance network reliability, coverage and performance. Learn how Ericsson and Snowflake are transforming enterprise connectivity with scalable, adaptive 5G solutions that drive smarter decisions and minimize downtime.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Naveen Alan Thomas is a Principal Cloud Data Architect at Snowflake (UK) and a member of the Apps Specialist Team. He collaborates with customers and partners to accelerate app development and deployment. With over a decade of experience in data and consulting, he brings deep expertise to his role at Snowflake.

Wassim is a chief architect and an intrapreneur within Ericsson Group Function Group Support (GFGS). His current focus is on building new platforms to enable companies to migrate their IT infrastructures and services to 5G. He is also active in standardization forums and has more than 60 granted patents.

title: Ingestion Best Practices for Snowflake Native and Apache Iceberg™ Tables
abstract: Learn about all the new features and performance improvements that the batch and streaming data ingestion team has released over the last year to both standard Snowflake and Apache Iceberg tables with features such as Schema Detection, Snowflake Performance Index improvements, Replication, and more. Hear from experts on best practices for data ingestion for cost effective and scalable data engineering pipelines. This popular session is bound to impress with updates, demos, and guidance from the engineering team plus a customer story who has put these practices into production saving cost, time, and reducing complexity while increasing reliability and redundancy of data ingestion pipelines.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Manages the Data Ingestion effort at Snowflake
Software Engineer at Snowflake, specializing in data ingestion and Iceberg. Lead developer for Copy and various ingestion features.

title: Scaling Data Architecture to Petabytes from Startup to a $5B Valuation
abstract: Discover how Whatnot scaled their data architecture from startup to processing petabytes in just five years. We'll showcase our implementation of Snowpipe Streaming, Kafka integration and CDC processes, highlighting massive adoption by software engineers and support for dozens of critical product features and data consumer systems. Learn our battle-tested strategies for pipeline management, cost optimization and performance tuning, with real examples from our hyper-growth journey to a $5B valuation.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Emmanuel Fuentes is the Head of Data & AI at Whatnot, leading analytics, machine learning, discovery, and data platform initiatives. With over a decade of experience, he has scaled Whatnot's data & AI systems to support billions in annual GMV. Previously, he worked in roles advancing AI/ML in digital health, medical devices, and consumer marketplaces like GOAT. Emmanuel holds a BS in Electrical Engineering from Rice University, has multiple machine learning patents, and specializes in data architecture and machine learning at growth stage companies.

title: Seamless Cross-Cloud Data Replication at Scale
abstract: Discover Siemens' innovative solution for synchronizing 400 tables across five ERP systems between AWS Ireland and Azure Netherlands using Snowflake's advanced technologies. Our solution harnesses Snowflake Dynamic Tables and private listings, optimizing performance and ensuring interoperability across cloud environments. Gain insights into architecture design, technical challenges and scalable strategies driving both business and technical value.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Daniel is a seasoned IT professional with over 12 years of experience at Siemens, a global technology powerhouse. As a Product Owner for the Siemens Financial Services (SFS) Data Cloud, he delivers a robust, user-friendly data platform leveraging Snowflake's cutting-edge features, enabling seamless data exchange within and outside of the Snowflake ecosystem, along with various Azure services. Daniel's expertise in data management, cloud architecture, and development coordination, combined with his focus on market trends and user feedback, ensures the SFS Data Cloud supports a wide range of reporting and AI use cases while aligning with business objectives.

speakers.job_title: Data Platform Lead | IT Service Manager
speakers.bio: Francisco is responsible for managing a data sharing service across Siemens branches, ensuring that governed practices are maintained, and bridging the gap between those who need the data and those who produce or curate it. Simultaneously, Francisco leads, plans, and designs several Snowflake platform development initiatives and, along with other leads, maintains the roadmap for the Snowflake platform team.

title: Best Practices for securing data with Snowflake Open Catalog
abstract: Security and governance is a top priority in order to unlock the full value of data in open table formats. Join this session to dive into the latest features and best practices using Snowflake Open Catalog, a managed service for Apache Polaris™ (incubating).

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Ashwin Kamath leads the product efforts for Snowflake Open Catalog. Before Snowflake, Ashwin held product management roles at Google and Microsoft, where he worked on observability, streaming analytics technologies and developer experience. Ashwin holds a master’s degree in computer science from University of Illinois, Urbana-Champaign.

title: Building Production-Ready AI Agents
abstract: Weights & Biases co-founder and CEO Lukas Biewald will share practical insights and actionable lessons learned from deploying generative AI agents in real-world production scenarios. Drawing directly from hands-on experience at W&B, Lukas will unpack two detailed case studies: the step-by-step journey of launching an internal chatbot agent in production to reliably serve all customers, and the practical engineering behind the Programmer agent, which achieved top-ranking results on the SWE-bench leaderboard. This session is designed to be deeply practical, providing attendees with concrete advice on evaluating agent performance, overcoming scalability hurdles, managing costs and optimizing generative agents to deliver consistent, measurable value

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Lukas Biewald is the CEO and co-founder of Weights & Biases, the AI developer platform. He also co-founded Figure Eight (formerly CrowdFlower), a pioneer in the data-labeling space. Figure Eight was acquired by Appen (APX) in 2019. Lukas has dedicated his career to optimizing AI workflows, teaching AI practitioners, making artificial intelligence more accessible to all, and occasionally tinkering with robots.

title: Democratize Data Asset Discovery with Snowflake Horizon Catalog
abstract: Virtually every organization’s data is underutilized, in large part because it’s still hard for users to discover relevant data assets. They don’t have good search tools, need to look in different silos, can’t see what they don’t yet have access to, don’t know which assets are the right ones to use, can’t decipher unfamiliar assets, and don’t know who to ask. In this session, you’ll see how Horizon Catalog helps address these issues. Learn about how to use Universal Search, extend search to Iceberg data, “SEO” your metadata, reduce manual metadata management, make metadata discoverable while protecting sensitive data, and curate an internal marketplace of data products.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Riccardo Muti is a Principal Product Manager at Snowflake focusing on search & discovery capabilities in Horizon Catalog and Snowflake Marketplace. He has over 15 years’ experience building business intelligence and database platforms. Prior to Snowflake, he held product management roles at Google working on Looker Studio and BigQuery, and at Microsoft working on Power BI and SQL Server Reporting Services.

title: How LSEG Migrated On-Premises Sybase to Snowflake and Multi-Cloud Distribution
abstract: Learn how LSEG successfully migrated its on-premises Sybase product Datascope Onsite to the cloud with Snowflake, creating Datascope Warehouse. Discover how LSEG expanded a U.S.-only product to the global market by using private listings and cross-cloud autofulfillment. See how entitlements are securely enforced and data is shared in real time. Explore how LSEG scales distribution and collaborates with Snowflake to adopt future roadmap features such as Egress Optimization.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.job_title: Global Head of Pricing and Reference Services
speakers.bio: Kristin Hochstein leads the Pricing and Reference Services business for LSEG. She is an accomplished executive with more than 20 years in the data industry. Kristin previously led entity and regulatory solutions for Thomson Reuters. She also helped launch a legal entity data business for then Reuters and held various roles at Dun & Bradstreet, including Senior Director in the credit risk business. Kristin is the immediate past Chair of ISITC and Executive Sponsor of the Reference Data and Standards Group. She holds degrees with honors including an MBA from Columbia University as well as a B.S. in Business Administration and a B.A in French from the University of Richmond.
Building and operating multicloud data distribution at LSEG.

title: Real-Time Staffing Intelligence With Striim + Snowflake Dynamic Tables
abstract: Hospitals can’t afford delays when it comes to staffing. Patients still need care, and the right clinicians need to be in place — especially during labor disruptions. AMN Healthcare’s batch data processing for staffing and credentialing data wasn’t always up to date, making it harder to respond quickly and efficiently. That’s why AMN implemented Striim’s real-time data streaming and Change Data Capture (CDC) to move data instantly from their application data sources into Snowflake. Now, AMN can track staffing availability in real time, verify credentials faster, and make data-driven decisions without delays.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Mario Melendez is the Director of Data Management & Analytics at AMN Healthcare. He owns over 25 years of experience in Data Management and implementing large ERP applications. He specializes in leading Snowflake, SQL Server and PeopleSoft technology teams. His passions outside of technology include camping with his wife and two young daughters, surfing and Brasilian Jiu Jitsu.

John Kutay is Director of Product & Engineering for Data & AI at Striim, where he leads cross-functional teams building real-time data infrastructure and AI-driven products. With a background spanning software engineering, product management, and venture investing, John brings a multidisciplinary perspective to solving complex data problems. He is also the host of What’s New in Data, a podcast with thousands of global listeners, where he explores emerging trends at the intersection of data, AI, and infrastructure. John has over a decade of experience in streaming data systems, from academic research to driving innovation at Striim.

title: Recruiting Meet and Greet at the GROUP BY Lounge
abstract: Curious about life at Snowflake? Join us for an informal meet and greet with members of the Snowflake team at the GROUP BY Lounge. Whether you’re exploring new career opportunities or just want to learn more about our culture, our teams and the exciting challenges we’re solving, this is your chance to connect directly with recruiters and Snowflake employees in a relaxed setting. Swing by for good conversation, career tips and maybe even a few surprises.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

title: Using Synthetic Data and AI to Protect PII in Development and Testing
abstract: High-quality data applications need high-quality data, but compliance requirements can sometimes limit access by developers to production data. Join this session to understand how Snowflake’s governance capabilities, including synthetic data generation and sensitive data monitoring (with the help of an AI assistant!), can enable developers to work with realistic data — even sensitive data such as PII and PHI — while still following strong governance practices and ensuring sensitive data is not accidentally disclosed or misused.

speakers.bio: Ian is an Engineering Manager at Snowflake, where he supports the Data Governance team. He leads Snowflake's end-to-end sensitive data protection efforts, including Data Classification, Tagging, Policies, and Synthetic Data Generation. He joined Snowflake as part of the acquisition of LeapYear, where he was Head of Engineering.

Raja Balakrishnan leads the product team focused on building data and AI governance products for Snowflake. Prior to joining Snowflake, Raja held product management roles at Box, Rubrik and Cisco, where he worked on multiple data management, security, compliance and governance products. Raja has a master’s degree from Carnegie Mellon University.

title: What's New: Deploy Accurate Conversational Apps in Cortex AI with AI Data Agents
abstract: Learn how to build and deploy accurate conversational apps and bring AI-powered data agents into production with ease and trust across your enterprise. Join us for new product insights, feature overviews, best practices and live demos.

speakers.bio: As a Product Manager on the Snowflake Cortex AI team, Harshal Pimpalkhute leads initiatives across LLM inference and Agentic AI. With a deep understanding of cloud services and software engineering he is passionate about bringing AI innovations to customers.

Will is Director of AI products at Snowflake. Will came to Snowflake via the acquisition of TruEra where he was CEO/Co-Founder. TruEra built market-leading technology for evaluating, testing and monitoring predictive and generative AI applications. Will started working in AI/ML over 14 years ago when he was Chief Product Officer at BloomReach where he led the development of ML-powered commerce search, marketing and SEO software. Prior to BloomReach, Will worked in product management at Clearwell Systems, Juniper Networks and Netscreen/Neoteris. Will is a graduate of Harvard Business School and Dartmouth College.

title: What's New for Managing Organizations at Scale in Snowflake
abstract: Learn how global organization admins on Snowflake can use a range of new organization-level objects to manage Snowflake at scale for their accounts across regions and clouds. Hear about new use cases, such as data sharing and an internal marketplace, that these capabilities enable and hear about what's next on the roadmap.

speakers.bio: Srujana Kandimalla is a Senior Product Manager leading efforts to help customers manage Snowflake at scale. With a decade of experience creating products in Healthcare Financial Technology, Usage-based Billing and now Manageability, she is passionate about eliminating operational inefficiencies and bringing transparency to usage, governance and cost. An Aerospace Engineer from Cornell University, Srujana thrives on tackling intricate problems, and driving innovation at Snowflake.

Abhishek Narang leads Vendor Data Engineering and Data Modeling efforts globally at Goldman Sachs, responsible for helping businesses across the firm identify, license, prototype, and use data to meet their client and business objectives. He is also responsible for co-creating firm's cloud data strategy, partnering with divisional Engineering teams and implementing solutions to migrate existing data to the cloud. Abhishek leads firmwide efforts to provide rapid access to cloud-based vendor data repositories. In addition, he manages the Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) Beacon team, which focuses on identifying and curating ESG data for multiple businesses across the firm

title: Databricks to Snowflake: Migration Tools, Tips and Deep Dive
abstract: Join this session to learn about best practices and real-world examples for migrating data engineering and Spark workloads to Snowflake. We’ll cover legacy vs. Snowflake architecture and migration methods such as Iceberg and Delta direct, and we'll share pros and cons of integrating components such as Unity Catalog, Apache Iceberg™ tables and native Snowflake tables. Plus, we'll explore automated code conversion with the Snowpark Migration Accelerator, workflow orchestration with Snowflake Tasks and key data validation and observability techniques to ensure a smooth migration.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Tom is a Sr. Solutions Architect helping customers migrate workloads to the Snowflake data cloud. Tom has over 39 years experience in the development and data world.

I have been working in technology sector for about 18 years . With a deep understanding of data engineering, software development, and cloud solutions,I have successfully led and contributed to numerous projects that span various industries, including finance, healthcare, and e-commerce.

title: Delta Dental Advances Innovation and Cost Efficiency via Data Collaboration
abstract: Delta Dental Plans Association (DDPA) is transforming its nationwide data management using Snowflake's platform, unifying data across 39 member companies serving 80M+ Americans. The implementation enabled secure data sharing, better collaboration and significant cost savings. DDPA has saved $3M annually in marketing costs and improved patient targeting. The platform's security features protect patient and provider data while enabling advanced analytics and future innovations in dental healthcare.

Covered Topics: Data for good (climate, diversity, health, etc.)

speakers.bio: Matt Rossi is currently the Vice President of Strategy & Architecture for Delta Dental Plans Association and is responsible for establishing and executing a modern technology architecture and digital transformation roadmap for the organization. Matt has spent the last 25 years in roles ranging from engineering, architecture, product management, and executive leadership spanning Health and Life Sciences, Financial Services, E-Commerce, and Consulting. He has worked with organizations ranging from Fortune 500 to startups and has a strong passion for the modern data stack.

title: Feature Flags to Insight: How LaunchDarkly Uses Snowflake to Experiment and Grow
abstract: LaunchDarkly built a warehouse-native experimentation solution that seamlessly integrates with Snowflake while supporting a flexible hybrid approach. This session covers key architectural design choices, from embedding feature flagging and real-time experimentation directly into the AI Data Cloud to balancing in-warehouse processing with the performance needs of external services. We’ll explore the trade-offs between Snowflake Native and connected app deployment models, optimizing for scalability, and ensuring a seamless developer experience. Join us to see how we built an experimentation platform that powers both our customers and our own data-driven innovation.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Principal Architect at Snowflake helping customers create value through partnership and building innovative solutions on Snowflake. Studied applied mathematics and management, previously led a data team at a Snowflake customer, and before that spent years optimizing supply chain decisions and design through math, business acumen, and (of course) data.

title: Five Root Causes of Data Quality Issues
abstract: Through working with hundreds of data teams, we’ve identified the five root causes of data quality issues: input errors, infrastructure failures, incorrect transformations, invalid assumptions and differing definitions. We'll break down each cause with real examples and offer practical advice to detect, resolve and prevent issues using Snowflake and your broader data stack. In addition, we’ll dive into two frameworks for prioritization: knowable vs. unknowable and controllable vs. uncontrollable. You’ll leave with a framework to focus on what matters most: reducing the frequency and severity of data incidents while building greater trust in your data ecosystem.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Steve is Senior Director of Data & Analytics at Anaconda, where he leads internal data efforts. Earlier in his career, Steve was founder & COO at two data-related startups, both through to acquisition. For the past decade, he has focused in on building and leading data teams at growth-stage companies like Anaconda.

Guru is a frontend-engineer-turned-data-engineer and co-founder of Metaplane by Datadog. Having caused his fair share of data issues from messing with event instrumentation, Guru now works towards ensuring that data teams and software teams work in sync—stopping data quality issues before they occur.

title: How Medtronic Built a D&A Governance Operating Model to Deliver Trusted Data
abstract: Medtronic is the world's largest medical device company by revenue, operating in over 150 countries. Launching breakthrough medical technologies for 75+ years, Medtronic has long understood data to be business-critical, now with a goal to be a world leader in governance. Join Greg Baumhauer of Medtronic to learn how true partnership with the business, using Atlan as their governance platform of choice, has ensured Medtronic’s data is documented, owned and governed, and has grown adoption by over 5x in mere months, significantly accelerating time-to-insight.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: With 15+ years of data-driven leadership, Sharif has evolved from running analytics-powered business units at Adobe, to managing teams of data analysts at Asana, to now leading product & corporate marketing at Atlan. Atlan was recently named a Leader in the Forrester Wave for EDCs in 2024, and a Visionary in the Gartner Magic Quadrant for D&A Governance in 2025. Atlan serves hundreds of leading enterprises including GM, FOX, Unilever, Nasdaq, and HubSpot. Having experienced first-hand how data can either accelerate business outcomes or paralyze a company, Sharif is thrilled to help enterprises find, trust, and govern AI-ready data at scale.

Greg Baumhauer is a seasoned professional with over 13 years of experience in data governance at various leadership levels. Currently, he serves as the Atlan Product Owner, supporting data governance solutions and best practices. With more than five years at Medtronic, Greg has successfully implemented enterprise platforms and established operational organizations. He specializes in data and metadata management, security, risk, and compliance. Greg is an active member of the DAMA Minnesota Chapter and holds CISSP (Active) and CISA (Inactive) certifications. He graduated from the University of Minnesota in 1994 and enjoys spending time with his wife, Gretchen, and their four adult children.

title: Navigating the AI Transformation: Understanding AI's Impact on Work & Education
abstract: As AI trends evolve, the future of our professions and work will look very different, even a few years from now. While many transformations have been predicted, in this talk we’ll review industry data to see how AI use cases are evolving and being adopted. Join us for an evidence-based discussion of both the challenges and possibilities as we navigate this technological inflection point together and walk away with actionable insights on how to prepare for the fast-breaking changes brought on by AI.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Lisa Cohen is Head of Data for Anthropic. She leads Data Science and Analytics Engineering for the company, and enabling data-informed strategies for Product, Compute, Sales, Marketing, Societal Impact, etc. Previously she held Data leadership roles at Google as Director of Data Science for Gemini, Assistant and Search Platforms, as well as Head of Data Science & Engineering at Twitter. Lisa also worked at Microsoft for 17 years, leading Data Science for Azure and starting her career as a product manager in Visual Studio. She holds Bachelor and Masters degrees from Harvard in Applied Mathematics. You can follow Lisa on LinkedIn and Medium.

title: The Power of Task Graphs for Data Pipeline Orchestration
abstract: Join us for a demo walkthrough of the latest task graph capabilities for deploying, orchestrating, monitoring and debugging transformation pipelines. We will deploy a new version of a task graph using declarative pipeline definitions and Git repo scripts/notebooks and graph observability, including serverless configuration, live view and retry attempts, all in Snowsight. We will also cover an error debugging case and deploying a fix via a new version.

speakers.bio: Jan is the product manager for data pipeline observability at Snowflake since 2022.

title: Transforming Manufacturing Data: Illumina's Journey with Apache Iceberg
abstract: In the rapidly evolving landscape of genomic technology, Illumina stands at the forefront. Discover how Illumina modernized its data management using Apache Iceberg and Snowflake, enhancing data accessibility, real-time analytics and operational efficiency. Learn about the challenges faced, implementation strategies, and the significant business value achieved in Illumina's manufacturing processes. Join this session for valuable insights into Illumina's transformative journey, success stories and future directions.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Stephen Horn serves as a data architect at Illumina, where he merges his academic foundation in biotechnology with his self-taught proficiency in programming to bridge the divide between genomics and data technologies. With Spark, Kafka, Iceberg, and Snowflake, Stephen designed and implemented scalable data solutions within Illumina’s manufacturing. His work enabled real-time analytics/automation and enabled data-driven decision-making. Collaborating closely with cross-functional teams, he ensures that the data solution not only supports production goals but also facilitates the ramp-up of new product lines, driving innovation and efficiency across Illumina’s Global Operations.

title: Architecting Scalable AI Applications: Under the Hood
abstract: From model development to deployment, scaling AI applications requires a deep understanding of data pipelines, infrastructure and software engineering best practices. Join our panel of data scientists, data engineers and developers as they break down real-world AI workflows, discuss challenges like model drift and latency, and explore how to optimize AI systems for performance, efficiency and cost-effectiveness.

speakers.job_title: COO and Chief Data & Analytics Officer

speakers.bio: Maria J. Marti is the founder and CEO of ZeroError, a New York-based AI company focused on data quality. She is an engineer with more than 20 years of leadership experience in Fortune 100 companies, working in the USA as CFO and CRO of key divisions (e.g. American Express and MetLife) and in leadership positions advising CEOs and the Board of Directors. Maria has a strong background in Supply Chain & Operations and she worked in Accenture (Barcelona and Chicago) implementing SAP APO and BW over 6 years. Maria studied Industrial Engineering at Universitat Politecnica de Barcelona and holds an MBA from the University of Chicago Booth School of Business.

Belen is a seasoned professional with over 11 years of experience in developing and leading Analytics and AI teams within major corporations such as Siemens and Continental. Currently, she heads the AI team at Siemens Digital Industries, where she spearheads the creation of innovative AI solutions designed to enhance productivity for both internal employees and Siemens customers, ultimately driving revenue growth for the company

title: Build a Product Recommendation Engine in Snowflake for Real-Time Personalization
abstract: Join our hands-on lab to build a retail-focused product recommendation engine using Snowflake's Snowpark ML. Discover how to harness predictive analytics for dynamic personalization and integrate a real-time API in Snowpark Container Services for seamless deployment. This session covers feature engineering, model training, and API-driven personalization, empowering you to create tailored customer experiences and boost engagement with your customers.

speakers.job_title: Principal Architect, Customer Technical Advisor, Marketing and Advertising
speakers.bio: Luke is the Principal Industry Architect for Marketing at Snowflake, helping brands use Snowflake to power the core of their marketing data and AI needs. He has a decade of experience in customer engagement and communication platforms, working with enterprise customers on cross-channel communication strategy and automation. Before this role at Snowflake, he helped build the martech and adtech ecosystem on top of Snowflake as a Partner SE. Prior to joining Snowflake, he led the solutions engineering team at MessageGears, where he helped household brands make data platforms, like Snowflake, the centerpiece of their martech stack.

Aric Wang is currently a Senior AI Specialist Engineer at Snowflake. His role involves helping the world's largest Telecommunications, Media, and Entertainment Enterprises utilize Machine Learning and Generative AI technologies on the Snowflake Cloud Data Platform. A lifelong technologist, Aric has been professionally pitching, solutioning, and implementing technology for over a decade. In his spare time, Aric enjoys playing tennis, travelling, and hiking around the Pacific Northwest where he is based.

speakers.job_title: RVP, Solution Engineering - Communications, Media & Entertainment
speakers.bio: Drew has been at Snowflake for 3 years in Solution Engineering and FCTO. He currently runs Solution Engineering across Communications, Media, and Entertainment. Drew has over 10 years of Media and Entertainment experience in the data space at Warner Bros. Discovery, A+E Networks, and an analytics start-up who worked with many of his same customers today. As a former Snowflake customer, his passion for data is only superseded by his passion for customer success. In his down time, he enjoys working out, golfing, and being bossed around by his cat.

title: Building Agentic Applications in Snowflake
abstract: This hands-on bootcamp teaches participants to design and deploy intelligent data agents using Snowflake Cortex. These agents orchestrate multi-step operations by retrieving and reasoning over structured and unstructured data with Cortex Analyst and Cortex Search. The session covers securely integrating AI into enterprise workflows, managing governance, and driving faster, informed decisions with real-time insights. Attendees will build a functional data agent capable of processing natural language requests, retrieving enterprise data, and generating intelligent responses within Snowflake. Completion of the lab earns a custom bootcamp badge.

speakers.bio: With experience in big data, data science, and machine learning Dash Desai is able to apply 18+ years of full-stack, hands-on software and data engineering skills to help build solutions that solve business problems and surface trends that shape markets in new ways than imagined before. As a Senior Lead Developer Advocate at Snowflake, he is passionate about evaluating new ideas, trends, and helping articulate how technology can address a given business problem. Dash Desai has worked for global enterprises and in agile environments–for tech startups in the Bay Area in varying verticals, such as VoIP, Social Gaming, Digital Health, NoSQL database, and Data Cloud platforms.

Arun Agarwal is currently leading the AI product marketing initiatives at Snowflake. With prior experience at Salesforce, where he contributed to their product marketing team with a focus on data and integration, Arun brings over 15 years of diverse expertise. Holding a PhD in STEM, Arun’s achievements include co-authoring several patents and international scientific research articles. Passionate about cutting-edge technology, he is committed to driving innovation and shaping the future of the industry.

title: Building Data Trust from the Ground Up: Baylor Scott & White Health’s Journey
abstract: How do you build data trust when your data strategy is still evolving? That’s the challenge Baylor Scott & White Health is tackling head-on. With a growing footprint in Snowflake and a plan to eliminate data chaos, they’re laying the groundwork for better governance, stronger risk management, and higher-quality data for analytics and AI in Snowflake. Join Adnan Jafri, Director, Data Governance, as he shares how his team is launching a federated governance program and leveraging Ataccama to finally gain visibility, control and confidence in their data.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Adnan Jafri is the Enterprise Director for Data Governance at Baylor Scott & White Health, where he architected and deployed a federated governance model that embeds data quality, observability, and stewardship into enterprise pipelines. He led the MVP rollout of the Ataccama platform—aligning definitions across analytic and business domains and empowering a cross-functional Data Governance Committee as first-line guardians of trust. Adnan’s proven approach builds “first bricks” of trust by uniting policy, process, and people to drive confident, value-creating decisions.

title: C.H. Robinson Turns Complex JSON Data into Cortex Analyst Gold in Record Time
abstract: Learn how C.H. Robinson used DataPancake (a Snowflake Native App) to migrate their Databricks solution to Snowflake. In record time, C.H. Robinson created accurate and secure data pipelines using Dynamic Tables and policy-infused views to flatten complex JSON from Kafka streams. DataPancake also enabled C.H. Robinson to create a Cortex AI-enhanced data glossary and Cortex Analyst semantic models, resulting in cost savings, reduced engineering time, and increased data availability and quality.

speakers.bio: Jon Farr, Co-Founder and CEO/CTO of TDAA Technologies, leads the vision, execution, and development of DataPancake™, an innovative, patent-pending data engineering tool on Snowflake Marketplace. Jon has over 30 years of experience in the technology and data industry. He has a proven track record of developing innovative data models and enterprise applications across a wide range of sectors, including transportation, benefit management, low-code generation, real estate, and finance. His experience and expertise has fueled his passion to create disruptive data technology solutions that accelerate the availability of secure and accurate data.

Neil Rausch has been a Principal Data Engineer at C.H. Robinson for the past 13 years, where he has held various roles in data engineering. Over the last 3 years, Neil has focused on migrating on-prem SQL reporting to Snowflake. This includes end-to-end architecture, setting up authentication, managing privileges, and ingesting raw, semi-structured, and unstructured data. He has also helped build transformation layers and implement data governance. Recently, Neil has started enabling Cortex and AI within Snowflake, empowering teams to fully leverage its capabilities. His expertise ensures robust security, compliance, and optimized data infrastructure, driving innovation and efficiency.

title: DevDive: Real-World AI Conversations
abstract: Step away from the booth and into the conversation. DevDive is a new Dev Day experience designed for builders who want to connect over real-world challenges in AI development — no slides, no panels, just hands-on topics and open dialogue. Join small-group discussions hosted by community experts, each centered around a prompt designed to spark thoughtful, use-case-driven exchange. If you're interested in vibe coding, scaling agents or just getting started with AI and Snowflake, there’s a seat at the table for you.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

title: How HubSpot Efficiently Manages Spend on Snowflake
abstract: HubSpot has over 50 analytics engineers, all of whom can build models as needed with few blockers. While that encourages speed and creativity, it also means model performance isn’t prioritized and similar models spin up regularly — creating confusion and high costs! This was the impetus to expose Snowflake cost and data asset performance across teams. Learn how this initiative created cost visibility, encouraged stewardship of teams’ assets and ultimately enabled spend management and efficient scaling as reporting needs grew.

speakers.bio: I’m super passionate about making data feel less intimidating to people regardless of role. I have about nine years of professional data experience, five at HubSpot and previously at Toyota among other places. In those years, I’ve held different data-related roles such as Data Analyst, Analytics Engineer, Team Lead, and now an Analytics Engineer Manager. Fun fact is that I figured out I loved data while in college, but I believe my parents knew when I was around the age of 10 because I presented 15 pages of data to them concluding Santa is not real, and they affirmed my hypothesis by telling me to not show my younger siblings.

title: How Streamlit in Snowflake Boosts Productivity at Discover Financial Services
abstract: In this session, we will explore how the Analytics Team from the Customer Experience Office at Discover Financial Services leveraged Streamlit in Snowflake to automate the data entry process, significantly enhancing efficiency and productivity. By transitioning from manual data entry in Excel to a streamlined process using Streamlit in Snowflake, we eliminated data entry gaps and automated the generation of score card reports and productivity metrics. This end-to-end automation saves hours of manual work and ensures data accuracy and consistency. Join us to learn about the implementation process, challenges faced, and the transformative impact of this automation on our data pipeline.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Senior Business Reporting & Data Analyst with 6+ years of experience in data management, process automation, and team collaboration. At Discover Financial, I led ETL automation, cutting processing time by 40%, and implemented a Jira workflow to streamline development. I utilize SAS, Tableau, SQL, and Python, to transform complex data into actionable insights. I'm also very passionate about leveraging technology for business solutions, I thrive on innovation and continuous learning to drive meaningful impact.

At Discover, I lead a team of data scientists in the development and deployment of NLP-based deep learning models that classify consumer complaints, transforming customer feedback into actionable intelligence. I also lead the charge in implementing Gen AI strategies across our Customer Experience Value Stream, bridging the gap between advanced technology and impactful business outcomes. I am a strategic thinker, focused on leveraging data to drive innovation and informed decision-making.

title: PyData + Snowflake: Better Together
abstract: In this talk, we’ll highlight how Snowflake seamlessly integrates with the PyData ecosystem. We'll explore this through two key pillars: APIs and runtimes. First, we’ll dive into how we built pandas on Snowflake using the Modin open source framework and interoperability with other PyData libraries. Then, we’ll discuss our investment in runtime compatibility, ensuring our packages integrate smoothly with foundational PyData libraries through runtime environments in Snowflake Notebooks. Our philosophy is simple: Bring your favorite libraries and customize your runtime – with Snowflake, you get an accelerated PyData experience within a powerful, trusted and integrated environment.

speakers.bio: Doris Lee leads Python and data science experience across Snowflake's products. Previously, Doris was CEO and co-founder of Ponder (acquired by Snowflake in 2023), the company behind the open-source project Modin. Doris received her Ph.D. from the UC Berkeley RISE Lab and School of Information in 2021, where she developed open-source tools that help data scientists explore and understand their data. She received Forbes 30 under 30 for Enterprise Technology in 2023.

Savannah is a product leader for Python Developer Experience at Snowflake, working to make Snowflake the best platform for Python developers, data engineers, and data scientists. Before Snowflake, Savannah led product for Docker’s runtime, working on foundational technology for the container ecosystem, including Docker Engine (moby/moby) and Docker CLI. She also previously worked at Microsoft on the Azure Developer CLI and was the product manager for the Pylance language server. Savannah is also a Python Core Developer, maintaining the new JIT compiler introduced in Python 3.13 and the argparse module in the standard library.

title: Teeing Up Success: Accelerate Modernization to Snowflake with Informatica
abstract: Transitioning to a cloud-based data platform with a strong data foundation is crucial for data-driven innovation. Join us in this session to discover how TaylorMade is modernizing its enterprise data analytics platform with the Snowflake AI Data Cloud, leveraging Informatica’s Intelligent Data Management Cloud (IDMC). This enables the customer to reinforce its leadership position in the industry through omnichannel growth, new brand expansions and enhanced customer experience. Also learn how you can leverage Informatica Claire's AI-powered capabilities to streamline the data integration lifecycle, automate data management tasks and deliver a comprehensive understanding of enterprise data assets.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Errick Coughlin is the Chief Architect for North America at Informatica. Mr. Coughlin has extensive financial services experience including leading data architecturewhere he specialized in analytics around loan origination and servicing. Mr. Coughlin acted as a senior data architecture supporting key defense initiatives at USAF Air Force Material Command. Mr. Coughlin was chief data engineer for 10 years at Kroger (KR), a Fortune 20 retail chain, where he led cloud modernization efforts in multiple clouds for both data and AI for merchandising, supply chain and accounting modernization. Mr. Coughlin has been with Informatica since 2021 leading architectures for Fortune 100 companies.

title: The Simplicity Behind AI: Scaling Laws and the Road to Human-Level Capabilities
abstract: Anthropic co-founder Jared Kaplan reveals why AI's rapid advancement toward human-level capabilities is surprisingly straightforward — and potentially imminent. Through the lens of scaling laws and his unique perspective as both a physicist and AI researcher, Jared will explain how compute, data and simple architectural principles are driving unprecedented progress. This technical deep-dive will examine current AI capabilities, near-term challenges and what developers should expect as we approach this pivotal moment in technology history.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Vivek is SVP of Engineering at Snowflake. He previously co-founded Neeva, which was acquired by Snowflake in May 2023. At Neeva, Vivek pioneered the development of a next-generation AI search engine for consumers. He also spent over a decade at Google as a VP of engineering in various technical leadership roles, where he led the YouTube monetization team and was part of the core group that launched the Google Now assistant. Vivek holds both an M.S. and PhD in Electrical and Computer Engineering from the University of Illinois, Urbana Champaign, and a BS from IIT Bombay.

Jared Kaplan is the Chief Science Officer and Co-Founder of Anthropic, an AI lab whose research and products put safety at the frontier. As a public benefit corporation, Anthropic is dedicated to building AI systems that benefit humanity. Jared’s research on scaling laws revolutionized the AI industry by providing a framework for understanding and predicting the behavior of advanced AI systems, guiding resource allocation, and shaping the development of transformative technologies. Jared also helped pioneer Constitutional AI, an approach to AI development that aims to create AI systems that are constrained by and aligned with a set of predetermined principles and values. He spent the first 15 years of his career as a theoretical physicist in academia, and prior to co-founding Anthropic, Jared was instrumental in building GPT-3 and Codex at OpenAI. In addition to his work at Anthropic, Jared is an associate professor at Johns Hopkins University, where he has taught courses on the foundations of deep learning. He holds a Ph.D. in physics from Harvard University and a bachelor's degree in physics and mathematics from Stanford University.

title: Unlocking Alpha: AI-Powered Earnings Call Analysis with S&P Global & Snowflake
abstract: This hands-on lab explores the integration of Snowflake Notebooks and Cortex AI with S&P Global Market Intelligence’s Machine-Readable Transcripts dataset to analyze executive communication during earnings calls. Learn how to use LLMs and vector similarity techniques to score executives on transparency and responsiveness. Research shows that executives who proactively address market queries with clear answers consistently outperform their peers, while those who are reactive or off-topic tend to underperform. A portfolio strategy based on these communication styles has been shown to generate +515bps of annualized alpha. Join us for a hands-on experience.

speakers.bio: As an AI/ML Principal Archtect at Snowflake, Mats advises customers on how they can run their AI/ML workloads on Snowflake. Mats has been working with analytics and information centric systems since the mid 90s and since 2010 he has focused primary on AI/ML, started with the SPSS products, later with Oracle Advanced Analytics (R and in-database machine learning), Spark ML and Python open-source frameworks like scikit-learn to now Gen AI and LLMs. Mats has supported customers in multiple industries both in a hands-on capacity as well advising around strategies and roadmap

Henry Chiang is a Quantitative Researcher at the Quantitative Research & Solutions (QRS) team at S&P Global Market Intelligence, specializing in systematic equity research and alpha extraction using large language models (LLM). Leveraging his expertise in NLP and LLM, Henry led the technical development of several impactful AI research projects, including ‘Questioning the Answers’, ‘Earning Seasons Report’ and ‘Three tools for Trump Tariffs 2.0’. His work has been well-received at conferences and cited by major media outlets, including Financial Times, Forbes, etc.

title: Unlocking Customer Intelligence: How Zscaler Redefines Account Insights with AI
abstract: Zscaler is transforming customer intelligence with a powerful platform that aggregates data from numerous sources to provide a 360° view of customer accounts. Leveraging next-gen reports and ML models, they deliver deep account insights—helping account teams identify risks, prevent churn, and drive customer engagement. Powered by an enterprise chatbot with agentic workflows, these insights enable real-time, data-driven decisions. Join us to explore the future of AI-driven customer intelligence!

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Will is Director of AI products at Snowflake. Will came to Snowflake via the acquisition of TruEra where he was CEO/Co-Founder. TruEra built market-leading technology for evaluating, testing and monitoring predictive and generative AI applications. Will started working in AI/ML over 14 years ago when he was Chief Product Officer at BloomReach where he led the development of ML-powered commerce search, marketing and SEO software. Prior to BloomReach, Will worked in product management at Clearwell Systems, Juniper Networks and Netscreen/Neoteris. Will is a graduate of Harvard Business School and Dartmouth College.

Ra serves as the Head of Advanced Data Engineering and Analytics at Zscaler, leading the architecture, strategy, and execution of the Enterprise Data Platform. With a focus on scalability, security, and business alignment, he drives the development of robust data pipelines, transformation processes, and seamless integration of diverse data sources to empower innovation across the organization. As a forward thinking leader, he also champions the operationalization of machine learning workflows and the adoption of Generative AI to unlock advanced analytics, automate processes, and drive strategic impact

title: What's New: Advanced Analytics with Cortex AISQL, Native Semantic Views, and More
abstract: Master AI-powered analytics in Snowflake. Learn how to leverage Snowflake's powerful AI SQL functions for advanced analytics on unstructured, time-series, and geospatial data. We'll also demonstrate how to use Semantic Views to bridge the gap between data and business understanding.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated
Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Josh Klahr is the Head of Analytics Product Management at Snowflake, where he is responsible for driving the core database product roadmap and strategy. Josh has over 25 years of experience working with data and analytics. Prior to joining Snowflake, Josh was VP of product management for Splunk, where he was responsible for the Splunk Enterprise and Splunk Cloud product lines. He has also spent time in product management roles at Yahoo!, Pivotal Software, Greenplum, and AtScale.

Renee Huang is a seasoned product leader at Snowflake, where she drives the snowflake AI-SQL initiative, a core component of Snowflake’s cutting-edge unstructured data batch analytics. With a keen focus on enabling intuitive, large-scale data interactions using AI, Renee plays a pivotal role in shaping the future of AI-powered data analytics. Prior to her current role, Renee was the engineering lead for the Cortex Analyst (Text-to-SQL), where she helped pioneer natural language interfaces for data access—bridging the gap between human intent and SQL generation.

title: Accelerate Data Engineering: Terminal to Production-Ready Snowflake Pipelines
abstract: Data-driven success needs fast analytics, but manual pipeline deployments slow things down. See how organizations transform data operations using software practices in Snowflake engineering. Using terminal-first methods, version-controlled SQL and automated checks, teams cut deployment time while keeping reliability. Learn to implement Git-based changes, data-aware CI/CD, and infrastructure-as-code to speed up insights.

speakers.bio: Veteran tech innovator with over 20 years in the trenches. As an author and developer advocate, I'm on a mission to demystify data and cloud technologies. My passion? Empowering developers to push boundaries and create the impossible. With a track record of crafting enterprise solutions across diverse industries, I bring battle-tested insights to the dev community. Let's code the future together.

Myles Borins is a Toronto based product leader with deep experience in shaping technology, innovating product, and building developer platforms. He Leads Product for Developer Platform at Snowflake where he is focused on empowering individuals building on Snowflake's AI Data Cloud. He previously held roles at GitHub, Google, and IBM. As a founder of the OpenJS Foundation, a former TSC member of the Node.js project, and former vice-president of ECMA International, Myles spent close to a decade steering the evolution of the JavaScript ecosystem. Beyond his professional endeavours, he is passionate about the open web and fostering healthy communities.

title: Data Engineering on Apache Iceberg™ Data Lakes
abstract: Apache Iceberg provides an open storage standard that can democratize your data stored in disparate data lakes by providing freedom and interoperability to use various data processing engines. Join this session to explore Snowflake’s latest advancements for data engineering on Snowflake Iceberg Tables. We’ll dive into newly launched features that enhance interoperability as well as bring Snowflake’s ease of use to your Iceberg data lakes.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Sahaj Saini leads the metadata product area at Snowflake with experience in cloud data platforms, database metadata & transaction subsystems, analytics, and business continuity. At Snowflake, he led the development of Cross-Cloud Replication & Failover, Personal Database, performance optimizations, as well as go-to-market strategies and product packaging. Previously at Microsoft, he worked on data virtualization and cloud data services, helping enterprises integrate and analyze data across multiple platforms. Sahaj holds a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science w from Purdue University and is passionate about building products that simplify data management and analysis.

Ron Ortloff is a Product Manager on the Data Lake team at Snowflake. His primary area of focus is on Apache Iceberg integration with Snowflake. He is passionate about open source and the freedom of choice it provides customers. Prior to joining Snowflake, Ron worked for Microsoft where he held various roles from solution architect to engineering manager where he led building large, complex big data solutions.

title: Observe Inc and RelationalAI: Unlocking AI Power with Snowflake
abstract: This presentation explores how RelationalAI leverages Snowflake’s Data Cloud to build its AI-powered knowledge graph co-processor while utilizing Observe’s observability platform to analyze and enhance customer experiences. Join this session to explore the critical role of observability in developing AI applications on Snowflake and how the collaboration between Observe, Snowflake and RelationalAI delivers a secure, high-quality AI solution for Snowflake customers.

speakers.bio: Ary is a Senior Engineer and Tech Lead with a strong background in cloud infrastructure and observability. He leads engineering teams focused on building reliable, scalable systems and driving platform excellence. More recently, Ary has been working on a strategic collaboration between RelationalAI and Observe to operationalize Native App telemetry on Snowflake. Prior to his current role, he worked on graph analytics and cloud architecture at Oracle Labs.

Jiayuan Chen is a Staff Software Development Engineer at Observe. He is passionate about designing and building large scale data-intensive applications, and has been working in the areas of observability, data lake, query engine, ingestion, and analytics. He keeps up with latest technologies and innovates things that spark joy.

title: Rethinking Data in the Age of AI: Better Tools, Worse Decisions?
abstract: Two decades into the big-data era, we still get so much wrong in fundamental data analysis. Now, with the rise of AI promising easier-than-ever access to insights, are we actually going to become more "data driven" or are we going to make even more mistakes? Join this session to explore the common pitfalls we face today and how the role of data professionals can evolve alongside AI to work better with data.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Amanda Kelly serves as Director of Product Experiences at Snowflake, where she oversees all of Snowflake’s product experiences. Prior to Snowflake she was Co-founder and COO of Streamlit (acquired by Snowflake). Amanda also led product and operations for Zoox (acquired by Amazon) and several Google X projects. She has an MBA from Stanford GSB and holds several industry patents.

title: Rockwell's AI-Powered Maintenance Copilot: Transforming Industrial Operations
abstract: In industrial maintenance, accessing critical asset data quickly is essential for preventing downtime and optimizing efficiency. Rockwell's Fiix Maintenance Copilot, powered by Cortex Analyst, transforms how maintenance teams interact with data. Using natural language queries, technicians and managers instantly access insights on asset health, failure risks and maintenance practices, eliminating manual searches through documents. This AI-driven approach accelerates knowledge access, speeds up technician onboarding and prevents breakdowns. Join us to discover how we embedded Snowflake Cortex Analyst into our copilot and learn from early customer success stories.

speakers.bio: Luda is a creative data-minded product and people leader with a diverse and vast 18+ years of experience in Information Technology and Data Science, 11+ years leadership in large-scale organizations, and 4+ years driving startups, 7+ years in Product Management in cross-functional teams, with the most recent role as Group Product Manager in AI and innovative projects, and advanced academic background with Ph.D. in Computer Science and AI.

title: Anaconda Gateway's Transformative Real-Time Analytics Journey
abstract: Anaconda Gateway is a transformative product leveraging Snowflake's data infrastructure and real-time analytics. Join us to learn how we leverage Snowflake for three different use cases: Solve license tracking and customer value challenges, achieving 95% accuracy in license usage by connecting organization IDs with user activity. Conduct predictive analytics and AI-driven recommendations for package usage and security vulnerabilities. Integrate telemetry data to drive insights for both Anaconda teams and customers while maintaining open-source community trust through robust data governance.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Tom is the VP Product for Data at Anaconda and leads our effort to improve internal data infrastructure and capabilities as well as building data products for our external customers.

title: A Seamless Migration Story: Paramount's Leap from Redshift to Snowflake
abstract: Two years ago, Paramount wrestled with a complex, sluggish Redshift setup: slow loads, redundant code and scaling headaches. Enter Snowflake: a platform that promised to transform the fragmented system into a seamless, high-performance cloud solution. This talk covers Paramount's zero-downtime migration using homegrown tools such as Snowbridge and Avalanche, the Zero-Copy Cloning speed boost and the ways Snowflake Data Clean Rooms and Secure Data Sharing helped streamline ETLs. Join Paramount for a technical deep dive into how it scaled smarter, moved faster and redefined data agility — because in data, performance is king, stability is the kingdom, and they are building an empire.

Recommendations (Rule Based): Data Architectures, including Lakehouse, Mesh, Lake and Warehouse (Topic)

speakers.bio: Ian is a seasoned technology leader with over 20 years of experience in software engineering and project management, specializing in developing robust solutions and leading high-performance teams in dynamic environments. He’s worked across multiple industries from media to ecommerce, and is a Techstars alumnus. He holds a degree in Aerospace Engineering which he never used because he didn’t know SpaceX would be a thing.

Alexey is a veteran engineer with 20 years of experience. He has built and scaled systems across the entire stack, from data ETLs to building front-end applications—he’s done it all. His keen eye for design and user experience always ensures his software is intuitive and user-friendly, whether it be a beautiful UI or a command line interface.

title: Grow Your Career in Data and AI AMA
abstract: The rapid advancements in AI are reshaping the job market — creating both exciting opportunities and new challenges for career growth. Whether you're starting your career, switching careers or looking to future-proof your skills, this interactive panel and AMA session will provide practical insights on how to grow your career in data and AI. Experts from elearning platforms DeepLearning.AI and DataCamp and from VC firm A Capital will share their research on the skills most in demand, how to acquire them and how to land your next job. Bring your questions and leave with actionable advice to grow your career in data and AI.

Note: Theaters are first come, first seated

speakers.bio: Carrie Farrell specializes in helping entrepreneurs build a solid foundation for everything related to their recruiting and HR functions. Carrie, the second engineering recruiter hired at Google, spent twelve years at the company and was most recently Head of Talent Acquisition for Google Ventures. Her team worked with 200+ portfolio companies partnering with founders to create hiring strategies to acquire the best technical talent. Before Google Ventures, Carrie led Google’s Partnerships team for Google.org and the recruiting teams for Global People Operations groups. Carrie has led staffing and HR teams for Google in the United States, the United Kingdom, Australia, Africa and Japan. She received her bachelor’s degree in political science and urban planning from Cal Poly.
Ryan is an astrophysicist by training, and now works with Andrew Ng and a team of passionate educators and AI experts to create learning experiences that help people build with AI.

Jonathan Cornelissen is the CEO and co-founder of DataCamp, where he has led the company’s growth to upskill over 16 million learners and support 4,000+ enterprise clients in data and AI. He is passionate about data science, AI, education, and entrepreneurship, driving innovative learning solutions at their intersection. Jonathan holds a PhD in financial econometrics, specializing in statistical modeling, risk, and financial market behavior.

Arnnon Geshuri joined Snowflake as Chief People Officer in September 2024. Before Snowflake, Arnnon was the Chief People Officer at Teladoc Health, Inc., a global leader in virtual healthcare services and analytics. Previously, Arnnon served as Vice President of Human Resources (CHRO) at Tesla, where he led global people and workplace operations from 2009-2017. Under his leadership, Tesla grew from a 400-person scrappy startup to an integrated sustainable energy company with over 35,000 employees worldwide. Before his tenure at Tesla, Arnnon was the senior director of recruiting operations at Google. As the company’s first Chief Staffing Architect, he designed and built the company’s legendary recruitment organization and talent acquisition strategy, scaling the workforce from 2,500 to over 25,000 employees. Arnnon’s strategic approach helped Google become a destination for top-tier talent, setting new standards in technology recruitment. Arnnon also held leadership roles at E*TRADE Financial, where he was Vice President of Human Resources and Director of Global Staffing. During his time at E*TRADE, he navigated multiple acquisitions and spearheaded a significant talent expansion, adding 2,000 employees in just 18 months to position the company as a leading fintech innovator. Earlier in his career, Arnnon worked at Applied Materials where he managed the corporate talent acquisition function during a period of aggressive global growth, particularly in the Asia Pacific region. He also launched a startup focused on project management software for the service industry and served as an organizational effectiveness consultant for New United Motors Manufacturing (NUMMI), a joint venture of Toyota and General Motors. Beyond his corporate roles, Arnnon sits on several startup and academic advisory boards, including the Santa Clara University Leavey School of Business Master of Science Program in Business Analytics. Arnnon holds a bachelor’s degree in Psychology from the University of California, Irvine and a master’s in Industrial and Organizational Psychology from San Jose State University.

title: New Snowpipe Streaming Architecture: Deliver 10GB/s at 5s time-to-query latency
abstract: Take an in-depth look at our next-generation real-time data ingestion service, designed to address the challenges of today and scale to the needs of tomorrow. We will share Snowflake's vision on streaming ingest, then dive into how we enhanced the current Snowpipe Streaming architecture, with multi-language support, ingest-time clustering and transformation capabilities. We’ll demo unified ingestion endpoints to deliver seamless, dynamic throughput — from trickle to high throughputs — without any manual reconfiguration. Whether you’re seeking more flexibility or building real-time analytics without performance bottlenecks, Snowpipe Streaming works for any scale.

speakers.bio: Software Engineer working on exciting streaming related projects such as Snowpipe Streaming.

Chase Thomas is a Technical Product Manager at Snowflake, focused on real-time data streaming and Kafka-based architectures. He brings deep experience designing scalable, event-driven systems that enable fast, reliable insights. Before joining Snowflake, Chase worked on scaling cloud native Kafka services and building real-time dam safety monitoring systems, experience that sparked his passion for streaming data and time-critical infrastructure. Today, he helps Snowflake customers unlock the full potential of streaming technologies for modern analytics and operations.

title: What's New: Snowpark Container Services
abstract: Learn how to deploy the best-in-class OSS models in Snowpark Container Services for AI-driven insights in Snowflake. Join us for new feature overviews, best practices, and a demo featuring an advanced agentic use case.

speakers.bio: Muzz Imam is Product Lead for Snowpark Container Services (including Native Apps). Muzz has been in the tech industry for over 15 years. He has a Masters degree in Computer Science specializing in Artificial Intelligence from University of Southern California and holds a Masters of Business Administration (MBA) from University of Washington. He specializes in Containers, Compute and AI/ML. Previously he was the Product Manager for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) for a number of years before joining Snowflake to solve some advanced compute problems for AI, ML and Data Cloud.

speakers.job_title: Sr. Global Manager, Solutions Engineering - SI Partners
speakers.bio: Senior leader on the Partner Solution Engineering team primarily focused on System Integrators (SI) function and responsible for driving AI/ML and DE/DL initiatives.

title: [Code Block] Party
abstract: Expand your network, find new mentors, join meetups, earn exclusive swag and wrap up the day at our Dev Day [Code Block] Party!
